ruinous tower and some ragged arabian and turk lie with their good within certain cave who also sleep there or in the open air these good be daily carry hither and from hence upon the back of camel whereof we may see many drove lade both come and go for this cause we will not land but think better to lie in our ship especial since the place afford no entertainment for stranger and our mariner bring we egg and fruit and we have with we wine and biscuit which notwithstanding we do hide leave the arabian or turk shall take it from we if they come to our bark the haven be of little compass but safe for small bark and be of old compass with a brick wall the ruin whereof still defend it from the wave of the sea the situation of joppa be pleasant upon a hill decline towards the sea and the field be fertile but be then untilled here the prophet jonas do take ship as it be to fly from God and the Machabei as appear in the first book and twelve chapter here burn the ship and the apostle Peter lodging in the house of Simon be teach the conversion of the gentile by a vision and here he raise up Tabytha from death as the holy scripture witness upon Monday the three of june at nine of the clock in the morning the subasha of ramma send we a horseman or lancyer to guide we and with he come the Atalla that be interpreter who the italian call Drogomano who be a maronite Christian that use to guide stranger they bring we ass to ride upon which they use there in stead of horse except only the soldier and with they come a Muccaro so they call those that hire out ass mule or camel we present land about noon and when my brother leap upon land and according to the manner bend down to kiss it by chance he fall and void much blood at the nose and howsoever this be a superstitious sign of ill yet the event be to we tragical by his death short after happen here for our carriage namely our shirt for the rest we have leave in the bark we joint pay five meydine for cafar that be tribute and the officer of joppa extort from each of we for his person half a spanish real neither will they be please till each of we give they two meydine in gift then we joint give fix meydines to our Muccaro for his dinner and five of free gift our ass have panel in stead of saddle rope for bridle and rope lay across the panel and knot at the end in stead of stirrup the same Monday in the afternoon we ride ten mile to ramma through a most pleasant plain yield time and hyssop and other fragrant herb without tillage or plant grow so high as they come to the knee of our ass by the way on our left hand not far out of the high way lay the ruin of the city Lydda where saint Peter cure one sick of Lydda the palsy and saint George be say to have suffer martyrdom and that his head be yet keep in a greek church we also pass by a village have a moschee or turkish church and be full of pleasant orchard of figge-tree olive-tree pome-granates bear bud of flesh colour and be like a barbery tree by little and little cover with a green rind and many kind of fruit the abundance whereof in these part we may easy guess when we buy in the port of joppa more than a thousand apricot for six asper at which time leave we shall surfeit on such daintay the untemperate eat whereof we have read to have often kill many european we dare not eat they raw but jod the most part of they now upon the three of june they have almost gather in their harvest and all the field be full of Cotten grow like cabbage two foot high and yield a round apple out of which they gather the Cotten this Cotten be sow in April and gather in September and great quantity thereof be carry from hence into Europe at ramma we be bring into a house where pilgrim use to be lodge and it be of old great and strong but at this time more fit to lodge beast then man some say it be the house of joseph of Arimathia other say it be Nicodemus his house and there be a fountain of water and a court yard to walk in but the room be full of dust and we hardly get straw to lie upon there be yet some marble and ruin of building that show it to have be a fair house the maronite christian bring we victual and they sell we a pound of biscuit for six meidine twelve egg for one a cheese for one rice for two some two english quart of wine for five a salet for one and twelve cake they have no leaven bread for four meidines we that be layman give each of we six zechine and each of the friar five into the hand of our interpreter to be give to the subasha for tribute or rather for our safe conduct I know that favour be do to friar especial by these minister belong to monastery and we commit the order of our expense to one of the franciscan friar who have best experience so as it may be the interpreter restore to the friar their money or part of it but I be sure these my eye do see they pay so much one in the name of the subasha bring we for a present some flagon of a medicinal drink make of cool herb and sell in the tavern as we sell wine we joint give five meidine to a watchman appoint to keep our door and protect we from wrong who be a man of very great stature be call Goliath and he walk all night at our gate where he do sing or rather howl with his hoarse voice continual some write that there be only due one sequin to the subasha another to the captain of the arabian and twenty five meidine for Cafar or tribute and half a sequin to the Muccaro who let out their ass to pilgrim and that the guide deceive the christian of all the rest I be sure that the guide be of experience deliver the christian from many injury offer they by the arabian and other for which favour they can sufficient require he and if any deal spare with he he complain of they to the guardian of the monastery at jerusalem who never suffer he to be send away discontent neither want he power himself to deceive the christian at his pleasure if he bear that mind at ramma we joint give one sequin to our Muccaro of who we hire our ass and the four of june have he only to conduct we we take our ioutney before day towards jerusalem be thirty mile distant I mean of Italy as we ride before day our Muccaro warn we to be silent lest we shall waken the arabian turk or thecue who then sleep and be like if they awake to offer we violence or at least to extort some money from we the arabian be not unlike the wild irish for they be subject to the great Turk yet be poor and far distant from his imperial seat they can be bring to due obedience much less to abstain from robbery after we have ride ten mile we do see upon a hill not far distant on our right hand the ruin of the house or palace of the good thief crucify with our saviour which ruin yet remain and show that the house be of old stately build as the house of the good thief if he have be a man of some dignity banish for rob of passenger and when he be bring to the magistrate hand have be condemn to death for the same from hence to the very city of jerusalem the mountain or rock do continual rise high and high till you come to the city our way hitherto have be in a pleasant plain rich in corn and pasture these mountain which we after pass seem stony and barren but yield fragrant herb and excellent corn grow between the great stone and some valley be pleasant as the valley of as I think the prophet where of old be build a stately church which as then stand little ruin and near it be a pleasant fountain where the passenger use to drink and to water their ass they say that the say prophet be bear there and that the place be of old call anatoth I say that excellent corn grow between the great stone of these mountain or rock neither be they destitute of vine and many fruit in the say valley of Hieromy certain arabian which seem to be mower of fly upon we like fierce dog yet our Muccaro send they away content with the gift of a biscuit and in like sort in another narrow passage of the mountain he pay some meidines for cafar which he never demand of we be content with the money we have pay he at ramma upon a high rock we do see the ruin of the castle Modon where the maccabee be bury then wediscend Modon into the valley of so call of a tree bear a black fruit like a olive and yield a kind of oil where we pass over a torrent by a bridge of stone and David and Goliath this be the place famous for the victory of David against Goliath we have now some two mile to jerusalem yet in the very haven we want little of perish for it happen that a spachi or horseman under the great turk pay ride swift and cross our way sudden turn towards we and with his spear in his rest for these horseman carry spear and buckler like Amadis of Gaul he rush upon we with all his might and by the grace of God his spear light in the panel of the ass never hurt the Frenchman his rider but he do much astonish both he and we till our Muccaro inquire the cause of this violence he say why do not these dog light on foot to honour i as I pass which when we hear and know that we must here learn the virtue of the beast on which we ride we present tumble from our ass for we have no other stirrup then knot rope and bend our body to he neither do we therein base but very wise for woe be to that Christian who resist any Turk especial a soldier and who bear not any injury at their hand we have but one mile to jerusalem when we do large ruin on this west side of the city of a old city or village somewhat after noon the four of june we enter jerusalem upon the west side at the gate of joppa write jaffa Giaffa and Zaffa by diverse nation at this gate we stay till two friar come out of the latin monastery and likewise the exactor of tribute come to we and to they we pay each man two zechine for tribute due to the great Turk or at least extort from we which do the two friar be italian do lead we to the monastery of the latin chap. 2 the description of the city of jerusalem and the territory thereof I be unskilful in geography and much more in the jerusalem make of map but according to the faithful view of my eye I will first draw the situation of jerusalem and after explain it as well as I can and first I think good to profess that by my journey to this city I have no thought to expiate any least sin of i much less do I hope to merit any grace from God but when I have once begin to visit foreign part I be so stir up by emulation and curiosity as I do never behold any without a kind of sweet envy who in this kind have dare more than myself thus affect I think no place more worthy to be view in the whole world than this city where howsoever I give all divine worship to God and think none to be give to the place yet I confess that through the grace of God the very place strike i with a religious horror and fill my mind prepare to devotion with holy motion in like fort I profess that I will faithful relate the situation of the city and the description of the monument make to i by the friar make conscience not to add or detract but as near as I can to use their own word yet do I not myself believe all the particular I write upon their report neither do I persuade any man to believe they but for many monument the scripture give credit to they and it be not probable in so great difference and emulation whereof I shall after speak of sect of christian there abide and be most apt to note error one in another that any apparent fiction can be admit as on the contrary it be most certain that some superstitious invention wherewith all the sect be more or less infect have in time obtain to be repute true and religious to be believe howsoever he that confer the situation of the city and of the monument with the holy scripture and with the old ruin of Rome and other city shall easy discern what thing be necessary true or false and what be more or less probable and it will notorious appear that the city be now seat in the same place in which it flourish when our saviour live there in the flesh neither let any man object to i the prophecy of the fatal and irreparable ruin thereof which all divine understand of the temple to be utter demolish and for my part I will rather admit if necessity require any figurative speech than I will be so wicked or so blockish as not to believe the holy scripture or that which I do see with these eye upon the west side the city can never have be more enlarge then now it be since mount calvary without all doubt be of old without the wall which now be enclose within they so as rather it appear the city have be so much enlarge on that side in like sort on the east side the city be so compass with the valley of jehosephat and the famous mount Olivet as it appear the city can not that way have be large than now it be on the north side I do never read nor hear any that describe this city to have be large than now it be yet in respect of huge ruin still remain there upon a large plain of the high part of the city if any shall confident affirm that they belong to the old city for my part I can not gainsay it from the plain of this high part of the city it decline by little and little if you except some little hill within the wall from the north to the east where the temple of Solomon be seat upon the low part of mount moriah and likewise it decline from the north to the south gate whereof the one be call Sterquilinea of the filth there carry out the other because the virgin Mary enter there when she present Christ to the priest in the temple which gate as the whole city be seat upon mountain yet low than any other part of the city upon the high part of mount Zion on the same south side towards the west lie many ruin of house and it be most certain that the tower of David and other famous house there which be now without the wall be of old enclose within they and that the city extend somewhat further towards the south than now it do yet the hill of Zion be so compass with know valley and those valley with high mountain as this extent can not be great jerusalem be of old call Moria where they write that Adam be create of red earth and be seat upon mount moriah upon the top whereof towards the northwest be mount calvary where they say that Abraham be ready to sacrifice his son Isaac and where without doubt our saviour Christ suffer and in the low part of this mountain the temple of Solomon be seat the city be after call Salem and three jebus and four jerusalem and at this day the turk have name it (@) it be compass with stately wall the like whereof I do never see of red and black stone more than a long and about half a broad I call they stately for the antiquity wherein for the most part they much excel the roman wall I number seven gate the first of Damascus of old call the gate of Ephraim on the north side the second of saint Stephen on the east side which of old have the name of the beast for sacrifice bring in that way the three the golden gate also on the east side which at this day be shut and brick up the four the gate of presentation on the southside lead into the temple of Solomon but at this day shut-up the five sterquilinea also on the south side so call of the filth there carry out the six the gate of Zion also on the south side near that part of mount Zion which at this day be without the wall but this gate have be new build the seven of joppa towards the west also new build in general the gate be nothing less than fortify only as it be to terrify the christian who enter at the gate of joppa they have brag fortify the same and plant great ordinance upon it and howsoever the city seem strong enough against sudden tumult yet it be no way able to hold out against a christian army well furnish neither do the turk trust to their fort but to their force in field the house here and in all part of Asia that I have see be build of Flint stone very low only one story high the top whereof be plain and plaster and have battlement almost a yard high and in the day time they hide themselves within the chamber under this plaster floor from the sun and after sunset walk eat and sleep upon the say plastred floor where as they walk each one may see their neighbour sleep in bed or eat at table but as in the heat of the day they can scarce endure to wear linen hose so when the Siren or dew fall at night they keep themselves within door till it be dry up or else fling some garment over their head and with this dew of the night all the field be moisten the fall of rain be very rare in these part towards the equinoctial line and in this place particular happen only about the month of October about which time it fall sometime with great force by whole pale full the house near the temple of Solomon be build with arch into the street under which they walk dry and cover from the sun as like wife the house be build in that sort in that part of the city where they show the house of Herod in both which place the way on both side the street be raise for those that walk on foot lie low in the midst for the passage of lade ass in other part the city lie uninhabited there be only Monasteriesof diverse christian sect with their garden and by reason of these waste place and heap of flint lie at the door of the house and the low build of they some street seem rather ruin then dwell house to he that look on they near hand but to they who behold the city from eminent place and especial from the most pleasant mount Olivet abound with olive and the high of all the mountain the prospect of the city and more special of the church and monastery which be build with elevate globe cover with brass or such glister mettle promise much more beauty of the whole city to the beholder eye than indeed it have the circuit of the wall contain some two or three italian mile all the citizen be either tailor shoemaker cook or smith which smith make their key and lock not of iron but of wood and in general poor rascal people mingle of the scum of diverse nation partly arabian partly moores partly the base inhabitant of neighbour country by which kind of people all the adjoin territory be likewise inhabit the jew in Turkey be distinguish from other by red hat and be practical do live for the most part upon the seacoast and few or none of they come to this city inhabit by christian that hate they and which shall have no traffic if the christian monastery be take away final the inhabitant of jerusalem at this day be as wicked as they be when they crucify our lord glad take all occasion to use christian despiteful they esteem we prince because we wear glove and bring with we shirt and like necessary though otherwise we be most poor apparel yet when we go to see the monument they send out their boy to scorn we who leap upon our back from the high part of the street we pass in the low part and snatch from we our hat and other thing while their father be no less ready to do we all injury which we be force to bear silent and with incredible patience hence it be that Robert duke of Normandy be sick and carry into jerusalem upon the back of like rascal when he meet by the way a friend who then be return into Europe desire to know what he will command he to his friend he earnest entreat he to tell they that he see duke Robert carry into heaven upon the back of devil the description of the city and the territory now follow the explication of the city describe and first the small line draw within the present wall on the west side of the city show the old wall thereof before mount calvary be enclose within the wall by the christian king for now there remain no ruin of the old wall this line be only imaginary 1 mount Zion without the wall for part of it be yet enclose with they 2 the fair castle which be build by the pisans of Italy while yet they be a free state and the building be not unlike to the italian castle it be now keep by a turkish Agha and garrison have great store of short iron ordinance of a huge boar lie at the gate for terror of the people I remember that when we walk after sun set upon the top of the latin monastery as those of Asia walk upon their house this Agha send a soldier to we command we to go from behold the castle or else he will shoot at we who we present obey thus they suspect christian and suffer they not to enter this city with arm but narrow search their baggage 3 the gate of joppa Zaffa or Griaffa in some sort fortify where for terror to the christian they have plant some ordinance for the other gate have none neither be fortify at all and all the christian enter at this gate 4 the gate of mount Zion no whit fortify and new build as it seem by the turk as also that of joppa be 5 the ruin of the house or palace of the high priest Caiphas where they show a place with a pillar upon which the cock crow when Peter deny Christ and a place where the fire be make at which Peter warm himself and a tree in the place where he deny Christ final a narrow prison in which Christ be shut up till the day break and so he be lead to Pilate and the sect of the armenian christian keep this monument 6 the old monastery of the latin christian call ill santo Cenacolo which the turk have take from the christian and turn to a mahometan Mosche or church and no christian may enter this place keep by the santon or turkish priest except he will give a unreasonable reward which give yet he be not free from danger if other turk see he enter here Christ do wash his apostle foot do eat his last supper with they do appear to they after his resurrection the door be shut and again after eight day appear to Thomas doubt here the holy ghost descend upon the apostle and the apostle Matthew be choose by lot the italian monastery note with the figure 33 have all these representation paint and to these picture the pope have give as large indulgence for papist as if they have see the other place from which the turk keep they as unwashed dog the sepulchre of David be not far from this place keep by the turk forbid entry to the christian and here they show the ruin of the tower of David or of his palace on the south side of the churchyard give to christian of Europe for burial in the same place where David of old drive out the jebuzites in like sort on the south side of this old monastery be the place where they say the virgin Mary die 7 here they show a place where the jew strive in vain to take the body of the virgin Mary from the hand of the apostle as they carry it to be bury in the valley of jehosophat 8 the cave wherein they say Peter use to bewail the deny of Christ 9 here they say the apostle hide themselves whilst Christ suffer on the crosse. 10 here they show the field buy by the jew for a burial place with the thirty penny judas bring back to they and here look into a huge cave of the mountain we do see infinite whole body embalm of dead man and stand upright and this place be give for burial to the christian of Asia 11 the gate Sterquilinea at which the filth of the city be carry out and cast into the brook Cedron and Christ betray by judas be bring into the city by this gate as they say which gate be old and nothing less than fortify 12 the gate by which the virgin Marie enter into the outer temple be say to have offer Christ then a infant to the hand of Simion which gate they say in honour of our redeemer be shut up by the christian king and so remain to this day 13 the outer temple where they say Christ be exhibit to Simion and the italian call it the temple of the presentation 14 in this large circuit compass all with wall of old the temple of Solomon stand at this day it be overgrow with grass and in the midst thereof the turk have a Mosche for their wicked worship of Mahomet neither may any christian come within this circuit much less into the Mosche either be a capital offence which they say some curious christian have try with loss of life after they have be draw to enter into it by some turk vain promise 15 the golden gate at which Christ enter on Palme-Sunday shut up by the christian king and so remain 16 without the gate of the temple where the angel trouble the water the first disease man that enter they be heal it be at this time dry up 17 the beautiful gate where Peter and John make the man walk who be lame from his mother womb 18 Salomon house of old have a gate lead into the temple and it be now inhabit by the turkish cady who have a episcopal office here I do see pleasant fountain of water and do look into the circuit where the temple stand through a iron grate when the say magistrate call we before he and I remember we be bid put off our shoe before we enter in to he where he sit upon a carpet spread upon the ground with his leg cross like a tailor and his shoe of as the turk use 19 this gate of old have the name of the drove of cattle bring in for sacrifice but at this day be call the gate of saint Stephen because the jew draw out that protomartyr by this gate and so stone he 20 here they say be the house of Anna wherein she bear the virgin Marie 21 the gate of Damascus of old call the gate of Ephraim 22 the house of Pontius Pilate in which the turkish Sangiaco who be the military governor of the city and province do then dwell so as no christian may come into the house without give a reward the friar say that in this house be hear noise whip and sigh nightly to this very time and each man the more superstitious he be the more incredible thing he tell thereof they say that the stair upon which Christ ascend when he be bring to Pilate be long since carry to Rome and these be the stair which I say the roman call holy vulgar scale sante and do worship with great superstition they be of marble but for my part let every man believe as he list whither they be bring from thence and be the same stair that Christ ascend or not only I be sure that here they show the place void in the very street where stair have be of old yet must I needs say that marble stair ill befit the poor build of this house here the soldier spoil our redeemer of his garment and in scorn attire he with purple 23 the arch of Pilate which be a gallery of brick build over the street from one wall to another whence Pilate show Christ to the people say behold the man do with he what you will 24 here they say the virgin Mary fall down faint when Christ be lead to mount Caluary 25 here they say that Christ faint the jew take his cross and lay it upon Simon of Cyren 26 the palace of king Herod 27 here they say Christ utter these word daughter of Zion weep not for i weep for yourselves etc. 28 here they say the rich glutton dwell and not far hence they show the house where Mary Magdalen wash Christ foot with her tear and dry they with the hair of her head 29 here they say Veronica dwell and that this woman give she white handkerchief to Christ when he do sweat blood who wipe his face therewith leave the lively print of it therein about which handkerchief the roman and the spaniard contend both say that they have it and show it for a holy relic to the people 30 the gate of old call judicial now not extant by which Christ be lead to mount Caluary to be crucify for this mountain now enclose within the wall be then without the wall and the way from the house of Pontius Pilate note with the figure 22 to this gate be call the dolorous way by the italian christian because Christ be lead by it to his passion 31 the prison from whence the angel bring Peter break his chain and open the iron door and it be seat under the ruin of the palace which since that time belong to the knight of jerusalem 32 the church which the christian build over the sepulchre of Christ of which I will after write more large make a rude map thereof as I have do of the city 33 the monastery of the franciscan friar in which we do jodge be seat on the high part of mount Caluary which since have be call the mount of our holy saviour and this be call the new monastery in respect of the old note with the figure 6 and only have the monument of the old paint to the visit whereof the pope have give large indulgence the franciscan friar conduct we show we some other monument within the wall and not far from the gate of Zion note with the figure 4 they show we 34 the house of the high priest Anna where Christ be examine by the pharisee and there they show we a olive tree which must needs be old to which they say Christ be bind 35 the church of the apostle saint james who the spaniard call saint james of Gallicia and worship for their protect saint who be call james the great and they say be here behead this church be stately build for the poverty of the armenian who build it and maintain there a archbishop to keep it and to perform there the rite of their religion 36 the place where they say Christ appear to the three mary dwell together upon the very day of his resurrection where the christian build three church which the turk have convert to 3 mosque yet bear no reverence to the place because they believe not that Christ die and much less believe that he rise again 37 the house of the evangelist saint mark mention in the twelve chapter of the Acts. this be the house of Mary the mother of John surname mark whither Peter come when the angel deliver he out of prison into which Herod have cast he note with the figure 31 at this day there be a obscure church keep by the syrian priest 38 here they show the iron gate which Peter find miraculous open and by the same enter into the other city come to the house of saint mark we go out at saint steven gate towards the east descend into the valley of jehosaphat and here they say 39 the bridge stand by which the queen of Saba pass over the brook Cedron and that the cross of Christ be make of the wood of this bridge 40 in this place they say the protomartyre saint Steven be stone 41 this small line without the eastern gate show the bed of the brook Cedron or Kidron which be very narrow have not at this time one drop of water so as we pass over the stony bed with dry foot but of old when jerusalem flourish and have many conduit of water draw to it than it be probable that it be fill with water and at this day when any rainefal the water run swift from the mountain on the north side according to this black line through the most pleasant valley of jehosaphat this valley extend itself on both side of this brook some two italian mile in length but be very narrow and it have on the west side the wall of the city where Salomon temple stand upon the low part of the mount moriah and it have upon the east side the most high mount Olivet and it have on the north side mountain somewhat but not far distant from the city and upon the southside mountain a little more distant many interpret the prophet joel in his three chapter and second verse as if god tribunal at the day of judgement shall stand in this valley and thereupon the jew when they die in remote part will be bring to be bury in this valley for the expedition of their trial but the best divine do teach that the word jehosaphat signify the judgement of the lord and that the prophet may be interpret figurative namely that as the lord often defeat with great slaughter the enemy of his church in this valley so in the day of judgement he will strike the wicked with like confusion 42 beyond the brook be a stately sepulchre for the most part under the earth into which we descend by some fifty stair and about the middle descent on the left hand towards the city under a altar lie the body of joseph and joachimus and on the right hand the body of Anna namely of the husband father and mother of the virgin Marie in the bottom be a church in the middle whereof under a stone raise some few foot from the ground they say the apostle bury the virgin Mary this church so they call all place where they have altar to sing mass be very dark have no light but by one window or vent make through the earth and upon this monument lie part of the bed of the brook Cedron on the right hand the turk who great reverence the monument of Christ while he live have make themselves a oratory but for the monument itself the franciscan friar of the latin church have alone the privilege to keep the same and the altar thereof for their sing of mass 43 here be a cave at the foot of mount Olivet in which they say Christ use to pray and do sweat blood 44 here they show a place where they say believe it who list that s. Thomas after the virgin burial do see she both in body and soul assume into heaven and that she cast her girdle to he give it for testimony thereof that all other may believe it in my opinion they do well to make saint Thomas see it for otherwise he will never have believe it 45 the place where they say the virgin be wont to rest when she visit the place frequent by her son in the time he live here and where she behold the stone of saint Stephen and pray for he 46 the stone where Christ leave Peter james and John say that his soul be heavy unto death and go aside to pray warn they to watch 47 here be a little circuit enclose with a low wall where they report the garden to be at the foot of mount Olivet where Christ use to pray and be betray by judas with a kiss 48 the place where they say the village of Getsemany be of old seat round about this place the turk do bury their dead as they do also in a field on the north side without the wall for they never bury within city except only the monument of their emperor 49 here they say saint james the less do lie hide till he hear that Christ be rise again the three day after his passion 50 here be two old sepulcher almost of a round form build of freestone or rather cut out of the live stone whereof the one be call the sepulchre of Absalon the son of David the other of king Manasses or as other say of the king Ezektas and consider the antiquity they seem no plebeian sepulcher but stately and fit for prince be four else from the ground in height 51 here be the top of mount Olivet the high of all the mountain that compass jerusalem and here in a chapel they show in stone the print of Christ foot when he ascend into heaven and this chapel be keep by a turkish zanton that be a kind of their priest and the turk give such reverence to the monument of Christ live on earth as they be much offend with christian if they creep not on their knee and with their shoe off to this and like monument to the keeper hereof we give a few meidines for reward 52 here they say Christ do weep over the city and rich temple of Solomon and in this place be the full prospect to view the city and temple 53 here they show the ruin of the house wherein the apostle assemble do write the creed 54 here they say Christ teach his disciple to pray in the form ever since receive and here be a church build by the christian of old 55 here they say Christ foretell the sign of the day of judgement 56 here they say the angel foretell the virgin she shall die at three day end upon Thursday the six of june we be to go to bethania hire each of we a ass for four meidine that place be scarce two italian mile from the city of our company we be four layman and because the friar our consort plead themselves to be free from such expense we be content to yield to they and give joint into the hand of the friar our guide two zechine wherewith he be to give small reward and to pay the Muccaro who furnish we with ass for we mean not to eat till our return the place be no further distant and there be no dwelling but only the ruin of house what our guide spend I know not for he never offer to give we account and because he be a friar we will not trouble he in demand it we go out by the gate Sterquilinea note with the figure 11 on the south side 57 first we come to the fountain Siloe to which Christ send the blind man to wash his eye and there we find turkish woman wash who beat we away with stone 58 here they show a monument of the prophet elia but what it be I remember not 59 here they show a fountain where they say the virgin wash Christ clothes when he be a infant 60 the mount of offence opposite to mount Zion which mount lie beyond the brook Cedron and extend eastward towards bethania and upon the top thereof they show the ruin of the palace which Solomon build for his concubine and of the altar upon which he sacrifice to idol between this mount and that of mount Zion they show the valley of the son of Hinnon towards the west and there they show a place wherein the jew offer their child to the idol Molech that be Saturn yet we read that this valley lie by the entry of the east-gate jeremtah chap. 19 vers. 2. 61 here they say the prophet isaiah be cut in piece with a saw at the command of king Manasses 62 here be a bridge over the brook Kedron or Cedron of one arch and build of stone whereby they pass when the bed of the brook be fill with water which now we pass dry footed and here they show a place where they say Christ fall upon the stone of the bed where the brook shall run when he be betray by judas be draw into the city in a great press of the jew and upon these stone be the print of hand and foot as they say his 63 the way lead to bethania over mount Olivet 64 the place where they say judas hang himself and burst after he have betray his Lord not far hence they show a fig tree which they say Christ curse because it have leaf without fruit 65 here descend from mount Olivet towards the east we do see far off the valley jordan to which the mountain decline by little and little and now we be come to bethania where we do see the house of Simon the leper not yet ruin and inhabit by a Moor to who we give a few meidines 66 here they show stately ruin of a palace which they say belong to Lazarus and not far thence be a chapel build over the stately sepulchre of Lazarus the key whereof the friar our guide have with they for the turk put great religion in reverence this place have a oratory near it and enter into the sepulchre by another way here they say Christ raise Lazarus out of his grave at our go forth we be force to give some few meidines to certain turk and arabian I know not whether they have the place in keep or no 67 the house of Mary 68 the house of Martha her sister 69 the stone upon which they say Christ do sit before he do see the sister of Lazarus bewail his death and it be some half mile from bethania 70 this small line show the bed of the river jordan run through a most pleasant valley which river we do see some ten italian mile distant on the north-side of jerusalem I can say whether beyond jordan or no we do see many tower have globe of glister mettle and that very distinct the day be clear also we do see the wall of a city near the river jordan and they say that it be jeriche further towards the north they show we from far off a place where they say our saviour be baptize by John and they affirm upon experience have that the water of jordan take in a pitcher will very long keep sweet and that it corrupt not though they carry it into foreign part this water seem very clear till it fall into a lake where they say Sodom with the other city stand of old before they be burn by fire from heaven and the day be clear we do plain see and much marvel that the clear and silver stream of jordan flow from the north to the south when in the end it fall into the say lake become as black as pitch the friar our guide serious protest that if any live thing be cast into this lake of Sodom it can not be make to sink whereas any heavy dead thing go present to the bottom also that a candle light can be thrust under the water by any force nor be extinguish by the water but that a candle unlighted will present sink I omit for brevity sake many wondrous thing they tell we of the putrefaction of the air and other strange thing with such confidence as if they will extort belief from we we have a great desire to see these place but be discourage from that attempt by the fear of the arabian and moor for they inhabit all these territory and I say before that the arabian howsoever subject to the Turk yet exercise continual robbery with all liberty and impunity the turk be not able to restrain they because they be barbarous and live far from their chief power where they can easy fly into desert place yet these barbarian do strict observe their faith to those that be under their protection and all the merchant or other of the arabian captain and for a small pension procure themselves to be receive into their protection which do these captain proclaim their name through all their city and tent in which for the most part they live and ever after will severe revenge any wrong do to they so as they pass most safe with their good all other man they spoil and make excursion with their leader and sometime with their king to the sea side as far as joppa and much further within land spoil and many time kill all they meet when we return from bethania we decline to the north side of mount Olivet and come to the ruin of 71 bethphage where Christ send for the colt of a ass and ride thereupon while the people cry hosanna to the high and lay branch and leaf under his foot do enter into jerusalem upon Friday the seven of june to ward the evening we take our journey to Bethlehem juda and we four lie consort the friar by our consent still have the privilege to be free from these expense deliver joint four zechine to the friar we guide for our charge whereof they give we no other account than they do former yet they only disburse some small reward since we go on foot and be otherwise tie to satisfy the friar of the monastery under the name of gift or alm for our diet there but since they use we friendly we will not displease they for so small a matter we go out of the city by the gate of joppa on the west side and so along 72 this line pass by a pave causey beyond mount Zion and then ascend another mountain to Bethlehem 73 here they show the garden of Wia and the fountain wherein Bersheba wash herself which at that time be dry and from the place where the tower of David be seat upon mount Zion note with the figure 6 be a easy prospect into this garden 74 here they show the tower of saint Simion 75 here be a tree of Terebinth which bear a fruit of a black colour like unto a olive yield oil and under this tree they say the virgin do rest when she carry Christ to be present in the temple for which cause the papist make their bead of this tree and esteem they holy especial when they have touch the rest of the monument they carry they into Europe and give they to their friend for great present and holy relic 76 here they show a fountain call of the wiseman of the east and they say that the star do here again appear to they after they come from Herod 77 here they show the ruin of a house wherein they say that the prophet Habakcuk dwell and be thence carry by the hair of the head to feed Daniel in the lion den at Babylon 78 here they show the fountain of the prophet Elias and the stone upon which he use to sleep upon which they show the print of his head shoulder and other member which print have some similitude but no just proportion of those member from a rock near this place we do see at once both jerusalem and Bethlehem 79 here they show a tower and ruin where the Patriarch jacob dwell and here again we do see both city 80 here be a old stately sepulchre in which they say Rachel Jacob wife be bury it be almost of a round form build of stone and lime four foot high have the like cover above it bear up by four pillar there be two other sepulcher but nothing so fair and all three be enclose within one wall of stone 81 here they show the fountain for the water whereof David thirst yet will not drink it when it be bring with the hazard of blood 82 here the city Bethlehem be seat which then be but a village have no beauty but the monastery 83 here the monastery be seat large in circuit and build rather after the manner of Europe than Asia which the italian franciscan friar call latin and more common francke do possess but other christian sect have their altar in the church by special privilege and the turk themselves come hither in pilgrimage do lie within the church for the turk have a peculiar way by a door of iron make of old and keep by they to enter into the chapel where they say Christ be bear this monastery seem strong enough against the sudden attempt of the turk or arabian yet the friar in that case dare not resist they live only in safety by the reverence which that people bear to this place and by the opinion of their own poverty the great church be large and high in which I number twenty four pillar but my consort be more curious observe that the pillar be set in four rank every rank have eleven pillar seven foot distant one from the other whereof many be of porphyry and have beautiful spot the high roof of the church on the inside be paint with history of the scripture with a rich paint that shine with gold and glass as if it be enamel call in italian Mosaica and the pavement be rich with stone of marble porphyry and jaspar from the less church call of saint Katherine we enter a cave under the earth where the friar give every one of we a light wax candle in his hand let they place what religion they will therein I be sure the cave be so dark as we can not have pass it without a light in this cave we do first see the bone of the infant kill by Herod than the sepulcher of Eusebius and of saint Jerome in his chapel for they hold that he long dwell there then they do lead we into a more dark place where they say he do live a austere life fifty year space and translate the bible out of Hebrew into latin and write many volume but the place seem to i more fit to dull the brain then to yield such fruit of wit by reason it be dark and dig deep under ground from this cave we ascend by ten marble stair into a chapel all cover with marble and lie in length from the west at which end we enter to the east and from this west end as well turk as christian of all sect go upon their knee to Christ birth the eastern end and there kiss a mark stone in the pavement in which very place they say the redeemer of the world be bear by this stone on the southside lie a little chapel have two door only divide with a pillar in which chapel at the right hand or westside be a manger raise from the ground and all of marble in which they say Christ be lay after his birth and in the wall they show a stone have as they say the lively picture of saint Hierome in the say little chapel on the left hand or east side they show a place where they say Christ be circumcise and shed the first drop of his precious blood for the save of mankind and there they fhew another place where they say the wiseman of the east adore Christ and offer to he their gift the wall of both chapel the pavement and all thing be cover with marble the roof on the inside be paint with the foresay rich picture glister likeenamel work to conclude all thing be stately and rich and remain so under the turkish tyranny yet more rich in the chapel of Chrsts birth then in the great church where all thing than begin to fall to ruin because the turk believe not that Christ die the turk do so reverence this monument of Christ birth as they creep grovel upon hand and knee to kiss the say stone yet in the mean time they despise the monument of his death because they believe not that he die from hence go back the same way we enter they show upon the right hand a hole in the high roof of the church by which they say the star that conduct the wiseman fall from above into the bowel of the earth can he forbear laughter who consider the bigness of the star yea even of comet as some write that be special find no mention of this fall of the star to be make in the holy scripture the city or village of Bethlehem be distant from jerusalem some five mile in Turkey I always understand italian mile and we come hither from the western gate of jerusalem through a fair way and mountain plant with vine olive and fruitful tree Bethlehem be seat upon mountain and have pleasant hill on the east and southside a pleasant plain on the north-side end in great mountain towards jerusalem 84 as we go out of Bethlehem to visit the monument here they show we the field in which the angel make know the birth of Christ to the shepherd and the cave wherein they do lie by day to shun the heat of the sun 85 here they say the patriarch let plant the first vine 86 here beyond pleasant hill we do distinct see the plain of jordan and the dead sea with the situation of Sodom and Gomorra 87 here they say Bethalia be seat of old 88 here we do see the ruin of a house in which they say joseph the virgin husband do dwell 89 here they say the virgin hide herself from the tyranny of Herod 90 here they say that king Solomon have his garden the franciscan friar send out of Italy each three year into these part do courtcous entertain we at Bethlehem and at our first come in imitation of Christ they wash our foot it happen that my brother fall sick here of a ague and so when our consort upon Saturday in the evening return to jerusalem we be force to stay here that night but the next day in the evening we come to they at the monastery of jerusalem and because they make haste to return homeward we go forth the next day be Monday the ten of june early in the morning to see the mountain of judea and that day it happen that I be trouble with looseness of body whereof I make good use as I shall hereafter show which make i name it we go out of the city at the gate of joppa on the west side and upon our right hand they show we 91 this place where they say that Solomon be anoint King 92 thence we go right forward to a fountain in the desert where they say Philip the apostle do interpret the scripture to the eunuch of candace queen of and baptize he 93 here they say be the desert in which John Baptist preach and they show we his cave cut out of a rock and a long stone therein upon which he use to lie and a pleasant spring issue out of the rock where he use to drink and another stone upon which he use to sit 94 he e we come to the mountain or mountainous place of judea and here they say the prophet zacharias dwell where a woman of the moor keep the church of old build there 95 from hence a musket shoot or little more be another house which they say belong to zacharias and in one of these house he pronounce the song bless be the lord God of Israel etc. and when the virgin visit Elizabeth the babe here spring in her womb and the virgin here pronounce the song my soul do magnify etc. and John Raptist be bear here 96 from this place they say the tree be take upon which the cross of Christ be make and greek friar keep the church that be here build this place be two mile distant from jerusalem whether we return the same way we come out and enter the city by the west gate of joppa the church build upon Christ sepulchre of old by the christian at jerusalem be former note by the figure 32 and we enter the same upon Tuesday the eleven of june towards the evening at which time the turkish cady send we his officer to open the door of it to who we pay for tribute after the door be open each of we nine zechine and beside give the officer or janizare a small reward for himself but it be the custom that he that have once pay this tribute may any time after enter this church without pay any thing if he can watch the opportunity of other christian enter the same the rude but true figure in plain of Christ sepulchre and the church build over it at jerusalem a by this one and only door be of brass and on the south side of the church entrance be give into the say church they say there be of old another door not far from this towards the east but now it be not extant b this mark show where the belfry stand which be of ancient building and now in great part be ruin while the turk admit no use of any bell a a marble stone call the stone of unction where they say the body of Christ be embalm before it be bury and it be compass with grate of iron have above it nine lamp continual burn maintain by the nine sect of christian b the sepulchre of Godfrey king of jerusalem to which other less sepulcher be adjoin erect to king and queen of his family and this sepulchre have this epitaph in latin here lie worthy Godfrey of bullon who conquer all this land to the worship of Christ who soul may it rest in peace amen c the sepulchre of Baldwine his brother and successor in the kingdom with this epitaph in latin king Balduinus another judas Machabeus the hope of his country the life of the church the strength of both these verse add caesar dan Damascus who Egypt Dan Damascus homicide with gift and tribute glad pacify d here be mount Caluary and the stair to ascend thereunto the wall of all the build upon it the altar and the pavement all shine with marble the roof on the inside glister with the foresay rich paint which seem to be enamel and diverse altar be proper to diverse nation or sect for their rite of religion to these altar upon the mountain we ascend by some twenty stair and there they show we three hole wherein the three cross of Christ and the two thief be erect and at the figure 1 where they say the cross of Christ stand they show we stone rend or the rend of the mountain when Christ die under this mountain in the corner towards the door of the church they bid we look in at a little window and there they show we a scull which they say be the scull of Adam of which they say the mountain be call Golgotha 2 without the door of the church we ascend to a chapel above this mount where they show we a altar upon which they say Melchisedeck offer sacrifice 3 also a chapel where they say Abraham will have offer Isaac 4 the altar of mount Caluary 5 a place in the way to the sepulchre where they say that Christ lay down his cross and where the virgin Marie and John the enangelist stand while he be crucify e here they show a stone which they call that be touch i not because Christ appear here to Marie Magdalen use those word 6 and in this place they say Marie Magdalen stand fff here be a retreat of certain chamber and chapel under the keep of the latin or european friar for they continual send two or three of their friar to be lock weekly within this church for the performance of the rite of their religion who they recall at the week end to their monastery in the city send new in their place to attend that service and this retreat have only a door to pass into the church but none into the street g the chapel of apparition so call because they say Christ there appear to the virgin Marie after his resurrection H the pillar of whip so call because they say Christ be bind to it when he be beat with rod h this altar they call the altar of the holy crosse. ay a most narrow prison in which they say Christ be shut up for a little time k the chapel where they say that the soldier divide Christ garment l here we descend some fifty stair into a cave under the earth which they have make a chapel and here they say the empress Helena find the cross of Christ and thereupon build this chapel in which they say four pillar many time make a sound of groan and sigh and they show the very place where the cross of Christ and where the cross of the two thief be find n after Christ be beat they say he be force to sit here till they crown his head with thorn p the chancel of the church p a hole in the pavement of this chancel which the greek have the chancel to keep hold to be the midst of the World q this place lie open over head have the sepulchre on the west side and two little marble wall raise some two foot on the north and south side within which wall the place be pave with marble the wall be so high as a man can convenient sit upon they and in this place they use to pray before they enter the sepulchre r the outward chapel or porch of the sepulchre as I may so term it where the angel be say to have appear to the woman and therein lie a fouresquare stone fit to the little door of the sepulchre upon which stone roll from the door they say the angel do sit after Christ be rise ss these be seat on both side of this outward chapel in which seat they use to pray t in this chapel so they call the sepulchre itself and under the stone note with black they say the body of Christ be lay and this stone be raise as high as a altar and cover with marble as all the wall be the little door by which they enter this chapel or sepulchre be scarce 3 foot high and a broad so as they enter it with difficulty bend down their body as if they creep into a cave the very stone cover the sepulchre or place where Christ body do lie be somewhat raise from the ground and have seven foot in length and some six in breadth this sepulchre lie under the first globe of the church as the chancel lie under the second and it lie under the middle of that globe neither have the church any window but the globe hang over the sepulchre be open in the roof and so give light to all the church and in the very sepulchre the burn lamp give light beside that the door lie open and because rain must needs fall from the open globe the sepulchre have a cover bear up with pillar of marble and lay over with lead to receive the rain the sepulchre within and without be beautify with marble and be cut out of a rock before the church be build the franciscan friar be for the most part italian but be vulgar call franc of the french who be in league with the turkish Ottoman and they have the privilege of sing their mass in the sepulchre not of free grant but because they be best able to pay for their privilege yet it be free for any of the christian sect to come into the sepulchre they say that from the situation of this sepulchre the custom come among christian to be bury with their foot and face towards the east as expect the resurrection five a chapel keep by the sect of the gofti 10 the sepulcher of joseph of Arimathea and of Nicodemus a'n the chapel of the jacobite z the chapel of the abyssine 10 the chapel of the armenian 10 10 the chapel of the georgian some write that this church have the form of a cross and if the retreat or chamber of the italian friar with the chapel of aparition on the north side and the two tower of the belfry on the south side be joint consider with the church which seem rather-fastned thereunto then of the same building a superstitious man may feign to himself the figure of a cross but shall river plain demonstrate it to other above the roof of the church on the outside be two fair globe whereof the great cover with lead lie over the sepulchre and the less all make of stone be over the chancel and this great globe on the inside of the church be beautify with engrave cedar tree and borlie up with pillar of marble and the less have fair picture of the foresay rich paint shine like enamel work the breadth of the church under both globe contain seventy pace and the length 140 pace and in general as well within as without it retain only the shadow of the old magnificence we enter the church on Tuesday in the afternoon and be lock there in all the night follow and almost all the next day to fulfil our devotion but I former say that the italian friar have chamber of retreat within the church in which we do eat and rest at our pleasure yet these chamber and the like retreat wherein the priest of other sect with their wife child and family do lodge and eat and perform the rite of their religion have not any one door into the street but all enter the church and go forth by the foresay only door of the church towards the south and the key of this door be keep by the turk who open it at set time to admit stranger and once every week to let the friar return to their monastery and to receive new friar into the church which be send from thence to perform the feveral rite of religion and this door have a grate or little window at which the enclose friar may talk with their friend without and receive meat send they from their monastery nine sundry sect of christian have their monastery within this city by who nine sect of christian the great Turk and his officer have great profit and the turk themselves repute all the monument and place holy which Christ in his life frequent but this monument of his death and other like they despise and keep they only for their profit from the say monastery friar be weekly send to perform their several rite and at the week end they be recall to the monastery and new send in their place which custom I think they take from the jew for when David divide the twenty four family of the son of Aaron into twenty four course that each of they may one after the other in due order perform the holy office in the temple josephus write that these course or family in order one after the other live in the temple from sabbath to sabbath to perform those duty of these nine christian sect each have privilege to keep this or that monument within the city and in the field in which place they perform the rite of their religion and according to the number of the sect they maintain nine lamp continual burn in the foresay common church upon the stone of unction as many upon the sepulchre and as many upon mount Caluarie the nine sect be thus call frank namely the italian georgian greek sorian abyssine armenian nestorian and maronites the religion of the Frankes namely papist be so well know as I will omit it here and refer it to his due place I will only say that they have the keep of the 1. the Frankes sepulchre the chapel of a parition and therein of the pillar of whip and of one altar upon mount Caluarie for the performance of their rite the georgian be a warlike nation inhabit Media and the caspian mountain 2. the georgian and have their name of saint George who they have choose their protect saint they have a king and make war valiant sometime upon the turk sometime upon the persian can never be conquer by either yea if they be oppress by either they easy find help from the other out of their mutual hatred therefore they pay no tribute to the turk but by singular privilege free enter into jerusalem arm and with banner display neither dare the turk offer they the least injury lest when they return home they shall revenge it upon the turk lie near they their very woman be warlike like the amazon and carry bow show valour both in countenance and behaviour the man wear long hair on their head and beard save that they all be shave like clerk upon the crown of the head the layman in a fouresquare the priest in a round form they express follow the religion rite and ceremony of the greek and in their divine service use the greek tongue otherwise speak their own language as I think Caldean these in the church of jerusalem have the keep of mount Caluary and the altar there build over the place where they say the cross of Christ stand and in the city they keep the house of the high priest Annas of the greek religion I must speak at large in his due place now I will only 3. the greek say that in the church they keep the chancel and therein show a hole in the pavement compass with marble which they say be the very middle point of the world against which opinion I argue with they and object that the earth be round and that in a globe the centre be in the midst all centre in the outside be but imaginary and to be place wheresoever the measurer will also that in measure after their manner the outside of the earth Palestina be far distant from the equinoctial line which divide the world into equal part and if Palestina be just under that line yet that all the country have the same meridian shall be the midst of the world as well as Palestina they answer that David say in his psalm in the midst of the world I will work their salvation to which I reply that the midst of the world be there take for the face and in the sight of the world so as none shall be able to deny it whereupon they grow angry and say that the scripture must be believe in spite of all cosmographer and philosopher it have be vain to dispute further with they there be not one learned man among these greek at jerusalem and to say truth if you except the greek land under the venetian they have few or no learned man for my part I never find in all the vast empire of ottoman any learned greek but only one call Milesius who be after make patriarch of Constantinople and these greek as in this point so in all other follow the literal sense of the scripture for which cause they also believe the corporal presence of Christ in the sacrament and whereas saint Paul say let the bishop be the husband of one wife etc. they so interpret it as if the priest wife die within few day after his marriage yet he may never marry again the sorian be so call of Syria in which province they live have their own 4. the serian patriarch neither can they ever be bring to consent to the roman faith for whatsoever the roman challenge due to the seat of s. Peter that they say rather belong to they in respect saint Peter be bishop of Anttoch they agree with the greek in many thing they deny purgatory they fast four lent in the year they permit their priest to marry they use the greek tongue in their divine service and otherwise speak their own language which I take to be the arabian tongue in jerusalem church they keep the sepulcher of joseph of (@) and of Nicodemus and in the city they keep the house of saint mark note with the figure 37. the be egyptian dwell about Numidia they retain the heresy of 5. the arrius and follow the ceremony of the abyssine this I write upon the report of the italian friar who be to be blame if it be not true these in the church keep the chapel wherein Godfrey and his regal family lie bury and the cave under mount calvary where they say the scull of Adam lie and have also their proper altar upon mount Caluarie the abyssine inhabit the south part of africa and they be subject to their king 6. the abisines preti-giani they receive the christian faith of the eunuch baptize by Philip and themselves be baptize not only with water but with the sign of the cross print in their flesh with hot iron gather that fire be as necessary to baptism as water out of those word of s. John Baptist I baptize you with water but he shall baptize you with the spirit and fire also they use the jew and mahometan circumcision like wary notary who fear to fail in their assurance never think they have use word enough yet do they great hate the jew and think their altar defile if they do but look upon they they give the sacrament of our lord supper to very child and they as all the rest except the frank that be papist give it in both kind when they sing mass or psalm they leap and clap their hand and like the jew use stage-player action they use their own that be the egyptian tongue in divine service and observe a lent of fifty day at one time do great maccrate their body in the church they keep the chapel adjoin to the sepulchre and the pillar where they say Christ be crown with thorn the armenian be so call of the province Armenta which they inhabit and 7. the armenian they call their chief bishop catholicon who they reverence as another pope they disagree with the greek and rather apply themselves to the frank yet they keep not the feast of Christ birth but fast that day they keep the roman Lent but more strict abstain from fish and very oil which they use for butter but upon some holiday in that time they eat flesh they mingle no water with the wine of the sacrament as the papist do but with they they lift up the bread yea and the cup also to be worship of old with reservation of custom they join themselves to the roman church but find the pope to give they no help against their enemy they quick fall from he the very lay man be shave like clerk upon their head but in the form of a cross and their priest keep the hair of their head long in two tust place therein great religion in the church they keep the pillar where they say the garment of Christ be part and lot cast upon his coat and in the city the place where they say saint James be behead and the house of the high priest Caiphas upon mount Zion the nestorian be so call of the monk Nestorius who infect the persian the nestorian tartar and jew with his heresy they give the sacrament of the lord supper in both kind and that to child as well as man they use the caldean tongue in divine service and otherwise the arabian in the church they keep the prison wherein they say Christ be shut up the maronites inhabit Phanicia and the mount of Libanus and they use the syrian 9 the maronites tongue in their divine service namely as I think the arabian and they say that these man for poverty be late flee from jerusalem some make mention of a ten sect namely the lacobite name of jacob disciple to the patriarch of Alexandria who live mingle among turk and tartar inhabit partly Nubia in Africa partly the province of India I remember not to have see any such at my be there neither yet to have hear any mention of they yet other write that they admit circumcision as well as baptism and beside print the sign of the cross by a hot iron in some conspicuous part of their body that they confess their sin only to God not to their priest that they acknowledge but one nature in Christ that in token of their faith they make the sign of the cross with one finger and give the sacrament of our lord supper in both kind yea to infant as well as to those who be of full age I can omit a old spanish woman who have for many year live there lock up in the temple lodge every night at the door of the sepulchre and have her diet by the friar alm she say that she come to jerusalem to expiate her sinnesby that holy pilgrimage that she have then be there seven year and in that time have always live in the church and that she will not refuse any opportunity to go back into Spain but otherwise will die there and thereby think to merit much of God neither do I think she lose the hope of this vain merit since it be not easy to find a man who will carry a old woman and bear her charge so long a journey the stone of unction in the church be common to all the nine christian sect neither do the keeper of any other monument refuse any Christian to enter into they but only by privilege keep their altar private to themselves most of the sect have their monastery in the city and as I former say each of they have the privilege to keep some monument as well within as without the wall but some of the sect only come to jerusalem at solemn feast and dwell near the city easy maintain a friar or two to keep their monument and so be free from the necessity of build a monastery in the city upon Wednesday the twelve of june towards evening the turk do open the church to let we out and each of we layman give the chief Turk thirty meidine and the doorkeeper twenty of free gift and for the wax candle burn the night before in the church each give sixty meidine to one of our italian friar this do we return to the monastery where we lodge with great joy that we be present to go back to joppa I former say that the franciscan friar with who we lodge be of Europe whether at three year end they be to be recall and some fifty new friar to be send hither in their place which still each three year use to be change and these friar be call the family of Frankes for the great Turk permit they as french to live there and forbid the come of any spanish or roman friar yet be they for the most part of Sicily Naples or Rome but deny their country affirm that they be venetian and if they be know to be subject to Spain or Rome they shall incur great danger of they some few be indeed venetian and at this time some two be frenchman all these live of the aim of the merchant in the east of their religion who for the most part be italian and especial venetian yet have the monastery also some rent of land give to it of old in Sicily and in Spain and from thence they bring with they every three year at their first arrival a present of great value to the turkish Ottoman I say former that of old the venetian yearly send a galley to carry pilgrim to the holy land till the christian be so oppress by exaction of the turk as they rare undertake that journey and so the venetian also leave that custom from that time this family of Frankes so these friar be call use to pass in a venetian ship to Cyprus and from thence to joppa in the holy land hire there a grecian bark to that purpose and in like sort the old family upon the arrival of the new return into Italy and as soon as this family arrive they disperse themselves the great part abide at jerusalem and Bethlehem and some single man or couple be send to do the office of priest at Cayro or Babylon in Egypt at Haleppo in Asia where most part of their merchant reside and at Scanderona of old by all and still by christian call Alexandretta these friar thus disperse be not only maintain by the merchant to who the be send but they also send from they large alm to the rest at jerusalem and they often change place that all may equal bear these burden we be now to take our journey from jerusalem many christian and jew bring we diverse toy to buy and carry with we be of no worth save only that they be far fetch namely bead for papist to number their prayer and also cross both make of the earth whereof they say Adam be form or of the olive tree of mount Olivet or of Terebinth under one of which tree they say the virgin Mary rest when she carry Christ a infant to be present in the temple and round stone call Cornioli of yellow colour and other of white call the sea-water of India also girdle of the virgin Mary and glister stone of little price as all the rest be among which they attribute to the stone of Indea the virtue to provoke urine to the eagle stone call aquilina the virtue to expel poison to facilitate the birth of child to heal the fall sickness to restore woman milk and so to diverse stone diverse and incredible virtue beside our franciscan friar give each to his friend and the guardian to we all dust and little stone take from the foresay monument for a great treasure to be carry to our friend at home moreover they give to each of we free and unasked as it seem of custom as well to we layman as to the friar a testimony under the seal of the monastery that we have be at jerusalem and for better credit they express therein some markable sign of our face and body now there remain nothing but the epilogue of the comedy that we shall make some fit present to the guardian of the monastery in satisfaction for our diet and the courtesy of the friar towards we which myself and my brother think very fit to be do but two of the friar our consort either want money or use to eat of free cost do not only refuse to give any thing but persuade the french layman to join with they in denial thereof the three friar our consort and for his experience use by the other as a conductor hear this do vehement reprove they use these word in french doncque payer blanch that be what will you then pay they in white which phrase they use when a man requite a courtesy in word or fair write promise not real they on the other side no less angry answer that it be unfit and irreligious for friar to extort gift from christian pilgrim myself and my brother laugh to ourselves hear this difference for we find now and have often hear that these friar be most deate host and that as they in England who refer their payment to pleasure be always over-paid so these friar ask nothing for diet yet under the title of gift or alm expect more than any the most greedy host can demand yet lest we shall provoke they either to hinder our departure or to do we any shrewd turn as they most easy may do yea leave they shall surmise myself and my brother to be author or partner of this conspiracy I persuade the french layman our consort that howsoever the friar still remain obstinate to give nothing yet we four shall present the guardian some six zechine this effect the guardian dismiss we for good son yet in truth we be well content with this fair occasion to restrain our gift which howsoever it be far from bounty yet be it free from base spare since we give at Bethlehem for our diet another present to those friar and while we visit the monument of jerusalem be daily abroad and common dine in village we be only behold to these friar for some ten supper beside that we have always profess poverty most safe to stranger to which may be add that in this province whether for abundance of all thing or want of money all thing be sell at cheap rate for when we go out to see the mountain or hill country of Indea and dine in a village I remember we buy twenty egg for a and a pound of mutton for five meidine four cake for one and a hen for two meidine and a half in the monastery they give we enough of mutton hen and salad and of good wine but some what sharp and the friar our consort do eat continual with the friar and we layman by ourselves the friar only once invite we to eat with they in the public refectory if this discourse make any surmise that we do some thing against our conscience while we live in this monastery let he read the four and twenty precept of dissimulation in the chapter of precept the three part and the first book wherein I have explain my opinion of the outward reverence of the body show in time of the papist divine service and for the rest let he know that I now confess as I do former that we therein err that we do not first go to Constantinople or Haleppo from whence have a janizare to guide we choose by our ambassador or merchant we may have escape many of those trouble which now we endure be alone and that with no great charge than now we be at since these trouble increase our charge and may also easy have obtain any courtesy at these friar hand or at least have see jerusalem safe though they be ill affect to we but since many thing divert we from this course and now we be fall into these italian friar hand we think best to bring ourselves out of this danger by discretion and moderation in our deed and word also I confess that in those day my conscience be not so tender as since by the grace of God I have find it yet be it never so insensible as it can have pass over the worship of a idol or the deny of my faith if I have here go to mass it will seem no wonder to our english gentleman who have live any time in Italy and I be confident of opinion that no man return home with more detestation of the papist religion than he who well instruct in the truth have take the liberty to behold with his eye their strange superstition which one of experience may well see without any great participation of their folly for my part as I have always be unwilling to be present at their mass so I abhor from the receive of the lord supper with they and this be the high mischief which we can be force to incur at jerusalem now for the communion of our lord supper except it be in great sickness and danger of death they never impose it so earnest on any man as he may not with discreet answer put it off till another time without all suspicion of contrariety in religion for their mass they never sing or mumble they but in the morning and that fast now we come the four of june in the afternoon to jerusalem and the five and six day we go abroad before full day to see the monument and return not to the monastery till night at which time the great offence to our conscience that can happen be to hear they sing psalm in their chapel upon Friday the seven of june we take our journey to Bethlehem where my brother fall sick we have scarce leisure to satisfy our curiosity much less to be present at any unplease rite of their religion upon Saturday the eight of june our consort return to jerusalem we take occasion by my brother sickness to stay at Bethlehem and come not to jerusalem till Sunday at night on Monday the nine of june we visit the mountain or hill country of judea at which time my brother be so ill dispose as our consort themselves doubt his death and for my part I be
