be by authority of parliament adjudge to the crown and make a county with sheriff appoint yearly to be choose by the lord deputy upon the attainter of the say earl of Desmond and his confederate all the land fall to the crown be in acre of english measure about 574628 acre hereof great part be restore to the offender as to Patrick condon his country to the white knight his country to some of the geraldine and to other their confederate no small portion the rest be divide into signory grant by letter patent to certain english knight and esquire which upon this gift and the condition whereunto they be tie have the common name of undertaker these undertaker do not people these signory grant they and their heir by patent as they be bind with well affect english but either sell they to english papist such as be most turbulent and so be daily trouble and question by the english magistrate be like to give the most money for the irish land or otherwise dispose they to their best profit without respect of the public good neither do they build castle and do other thing according to their covenant for the public good but only seek their private end and so this her majesty bounty to they turn not to the strengthen but rather to the weaken of the english government in that province of monster touch the rebellion of the earl of Tyrone the worthy antiquary Camden mention tyrones rebellion Neale the great tyrannize in Ulster and great part of Ireland before the come of saint Patrick into that kingdom about the year of our lord 431 add that this family notwithstanding live after more obscure not only till the english enter to conquer Ireland about the year 1169 but after that to the time that the scot under Edward Bruce attempt to conquer that kingdom about the year 1318. in which turbulent time start up and in his letter to the pope style himself king of Ulster and true heir of all Ireland further Camden add that after the appease of these trouble this new king vanish and his posterity lurk in obscurity till the civil war of England between the house of York and Lancastar the seed whereof be sow by Henry the four of Lancastar family depose Richard the second of York family and usurp the crown though Henry the four and his son Henry the five by their valour so maintain this usurpation as no civil war break forth in their time nor so long as the noble brother of Henry the five and uncle to Henry the six live after between Henry the six of Lancaster family and Edward the four of York family this bloody war be long continue but end in the death of the next successor Richard the three a double usurper both of the house of Lancaster and the heir of his brother Edward the four of the house of York after in the marriage of Henry the seven with the daughter and heir of Edward the four both these house be unite and so this bloody war well end from this time behold the pedigree of the omales Owen oneale Hugh mac Owen be Hugh Neale Moor mac Art Hugh Mac Neale Moor Owen Mac Hugh Neale Moor offer to serve against traitor Hugh four son Tirlogh Hugh Bryan and Henry live when Hugh oneale rebel phileme Roc mac Art Henry Mac phelime roc. Turlogh Mac Henry of the fuse rebel with Hugh five son then live Henry Mac Owen oneale marry the daughter of Thomas earl of Kildare a Giraldine Con more or great marry the daughter of Gerald earl of Kildare his mother niece who father and himself wax bold upon the power of the earl of Kildare tyrannize over the people and despise the title of earl marquis duke or prince in regard of that of oneale surname bacco or lame succeed oneale who curse his posterity if they shall learn english sow corn or build house to invite the English his power be suspect of Henry the eight and the king power after the suppression of the earl of Kildare be fear of he who have rebel with the earl he fail into England and renounce the name of oneale and surrender his inheritance hold by the irish law of tanistry by which a man be prefer to a boy and the uncle to that nephew who grandfather overlive the father and common the most active knave not the next heir be choose have his land regraunt to he from the king under the great scale of England as to his vassal with title of earl of Tyrone thus in the three and thirty year of Henry the eight a act of parliament be make in Ireland with consent of the three estate of that kingdom whereby the usurpation of the title of oneale be make capital to this family and king Henry and his successor the former style of lord be change be style king of Ireland and the law of England be receive to be of force in that kingdom phelime Hugh elder son Turlogh Brasilogh six son at least then live and able to serve the queen shane or John oneale succeed his father by kill his brother Matthew and vex his father to death be cruel and barbarous and tyrannical challenge the neighbour lord to be his subject as Mac Gennys Mac Guire Mac mahow o Realy o hanlon o Cahon Mac brien o hagan o Quin Mac Cartan Mac donnel galloglasse and when Henry Sidney expostulate this be lord justice in the absence of the earl of Sussex lord deputy he offer to prove by write that his ancestor have this authority over they deny that his father have any power to resign his land to the king which he hold only for life by tanistry law without the consent of the people be to choose oneale that be the chief of the name he make war against o Realy and imprison Collogh Mac donnel but when Thomas earl of Sussex l. deputy lead the english force against he he by the counsel of the earl of Kildare sail into England and submit himself too q. Elizabeth and after for a while conform himself to obedience and civility but when he tyrannize over the irish lord and they crave succour of Henry Sidney lord deputy in the year 1565 he lead a army against he seng Edward Randolph with seven company of foot and a troop of horse by sea to derry and Loughfoyle to assault the rebel on the back against who the rebel turn all his force be so defeat as he flee for succour to the scot who brother he have kill and they at first entertain he well after fell to word and kill he in the year 1567. after in a parliament at Dublin he be condemn of treason and his land confiscate and a law make that no man shall after that presume to take the name and title of oneale he have three son Henry Con and Tirlogh cast in prison by Hugh the rebel Matthew Okelly till 15 year age repute the son of a black smith at Dudalke give Con o Neale by a smith wife at her death this bastard he appoint to succeed he by the king letter patent at which time he be create baron of Dungannon but he be kill in his father life time by shane the legitimate son of Con who bastard this Matthew be Brian kill by Odonnel at the instance of shane o Neale Hugh preserve by the english from shane marry the daughter of Tirlogh Linnogh oneale who he put away by divorce and after prove a arch-rebel this Hugh son to the bastard Matthew bear of a smith wife and repute the smith son till he be fifteen year of age live sometime in Ireland and Hugh earl of Tyrone much in the court of England and be support against Turlogh Linnogh oneale with the title of baron of Dungannon by his father right he have a troop of horse in queen Elizabeth pay in the late war of the earl of Desmond in which and all occasion of service he behave himself so valiant as the queen give he a yearly pension of one thousand mark he be of a mean stature but a strong body able to endure labour watch and hard fare be with all industrious and active valiant affable and apt to manage great affair and of a high dissemble subtle and profound wit so as many deem he bear either for the great good or ill of his country in a irish parliament he put up his petition that by virtue of the letter patent grant to his grandfather to his father and his heir he may there have the place and title of the earl of Tyrone and be admit to this his inheritance the title and place be there grant to he but the inheritance in regard the king of England by the attainder of shane be thereof invest be refer to the queen pleasure for the obtain whereof sir John perrot then lord deputy upon his promise of a great rent to be reserve to the crown give he his letter of recommendation into England where he so well know to humour the court as in the year 1587. he get the queen letter patent under the great seal of England for the earldom of Tyr-Oen without any reservation of the rent he have promise to the I deputy wherewith though his lordship be offend in that the patent be not pass in Ireland and so the say rend omit yet in reverence to the great lord who have procure this grant in England he do forbear to oppose the same the condition of this (@) be that the bound of Tyrone shall be limit that one or two planet namely that of blackwater shall be reserve for the build of fort and keep of garrison therein that the son of shane and Tirlogh shall be provide for and that he shall challenge no authority over the neighbour lord border upon Tyrone or any where out of that county and such be his endeavour in the queen service such his protestation of faith and thankfulness as Tirlogh Linnogh by the queen intercession be induce upon certain condition for his maintenance to surrender the county and all command in those part unto he (@) Cormoe preserve from shane by the english now rebel with Hugh Neale Conuelagh Turlogh Lynnogh take the title of oneale after shane he be age and so love quietness the rather for fear of the child of shane and of Matthew the bastard he be obedient to the queen but make war upon Odonnel and the island scot of who he kill in the field Alexander Oge who murder shane oneale sir Arthur o Neale knight live in this rebellion this sir Arthur serve the queen against Hugh the arch-rebel who have two of his son in prison but two or three other son be with their father at Laughfoyle among the English the spanish forsooth invincible navy send to invade England in the year 1588. be disperse and prove nothing lessethen invincible many of they be wreck ann. 1588. on the coast of Ireland whereof some be harbour by the earl of Tyrone with who since he be think to have plot the follow mischief and short after in the end of this year or beginning of the next sir John perrot 1589 sir William fitz-williams lord deputy be revoke sir William fitz-williams be send lord deputy into Ireland I have hear that he have be former lord deputy when he return and sue for recompense of his service a great lord shall answer he that such employment be preferment and not service to challenge reward and therefore it in this new employment any shall think that he follow this counsel seek to make it a preferment to he and his family I do not much marvel thereat this I write of hearsay but as in the general relation follow I purpose to write nothing which be not warrant either by relation present to the queen by the principal councillor of Ireland or by letter interchange between the state of England and Ireland or like authentical write so for the particular of the above name lord deputy if perhaps some may think any thing observe by i to derogate from he I protest that whatsoever I write be in like sort warrant and may not be omit without the scandal of historical integrity be objection frequent make by the rebel for excuse of their disloyalty aswell in all their petition as treaty of peace but howsoever I can but mention these imputation yet I advise the reader to judge of they as objection of the rebel who in their nature be clamorous and can no way make their excuse so plausible as by scandalize the chief governor and I further protest that as I shall in the due place once mention a honourable answer of this l. deputy to part of the chief complaint make by the irish against he so I will most willing have insert his full justification if any such memorial have come to my hand sir William fitz-williams be lord deputy of Ireland sir John Norreys be lord precedent of monster who make his brother sir Thomas his vicepresident and sir Richard bingham be governor of Connaght this lord deputy now again enter the government of Ireland that kingdom be in the best estate that it have be in of long time not only peaceable and quiet so as any the great lord call by letter or messenger ready come to the state there and none of they be know to be any way discontent but also most plentiful in corn cattle and all manner of victual but within three month after his take of the sword some irish inform he that the above name spaniard last year wreck on the coast of Connaght and Ulster have leave with the inhabitant in who hand they fall great store of treasure and other riches this the lord deputy as the irish say do greedy seek to get into his hand but sure he pretend the queen service as may appear by a commission by which he first assay to seize the same this not take any effect he take a journey himself into those part with charge to the queen and country as they say and that in a unseasonable time of the year after Allhallontide where altogether fail of his purpose he bring thence with he as prisoner two of the best affect gentleman to the state in those part who he deem to possess the great part of those riches namely sir Owen Tooly father in law to the earl of Tyrone who have long enjoy a yearly pension of one hundred pound from the queen and have keep Odonnel in a good course of opposition against Tyrlogh Lynnogh oneale and sir John Odogherty of Ulster lord best affect to the English whereof the first refuse as they object to pay for his enlargement continue prisoner till the beginning of sir William russel government who in pity discharge he but the old gentleman heart be first break so as short after he die the second be release after two year restraint not without pay for his liberty as the irish say at this hard usage of those two Ulster gentleman all the great man of the irish especial in those northern part do much repine in the month of May 1590. the earl of Tyrone come into England where he be after a easy manner restrain of his liberty because he come without the lord deputy ann. 1590. licence which fault repair by his submission he be free of his restraint in the month of june the earl agree before the lord to enter bond with good surety of the pale to keep peace with all his neighbour namely sir Tirlogh Lynuogh who since the renounce the title of oneale and yield at the queen intercession the government of those part to the earl be knight and at his return to put in pledge to be choose by the lord deputy and counsel for more assurance hereof and of his loyalty as also the performance of certain article sign by he provide that the pledge shall not lie in the castle but with some gentleman in the pale or merchant in Dublyn and may be change every three month during her majesty pleasure the article be to this effect to continue loyal and keep the peace to renounce the title of oneale and all intermeddle with the neighbour lord that Tyrone shall be limit and make a shire or two with jail to be build for hold of session not to foster with any neighbour lord or any gentleman out of his country not to give aid to the island and irish-scot nor take any of they that if for his defence he need force he shall levy none out of his country without special licence of the state in which case he may have english band to conclude with the lord deputy within ten month about acomposition of rent and service to her majesty for all his country according to the above mention composition of Connaght make in the year 1577. not to impose any exaction without licence of the state on his country above ordinary except it be for necessary force for his defence and that also with licence not to make any road into neighbour country except they be within five day after a prey take that none of the country receive any stealth from neighbour-countries nor steal from they but he to bring forth the thief or drive they out of Tyrone that he execute no man except it be by commission from the lord deputy under the broad seal for martial law and that to be limit that his troop of 50 horse in her majesty pay be keep complete for her service and that beside he answer arise out at every general host that he meddle not with spiritual live nor lay any charge on they not to maintain any (@) or friar in his country not to have intelligence with foreign traitor that he take no black rent of any neighbour to cause the wear of english apparel and that none of his man wear glibbe or long hair that he answer for his brother Tyrlogh Mac Henry captain of the fewes that in time of necessity he sell victual to the fort of blacke-water these he promise to perform upon his honour before the lord in England and that his pledge to be put in shall lie for performance of they to his power and order be give that all the neighbour lord shall be draw to like condition that so they may not spoil Tyrone in the month of july 1590. shane that be the son of shane o neale accuse ann. 1590. Hugh earl of Tyrone of many practice to make himself great in the north and that after the wreck of the above name spaniard he conspire with those which fall into his hand about a league with the king of Spain to aid he against the queen these article the earl answer before the lord in England deny they and avow the malice of Con to proceed of her majesty raise he to be earl of Tyrone and Cons desire to usurp the name of oneale as his father have do which name be labour to extinguish he can have speak nothing more please to this state as he well know and therefore his answer be approve but the event show his dissemble for within two or three year sir Tyrlogh Lynnogh die and then the earl take this title of oneale to himself which be treason by act of parliament in Ireland still excuse himself subtle that he take it upon he leave some other shall usurp it promise to renounce it yet beseech that he may not be urge to promise it upon oath Camden affirm that Hugh ne-gavelocke bastard to shane o neale exhibit these article against the earl who after get he into his hand and cause he to be hang hardly find any in regard of the general reverence bear to the blood of the oneal who will do the office of hangman and that the queen pardon the earl for this fact I doubt not but he write upon good ground and I find good warrant for that I write the same to be exhibit by and both may be reconcile by the exhibit of the petition by Hugh in the name of con. sure I be that the earl dare never enter into rebellion till he have get the son of shane oneale to be his prisoner two of they in this time of sir William fitz-williams his government be now in the castle of Dublyn and if they have be fast keep they be true heir of Tyrone before their father rebellion will have be a strong bridle to keep the earl in obedience but they together with Philip oreigh a dangerous practiser and with the elder son and heir of old Odonnel both imprison by sir John perrot in his government short after escape out of prison be all prisoner of great moment who enlargement give apparent overture to ensue rebellion neither do the irish spare to affirm that their escape be wrought by corruption because one Segar constable of the castle of Dublin by patent have large offer make he to permit the escape of oreigh and acquaint the lord deputy therewith be short after displace and one Maplesdon servant to the lord deputy be put in his place in who time those prisoner escape to return to the orderly course of my relation the earl on the last of August and the same year 1590. do before the lord deputy and counsel of Ireland confirm the above mention article send thither out of England faithful promise by word and under his hand to perform then but still he delay and put off the performance by letter unto both state entreat that equal security may be take of sir Tyrlogh Lynnogh and in general of all the border lord which he know at that time most difficult to effect and by many subtle shift whereof he have plenty about this time Mac mahow chieftain of monaghan die who in his life time have surrender this his country hold by tanistry the irish law into her majesty ann. 1590. hand and receive a regrant thereof under the broad seal of England to he and his heir male and for default of such to his brother Hugh roe mac mahowne with other remainder and this man die without heir male his say brother come upto the state that he may be settle in his inheritance hope to be countenance and cherish as her majesty Patentee but he find as the irish say that he can not be admit till he have promise to give about six hundred cow for such and no other be the irish bribe after he be imprison the irish say for fail in part of this payment and within few day again enlarge with promise that the lord deputy himself will go to settle he in his country of monaghan whither his lordship take his journey short after with he in his company at their first arrival the gentleman be clap in bolt and within two day after indict arraign and execute at his own house all do as the irish say by such officer as the lord deputy carry with he to that purpose the irish say he be find guilty by a jury of soldier but no gentleman or freeholder and that of they four english soldier be suffer to go and come at pleasure but the other be irish kern be keep straight and starve till they find he guilty the treason for which he be condemn be because some two year before he pretend a rent due unto he out of the ferney upon that pretend lovy force and so march into the ferney in warlike manner make a distress for the same which by the english law may perhaps be treason but in that country never before subject to law it be think no rare thing nor great offence the great part of the country be divide between four gentleman of that name under a yearly rent to the queen and as they say not without payment of a good fine under hand the martial sir Henry bagnol have part of the country captain henslowe be make seneschal of the country and have the gentleman chief house with a portion of land and to diverse other small portion of land be assign and the irish spare not to say that these man be all the contriver of his death and that every one pay something for his share hereupon the irish of that name beside the former allegation exclaim that their kinsman be treacherous execute to entitle the queen to his land and to extinguish the name of Mac mahowne and that his substance be divide between the lord deputy and the marshal yea that a pardon be offer to one of the jury for his son be in danger of the law upon condition he will consent to find this his kinsman guilty great part of these exclamation be contain in a complaint exhibit against the lord deputy after his return into England to the lord of her majesty council about the end of the year 1595 in the name of Mac Guire and ever Mac cooly one of the Mac mahowne and chief over the irish in the Ferny to which sir William fit and Williams then sick at his house see his answer in writing there first he avow to the lord that the fact of Mac mahowne be first adjudge treason in England and that his call in question for it be direct from thence and for the manner of proceed herein not prescribe that it be (@) and contrary to their calumnious allegation who complain against he he further answer that the most part of the country be not bestow on the martial sir Henry bagnall but that seven of the chief in that country have the great part of it that three hundred freeholder be raise to her majesty with eight hundred pound yearly rend and that all the country seem then glad of his execution and joyful receive the english law the rest of the complaint he deny and for the bribe of cow in particular do (@) that ever Mac goodly one of the (@) offer he seven thousand cow to make he chief of the name when he may have learn that his mind be not so poor to prefer cow or any bribe before the queen service to return to our purpose certain it be that upon Mac mahowne execution heartburning and loathe of the english government begin to grow in the northern lord against the state and they shun as much as they can to admit any shiriffes or any english to line among they pretend to fear like practice to overthrow they the sixteen of july 1591. the earl of tirone write unto the lord of England excuse himself that sir Tyrlogh Lynnogh be wound by his man while he seek ann. 1591. to prey his country in the same month he suffer his country of Tyrone to be make shire ground be by certain commissioner bound on every side and divide into and barony and the town of Dungannon make the shire town where the goal shall be in the month of October he write again to the lord justify himself against the complaint of the martial sir heury bagnol avow that he have not steal his sister or take she away by force but that after her brother many delay she willing go away with he he marry she and that he have no other wife be lawful divorce from she who the marshal term his wife he complain against the marshal that he reap the benefit of all that in Ulster which by his have be bring to her majesty obedience that he have obtain under the great seal a superiority over Ulster which he exercise over he about this time the Northerno lord be think to have conspire to defend the romish religion for now first among they religion be make the cloak of treason to admit no english shiriffes in their country and to defend their liberty and right against the English in the month of August 1592. the earl of Tyrone by his letter to the lord in ann. 1592. England justify himself against the complaint of sir Tyrlogh Lynnogh apparent show that his son Con oneale do not disturb the commissioner sit in monaghan but that they have one hundred foot for their guard be afraid of two horseman which they discover he write further that he have bring Odonnel into the state who since his abovementioned escape out of prison have stand upon his defence and that he will persuade he to loyalty and in case he be obstinate will serve against he as a enemy and further crafty entreat the lord that he may have the marshal love that they be neighbour may concur the better for her majesty service and that their lordship will approve of his match with the marshal sister for who content he do the rather desire his love in the begin of the year 1593. or about this time a northern lord Mac Guire ann. 1593. begin to declare himself discontent and to stand upon his defence upon the execution of Mac mahowne and the jealousy then conceive by the northern lord against the English this Mac Guire chiestaine of Fermannagh avow that he have give three hundred cow to free his country from a sheriff during the lord deputy government and that not withstand one captain willis be make sheriff of Fermannagh have for his guard one hundred man and lead about some one hundred woman and boy all which live on the spoil of the country hence this barbarous lord take his advantage set upon they and drive they into a church where he will have put they all to the sword if the earl of Tyrone have not interpose his authority and make composition for their life with condition that they shall depart the country whereupon the lord deputy sir William Fitz Williams send the queen force into Fermannagh win Mac guire castle of Exiskillen and proclaim he traitor and the irish avow that the lord deputy there let fall threaten speech in public against the earl of Tyrone call he traitor these speech come to the earl hear he ever after pretend that they be the first cause that move he to misdoubt his safety and to stand upon his defence now first combine himself with Odonnell and the other lord of the north to defend their honour estate and liberty when Tyrone first begin to plot his rebellion he say to have use two notable practice first his man be altogether rude in the use of arm he offer the state to serve the queen against Tyrlogh Lynogh with six hundred man of his own and so obtain six captain to train they call by our man butter captain as live upon cease and by this mean and his own man in pay which he daily change put new untrained man in the room of other he train all his man to perfect use of their arm second pretend to build a fair house which our state think a tie of civility he get licence to transport to Dungannon a great quantity of lead to cover the battlement of his house but ere long employ the same only to make bullet for the war but I return to my purpose sir Henry bagnol Martial of Ireland have former exhibit to the state diverse article of treason practise by the earl of Tyrone who now will not come to the state without a protection to these article the earl answer by letter say that the marshal accuse he upon envy and by suborn witness and that he together with the lord deputy apparent seek his overthrow Further complain that the marshal detain from he his sister portion who he have marry and that according to his former complaint he usurp jurisdiction over all Ulster and in particular exercise it over he yet these article of treason against the earl be believe in England till he offer by his letter to stand to his trial either in England or Ireland and according he answer to the say article before the lord deputy and council at dundalke in such sort as they who have write into England against he now to the contrary write that he have sufficient answer they whereupon the lord of England write to the earl of Tyrone in the month of August of the follow year that they approve his answer and that in their opinion ann. 1594 he have wrong to be so charge and that public before judge and especial that his answer be for a time conceal further they commend he for the token of loyalty he have give in deal with Mac Guire to submit himself exhort he to persist in his good course and charge he the rather for avoid his enemy slander not to meddle with compound of controversy in Ulster out of tirone without the lord deputy special warrant at the same time their lordship write to the lord deputy tax he and the marshal that they have use the earl against law and equity and that he the lord deputy be not indifferent to the earl who offer to come over into England to justify himself thus be the earl clear in show but whether through fear of his enemy or the guiltiness of his conscience he show himself ever after to be diffident of his own safety in the begin of the year 1594 Mac Guire break into open rebellion he enter with force into Connaght where the burke and Orwarke in Letrim common call orwarke country for disobedience to the state have be prosecute by sir Richard bingham governor of that province this foretunner of the great conspirator short after second by Mac mahowne be persuade to enter connaught by Gauranus a priest who the pope forsooth have make primate of all Ireland and be encourage thereunto by his ominate of good success but by the valour of sir Richard bingham the governor Mac Guire be repel with slaughter of many of his man among who this pretend primate be kill against this Mac Guire the earl of Tyrone serve with the queen force and valiant fight be wound in the thigh yet this earl provide for his security about this time imprison the above mention son of shane oneale who have escape out of Dublin castle and if they have be there keep will have be a sure pledge of his obedience neither will he restore they to liberty though he be require so to do but still cover his treacherous heart with ostentation of a fear conceive of his enemy he cease not daily to complain of the lord deputy and marshal envy against he and of wrong do he by the garrison soldier thus the fire of this dangerous rebellion be now kindle by the above name cause to which may be add the hatred of the conquer against the conqueror the difference of religion the love of the irish to Spain whence some of the be descend the extortion of sheriff and sub-sheriffe buy these place the ill government of the church among ourselves and the admit popish priest among the irish and many such like and this fire of rebellion now kindle shall be find hereafter to be increase to a devour flame by slow and slender opposition to the first erruption before they have liberty to combine and know their own strength by not lay hand timely on suspect person of quality to prevent their combine with the rest especial in monster be as yet quiet by entertain and arm of irish man a point of high oversight begin by Sr joh. perrot and increase by Sr Will fitz. Williams the present l. deputy who at the first send of force into Formannagh give power to certain irish man to raise company which they do of their own country man so as this ill custom be after continue it both furnish the enemy with train man and fill our band with such false heart soldier as some doubt whether we have not better have they enemy than friend by a treaty entertain at the very entrance of the rebellion before any blow be strike which make the traitor proud and daunt the heart of good subject by ensue cessation long cotinue and give liberty to the traitor to strengthen their combination and to arm themselves in foreign part and at home whereupon all idle and discontent people have opportunity to draw into Tyrone and the traitor earl of Tyrone have mean to oppress the border lord of country adjoin whereof many feel once his power some for fear some for love join with he beside that the army in the mean time be not only a excessive charge to the queen but lay idle and in stead of hurt the enemy oppress the subject thereby daily drive many into rebellion last for I will not more curious search the cause be not suitable to so brief a narration as I intend the rebellion be nourish and increase by nothing more then frequent protection and pardon grant even to those who have former abuse this mercy so as all enter and continue to be rebel with assurance to be receive to mercy at their pleasure whereof they spare not to brag and this hearten the rebel no less than it discourage the subject this present year 1594 about the month of August sir William fitz-williams 1594 sir William Russel lord deputy the lord deputy be recall into England sir William Russell take the sword about this time Ulster man in open hostility distress her majesty force and Tyrone so I will hereafter call he deserve no addition of title have long absent himself from the state be undoubted repute a party in their rebellion when his sudden and voluntary appearance before this new lord deputy at Dublin in the very first month of his government make many hope better of he he most assure promise all humble obedience to the queen as well before the state at Dublin in his own person as to the lord in England by his letter and make his most humble submission to her maresty beseech to be restore to her former grace from which he have fall by the lie slander of his enemy not by any his just desert the martial sir Henry bagnol be then ready to prove before the lord deputy article of high treason against Tyrone and to avow that he send mac Guire with his primate into Connaght that he have secret intelligence with the traitor Mac Guire and Odonnell and have communicate counsel with they and give they aid in the waste of Monnaghan and the besiege of Eniskellin by his brother cormac mac baron and by Con his own base son and that he by threat have draw the captain of Kilulto and Kilwarny from their faith and allegiance to the queen it be in council debate whether Tyrone shall be stay to answer hereunto and the lord deputy be of opinion he shall be stay but most of the counsellor either for idle fear or inclination of love to Tyrone think best to dismiss he for that time and the counsel of these as more in number and best experience in irish affair the lord deputy follow this much displease the queen since this fox treasonable practice be now so apparent and herself have forewarn that in case he come to the state he shall be stay till he have clear himself of all impute crime and the lord in England by their letter thence sharp reprove the lord deputy for so dismiss he which may give the rebel just cause to think that they dare not charge he with treason for fear of his force and their lordship profess to doubt that tyrones performance will not be such as may warrant this act the lord deputy short after take the field and leave for martial cause the earl of ormond for civil cause the lord chancellor to govern Lemster and those part in his absence draw the force into Fermannagh that he may relieve eniskellin and expel mac Guire out of his country this winter follow it seem there be some negotiation on both side about peace for in the month of February the lord of England write to the lord deputy of her majesty dislike of certain write send over from Odonnel and sir Arthur oneale namely that in their petition they include the pardon of mac Guire and orwarke common call Orurke that they indent with the lord deputy that he shall come to dundalke within a month and especial that the lord deputy by sir Edward More shall desire a fortnight more for his come thither their lordship also signify that the queen send over 2000 old soldier which have serve under general Norreys in Britanny give order that they shall be divide into hundred and so many captain beside that 1000 soldier be levy in England to be send thither and because their lordship judge that all the practice of the northern lord come out of tyrones school how soever he gross dissemble the contrary their lordship advise the lord deputy to offer Odonnel pardon so as he will sever himself from Tyrone and that the rather because he be put into rebellion by sir John perrot imprison he without any cause Tyrone hear that supply of soldier and namely the old soldier of Brittany be come for Ireland and that garrison of english be to be plant at the castle of Ballishanon and belike lie upon the lake earn think it no long time to temporize wherefore about this time of this year end or the first entrance of the year 1595 he draw his force together and in open hostility sudden assault the fort of blackwater build upon the passage into Tyrone on the south side and take ann. 1595. the same raze it and break down the bridge and now the northern rebel with banner display enter the brennye yet at this time Tyrone subtle make suit for pardon and promise the treasurer at war sir Henry Wallop that he will continue his allegiance to the queen at this time likewise feagh Mac Hugh Walter Reagh and many lemster man begin to enter into action of hostility against the English the lord deputy who see this storm of rebellion will lie heavy on his l. deputy l. general together shoulder in his letter to the I ords in England have let fall a request that some olderperienced commander may be send over to he for his better assistance mean no doubt such a captain as shall be command by the supreme authority of the lord deputy but the lord either mistake his intent or because they so judge it best for her majesty service send over sir John Norreys a great leader and famous in the war of the low country and France give he the title of lord general with absolute command over military affair in the absence of the l. deputy this great commander be not like to be willing command by any who have not bear as great or great place in the war than himself so as whether through emulation grow between he and the lord deputy or a decline of his fortune incident to the great leader howsoever he behave himself most valiant and wise in some encounter against Tyrone and the chief rebel yet he do nothing against they of moment about the begin of june the l. deputy and the lord general draw their force towards armagh and now Tyrone have send letter of submission to they both entreat the lord general more special for a mild proceed against he so as he may not be force to a headlong breach of his loyalty these letter shall have be deliver at dundalke but the marshal bagnol intercept they stay the messenger at the Newrye till the lord deputy return at which time because in this journey Tyrone have be proclaim traitor he refuse to receive they in respect of her majesty honour yet short after at tyrones instance sir Henry wallop treasurer at war and sir Robert Gardner chief justice of Ireland be by commission appoint to confer with he and his confederate rebel Tyrone in this conference complain of the marshal for his usurp jurisdiction in Ulster for deprive he of the queen favour by slander for intercept his late letter to the lord deputy and lord general protest that he never negotiate with foreign prince till he be proclaim traitor his humble petition be that he and he may be pardon and have free exercise of religion grant which notwithstanding have never fore either be punish or inquire after that the marshal shall pay he one thousand pound for his dead sister his wife portion that no garrison nor sheriff shall be in his country that his troop of fifty horse in the queen pay may be restore to he and that such as have prey his country may make restitution Odonnell magnify his father and progenitor service to the crown complain that captain boyne send by sir John perrot with his company into his country under pretence to reduce the people to civility and be well entertain of his father have beside many other injury raise a bastard to be Odonnel and that sir John perrot by a ship send thither have take himself by force and long imprison he at Dublin and that sir William Fitz Williams have wrongful keep Owen o. toole above mention seven year in prison his petition be for pardon to he and he and for freedom of religion that no garrison or sheriff may be place in his country and that certain castle and land in the county of sligo may be restore to he shane Mac Brian Mac phelime oneale complain of a island take from he by the earl of Essex and that he have be imprison till he surrender to the martial a barony his ancient inheritance Hugh Mac Guire complain of insolence do by garrison soldier and by a sheriff who beside kill one of his near kinsman Brian Mac Hugh Oge and Mac mahowne so the irish call the chief of that name survive and ever Mac cooly of the same family of Mac mahowne complain of the abovementioned unjust execution of Hugh roe Mac mahowne in the government of sir William Fitz Williams the commissioner judge some of their petition equal other they refer to the queen pleasure but when on the queen part they propound to the rebel some article to be perform by they they be grow so insolent as judge they unequal the conference be break off with a few day truce grant on both side when the queen for spare of blood have resolve to give they any reasonable condition this truce end the lord deputy and the lord general about the eighteen of july draw the force to armagh with such terror to the rebel as Tyrone leave the fort of blackwater burn the town of Dungannon and pull down his house there burn all village and betake himself to the wood they proclaim Tyrone traitor in his own country and leave a guard in the church of armagh they for want of victual return to Dublin and by the way place a garrison in Alonaghan and when the army come near to dundalke the lord deputy according to his instruction from England yield the command of the army to the lord general and leave he with the force in the northern border return to Dublin the three of September Hugh earl of Tyrone Hugh o Donnel Bryan o rourke Hugh Mac Guire Bryan Mac mahowne sir Arthur oneale art Mac baron Henry Oge oneale Turlogh Mac Henry oneale cormac Mac baron tyrones brother Con oneale tyrones base son Bryan art Mac Brian and one Francis Mounfoord be for form of law indict though absent and condemn judicial of treason in the county of Lowthe near the border of the north. from this time the lemster rebel begin to grow very strong for Feegh Mac Hugh of the obirn and Donnel Spanniah of the cavanaghs when they be decline and in want of munition be not prosecute but upon feign submission be receive into protection and so have mean to renew their force and supply their want so as this year about this month of September they begin to oppress all the subject from the gate almost of Dublin to the county of wexford the most ancient english county and ever much care for by the queen which they spoil want force to defend it and so deprive the english soldier of great relief he may have find therein the like may be say of the Oconnors in Ophalia general Norris be leave by the lord deputy on the northern border with full command of the army the winter pass without any great exploit there be in many thing no small emulation between the lord deputy and he and no loss in tyrones particular the lord deputy seem to the lord general to be unequal and too tharpe against Tyrone with who he wish no treaty of peace to be hold which he wise do have experience his false subtlety and know that he seek delay only till he can have aid from Spain but the lord general whether it be in emulation of the lord deputy or in his favour and love to Tyrone be willing to reclaim he by a gentle course which that crafty fox can well nourish in he and it seem some part of the winter pass while this project be negotiate between they for in the begin of the year 1596 a commission be procure out of England whereby her majesty though just offend with Tyrone and his associate about ann. 1596. their demand in the former conference with sir Henry wallop and sir Robert Gardner yet in regard of their letter of humble submission since that time present to she do signify her gracious pleasure to sir John Noreis lord general and sir Geoffrey Fenton her majesty secretary for Ireland give they authority to promise pardon of life and restore of land and good to the say lord seek with due humility her royal mercy and to hear they with promise of favourable consideration in all their complaint and thus much the commissioner signify to Tyrone and Odannell by captain Sant Leger and captain Warren send of purpose unto they with instruction date the eleven of April this present year 1596 and with reference of other particular to a meeting appoint to be at dundalke the twenty of the same month Tyrone at dundalke before these commissioner crave the queen mercy on his knee sign with his hand a most humble submission in writing vow faith in the presence of almighty God who see into the secret of all man heart and to use still his own word most humble crave her majesty mercy and pardon on the knee of his heart his first petition for liberty of religion be utter reject for the second touch freedom from garrison and sheriff he be answer that her majesty will not be prescribe how to govern in the three intercede for orelyes pardon it be dislike that he shall capitulate for other yet give hope of his pardon upon his own submission for the four concern the jurisdiction of armagh the answer be that her majesty will reserve all the bishop right for the five concern the free of shane oneales son it be refer to her majesty further pleasure final he promise to (@) from aid the rebel and from intermeddle with the neighbour lord to make his country a shire to admit a sheriff to renounce the title of oneale to confess upon his pardon all his intelligence with foreign prince and all his past action which may concern the good of the State to rebuild the fort and bridge of blackwater and to relieve the garrison for ready money at all time to deliver in sufficient pledge to dismiss all his force and to pay such reasonable fine to her majesty use as shall be think meet by her majesty Hugh Odonnel at the same time do agree to diverse article for the good of his country and make his like humble submission the like do Hugh mac Guire Bryan Hugh ever Oge roe mac cooly Bryan Orewark call Ororke shane Mac Bryan Philip o Reyly and other to each one be give under the commissioner hand a promise of her majesty pardon upon put in of pledge and proclamation be make to give notice hereof to all the queen subject that in the meantime no act of hostility may be do against any of those who have thus mitted themselves thus the Ulster rebel by a submission too honest to be true intend by they while pledge be expect and pardon draw be free from the prosecution of the queen force this summer and even at this time do Tyrone solicit aid in Spain and two or three messenger come secret to the rebel from thence by who many of they as Ororke Mac William etc. send a writing sign to the king of Spain covenant that if he will send sufficient force they will join they to he and if he will at all relieve they in the mean time they will refuse all condition of peace but Tyrone though consent yet be too crafty to sign this covenant yea crafty he send the king of Spain answer to the lord deputy while he notwithstanding rely on the promise succour I find nothing of moment do this summer by the force with the general be restrain by the last agreement at dundalke only about the end of August he write out of Connaght unto the lord deputy complain of diverse want and desire more force to be send he to who the lord deputy answer that his lordship have warrant to supply some of his want in the country and deny the send of any force to he because himself be to go into the field by this time the rebel of Lemster be as I former mention grow strong Feogh Mac Hugh break his protection enter into act of hostility and he together with the O More o connors o birnes o tool the cavenagh butler and the chief name of Connaght animate by the success of Ulster man combine together and demand to have the barbarous title of o and Mac together with land they claim to be restore to they in the mean time spoil all the country on all side about the month of january sir Richard bingham governor of Connaght who have valiant beat Ororke out of his country and prosecute the bourk and other rebel be call into England upon complaint of the irish and sir conyer Clifford be send to govern Connaght this gentleman complain off by the irish be valiant and wise but some of our english statesman think he too severe and that he have thereby drive many into rebellion howsoever himself very well experience in the country and those who best understand the irish nature find nothing so necessary for keep they in obedience as severity nor so dangerous for the increase of murder and outrage as indulgence towards they his answer to their complaint can not be so admit as for the time some discountenance fall not on he which reward of service he constant bear till in short time after to his great grace the state think fit again to use his service in a place of great command in the army sir John Norreys lord general sir George Bourcher master of her majesty ordinance and sir Geoffrey Fenton her majesty secretary for Ireland be by commission direct to treat again with Tyrone do by their letter date the twenty of januarie remember he of the favour he have receive at the last treaty at dundalke and charge he as former he have be charge with high ctimes since that commit by he to the violation of the article then agree on therefore advise he that since they be for her majesty service to draw to the border he will there testify to they his penitency for offence do since his pardon by such a humble and hearty submission as they may recommend into England from he Tyrone by his answer of the two and twenty of januarie acknowledge under his hand her majesty mercy therein extend to he and confess offence and breach of the article there sign withal desire they to examine the wrong and provocation by which he have be drive thereunto and protest his sorrow for these offence the same day he meet the commissioner near dundalke where he be on the one side of the brook they on the other he put of his hat and hold it with great reverence in his hand say to they that he be come thither not only to show his duty to they as her majesty commissioner but his inward desire to be make and continue a subject when he will have remember the wrong since his late pardon provoke he to disloyalty they cut he off by remember he of all the benefit and that of his last pardon receive from the queen which shall have counterpoise his wrong and have keep he in duty he confess this with show of great remorse and protest before God and heaven that there be no prince not creature who he honour as he do her majesty nor any nation of people that he love or trust more than the English protest further that if her majesty will please to accept of he again as a subject and to take such course as he may be so continue thus still he reserve pretence of wrong to shadow his future disloialty than he doubt not but to redeem all his fault pass with some notable service beside he give answer to diverse question and sign they after with his hand first ask what message and letter have pass between Spain and he he answer never to have receive any but encouragement from Spain and assurance of a army to aid he that he never have further contract with the spaniard and that he have send the king of Spain letter above mention to the lord deputy and counsel that he never receive thence any money or aught of value nor any of his confederate to his knowledge only Odonnel have some fifteen barrel of powder whereof he shall have have a portion but never have it second for the late submitty pardon and pledge he undertake that with all speed the pledge shall be send to Dublin with agent to sue out the pardon grant in the last treaty at dundalke three for his make o kealy he vow that the gentleman of the country make he and that he will hereafter never meddle in the cause of the Brenny four for the rebel of lemster and the butler he answer that he never have confederacy with any but Feogh Mac Hugh and for the butler he never have any thing to do with they five for agent in Spain he deny to have any or to know any his confederate have six for his jealousy of the state he avow it to be upon just cause which he will after make know this do he desire captain Warren may come over the brook to he and then by he he request that himself may come over to the commissioner in token of his faithful heart to her majesty which grant he with great reverence salute they and with hat in hand lift up his eye to heaven desire God to take vengeance on he if her majesty vouchsafe to make he a subject and to cause the article of dundalke to be keep to he he will not continue faithful and desire never to see Christ in the face if he mean not as he speak he confess that the spanish ship late arrive in the north have bring Odonnel the king letter signify that he hear the earl of tirone to be dead and the irish to have receive a great overthrow desire to be advertise of their State and that Odonnel before his come have give answer that if the king send a army he will take his part and hope the like of the other irish. but at his come that the spanish captain excuse that the king have not write to he he only tell he that promise have not be keep with he by the english and therefore he will not refuse the king promise aid and with many execration swear that the captain leave neither munition nor treasure with he and that he never receive any thing from the king of Spain but that letter ahove mention which he send to the lord deputy and that he never write but three letter into Spain all about one time and as he think all intercept last he vehement deny to have incite any monster man to rebellion since his last pardon so with like reverence as former he take his leave upon advertisement hereof into England the commissioner receive ample power to conclude all thing with Tyrone thus much they make know to he by letter send to he by his old friend captain Warren the nine of march with instruction to appoint the second of April the day of meeting at dundalke which Tyrone accept with show of joy to be receive to her majesty mercy the sweetness whereof he have often experience and of fear to be pursue by her force which he profess himself not able to resist but by his letter the fifteen of march he make doubt of meeting pretend that his pledge be not change according to covenant nor restitution make he by those that have prey his country and that his confederate can not come so soon the commissioner reply by letter the two and twenty of march that these be but delay since the pledge at the meeting upon his put in his elder son for pledge shall be restore and he in all thing reasonable satisfy protest that if he refuse this occasion they can do no more for he since her majesty will be no long abuse by his fair promise and delay add that he must conform himself to the direction they have and can not alter master secretary write out of England unto the commissioner the two and twenty of march that her majesty be displease to have the treaty thus delay and charge to have the meeting in a town as a submission of the rebel not in the field as a parley that her majesty prepare for the war resolve not to have any more treaty if this take not effect last desire they to acquaint the lord deputy with all their direction and the issue and to excuse his not write to his lordship think that the commissioner be not at Dublyn with he upon the ten of April in the year 1597 the commissioner again press Tyrone by letter not to slack his own great good by delay and appoint for an. 1597. the last day of meeting the sixteen of that present month and that his confederate not able then to come shall draw after as soon as they can protest that this be the last time that they will write unto he Tyrone on the seventeen of April send his reason of not come first justify his relapse into disloialty by the truce not observe to he and because restitution be not make he of prey take from he which be promise then excuse his not meet because his pledge by the truce be from three month to three month to be change be still detain yea his pledge the second time put in be keep together with the first and say that he dare not come to the lord general because many promise by he make be not keep he know it be much against his honourable mind and so can not be persuade but that the lord general be overrule by the lord deputy so as he can not make good his promise without the lord deputy consent who show malice to he and be no doubt the cause of all the breach of such promise as have be make unto he again in regard he hear that the lord bourgh be to come over lord deputy who be altogether unknown to he he protest to fear that the act of the lord general with he will not be make good wish that rather the lord general may be continue in his command for than he will be confident of a good conclusion final he desire a meeting near dundalke the six and twenty of April but this appointment for the day be against the last final resolution and for the place against her majesty direction there be no more speech of this treaty in the mean time sir William Russell lord deputy by the manage of those and like affair find himself not due countenance out of England in the place he sustain have make earnest suit to be call home and according about the end of may he be revoke and the lord bourgh so he himself write other write burke and Camden write borough come over lord deputy the ill success of the treaty (@) (@) and small progress of the war together with this unexpected change of the lord deputy come with supreme authority as well in martial as civil cause break the heart of sir John norryes lord general a leader as worthy and famous as England breed in our age of late according to vulgar speech he have displease the earl of Essex than a great favourite in court and by his merit possess of the superintendency in all martial affair for sir John norrye have embrace the action of breast fort in Brittany and the war in those part when the earl himself have purpose to entertain they and prevail against the earl by undertake they with less force than the earl desire for the same and it be think that the earl have prefer the lord bourgh of purpose to discontent he in regard the say lord bourgh have have a private quarrel with the say general in England and that beside the superior command of this lord though otherwise most worthy yet of less experience in the war then the general have can not but be unsupportable to he esteem one of the great captain of his time and yet have inferior command of the presidentship of monster in the same kingdom certain upon the arrival of this new lord deputy present general norrye be command to his government of monster and not to stir thence without leave when he come thither this grief so wrought upon his high spirit as it apparent break his brave and former undaunted heart for without sickness or any public sign of grief he sudden die in the embrace of his dear brother sir Thomas Norreys his vicepresident within some two month of his come into monster the lord bourgh at his entry into the place of lord deputy find all the north in rebellion except seven castle with their town or village all but one lie towards the sea namely newry Knockfergus carlingford greene-castle armagh Dondrom and Olderfleet and all Connaght be likewise in rebellion together with the earl of ormonds nephew the butler in monster in this month of May Ororke be send into England by the king of scot and there execute this Ororke seem to have be expel his country when sir Richard bingham be governor of Connaght but those of his name and the chief of they usurp the country of letrym still continue rebel Tyrone hitherto with all subtlety and a thousand sleight abuse the state when he see any danger hang over he by feign countenance and false word pretend humble submission and hearty sorrow for his villainy but as soon as opportunity of pursue he be omit or the force be of necessity to be draw from his country with the terror of they all his loyalty vanish yea he fail not to mingle secret the great counsel of mischief with his humble submission and these course have be nourish by the sloth of our leader the frugality of some of our counsellor and the queen inbred lenity yet of all other he have most abuse the late lord general love to he and his credulity which special grow out of his love now of this new lord deputy by letter he request a truce or cessation which it seem good to the lord deputy to grant for a month in regard of the conveniency of her majesty present affair not any way to gratify the rebel for he have no purpose to entertain more speech of his submission or to slack the pursuit of he and his confederate to which he be whole bend he see the lamentable effect which these cessation together with protection have hitherto produce and among other evil do special resolve to avoid they therefore assoon as the month of truce be expire the lord deputy aswell by his first action to give lustre and ominous presage to his government as because he judge it best for the service to strike at the head present draw the force towards Tyrone the irish in a fastness near armagh so they call straight passage in wood where to the natural strength of the place be add the art of interlace the low bow and cast the body of tree across the way oppose the passage of the english who make their way with their sword and find that the irish resolute assault will easy give ground then the lord deputy assault the fort of blackewater former build by the english upon the passage to Dungannon whence the eurle at he first enter into rebellion have by force expel the english as careful as he will have drive poison from his heart this fort he soon win and repair the same put a company of english soldier into it to guard it but (@) the lord deputy with the whole army be render thanks to God for this good succesle the (@) show themselves out of the thick wood near adjoin on the north-side of the fort so as the prayer be interrupt by call to arm the english enter (@) and prevail against they drive they to styeinto the thick of their den in this conflict be kill Francis Vaughan brother to the lord deputy wife and Robert Turnour sergeant mastor of the army and two foster brother to Henry (@) of Kildare who with his troop of horse valiant serve upon the rebel and take the death of his foster brother so to heart after the education of the irish as he short after die many also be wound among who Thomas walker be of chief name when the lord deputy first resolve to draw up to blackewater he send direction to sir conyers Clifford to come up with the Connaght force by the way of Ballyshainnon and to meet he there which he in like sort attempt but be over match by the rebel lie in his way can not pierce so far but be force to retire and by that retreat win great reputation to himself and the man under he for have with he some six or seven hundred foot only of which part be of the old Britan soldier and be assail by more than 2000 rebel during thirty mile march he valiant repel they and safe retire to the garrison the lord deputy lean the fort at the blacke-water well guard to the charge of captain Thomas Williams withdraw the force towards the pale now the rubel toss between hope fear and shame resolve to besiege the fort and Tyrone think his reputation lose if he recover it not and so with joint force they compass and assay lead the same whereof the lord deputy be advertise with all possible expedition gather the force to lead they to the relief of that fort and the rebel hear of his lordship approach quit the siege of the fort and retire into their strength whereupon the lord deputy march forward and have pass the blackwater fort and purpose to enter and pass the pace lead to Dungannon tyrones chief house he fall sudden sick and be carry back in his horse litter to armagh and thence to the newry die in the way to the great joy of the rebel deject with his sharp prosecution and bold adventure and to the no less grief of the english erect with hope of good success howsoever many of good judgement hold his purpose of pass to Dungannon very dangerous and altogether fruitless since no garrison be plant to gain ground no other issue can be hope in the best event than a brag of courage in pass to tyrones chief feat which no other deputy have yet attempt and as they great commend the lord deputy valour in these action so they fear the engage and loss of the queen army by this or some like bold attempt after his death sir Thomas Norreys lord precedent of monster be under the great seal of Ireland provisional make lord justice of the kingdom as the customeis lord justice in such sudden change who repair to Dublin and there execute his place for one month as I think of September and no long for he be sick and cast down in mind by the great sorrow he have conceive for the late death of his worthy brother make great suit to the queen and the lord in England to be ease of this burden of be lord justice and to have leave to retire himself to his government of the province of monster and so Adam loftus lord chancellor of Ireland and lord archbishop of Dublin lord lieutenant and lord instice and Robert Gardner chief justice of Ireland by letter out of England the thirteen of October be make lord justice for the civil government and the earl of ormond with title of lord lieutenant of the army be authorize to command in chief for all martial affair Tyrone after his old custom fly unto the lord lieutenant with protestation of loyalty and complain of wrong enforce his disloyal course which his lordship advertise into England receive authority from thence to treat with Tyrone about his submission have sir Geoffrey Fenton secretary of Ireland join with he for a assistant hereupon ensue a meeting at dundalke on the 22 of December where Tyrone make his most humble submission in writing acknowledge her majesty great mercy in give he and his associate their pardon upon former submission and upon the knee of his heart as he write profess most hearty penitency for his disloyalty and especial his foul relopse thereinto humble befeech the lord lieutenant to be a mean to her sacred majesty for his pardon withal make know his grievance which how soever they can not justify his offence yet may in some measure qualify the (@) thereof and till these may be book to be send over with his submission most humble crave of his lordship to grant a truce or cessation of arm for eight week follow and further to the end it may appear that his submission proceed from his heart promise that for the time of this cessation there shall be no impediment give to her majesty minister bring victual to blackwater fort yea that for a poor token of his humble duty he will voluntary give to the hand of the captain forty beef and suffer the soldier to cut and fetch in wood or any other provision for his performance whereof he offer present to give pledge to his lordship the same day he subscribe the follow article propound to he by the lord lieutenant first he promise for he and his associate faithful to keep her majesty peace during the cessation second that he will present recall all Ulster man send by he into Lemster leave those who shall not obey his direction to the lord lieutenant discretion three it any during the truce shall break into rebellion he promise not to aid they so as none depend on his truce be in the meantime take in by the state without his consent four he agree to a general liberty of buy necessary for his man in the pale and for the queen subject in Ulster and nothing to be forcible take on either side five that upon pretend wrong no revenge be take but restitution be make within ten day after complaint six that during the truce he shall have no intelligence with the king of Spain or other foreign prince but acquaint the state with any message he shall receive or project he shall hear seven that he shall present draw a book of his grievance such as he can prove without mention of frivolous matter unworthy her sacred majesty view eight that he will deliver into the Fortforty beef and give safe conduct to her majesty minister to victual the say fort of blackwater and suffer the soldier to cut and fetch wood on the southside of armagh and for all other necessary permit they to agree with the owner so as they come not of themselves into his country but have his man with they in company nine that any prey be track into his country he shall make restitution and deliver the thief to be execute and if any be stop from follow of his tract the stopper shall answer the good so track which course the lord lieutenant promise likewise to hold towards he and his associate the four and twenty of December Tyrone advertise the lord lientenant that he serve the fort with forty beef but the captain have refuse ten of they wherein his discretion be tax by the lord lieutenant since they be of voluntary gift yet Tyrone promise to send ten other of the best he have in lieu of they the eighteen of February Brian Oge orwarke common call Ororke lord of Letrym common call ororke country submit himself in a great assembly on his knee to her majesty before sir conyers Clifford governor of Connaght subscribe further to these article first that he and his follower promise in all humbleness to perform all duty to her majesty as become good subject second that he will receive her majesty sheriff and yield they all due obedience three that he will pay to her majesty her composition or rent and yield to her highness all service according to his new patent to be grant four that he shall send out of his country all stranger to their own welling place five that he will apprehend all rebel thief or malefactor come into his country send they and their good to the governor six that he will deliver pledge for his sept or family and the chief sept with he within twenty day hereof sir conyers Clifford advertise the lord justice pray that in regard of the strength and fastness of Ororke country he may not be discontent with have take from he for relief of the army without payment of ready money for they since that course have already grieve all the submitty further he show that the country of Ororke be most necessary to be defend for howsoever it be hold by sir Richard bingham the last governor as by conquest upon expel of the above mention Ororke yet than it be all waste so as the rebel can make little use of it whereas now it be most replenish with cattle and therefore like to be assault by Tyrone and Odennel incense against Ororke by reason of this his submission beside that the queen force can lie no where so fit for service as upon the earn nor there be relieve but by Ororke nor receive relief with his contentment but by pay ready money last he show that all the people upon the earn and in those part except Mac William have submit themselves to her majesty and deliver pledge for their loyalty be glad to live under her majesty law and only terrify with the burden of relieve the soldier without payment for their cattle therefore he desire that two of the privy counsel may be send over to take knowledge of such grievance as the submitty shall present unto they and to take order for their satisfaction these goodly submission have all the same issue as follow in that of the famous faith-breaker Tyrone since the last meeting of the lord lieutenant with Tyrone at dundalke his lordship have send over into England tyrones humble submission and the book of his grievance and have receive authority from her majesty to make a final conclusion with the rebel and now at another meeting in dundalke on the fifteen of march the lord lieutenant signify to Tyrone that her majesty by his humble submission have be induce again to receive he to mercy and to give he and all the inhabitant of Tyrone her gracious pardon upon condition follow first that he renew his humble submission to the lord lieutenant on her majesty behalf in some public place 2. that he promise due obedience of a subject and not to intermeddle with the irish nor his adherent not only hereafter but now leave they to themselves that they may become humble suitor for their own pardon in which case it be promise they also 3. that he dispierce his force upon receipt of his pardon and dismiss all stranger irish scot or other 4. that he renounce the name and title of oneale 5. not to intermeddle with her majesty vriaghte so the irish call the border lord who the Ulster tyrant have long claim to be their vassal 6. that he build up again at his own charge the fort and bridge of blackewater and furnish the soldier with victual as former he do 7. that he deliver to the lord lieutenant the son of shane oneale who be her majesty prisoner till break out they fall into his hand and be imprison by he 8. to declare faithful all intelligence with Spain and to leave it 9 that he receive a sheriff for Tyrone as all other country do 10. that he put in his elder son for pledge and at all time come to the state be call 11. that he pay a fine in part of satisfaction for his ofsence according to her majesty pleasure 12. that he aid no rebel nor meddle with the inhabitant on the east side of the Ban yet so as he may enjoy any land or lease he have there 13. that he receive not any disloyal person but send such to the chief governor to the first and second article Tyrone agree so as time may be giveu for the other lord his associate to assemble that they may herein lay no imputation on he to the three he agree crave a general passport for all such stranger to the four he agree for the five he say that he desire nothing of the vriaght but such duty as they yield since his grandfather time to the six he agree the seven he refuse because he have not those prisoner from the State to the eight he agree to the nine he agree according to the statute appoint a gentleman of the country to be choose yet crave for (@) for a small time the ten be refuse for the pledge in particular (@) the eleven he agree to a (@) of five hundred cow yet pray the lord lieutenant to (@) (@) to her majesty for the remittall thereof to the twelve he agree (@) (@) the last he agree provide that he will deliver no man to the state who come to he for cause of conscience (@) final in regard Odonnell and other of Tyrone (@) do not then appear and in that respect the lord lieutenant have be please to grant he further day (@) for ten of April follow he promise upon his (@) (@) (@) (@) and by his hand write that in case they or any of they shall not then appear and submit themselves yet he at that time will (@) submission and humble crave and receive her majesty gracious pardon and go (@) with all thing requisite for a perfect conclusion and to deliver in two pledge of his faith to be choose out of a schedule present to the lord lieutenant the same to be change according to the agreement and if the moor and conners for who he have obtain protection shall violate this (@) that he will no way give aid or assistance to they hereupon at the instance of the lord lieutenant the lord justice cause tyrones pardon to be draw and seal with the great seal of Ireland bear date the eleven of April in the forty year of her majesty reign and of our lord the year 1598. Tyrone receive his general pardon but continue still his distoyall course never an. 1598. plead the same so as upon his above mention (@) in September 1595. you shall find he after (@) in the year 1600. the irish kern be at the first rude soldier so as two or three of they be employ to discharge one piece and hitherto they have subsist especial by treacherous tender of submission but now they be grow ready in manage their piece and bold to skirmish in bog and woody passage yea this year and the next follow become so disastrous to the english and successful in action to the irish as they shake the english government in this kingdom till it totter and want little of fatal ruin Tyrone want not pretence to frustrate this late treaty and to return to his foreman disloyalty and the defection of all other submitty depend on he follow his revolt first he send aid to Phelim mac Feogh chief of the obirne the son of Feoghmac Hugh kill in sir William russel time to the end he may make the war in lemster against the english and because the english fort of blackewater be a great eye sore to he lie on the chief passage into his country he assemble all his force and assault the same but captain Thomas Williams with his company under he so valiandy repel the great multitude of the assailant with slaughter of many and the most hardy attempt to scale sort which be only a deep trench or wall of earth to lodge some one hundred soldier as they utter discourage from assail it resolve to besiege it a far off and know they want victual presume to get it by famine this captain and his few warder do with no less courage suffer hunger and have eat the few horse they have live upon herb grow in the ditch and wall suffer all extremity till the lord lieutenant in the month of August send sir Henry bagnol Martial of Ireland with the most choice company of foot and horse troop of the english army to victual this fort and to raise the rebel siege when the english enter the pace and thick wood beyond armagh on the east side Tyrone with all the rebel force assemble to he prick forward with rage of envy and settle rancour against the marshal assail the english and turn his full force against the marshal person have the success to kill he valiant fight among the thick of the rebel whereupon the english be dismay with his death the rebel obtain a great victory against they I term it great since the english from their first arrival in that kingdom never have receive so great a overthrow as this common call the defeat of blackewater thirteen valiant captain the defeat of
