Totall of horse 1198. Totall of foot 14150. from dundalke the lord deputy with his servant and voluntary horseman ride to Dublin the seventeen of November within few day upon sir Arthur savage his intreatic to go for England about his private affair his lordship give he licence and appoint sir John Barkely to supply his place of provisionarie governor of the province of Connaght at the same time his lordship write into England for authority to pass unto certain submitty their country with reservation of her majesty right and some other condition for her profit and service more particular on the behalf of connor roe Mac guyre who be put from the Chiefery of his country by Tyrone have quit all his possession and good to come to the queen service when Tyrone have two of his son for pledge of which the elder late escape from the rebel have likewise submit himself and they both have serve valiant in the late northern journey so as the father have his horse kill under he and the son kill three rebel with his own hand and from thence both go into fermanagh have draw many of that country to follow they in the queen service divert all the country from assist Tyrone beside that in a late skirmish they have take Cormock tyrones brother elder son a young man of the great hope in the north who the rebel purpose to create oneale after tyrones death for which respect he be a better pledge than any of tyrones son this youth they have bring to the lord deputy with great hazard to convoy he and that when 3000. pound and other ample condition be offer they for his ransom in the same month of November many of the northern rebel with great troop among they a monster man Piers Lacie of english race a famous rebel draw into the Brenny mean to pass to the shannon side and so into monster after they have strengthen the break rebel of the pale with some assistance but this their passage be so stop as it take no effect the six of December his lordship be advertise from a honourable friend in court that his late proceed be mention by all man with much honour and most of all by the queen who utter to himself the most gracious and kind speech of his lordship and the most extol his valour and worthy part that ever he have hear she use of any till this time the rebel of the mountain near Dublyn call the glinne give alarm almost every night in the suburb of Dublyn but the time when the insolency of some of they shall be chasten be now come the obirne have Phelim Feogh the chief of their sept after the death of Feogh mac Hugh former mention inhabit the glinne border on the plain of Dublyn extend some four or five mile that way and these be near than the o tool and other their confederate be most insolent upon that city and the counsel there reside when the lord deputy be far off in any service with the horseman now his lordship be purpose to scourge they and according to his singular secrecy do so keep his counsel from divulge and so cunning mask his intent as he come upon they when they be most secure it be confident give out that his lordship mean present to undertake some service against the omore of Leax and Oconnors of Ophalia and to that purpose mean to lie with his household at Monastreven a great house keep by a constable for the queen yea to make this project more believe his lordship send arras hang and many provision to that house and now the force have be refresh his lordship the twenty two of December be Monday ride to the nasse twelve mile distant from Dublyn where the rendezvous be appoint that day for the lemster garrison for it be fit those border on the north shall be leave strong on Wednesday his lordship send most of his household right forward to Monastreven thirteen mile distant but himself with the rest of his servant and the force sudden turn on the left hand into the glinne and after a day and night tedious march over steep mountain cover with snow he arrive on Thursday be Christmas day at Phelim Feogh his house so sudden as his wife and elder son be take and himself hardly escape at a back window and naked into the wood where he keep a cold Christmas while my lord live plentiful in his house with such provision as be make for he and his bonnagh and kern to keep a merry Christmas to vent his anger he daily offer slight skirmish upon advantage but his heart be nothing ease therewith be continual beat his lordship with the queen force lay in this country till about the twenty of january in which time his troop spoil and ransack the country of Rannelagh and Cashay sweep away the most part of their cattle and good burn all their corn and almost all their house leave little or nothing to relieve they and to finish the work his lordship plant two strong garrison upon they the one at wicklo on the east side not able to come near because a ship with our tool and instrument be beat back by ill weather and can not arrive in time the other at Tullogh upon the west so as they can not long hold from submit or fly be thus hedge in this do his lordship come to Monastreven with purpose to undertake the moor and connors but have in few day settle a correspondency for proceed in that service between our force in those part and the neighbour sept of odempsy and some suspect subject of who faith till then his lordship stand not assure and discern the moor to be weak in leax after the kill of their chiefecashe Oway mac rory and the burn and spoil in the leax journey so as they have not mean to keep their bonnagh and hear that the Oconnors be flee far from that part of Ophaly so as neither of they can be find to make resistance to any reasonable strong force his lordship leave in these part some few company to assist the subject ride from Monastreven the twenty nine of january to abiconal nine mile pass by the ruin city of Kildare now altogether disinhabit the thirty we pass the liffye and come to Milhussy one master hussyes castle eleven mile pass by some pleasant village and by menouth a fair house belong to the earl of Kildare now in the hand of the countess Mabell a old widow the thirty one we come to trim eight mile champion ground this be a pleasant town for seat if the inhabitant be suitable through which the boyne run and it have the ruin of a sumptuous castle this place his lp thought fit for his present residence for if captain tirrel now the chief rebel in Ophalia shall draw his force to the south of the country from hence his lordship may easy fall back on he if the rebel in the west desire to pass into monster as they intend than our force be so dispose as they can not escape without fight with we upon diladuantage to they and if neither fall out than his lordship purpose to plant a garrison at the cavan in the Brenny and to settle our above mention Mac Guire in fermanagh at this time his lp desire to have authority out of England to pass Tirconnel the county of Odonel to Neale garue reserve eight hundred acre about Ballishannan and the fish of the erne to her majesty and such be the opinion of the service his turbulent spirit can do the state as he have the grant of three hundred foot and one hundred horse in her majesty pay on condition he shall bring the man serviceable and maintain they so without further charge to her majesty from trim lie in east meathe his lordship the eleven of February pass by the baron of trimblestone house ride to the lord of deluins house in west meath eleven mile distant the twelve we pass ten mile further to the shire town of west meath compass with bog thence the fourteen we go to Ballymore sir Frances Shanes house ten long mile the sixteen to sir Tibbot dillons house seven mile thence the seventeen to athlone five mile where the governor of the province of Connaght use to lie in a strong castle belong to her majesty which be situate in Connaght be divide from the town by a river and a fair bridge of stone with eight arch lie in west meath and all this country be champion whereof the great part lie waste his lord return back the eighteen of February to sir Tibbot dillons house and the nineteen to danoar twelve mile be Brian Mac gohagans castle in west meath while his lordship lie in this castle he ride forth the twenty of February to view a strong hold seat in a plain and in a little island compass with bog and deep ditch of run water and thick wood in which fastness captain tirrel with some of the bold rebel than lay at the first approach to the bog two shot of the rebel come out our horseman stand on a hill move continual but myself be a raw soldier stand still and because I have a white horse I give the rebel a fair mark so as the first shot fly close by my head and when I apprehend my danger turn my horse the second fly through my cloak and light in my pad saddle which save my life and bruise my thigh present his lordship seat sir Christopher saint Laurence captain Windsor captain roper and captain rotheram with wing of foot into the wood to discover the fortify island and on the other side send captain leg to the same purpose while these skirmish with the rebel lie entrench master darcy ride by the skirt of the wood be shoot in the neck the two and twenty day his lordship draw forth again and we carry hurdle and faggot to pass into the island but the water carry they away and his lordship guard be not well second by the irish we come off with loss and captain rotheram be shoot before I proceed I must digress a little to other matter in this journey begin the twenty two of December his lordship receive commandment to pardon all such in monster as shall require it and shall be commend by the lord precedent with assurance that Spain be so entangle with the war of Savoy as the irish rebel can at this time have small succour thence his lordship write to master secretary to procure he leave to start over into England to kiss the queen hand and to confer with he about the irish service profess that he repute he his honourable friend and do much disdain that humour in any subject if any such be which will think he tie by any respect from have his affection free to love he in the begin of February the lord precedent of monster excuse himself to the lord deputy that he have make stay of some force his lordship have direct to come from thence because he have intelligence that some northern rebel be send to invade monster but his lordship know that he have stop their passage and that they can not go with any great number if perchance they escape do again require that these force may be send unto he at this time there be a plot for tyrones head the manage whereof be commend to sir Richard moryson governor of dundalke whether sir William godolphin be send with his troop of horse to second this plot which take not the wish effect the undertaker Henry Oge oneale fail in his courage or in his faith now I will return to his lordship action while he lie at Maghogans castle the same two and twenty of February his lord receive a packetout of England by which he understand that the earl of Essex be commit to the tower for treason which much dismay he and his near friend and wrought strange alteration in he for whereas before he stand upon term of honour with the secretary now he fall flat to the ground and insinuate himself into in ward love and to a absolute dependency with the secretary so as for a time he estrange himself from two of his near friend for the open declaration they have make of dependency on the earl of Essex yet rather cover then extinguish his good affection to they it be not credible that the influence of the earl malignant star shall work upon so poor a snake as myself be almost a stranger to he yet my nearness incloud to one of his lordship above name friend make it perhaps seem to his lordship improper to use my service in such nearness as his lordship have promise and begin to do so as the next day he take his most secret paper out of my hand yet give they to no other but keep they in his own cabinet and this blow I never full recover while I stay in Ireland in truth his lordship have good cause to be wary in his word and action since by some confession in England himself be taint with privity to the earl practice so that how soever he continue still to importune leave to come over yet no doubt he mean nothing less but rather if he have be send for be purpose with his say friend to sail into France they have prinate fit themselves with money and necessary thereunto for howsoever his lordship be not dangerous engage therein yet he be as he private profess full resolve not to put his neck under the file of the queen attorney tongue but his lordship former service and the necessity of his future employment together with his good success so strengthen he as without great unthankfulness and popular obloquy he can not have be question upon this weak ground the same twenty two of February his lordship in colmsell resolve to proclaim that all such as have any rebel good shall discover they or be guilty of treason that none upon pain of death shall parley with the rebel that the country shall bring in victual to the camp which no man upon pain of death shall take from they without pay the price of the market and thus purpose to force the rebel out of the fortify island and then to plant a garrison at the abbey near adjoin and to charge the new submit subject to loin with this garrison in the service as also to take order for the safe victual of the same when he shall be go his lordship resolve the next day to make another attempt against the island wherein terril lie prepare all thing to second the same and take order to bring victual to the camp from all part and especial from athlone by boat the twenty three of February his lordship draw forth to the abbey where he have lodge four hundred soldier there he dine and proclaim terril head at two thousand crown and after dinner draw to the island he divide the force send part to put boat into the water and so to assail the island and cause the rest to be lead into the wood to fetch out the rebel corn and to burn the house and such thing for their relief as they can not bring away the twenty four of February be Shrove-tuesday there fall a great snow so that we be force to lie still and the next night the rebel do steal away leave the island to his lordship where the next day we find much corn some morion and piece eight cow and some garron the twenty six his lordship draw the force beyond the island into a pleasant valley wherein be a ruin house of sir Edward herbert and the ground be well plough by the rebel our man burn house and corn and his lordship give a angel to a soldier to swim over the water and burn the house in another island then we come to a river which divide west meath and Orphaly into which country his lordship send diverse company under sir Christopher saint Laurence to spoil the same the twenty seven his lordship ride six mile to sir John tirrel a strong castle we pass by the way tirrel pace compass with bog and hilly wood this knight be a subject and here his lordship rest the next day the first of march his lordship ride to Klonegave the house of sir terrence Odempsey in Ophalia be twelve mile in the first part whereof we pass a dangerous part of tirrel fastness here his lordship receive a gracious letter from her majesty whereby she make know unto he the earl of Essex his death and to use she own word profess that in regard of his approve fidelity and love it be some allevation of her grief to eiaculate the same to he first her majesty require he to look well in general upon the disposition of all his captain whereof some prefer by the earl may perhaps have hollow heart towards her service for as she be please to pardon those who by his popular fashion and outward profession of his sincerity have be seduce and blind lead by he so she be careful to sever the chaff from the corn and to deprive the malicious of mean to prejudice her service second whereas the secretary in his lordship name have move her majesty that he may have warrant to come over yet in regrad the spanish ship have not yet pass the narrow sea into Flaunders whether sure they be send and nothing less than for Ireland howsoever the traitor make use of like rumour her majesty wish that he will conceal this his desire for a time with promise to call he home the next winter and use his service near her person the same time his lordship receive letter from the lord in England give allowance in her majesty name for the pass of Tirconnell to Neale Garne upon the above mention condition yet advise that hereafter no country shall so absolute be pass as all the inhabitant shall depend upon one man which will still kindle new flame of rebellion by the same letter his lordship understand that the supply of money victual and munition be ready according to his demand and their lordship advise the plantation of a garrison about Strangford to prevent the assistance which the scot give to the rebel the three of march his lordship ride ten mile to baly Britton sir Henry warrens house in leax which be keep for the queen by a constable and warder in the midway we pass by Phillipstowne otherwise call Dyngen a strong fort in Ophalia otherwise call the king county and that day his lordship send out many party of soldier into the wood against tirrell and the Oconnors scatter lurk in those part here his lordship receive from the lord direction to (@) the silver money and to proclaim a new coin three ounce fine which base money be send over only to impoverish the rebel as be pretend who make war against the queen with her own treasure but in conclusion it be the undo of all the queen servant there for no man care to lay it up and all thing be buy at excessive rate after the exchange in England once fail this exchange be proclaim to be hold at three city in England and four in Ireland but by reason that great sum be coin by rebel and stranger and for other abuse of the same as namely of the merchant who notwithstanding that the money be due change do excessive raise all price this exchange soon fail and our heart therewith for we serve there in discomfort and come home beggar so that only the treasurer and paymaster who be thereby infinite enrich have cause to bless the author of this invention the four of march his lordship ride five mile to sir Edward fitzgeralds house situate in meath in a pleasant and fruitful country the five of march we ride ten mile to mormeere a very pleasant house belong to sir james dillon and thence the next day two mile further to trim sir Richard moryson Governonr of dundalke have late advertise his lordship that Turloghmac Henry tyrones brother captain of the fewes have take his oath to he before a priest and upon a mass book that he will submit himself to her majesty mercy without any condition at or before St Patrick day next follow and further have advertise that the lord of Clancaruin humble desire to be receive to mercy with he for better ratify hereof the say Sr Richard moryson now bring the say Turlogh in person to his lordship lie at trim. the fifteen of march his lordship draw to Arbrachin the bishop of meaths house six mile distant where his lordship have appoint the adjoin garrison to meet he the next day and present after their arrival his lordship take horse towards evening and thence we march all night be very dark and in the morning sudden fall into the ferney the possession whereof ever Mac cooly one of the Mac mabow then usurp and there we burn the house and spoil the good of the inhabitant sir Richard moryson governor of dundalke with that garrison and sir Oliver Lambert with other troop and captain Thomas Williams with the force of Ardee come in diverse way and meet his lordship in that country with small or no resistance make by the rebel to either party the nineteen we march five mile to Ardee the twenty seven mile to mellisant sir Edward Moor house the twenty one two mile to Drogedagh where his lordship stay till the sixteen of April and so return to Dublyn at Drogedagh his lordship alter the list of the foot the horse stand still as before the disposal of the foot into garrison the 23. of march 1600. at the newry under sir Oliner St john 750. at carlingford captain Hansard 100 at mount Norreys under sir Samuel bagnol 450. at dundalke under Sr Richard moryson 400. at Arde a refresh but no stand garrison 350. at Luscanon 400. at Tullogh 350. at Wickloa 250 at the navan 300. at the nasse 100 in Westmeath 450. in Ophalia 200. in Leax 300. at Athy 100 at Monastreven 300. in Connaght sir John Barkely deputy governor 200. the lord of Dunkellin now upon his father death earl of Clanrickard 150. more under four captain 500 foot in Galloway and Odoynes country three captain 400. capt. Tho: roper 150. at Rebon 150. in ocarrol country 100 in in (@) 150. at Dablyn the lord deputy guard 200. at Carickfergus under sir Arthur chichester 550. of new company 1150. be cast and 50. make over to (@) garrison remain 800. of Sr Charles percy company 100 be make (@) to other captain and 50 be add to loughsoyle garrison these company together with the foot in (@) and at loughsoyle do make the new list of foot 13250. her majesty charge in Ireland from the first of April in the begin of the year 1600. to the last of march in the begin of the year 1601. her majesty allowance by establishment and by her letter for increase amount to two hundred seventy six thousand nine hundred and fourteen li nine s. four d. qu. demi hereof save by the lord deputy his providence fifteen thousand two hundred sixty two l. fix s. five d. save also by check impose on the army seventeen thousand twenty nine pound sixteene nine d. so her majesty charge for the army this year beside munition and like extraordinary amount to two hundred thirty four thousand six hundred twenty two li. five s. two d. qu. demi it remain brief to collect out of the lord precedent letter to the lord deputy the service do in monster the year 1600. now end about the sixteen monster of April in the begin of the year 1600. sir George Carew lord precedent of monster depart from kilkenny where he have be some day detain by the earl of ormonds surprisal at a parley with the rebel come to Waterford and Thomas Fitz-Iames bastard son to james fitzgerald late lord of the chief rebel in the county of Waterford fear present prosecution make sure to be receive to her majesty mercy which the lord precedent grant aswell to draw from the titulary earl of Desmond some part of his strength as to open the passage between Waterford and Yoghall by land former shut up so as nothing can pass any way but by sea the twenty three of April at dungaruen his lordship receive advertisement that Florence carty after many favour from the state be whole hispaniolize have great power in Carbry and Desmond and according to his plot with Tyrone at his be there be enter into open action so they term rebellion that captain flower sergeant mayor of monster have hereupon enter Carbry with 1200 foot and 100 horse burn and spoil the same and kill many rebel that Florence have levy of the provincial and bonnagh so they call wage soldier 2000 foot yet never attempt the english till in their return they come within five mile of cork where in a fastness the midway between cork and kinsale they assail the english and be beat by they some 100 of the rebel be slay in which conflict captain Flower have two horse slay under he the twenty four the lord precedent come to cork where he receive the state of the province by the relation of sir Henry poor sole commissioner for monster since the kill of his partner sir Warham St Leger by Mac Guire likewise kill in the fight and understand the rebel to be strong and master of the field supply with all necessary from the town through the persuasion of priest and the covetousness of the townsman about this time fitzgibbon call the white knight either ill use by Tyrone at his be in monster or fear prosecution submit himself to her majesty mercy likewise Florence carty by persuasion of friend and upon safe conduct come to the lord precedent and protest loialty to her majesty but refuse to give his son for pledge leave his wage soldier shall cast he out of his country till his lordship threaten to lay aside all other service sharp to prosecute he whereupon he consent for his pledge but require to have the county of Desmond give to he and his (@) with title of Mac carty more or earl of clanoar with like high demand which be reject he desire leave to sue for these grace in England with promise not to serve against her majesty force in the mean time wherewith the lord precedent be satisfy have no other end for the present then to make he stand neutral while the whole force be employ against the titulary earl of Desmond james Fitzthomas call the suggon earl by nickename now one Dermod Oconnor have no land yet by marriage with the daughter of the old earl of Desmond and his great valour have the lead of 1400. bonnagh and because the lord precedent hope to ragine the rebel one by another at this time by the wife of the say Dermod and other agent his lordship plot with he upon promise of great reward to kill james the titulary earl of Desmond and in like fore one John nugent a rebel upon promise of pardon and reward do within few day undertake to kill John the say earl brother about the begin of May redman burke lead 500 rebel lose 120. of they while he adventure to take a prey in (@) country (@) be nourish by the lord precedent with hope to be baron of Letrim draw his man out of (@) into ormond with purpose to lead they into Connaght and Tyrrell leader of the northern man stay not long behind he pretend discontent against Dermod Oconnor but indeed fear some plot against his head it have be long rumour that the lord precedent will take the field the six of May which make the rebel draw to a head and spend their victual so as after ten day they be force to disperse themselves the twenty of may the lord precedent take the field and march towards Lymbricke settle warder in some castle to secure the passage thither from Kilmallock at Lymricke his lordship understand that John nugent above name be ready as he have undertake to kill John brother to the titulary earl of Desmond be by accident hinder from discharge his pistol and be apprehend be put to death but as well John as the titulary earl his brother be so terrify herewith as they dare never keep together and think themselves least secure in the head of their own man from like practice the lord President march into John burkes country and spoil the fame force he to seek her majesty mercy on his (@) which at last he obtain though with difficulty his lordship have gain here plenty of grain for the army send five hundred foot into omulrians country who spoil the same and kill many rebel then his lordship return to Limricke without any loss and in the begin of june divide the army into garrison not far distant which his lordship do though the time be fit for service that he may attend the plot with Dermod ocannor for kill the titulary earl of Desmond which can not well be do except the rebel be disperse who will keep together as long as the english army be in the field beside his lordship upon their break take advantage to settle a garrison at Asketon without any resistance Dermod ocannor take the titulary earl prisoner in the name of oneale pretend by a forge letter that he have plot his death with the lord precedent and present send his wife for the money promise in reward wish the lord precedent to draw his force to Kilmalloch where he will deliver he the prisoner which his lordship do according the sixteen of june but the rebel have notice hereof draw together four thousand in number stop the passage set the titulary earl at liberty and besiege Dermod ocannor in a castle till the lord precedent march thither the 29 of june force force they to leave the siege his lordship keep the field take the chief castle of the knight of the valley wherein be slay threescore warder take other castle and do many good service the rebel in great number lie near he but never (@) to fight by reason of the jealousy between they whereupon 2500. Connaght man be suitor to his lordship to return home without impediment from his (@) at this time Ocannor kerry yield his castle to the queen and be receive to mercy and the lord precedent at last grant a pass to the rebel of Connaght but the lord burke not know thereof for a private revenge set upon they as they march home (@) (@) three score of they beside many drown the sixteen of july the lord precedent bestow the army in garrison the 23 of july his lordship (@) the (@) again to relieve the man he have former send into kerry and march thither take (@) the chief house of the lord Fitz Morrice and many other castle for grief whereof the say lord die yet leave a son then as dangerous as himself the lord precedent return to cork about the eighteen of August leave sir Charles Wilmot governor of kerry a valiant gentleman a chief commander under he and in the first rank of those instrument he use in all service who in short time bring most of the freeholder of kerry to due subjection and drive the titulary Desmond out of those part all the garrison in time of harvest gather as much come as they can and destroy the rest which make the rebel not able to subsist the year follow sir George thornton hear that the titulary earl of Desmond pass near Kilmalloch send the garrison out and captain greame charge they with his troop of horse kill 120. of they in which conflict the english get 300. garon lade with baggage 150 pike and piece with other weapon and 40. horse but the english have 16. horse kill in the fight the titulary earl of Desmond can never after draw 100 man together and be force to fly into tipperary with his brother John Pierce Lacie a archrebel and the knight of the glin whence his brother John haste into Ulster for relief from tirone and in the end of this summer upon the departure of the bonnagh of Connaght and Ulster and the good success of the english many of the provincial submit themselves yet send to Rome for dispensation of their so do about the midst of October james fitzgerald who have long be imprison in the tower of London be the next and true heir to the last earl of Desmond and release by the queen with title of earl by letter patent send to the lord President and promise of a good proportion of land to support his dignity at the end of the war according to his desert in her majesty service and in the mean time to live upon pay in the army land at yoghal and the eighteen day come to the lord precedent at Mallogh and be industrious in the queen service Desmod o connor be in Connaght and hear of the young earl of Desmondi arrival upon promise of great service have the lord president protection to come unto he but be set upon by tybot we long his man defeat he take and hang whereupon Tibet have then a company in her majesty pay be casher Florence mac carty have all this while practise underhand many thing against the state and put still off his appearance by delatory excuse at last in October by the desperateness of his estate be force to submit and obtain pardon upon pledge of his loyalty the (@) earl of Desmond steal back into monster live as a wood-kerne never have more than two or three in his company in November sir Charles Wilmot take the last and only castle the lord Mac Morice have in kerry and his elder son therein betray by a priest for safety of his life and great provision lay up in that castle in these two last month sir Richard Percy lie in garrison at kinsale twice pass into the country and take prey of five hundred cow kill many rebel in December the lord precedent have notice where the titulary earl lurk and sentman to surprise he but he escape in such haste as he leave his shoe behind he and now there be not a castle in monster hold for the rebel nor any company of ten rebel together though there want not loose (@) bond disperse in all corner so as his lordship have leisure to look into the corporate town be aider abetter and procurer under hand of this rebellion all the queen treasure be spend in they by the soldier and they underhand supply the rebel with all necessary though at excessive rate the rebel flee outof monster into tiperarie and ormond have hitherto live there among the be subject without any disturbance the rather for the earl of ormonds mount for the death of his most worthy and virtuous lady but in january his lordship send some force against they who kill many and force the rest to fly where of some be drown pass the water then very high and some chief rebel be take and hang at kilkenny about the end of january the lord precedent send (@) foot of the monster list to be dispose by the lord deputy as he have direction to do his lordship to settle the country the better refuse to renew any protection so as all be force to sue their pardon and in two month a space before the end of February upon his lordship recommendation morethen fourethousand monster man have their pardon grant by the lord deputy and pass under the great seal the second book chap. i. of the lord deputy particular proceed in the prosecution of the rebel and of the speniard invade Ireland in the year 1601. while the lord deputy lie at drogheda namely from the the one and twenty of march till the sixteen of April upon which day he return to Dublin his lordship assemble the counsellor of state to attend he there and upon the eight and twenty of march 1601 the lord deputy and counsel write from drogheda vulgar call Tredagh their joint letter to the lord in England whereby they advertise that the lord deputy have spend the great part of winter in the irish country of lemster have by burn their corn consume their cattle and kill many of they so scatter their main strength as certain of the chief have since submit to the queen mercy and the rest be sever into small company and unlike to draw to any dangerous head yea tirrel in opinion the great among they take for tyrones lieutenant in Lemster be force out of his great fastness now with a few base kern follow he be drive to wanderin wood and boggs seek to escape into the north as short after he do notwithstanding that certain english company be leave to hunt he in his walk and to stop his passage that his lordship desirous to be at hand to watch all opportunity of service upon the northern border have pierce into the Fearny and that sir Richard moryson governor of Dundalk with his garrison have former waste and now pass through the fewes and meet his lordship there so as both these country be spoil ever Mac cooly chief of the Fearny and Turlogh Mac Henry captain of the fewes have both be humble suitor for her majesty mercy and be command to appear short and make their humble submission which course likewise the sept of the Brenny be (@) to take for many of they chastise by the army and utter discourage have already diverse time offer most humble submission that his lordship hereupon have call the counsellor to Tredagh there to consider of the circumstance and condition to be observe in take these (@) as also to deliberate how the army may be employ most to vex Tyrone till the summer come on at which time his lordship purpose to dwell upon he and put he to trial of his uttermost fortune that it be resolve in counsel to accept the submission of the chief of Fearny and the captain of the fewes above name as likewise of the sept of the brennye these three country be a hedge between the english pale and the north and yield many commodity to pass into tirone with her majesty force that it be resolve to send Mac guyer into fermanagh with 200 man to help he for a time against the rebel Mac guyer who he and his son have already much impoverish for he be think a fit instrument in case he prevail aswell to entangle Tyrone and infest Ororke as to help the plantation at Ballishannon intend to be put in execution about june follow when forage can be have for horse they further solicit by these letter for supply of victual munition and money and that the victual and munition shall be address some part to Dublin and Tredagh but the great part to Galloway be intend for the force to be plant at Ballishannon and those to invade Tyrone that way and the rest to carlingford intend for the force to invade Tyrone by the way of the newry which invasion be purpose about the midst of june when forage may be have for the horse and this they pray because the unship and reship of the victual at Dublin cause great expense of money and waste of the victual Tirlogh Mac Henry captain of the fewes and ever Mac cooly of the family of the mac mahowne chief of the Fearny do about this time declare themselves to be subject and humble make their submission on their knee sign certain article of subjection under their hand and put in pledge for performance thereof and the say ever in particular confess in the article under his hand that he be not lord but farmer of the fearnye bind himself to pay her majesty his old rent the one and thirty of march 1601 her majesty sign the follow establishment an. 1601. officer general the lord deputy for his diet one hundred li. a band of horse three li. four s. fifty foot each at eight d. for allowance in lieu of cease ten li. beside his company of horse and foot in the army in all twelve li. six s. six d. qu. four thousand four hundred forty four li. seventeen s. one d. qu. the lieutenant of the army three li. one thousand fourscore fifteen pound the treasurer at war thirty five s. six hundred thirty eight li. fifteen shilling the marshal beside his thirty horse at twelve d. without checque in the army five s. nine d. one hundred four li. eighteen s. nine d. the sergeant Maior of the army twenty s. three hundred sixty five li. the master of the ordinance twenty six s. eleven d. four hundred ninety one li. four s. seven d minister of the ordinance twenty five s. two d. four hundred fifty nine li. five s. ten d. muster-master general eleven s. six d. two hundred nine li seventeen s. six d. comptroller of the victual ten s. one hundred eighty two li. ten s. five commissary of victual one at eight s. and four each at six s. thirty two s. five hundred eighty four li fourteen colonel each at ten s. seven li. two thousand five hundred fifty five li. scout-master beside six horse each at twelve d. part of the army six s. eight d. one hundred twenty one li. thirteen eight d. his diet and the counsel at ten li. the week his retinue of thirty horse and twenty foot at thirty s. seven d. three li. six s. fix d. one thousand two hundred thirteen li. thirteen s. four d. qu. provost Martial in monster fourteen s. two hundred fifty five li. ten s the commander of the force in Counaght at ten s with a increase of one hundred li. fifteen s. five d. qu. two hundred eighty two li. ten s. provosh marshal in Connaght beside twelve horseman of the army five s. seven d. one hundred two li. fourteen s. one d. commander of the force at Loughfoyle beside his pay of ten s. as colonel have three s. four d. sixty li sixteen s. eight d. provost Martial there four s seventy three li. provost Martial of Ballishannon four s. seventy three li. lieutenant of the queen county six s. eight d. one hundred twenty one li. thirteen s. four d. provost Martial in lemster for himself and six horseman five s. seven d. one hundred two li. fourteen s. one d. warder in one thousand three hundred ten li. nineteen s. two peace warder in eight hundred twenty one li. five s. warder in five hundred forty two li. eighteen s. nine d. warder in two hundred li. twelve hundred horseman distribute into four and twenty band the captain four s. the lieutenant two s. six penny the cornet two s. and three hundred horseman each at eighteened on condition they be english both horse and man or else to have but twelve d. and 200 horseman at fifteen d. and seven hundred horseman at twelve d. twenty nine thousand two hundred seventy three li. fourteen thousand footman distribute into one hundred forty band the captain four s. lieutenant two s. ensign eighteen d. the day two sergeant a drum and a surgeon each at twelve d. a piece and each soldier at eight d. one hundred ninety nine thousand seven hundred fifteen li. sixteen s. eight d. pensioner in the muster-book one thousand eight hundred nine li. fifteen s. ten d. pensioner by letter patent eight hundred seventy four li. five s. nine penny thirteen almsman eighty eight li. nineteen s. four d. officer of the muster which be payable out of the check namely one muster-master at six s. eight d. a controller at ten s. and twenty commissary each at three s. four d. one thousand five hundred twenty li. sixteen s. eight d. extraordinary allowance for messenger spial post-barke reward of service etc. six thousand li. Totall of this establishment two hundred fifty five thousand seven hundred seventy three li. fourteen d. qu. denny memorandum that the dead pay allow to the captain in each company of horse or foot be herein contain but the charge of munition of levy horse and foot for reinforcing the army with many like charge be not herein contain the six of April 1601 his lordship receive advertisement from captain Io badley at the nowry that he and captain Edward blony governor of the fort of mount-norreys purpose to surprise loghrorcan can not carry a boat which they have provide to that purpose but he carry certain firework provide in case the boat shall fail go to the fort and join with captain blany march towards that island where they arrive by eight of the clock in the morning and lean their force behind a wood they both go together to discover the island which do captain bodley make ready thirty arrow with wildfier and so they both fall down with one hundred shot close to the water where the shot play incessant upon the island while the other deliver their arrow sudden the house fire and burn so vehement as the rebel lodging there forsake the island and swim to the further shore that after they (@) burn to the ground they fire a great house upon their side of the shore and kill there six kerne gain their arm beside churl and calliach and after the burn of other house also they bring away some cow and sheep with other pillage and they understand by a prisoner that there be about thirty person in the island whereof only eight swim away of which four be shoot in the water so as the rest either be kill or lie hurt in the island likewise they understand by the say prisoner that great store of butter corn meal and powder be burn and spoil in the island which all the rebel of that country make their magasine further that some forty kern skirmish with they at place of advantage in their retreat for two mile march but howsoever the common opinion be that the rebel sustain great loss by this service yet of the english only two be slay and seven hurt the seven of April sir Henry Dockwra governor of Loughfoyle write to his lordship that he have take the submission of Hugh boy boy of who service to her majesty he be confident to make manifold good use as well for the present settle sir John odogherties country after his late death as for reveal the rebel secret counsel well know to he among which he confident anow that the king of Spain have promise to invade Ireland this year with six thousand man and to land at some town in Munster swear that three of the chief city have promise to receive they add that Florence Mac carty have write to Odonnel that he have submit to the queen only upon necessity and that upon the spaniard come he will join with they this governor further advertise that Phelim Oge chief of a contrary faction in odogherties country desire to make his humble submission to the queen mercy upon these condition to leave of the name of Odogherty and obey any man to who her majesty shall give that country to pay all debt his man do owe to any subject to discharge his soldier to return to the owner twelve hundred beef he have cut for Odonnell to make satisfaction for a bark come to the liff which his people have take and spoil and to yield up to he the gonernor all the cattle shall be find in his country belong to Odonnell add that sir John Bolles in a journey make upon Ocane have kill fifty of his people have burn many house and much corn and that the garrison of the liff have spoil Tirconnel have slay many and have bring away two hundred cow and great booty the ten of April sir Oghy Ohanlon a northern lord submit himself on his knee to her majesty mercy at Tredagh and sign certain article for the performance whereof he take his oath and because these article except there fall out some special reason to leave out some of they and to add other be the same to which all submitty at this time be tie I will once for all add the brief of they after his acknowledgement that queen Elizabeth by the grace of God queen of England France and Ireland etc. be the true absolute and sovereign lady of this realm of Ireland and of every part and of all the people thereof with humble confession of his former disloyalty and of his penitency and like profession that he have feel the weight of her majesty power this do further to the example of all other offender he testify that he make this his humble submission and protestation of his penitency his future loyalty and endeavour to redeem his fault by his good service then he acknowledge under his hand that now before the lord deputy and counsel he take a corporal and religious oath for all and several article follow namely that he will ever continue a loyal subject that for performance thereof and of all the follow article he will put in sufficient pledge that he do renounce all manner of obedience to any foreign power or potentate depend only on the queen his sovereign that he renounce all rebel and will not aid they but serve against they when he be command that he will to the utter most of his power withstand and confound any disloyal subject or foreign enemy attempt against the sacred person or estate of her majesty or the quietness of her faithful subject more especial against the archtraitor Tyrone and the king of Spain support he that he will come to the state whensoever he be command neither will upon wrong seek to right himself but will seek redress by course of Law that he will reveal all conspiracy of treason which he shall hear that he will sue out her majesty pardon within certain day for he and his follower and answer for their good behaviour that he will book these follower within certain day that he will suffer all subject safe to trade in his country that he will extort no black rent or make other exaction on his people but by due course of a subject for sincere performance hereof he testify that he have take his corporal oath upon his knee before the lord deputy and council the same oath be solemn minister to he and take by he in the say assembly and do again vow the same upon his salvation religious profess that if he shall break those article or any of they he will acknowledge himself not only to be worthy of all infamy and extreme punishment but ever after to be most unworthy to bear the name of a christian or to enjoy the society of man to which as he have unfeigned swear so now in witness thereof he do in this write form of submission set to his hand with addition of the day of the month and of the year when this act be do by he the sixteen of April the governor of Loughfoyle by his letter entreat that a pardon may be parse to Hugh boy which business the bearer have undertake to solicit and that Neale garue to who the queen have grant odonnel country may be send back from Dublin because the irish be confident that upon his arrival all the people of Tirconnell will flock unto he Further advertise that the garrison of the liff have burn the newtowne and kill twelve kern and thirty eight of other people and have bring back some three hundred cow and that the garrison of Donnegall have burn in Ocanes country a great village and many woman child and cow with the house and have kill kern and churl the three and twenty of April his lordship keep s. George feast at Dublin with solemn pomp the captain bring up his meat and some of the colonel attend on his person at table to which feast the rebel be invite who his lordship late receive to mercy under her majesty protection till their pardon may be sign namely Turlogh Mac Henry captain of the fewes ever Mac cooly chief of the Fearney Obanlon a lord of Ulster Phelim Mac feagh chief of the Obyrnes and donnel Spaniagh chief of the cavanaghs in Lemster these be entertain with plenty of wine and all kindness his lordship assure they that as he have be a scourge to they in rebellion so he will now be a mediator for they to her majesty in their state of subject they stand firm and constant to their obedience and no doubt as there be a secret mystery of state in these solemn pomp and as his lordship therein for his person and carriage be most comely and if I may use the word majestical so the magnificence of this feast wrought in the heart of those rebel and by their relation in the heart of other after submit both have first experience the sharpness of the queen sword such a awful respect to her majesty and such fear temper with love to his lordship as much avail to contain they in due obedience from the end of march to the begin of May upon the lord president intercession by letter to the lord deputy many pardon be grant for life land and good to chieftain of country and gentleman in monster namely to Mac carty Reough chieftain of Carbery and two hundred and ten follower as well man as woman and child to Oswyllivan bear and some five hundred twenty eight follower as also to Oswyllivan brantry to John odoyre of Tiperary and some one hundred fifty follower to Fitz james Gerrald with some three hundred seventy follower and to teig Mac Mereretagh Obrian in the county of Lymrick with some two hundred twenty one follower and some other which for brenity I omit and it be conclude at the council table on the last of April that the two follow proviso shall be insert in all pardon and charge be according give to the queen learned counsel and to the officer and to his lordship secretary who hand all pardon pass that the say proviso shall be continual insert namely first in regard some notorious rebel of the pale may pass as follower to remote lord that the pardon be not available to any but to the natural inhabitant tenant and know follower of the lord so pardon second in regard many rebel take and to be judge according to the law may by oucrsight be pardon proviso be to be enter that no pardon shall avail any who be already in prison or upon bail the second of May his lordship write to the lord in England that monster be not only well reduce but begin to taste the sweetness of peace that the like may be say of Lemster except the moor and conners who be scatter and have seek but can not obtain of he the queen mercy that the northern border of Ulster be assure namely ohanlon country the fewes Clancaruill the ferney most of the galloglass and many of the Mac mahowne and that a garrison be plant in the Brenny and the queen Mac gwyer settle in fermanagh that sir Henry Dockwra at Loughfoyle and sir Arthur chichester at Carickefergus common call Knockefergus have make their neighbour sure to the state and both have do her majesty excellent service that only Connaght most easy to be reduce be most out of order that for this reason he think fit to plant Ballishannon garrison through Connaght which may be reduce with the very pass of the army and therefore have persuade the magazine of victual at Galloway special since from those part his lordship may easy join with the lord precedent in case Spain shall invade monster that in the mean time his lordship will draw one thousand foot out of monster to serve in Ulster and for a time borrow thence five hundred foot and fifty horse for Connaght journey the force remain be sufficient to guard monster and great than he have leave in lemster in the peace whereof he may seem to have more proper interest but if Spain shall invade monster than all the army be to be draw thither and great supply send out of England since the defection of the irish be like to be great even of those who yet have never declare any malice against the state yet that his lordship desire present no supply in regard of her majesty excessive charge in levy and transport they trust that by the rebel force diminish occasion will be give to cast some of the army with which cast company the defect of the stand may be supply wherein his lordship promise to proceed without prefer such as quen with their blood shed in his fight deserve advancement or satisfy some worthy commander who entertainment he have rather lessen or pleasure those who may just challenge preferment from he therefore pray that her majesty will not command he to bestow new company as of late she have do upon such as of late have be absent and have only serve at the lose of the kingdom so as they be least fit to be prefer before those who have hazard their life in regain it add that he write not this to uphold any private dependency on himself esteem it a great vanity so to do but only to strengthen himself so long and no long than he shall be employ in her majesty service that in stead of new supply he desire leave to entertain some of the irish submitty in pay by they to consume the rebel and by the rebel to diminish their number since two thing remain to settle the kingdom first the rid Ireland of the swordman to which end the irish affect some journey into the low country or the indies which can not make they any whit more able soldier then now they be not add to their knowledge of war fit for Ireland which they now have but three part of four be like never to return if they be engage in such a voyage second the make of the english owner fit to inhabit their land which be most difficult in regard of their poverty and of the great quantity of land they possess since in particular of some gentleman of leax and Ophalia each possess as much land as be well inhabit will maintain more man than all the rebel of those county be in number about the ten of may his lordship give warrant to pass the pardon of Phelim mac Feogh Obirn of the glinne with his follower and likewise of Phelim mac Feogh o tool of the Fartrey with fifty six follower and upon the humble submission of Rossemac mahowne his lordship grant he her majesty protection till he may sue out his pardon about this time his lordship have advertisement from sir Henry Deckwra governor at Loughfoyle that he have take in odogherty country and secure the passage into it as well against Odonnell as the false inhabitant that he have gather the force to spoil Hughmac Hugh duffe country the project be frustrate by and Irishman steal from the army and give they intelligence hereof so as they drive the prey far oft into remote part that Neale garue with cormocke o Neale dispatch late from Dublin be arrive at loughsoyle that he the say governor defer the prosecution of Ocane because he have no haven in his country for the land of spaniard nor can escape from the english force though spaniard shall land the same time resolve to enter Hugh Hugh duffe country as more fit to receive foreign force and to supply they with victual or other necessary and to this end that he have assemble the force to the liff that shane Oge Odonnell command certain land in the Sen do there offer to submit but upon such condition as be unfit yet the irish extol his valour and entreat for he and neaie garue for the time be content to spare he of the man allow he fifty foot and twenty five horse that he the governor have further promise he upon acceptable service to procure he as many more man in her majesty pay whereupon he have take his oath of obedience and have secret send word to his people to spoil rory odonnel country who then have he in no suspicion this do that he the governor sudden enter Hugh duffe country aforesaid and spoil the same take a prey of more than one thousand cow with great number of garron sheep and goat that thence he march into Fanaght where (@) Oge mac Swinedoe lord of the do meet he on the border and deliver the chief pledge of his country for he and their loyalty take his oath of obedience to her majesty that hear of odonnel draw into those part he thence retire with great part of the force leave Neale garue with his irish and some english company for his assistance to spoil and absolute waste Fannaght to who Mac swine Fannaght lord of the country present deliver pledge of his loyalty take his oath of obedience to her majesty at which time likewise Mac swine bane and o boil earnest solicit the governor to be receive to mercy that Neale garue by the keep of Tirconnell grant he at Dublyn for the time till her majesty may please to pass the same to he by letter patent and by great gift he have there receive be puff up with pride desire present possession of the country and call the people his subject and say to the governors face that he will punish exact cut and hang they as he list but that he have calm he with severe speech and with charge not to meddle with any man or any part of the country upon his allegiance since he have no right but from her majesty bounty not yet full express and that not sovereign but limit so as might best stand with the people good who be not he but her majesty subject that he find he to be in his nature proud valiant miserable tyrannous unmeasurable covetous without any knowledge of God or almost any civility good to be use while he be satisfy which he can hardly be be like a quince require great cost ereit be good to eat or whilst he be keep under which be the fit course to be hold with he yet that he think he sure to the state in regard of the pledge he have give but much more because he can no way better his estate by leave the queen service nor be secure of any word from Odonnell who brother he have kill that cormacke o Neale be of late come from Dublyn can hitherto have do no service but that he be of reasonable esteem among the people of his country and be of a mild honest disposition willing to serve without grate beggary or unreasonable demand yet be irish and little less barbarous than the better sort of wood kern that come out of the wood without friend or kinsman he can then give no pledge but his wife and child be since come to he and within the governors power beside that he seem not to be incline to treachery neither can mend his estate by leave the queen service to which he come in voluntary without call force or composition and therein remain with his desire limit and to be content with reason that Hugh boy be subtle wise civil a Papist and alien but not deep malicious against odonnel person yet firm in his allegiance have come in with his country and deliver his chief pledge offer any other to be deliver upon command and have show the passage into his country and himself solicit and further the fortify thereof daily give sure and important intelligence to the great furtherance of the service beside that all his wealth lie within the power of the queen force last that between these submitty be faction and heartburning which discreet measure can not but advantage the service the fifteen of may the lord deputy receive by the hand of sir George Cary treasurer at war a ploclamation sign by the queen to be publish for make the new standard of mix money to be only currant in this kingdom all other coin be to be bring in to the treasurer and likewise a letter from the queen require the lord deputy and counsel to further the due execution of the content of this proclamation and by some plausible grace do in general to the subject in the establish a exchange of this coin into sterling money of England and take away the imposition on sea coal transport into Ireland and in particular to the captain of the army in allow their dead pay in money after the rate of eight penny and some like favour invite all to swallow this bitter pill which impoverish not only the rebel but her majesty best servant in this kingdom only enrich her paymaster sit quiet at home while other adventure daily their blood in the service the twenty of may the lord deputy and counsel advertise the lord in England that they have give order to print 300. of the proclamation for the new coin to be publish through all part of Ireland at one time that they have in counsel agree upon a general host for this year to begin the last of june follow and in the mean time while that be prepare that the lord deputy will draw the force to dundalke upon the northern border there to watch opportunity of service and special by his presence to animate the new submitty to attempt some thing against the archtraitor Tyrone and to put they in blood against he and his confederate and that his lordship towards the time of the say hoa purpose to return to Dublyn and to the end he may find there all thing in readiness for his intend prosecution of Tyrone in his own country they beseech their lordship that victual and munition may with all possible speed be send thither out of England the foresay general hoa be a rise out of certain foot and horse find by the subject of the five english shire and the irish submitty to assist the queen force and these together with some of the english company his lordship use to lay in the pale for the defence thereof at such time as the force be to be draw into Ulster three of the county of Westmeath 60. 2 four of the county of Kildare five of the county of lowth 207 374. Totall both 581. the rise out of the irish lord and their captain the Obyrnes over who after the death of sir Henry Harrington his son sir William Harrington be captain by the late queen letter patent grant to his father and he horseman 12. kern 24. the cavanaghs have then no captain over they horse 12. kern 30. other particular sept beside those which be in rebellion horse 104. kern 307. Totall horse 128. kern 361. the project of dispose the queen force for the follow Summers service out of monster we think fit to be spare and to be draw into Connaght 1000 foot and 50 hose since there shall still remain in monster 1600 foot and 200 horse for any occasion of service foot 1000 horse 50. in Connaght be already beside tybot ne long company foot 1150 horse 74. these to be place as follow to keep at Galloway and athlone in Connaght foot 350. to leave at the abbey of boil in Connaght under the command of the late lord of Dunkellen now earl of Clanrickard foot 1000 horse 62. these to further the plantation of Balishannon to leave at the annaly in Lemster side of the shannon under the command of sir John Barkeley foot 800. horse 12. these fit to join with the undermentioned force of Westmeath Kels and the rest upon the northern border to stop the Ulster rebel from come into lemster or if they shall pass they then to join with the force of Ophaly and the rest southward Tybot long the payment of who company have long be stop be to be keep in good term Oconnor sligo to be threaten that if he do not submit and declare himself against Odonnell before the plant of Ballishannon he shall have no hope of mercy the force at the abbey of boil be to infest Oconnor sligo and to keep Ororke from join with Odonnell those at the annaly to infest Ororke beside the above mention lie between any force that may come out of the north into Lemster and to follow they if they shall escape it be likely that about harvest time Tyrrell and the Oconnors will gather strength if they possible can to return and gather the corn they sow last year in Leax and Ophaly and thus be dispose the above say foot 2150. horse 124. the force towards the south of (@) to lie thus in Ophaly the earl of Kildare (@) George Bourcher 100 sir Edward (@) (@) capt. Totall in list 3000. for the field 1675. thus at Lougsfoyle with these english foot and one hundred english horse together with five hundred irish foot and one hundred irish horse and the help of the submitty especial of Neale Garne and of cormocke oneale it be think that sir Henry Dockewra may plant a intend garrison at Ballishannon as by his own offer he have project in England and beside keep his own may also draw out sufficient force to meet the lord deputy in the heart of Tyrone if the project of plant Ballishannon can take such effect as be hope to the furtherance whereof I do at this time upon his lordship command devise a cipher to pass between his lordship and the governor of Loughfoyle Knockefergus and lecaile to the end that if the rebel shall light upon any their letter contrive this meeting or other service yet they may not be able to discover any their secret purpose especial since they be so ignorant as they can not attain the decipher of those character or any like though far more easy and this cipher be present send to the above name governor his lordship further resolve in council to write to the lord in England to have six thousand of the train band in readiness to be send over present upon the suspect invasion of foreign power and to have a magazine of victual and munition at Limricke aswell to answer the service in monster if they shall make descent in those part be most likely as to be draw thence to Galloway in case no such invasion be make there to answer the prosecution of the Connaght rebel intend the winter follow all thing thus project for the follow prosecution of this war his lordship on the two and twenty of May begin his intend journey above mention from Dublin and the twenty three come to Tredagh and the twenty five to dundalke where his lordship lay till the disperse company can be draw nethither and victual bring here he compose all controversy between the late submitty and settle a correspondency between they aswell to make they concur in the defence one of another as also in the defence of the pale here his lordship receive the twenty eight of May letter from the lord in England require that no captain shall supply his company with passe-volants at pleasure but only with such man as shall be send out of England for supply that the captain refuse to show their company when they be require by the commissary of the muster shall be check two month pay that such pensioner shall be cheke as without special licence shall be absent from any service and that special care shall be have to punish and prevent such soldier as dismiss by their captain pass or run away from their colour do due return into England the nine and twenty of May upon the intercession of the lord precedent by his letter according to the course hold by direction out of England the lord deputy grant his warrant for draw of her majesty pardon to Cuocher Omulrian a Munster rebel chief of his sept or name and eighty three follower aswell man as woman and child of that sept. the second of june it be resolve in council that letter shall be write to the lord precedent of monster require he to draw the force under he towards Lymricke and in those part to employ they most part of the follow summer as well ready to attend the descent of any foreign enemy as fit lay to give countenance to the prosecution of the rebel in Connaght whether the say lord President be to be further direct to send a thousand foot and fifty horse according to the above mention project to the end that the rebel be prosecute in that province may have no leisure to join with those of the north for disturb the plant of a garrison at Ballishannon which sir Henry Dockwra be to plant from the way of Loughfoyle the five of june the lord precedent advertise that warning have be give to those of monster for the send of their man to the general hoa above mention which the lord deputy have appoint to meet according to the old custom at the hill of Tarragh but that he fear the scarcity of victual and want of furniture will either hinder their full appearance or make they of small use to the service the six day upon the lord precedent letter warrant be give for a charter of pardon without fine to be grant to one hundred fifty one inhabitant about moghe in the county of cork as well man as woman and child for life land and good and the like be grant to Oswillivan more of that province with 481 follower the eight of june be Monday the lord deputy draw the force out of dundalke and march two mile to the hill of Fagher near the pace of the Moyry where he encamp and while he lay there his lordship cause a fort to be build in the say pace at the three mile water not rise from thence till he have make this fort defensible so as leave some warder in it the workman may in his absence finish the build the thirteen of june in the camp at the Fagher his lordship publish the proclamation of the new coin all other money have be decry three day before and by his lordship direction like proclamation print at Dublyn and thence former send to Loughfoyle and Knockfergus and into the province of Connaght and monster be at the same time publish together in all place the fourteen in the same camp his lordship and the counsellor there write the follow letter to the lord in England it may please your most honourable lordship perceive by your lordship letter of the eighteen of May that the victual expect to answer our purpose of plant Ballishannon by Connaght can not arrive in such quantity nor time as may enable we to proceed in that journey and receive some argument of your lordship inclination to sir h. Dockwra his offer to plant that garrison from Loughfoyle we grow into a new consultation in what sort to make the war this summer first it be propound with the army to march by lecaile and those part into Colrane the end whereof shall have be to have bring in subjection all the woodman and utter take from Tyrone all that part of Ulster between Colrane and Loughsidney to the blackewater from whence heretofore the traitor have gather his great strength the passage be not very dangerous and we have the commodity of the sea to supply we we shall have make the war that way to great purpose and with good conveniency and perhaps may have fall over the Banne into Tyrone all other way be of extreme danger to enter into that country except that one by Loughfoyle the chief difficulty that do arise against this project be the danger wherein we shall leave all thing behind we if the Spaniard shall land when we have carry the chief force of the kingdom into the uttermost corner thereof and the next be that we be not able to leave any great guard for the pale shall have leave it naked to any attempt of Tyrone and the new reclaim rebel to the mercy of he as the pale to the mercy of both but in the end we grow to this resolution first in the interym between this and the appointment of the general host by the which we shall be supply with carriage and about which time we expect victual and munition out of England of the first whereof we be more spare provide then may warrant the engage ourselves into any great business and of the second so utter unfurnished as we scarce have powder to maintain a good day fight nor tool nor other provision to fortify which must be our chief work as we carry the rebel before we to dwell by they we determine to assure the passage of the Moyry then to plant a garrison at lecaile and to convey some more man to sir Arthur chichester governor of Carickefergus who with that garrison and those supply together with the advantage that our stir in all other place will give he may go near to work little less effect than we with the whole army shall have do and last we purpose to lie with the force as near Tyrone as we can after when victual and munition shall be arrive which we hope to receive by the last of june be the time appoint for the general host we purpose God will as near as we can to employ her majesty force according to the enclose project this project I have former set down with the particularity of sir h. Dockewra his purpose to plant Ballishannon send by captain Vaughan to your lordship we be not acquaint only master treasurer have tell we of such a proposition in general but we doubt not that withal he have propound to your lordship for such mean to accomplish his work as must be supply from thence for from we he can receive little other assistance than our employ the whole force according to the enclose project which in every part be do as much as may be for his advantage neither which be worse can we easy have any intelligence from he or often hear one from another but if we perceive that he shall find any impossibility to plant Ballishannon we think to advise he with the whole gross of his strength to fall into Tyrone about such time as we shall be at blackewater whereby it may fall out that we shall with the help of God meet at Dungannon and utter waste all the country of Tyrone unto the which course if we be drive we must resolve to make the war this follow winter in Connaght first leave the northern border in good strength which we hope will reduce that province and ruin o Donnel for if we keep he out of Connaght he can long subsist and so we hope for the continual assurance of that province to plant the next year at Ballishannon with facility but if the plant of so many garrison do seem by continue the greathesse of the army to draw on too long her majesty charge we do first think that to recover this kingdom and to preserve it from be hereafter chargeable it will be necessary that Ballishannon Loughfoyle some garrison on the Ban Lecayle mount Norreys armagh blackwater and some other place be continual keep all which place may be ever victual by sea or they be near together without any dangerous passage between they by land may be victual by sea and land without any further force than their own and if there be in every fort some little keep or tower of stone build then as the war decrease or occasion shall serve the place may be guard with a few man and so continue bridle in peace and fit place to put in more man to great purpose when the rebellion shall at any time break out neither need these little castle be work of any great charge for they may be easy make such as this people will hardly force they to proceed in our project of this summer service the victual already contract for must-arrive in due time and your lordship supply we with good quantity hereafter for our only way to ruin the rebel must be to make all possible waist of the mean for life which do if we be not supply out of England we shall aswell starve ourselves as they but especial where we must make the war which be far from the relief of any friend and where nothing be to be get from the enemy except it be by great chance since what be in their country they will light either hide or spoil or convey to inaccessible fastness and because the great service here be to be do by long and sudden journey which can be do without victual and no victual but cheese well carry by the soldier without garon or carriage jade we must humble desire your lordship to send we some great quantity of cheese in the provision whereof whatsoever inconvenience your lordship shall find we assure you they will be ten time countervail in the service last because the army be already weak of english and this journey without the extraordinary favour of God must needs diminish they much as well by the sword as sickness we most humble and earnest desire your l ps. assoon as convenient may be to send we 1000 shot to carlingford for supply that at our return we may both strengthen those english company which we mean to leave behind we in the north and such as we carry with we for the winter service the time will be exceed fit for their arrival for beside the succour we may receive from they if we grow very weak at our return they will come over well clothe against the winter and may have time to rest and to be season till Christmas till when in these war it be the most unactive part of the year and then may be employ till the end of May which be the only season to plague these rebel and when the summer be pass wherein those rogue revive and live like fly than our garrison be well plant and the army strengthen with english we may begin to cast the irish company and to clear the english company of they for they must continue good subject or starve if they go out and have the queen sword hang over they wheresoever they go in the mean time we think they necessary entertain for we take so many man from the rebel and by they give unto ourselves facility to plant the foundation of their own ruin and both with we and against we to waste they by themselves for if we shall not entertain they they would-lie upon some countric of the subject and except it be defend by as many as themselves they will waste and live upon it so that in effect the very number entertain will grow all to one reckon and for a more particular instance of the benefit that ensue the entertain these irish we think we can give your lordship a account of above one hundred that have this year be kill with the bullet fight on our side who be former rebel for of such we speak and questionless will have be so again if they have live and shall have be put out of the queen pay we humble desire your lordship to make a favourable construction both of our counsel and the success since those ground whereupon we do now just build our resolution may by their alteration give we just cause to alter our course and the want of such mean either in matter or time as we expect may utter hinder it and that more especial in case any foreign succour do arrive for then the whole frame of this our project be break and we must be present relieve out of England or else we with this kingdom shall suffer much hazard and because your lordship in your last letter give we some light that it may be and leave to inform you what likelihood we can here receive that it will be we have first the intelligence which we send your lordship with many other report next we have a constant and of late a extraordinary conceive confidence in this people and last we judge what a wise and a powerful enemy will do by that which be best and easy for he to do so as we have many reason to think that Spain will send they help this year and few to think otherwise save that he have so often deceive their expectation for if the malice of Spain continue to England they have a easy and dangerous step thereto by Ireland and if they do not embrace the occasion of this year there be no doubt but the next will for ever loose it unto they now because it must please your lordship to proportion our succour to the force we be likely to be offend with and that from we you will expect our own effate it may please your lordship to consider that the power of this kingdom consist of her majesty english army of such irish as be here in company by themselves or in english company to serve as mercenary of the nobility town and inbred people of this nation which live as subject and last of such mere irish lord and their people as be late reclaim or still remain in rebellion the english be few and far disperse the irish that serve (@) (@) (@) many and therefore likely to follow their golden hope of (@) the (@) town and people be of so obstinate a contrariety in religion (@) without question they be grow (@) malicious to the government and affect (@) (@) of the power of Spain to declare themselves the irish lord (@) (@) the same motive as they against we in their last necessity to join with (@) and all these especial the town be more stir on by this new coin (@) though if the (@) of Spain do not arrive may secure be establish yet if (@) it (@) breed many dangerous
