that it be the fault of the commissioner and commissary for victual if there be any want thereof since the proportion require by they be arrive in Ireland as likewise that the soldier make not some part of provision for victual by money especial in part near the sea and like place where victual be to be buy since by these great provision of victual in England with sterling money her majesty lose the three part of the profit she hope to make by the new standard of Ireland and which may be make if victual be provide by the soldier in Ireland have full pay in that mix money also advertise that her majesty have send for Ireland twenty last of powder with all munition in proportion necessary half by land and for spare of carriage half by sea pray that care may be have in issue thereof since they be inform that great waist thereof have be former make by the irish band convert the powder to their private gain and by the whole army under pretence of her majesty remittall of powder spend in service which have be defalk out of the soldier pay but be after hold a hard course to punish they for their good defert now charge upon her majesty all wilful and fraudulent consumption of powder Further signify that sir Henry Dockwra his fail in correspondency with his lordship this summer for want of match be distasteful to they have he not make amends by surprise of Donnegall which will faciliate the plant of Ballishannon that her majesty refer the garrison of loughsoyle whole to his lordship direction and the transpose any part thereof to the enable of sir Arthur chichester at Knockfergus the charge of that garrison be exceed burden some to her majesty by reason that coast in winter be so subject to storm and for that it be supply with all provision out of England buy with sterling money and small quantity of the irish mix money can be there issue to any such purpose in which regard her majesty wish that the irish in those part in who service no profit be find shall either be cast and pension of mix money give to the chief lord or at least shall receive no victual out of the store but have their full pay in that standard to provide therewith for their company touch the expect land of the spaniard their lordship be of opinion that they will present land in monster advertise his lordship that two thousand man be embark for that province and two thousand more shall be ready within twenty day at the seaside to come where his lordship shall direct they touch the exception above mention which his lordship have take that part of the officer for the company send into monster be leave to the lord president disposal and all the rest be bestow in England their lordship profess that as in all circumstance of honour and contentment they desire to respect his lordship so they pray he to consider that it stand with the reputation of a counsel of state to confer some such employment and keep man of quality at court to be upon all occasion use in her majesty service wherein notwithstanding they have prefer few or none who have not his lordship letter of recommendation to that board and now refer they all to be continue or cass at his pleasure last whereas their lordship be inform that some be apprehend in Ireland for coin of the new mix money they signify her majesty pleasure that those man shall be execute the rather to prevent the great inconvenience may arise in maintain the exchange for such counterfeit money and otherwise the four of September his lordship write from trim to sir Robert Cecyll the follow letter sir at my come into these part I find they not so distemper as I be bear in hand I shall so as I make no doubt at all but if the spaniard do not come I shall be able to give her majesty a good account of my charge here and I be not out of hope but rather of opinion since they have stay so long that they will not come this winter though I desire not to lead you into that conceit nor omit not myself to provide for the worst may happen and therefore have send master Martial towards Leax with almost a thousand foot and some horse both to be near the lord precedent of monster for what may fall out that way and to prosecute Tyrrell in the mean while who with some two hundred rogue be get thither and with the remain of the moor connors and their follower who I can not cut off the last year be altogether draw to be above four hundred for Connaght I have appoint sir Oliver Lambert with as many company as I can spare he until I may understand her majesty further pleasure because I know he to be very active and find a necessity to employ some force that way so long as the brute of the spaniard come do continue especial now that Odonnell do make his residence in that province about sligo and may otherwise do what he lift without impeachment for myself I think it fit to stay hereabout a while for from hence I may apt draw towards monster or Connaght as need require or fall back towards the north so soon as we can gather any certainty of the spaniard not come and if we may be supply with the 1000 shot so earnest desire by our former letter and without which our foundation will be in a manner overthrow to strengthen the english company here I assure you grow exceed weak otherwise I will not put her majesty to that charge I make no doubt but we shall be able to do her majesty that service there this winter those shot be land at carlingford or the newry with the victual munition and other mean desire that the spaniard shall not from thenceforth be able to get foot to do we any great annoyance especial if it will please you to procure for a addition to the rest two hundred shot to be send for the supply of sir Arthur chichester at Carickfergus for from that place we have discover such a entrance into the heart of Tyrone as in all likelihood will soon ruin that arch-traitor if sir Arthur may be enable with mean as from i he shall not want what I can yield he I have here enclose send a note that you may see how the garrison be plant north-ward and who it be that command in each of they in the absence only of sir Francis Stafford for he have the chief command over they as the best mean to make they join upon all occasion of the service the nine of September his lordship receive advertisement from the lord precedent that the two thousand man embark in England for monster be arrive part in Corkharbour part at Waterford of which company some be leave by the lord of her majesty counsel in their direction to his disposal but he leave they to his lordship pleasure know the duty he ought to his general and whereas the lord of the counsel in the same letter give direction that the foot company of the lord President and earl of Thomond be each 150 shall be increase each to two hundred the lord precedent avow that it be obtain by the say earl join he for countenance of the suit altogether without his privity which he pray his lordship to believe for since his lordship have promise that favour to he upon the first occasion he protest that he never have any thought to make so needless a request in England therewith he send his lordship the list of the say company new arrive be one thousand four hundred under fourteen captain name in England one hundred for the increase of the lord president and earl of thomonds foot company and five hundred which he the lord precedent by virtue of the lord letter the lord deputy please to give his admission assign to five captain be in all two thousand foot his lordship have dispose the force as be above mention and write from trim to the lord precedent desire he to meet he upon the border of lemster meaning kilkenny as the fit place for that meeting take his journey thitherward and arrive at kilkenny the thirteen of September the same night receive advertisement from the lord precedent that the spaniard be meet at sea bear for Ireland and therein as he be inform for monster so that he crave pardon that he come not to meet his lordship who pleasure he conceive to be that in this case he shall not be absent from those part where the enemy descent be expect and he further pray his lordship so to fashion his affair in Lemster and the north as the force he mean to bring may be in readiness withal protest that he stay only for a second direction which if he receive he will come without delay to his lordship the next day his lordship write the follow letter to sir Robert Cecyll her majesty secretary sir have leave the northern border as well guard as in providence I can the command whereof I leave to sir joh. Barkeley and have send sir Oliver Lambert into Counaght to settle those part and sir rich. Wingfeild the marshal into Leax to prosecute tirrel with his adherence I write to the precedent of monster to meet i about kilkenny if he convenient may with a desire to establish a full correspondency for the resistance of foreign force if they shall arrive or otherwise for make the war in all part this winter the rather because I know not how for the present Galloway and consequent asherawe if it be plant may be supply of munition and some other provision but out of and by monster and further my be in those part seem to i of no small purpose to divide the birnes and cavenagh from hold intelligence or join with Tyrrel and to nourish the overture I have late entertain from o. m. s. the chief of the moor to bring i tirrel alive or dead which he desire shall pass as a secret between only i himself and omoloy to who he have already give a pledge to perform it now that I may not disinable any of the force I be come to kilkenny only accompany with some threescore horse without any one commander or captain of the army have leave they all with commandment to be resident on their charge only when I come near master Martial I send to confer with he be before accompany with none of the counsel but only sir Robert Gardner chief Iustice as I enter into kilkenny I receive intelligence from the lord precedent of the spaniard be at sea and return his messenger desire he not to stir from his charge but to advertise i often of occurrent myself purpose to return present to Carlogh whether under the colour to prosecute tirrel I will draw as many of the force as I can to employ they in the mean time and to be ready to answer such occasion as shall fall out in monster that be as thing stand the place best to give direction to all part and to assure the most dangerous now sir what I shall desire or advise from hence on so great a sudden as I think it fit to make this dispatch and in so great a matter I be not very confident but propound to your much better judgement what I think first and fit to be think of that it may please the lord to send over the two thousand man by their last letter signify to i to be at Chester with all expedition one thousand of they to carlingford the other to Dublin these I intend to thrust into other company to make they full if I can to a man whereby the queen shall be serve with all their body and yet she list no way increase nor other charge but transportation I desire so many at the least may be send to carlingford because I be confident that it be the best counsel whether the spaniard land or no to strengthen that part of the army which will be able to assure the pale that way and to ruin the northern rebel in such sort that it shall not be in the power of foreign force to make they live and if the worst happen they be thereby enable to come off to we if we send for they where now they can except we fetch they in general for such a war you must send great magazine of munition and victual and when you resolve how many man you will send or have send the proportion will be easy cast up by such minister as you have there in those kind the best place for the great quantity will be Dublin for from thence we may find mean to transport what other place shall have need of except th^ war be in Connaght for then only from Lymrick and Galloway all our provision must come and in Connaght I chief expect the spaniard first descent yet there with most difficulty can front they with any war before Galloway or athlone from Lymricke be thorough supply with provision if foreign force do not arrive these provision will not be lose for this winter Odonnell must be force out of Connaght or else he will get there what he have lose in Tyrconnell and so this winter we must do our endeavour to do the like in Ulster to ruin Tyrone which be a work of no small difficulty but of so great consequence that I be persuade it will not only turn the profession of this people but even their heart to her majesty obedience for such as love Tyrone will quit their affection when the hope of his fortune fail and such as do not their dependency on he will fall when their fear of his greatness shall be take away for believe i sir I observe in most if I be not much deceive of the irish reclaim lord great desire to continue subject if they may once see appearance of defence though perchance not so much out of their honest disposition as the smart they yet feel of a bitter prosecution if you hear that foreign power in any great number be arrive you must resolve to send at the least 200 horse out of England and two thousand man more well arm for you must believe sir that then it will not be the war of Ireland but the war of England make in Ireland if we beat they both kingdom will be quiet if not even the best in more danger than I hope ever to live to see if you provide we more man when we send you word that the spaniard be land we will write whether we desire they shall be send howsoever I presume her majesty shall not repent the put over so many man hither for we hope to ease the charge in the shortness of the work if this aid arrive not here and if any foreign force arrive in England the which we gather by some intelligence may be then if you send hither new man to assure place fit to be keep we may bring you over old soldier and captain two or three thousand which I will undertake shall strike as good blow as ten thousand ordinary man I have make some of the subject late reclaim and in these time suspect put themselves in blood already since my come hither for even now I hear my lord mountgarret son have kill some of the clarcheere and some of tyrrel follower since I contest with their father about somewhat I have hear suspicious of they sir I will again advertise you of our affair here very short and desire you now to pardon my haste from kilkenny this fourteen of September 1601. your most assure friend to do you service Mountioy his lordship return from kilkenny to Carlogh where he dispose the force to answer the service in those part of Lemster thence he write to the lord precedent to meet he some time at kilkenny if possible he can and within few day hear that the lord precedent have leave sir Charles Wilmot with the force at cork be on his journey towards he his lordship part from Carlogh and the nineteen of September meet he at laughlin whence they ride together to kilkenny before I proceed further I will brief add the affair of monster till this time monster collect out of the lord precedent letter the settle of peace in the year 1600. be interrupt by the alarm of a spanish invasion general give in the begin of this year 1601. and in the month of April the monster rebel which flee the last year into Connaght and Ulster attempt again to return into monster have be strengthen by Tyrone but the lord precedent send captain flower with one thousand foot to the confine and these force of monster on the one side and sir John Barkeley with the Connaght force on the other side so pursue they as the same month they be force to break and return into Ulster Florence mac carty notwithstanding his protection have procure the send of the say rebel out of the north and beside many rebellious practice about this time lade a bark with hide which shall bring he munition from foreign part the lord President cease not to lay continual plot to apprehend the ticulary earl of Desmond and have often drive he out of his lurk den in which service the lord barry have a company in her majesty pay do noble endeavour at last the lord precedent understanding that he lurk in the white knight country his lordship do so exasperate he with fear of his own danger as in the month of may he take he prisoner and bring he to cork where he be condemn for treason to entitle the queen in his land and for a time keep prisoner there in the month of june the lord precedent receive this gracious letter from the queen write with her own hand my faithful George if ever more service of worth be perform in short space than you have do we be deceive among many eye wituess we have receive the fruit thereof and bid you faithful credit that what so wit courage or care may do we true find they have all be thorough act in all your charge and for the same believe that it shall neither be unremembered nor unrewarded and in mean while believe my help nor prayer shall never fail you your sovereign that best regard you e. r. in the begin of july the lord precedent advertise the lord deputy that according to his direction he will present send into Connaght 1000 foot and fifty horse of the monster list though upon good and fresh intelligence the arrival of spaniard be daily expect in that province and the force remain with he be not sufficient to guard kinsale Waterford Yoghall Killmalloch Lymricke and cork the last whereof according to his lordship direction he will have care special to strengthen that he have give the chief leader of the say force sir fran. Barkely direction to return to he upon his letter if her majesty service in his opinion shall require it pray the lord deputy to allow of this direction since he mean not to recall they but upon sudden revolt of the provincial or arrival of spaniard that the prisoner usurp the title of earl of Desmond and many other evidence make manifest that the rebel of Ulster and especial the spaniard do most rely upon the help of the say prisoner and Florence mac carty which Florence though protect have assure they of his best aid and have prevail in a council hold in Ulster that the spaniard shall land at or near cork and that hereupon he the lord precedent have apprehend Florence and send he together with the say earl prisoner into England where they be safe in the tower which be in time know to the spaniard may perhaps divert their invasion of Ireland and no doubt the lay hand on these two archrebel much advance her majesty service in the follow invasion whereby the lord precedent deserve win great reputation thus much I have brief note to the time above mention when the lord deputy write to the lord precedent to meet he on the confine of monster they meet as I say at laughlin ride together to kilkenny where the twenty the land of the spaniard day of September they sit in counsel with the earl of ormond and the rest of the counsel with purpose so soon as they have resolve of the meet course for the present service to return to their several place of charge but the same day news come by post for post be new establish for the same purpose that a spanish fleet be discover near the old head of kinsale whereupon they determine to stay there all the next day to have more certain advertisement thereof the three and twenty day another post come from sir Charles Willmot advertise the spanish fleet to be come into the harbour of kinsale and it be agree in counsel that the lord precedent shall return to cork and the lord deputy for countenance of the service in monster shall draw to clommell and gather such force as he can present to draw to kinsale nothing doubt but that this forwardness howsoever otherwise the army neither for number of man nor sufficiency of provision be fit to undertake such a task will both cover their many defect from be spy by the country and for a while at the least stop the currant of that general defection of the irish which be vehement fear this be resolve in counsel after the lord precedent have give they comfort to find victual and munition at cork for at first they be not so much trouble to draw the force thither as sudden to bring victual and munition thither for they but when they understand that his lordship have feed the soldier all summer by cease and preserve her majesty store of victual which they think to be waste they be exceed joyful of this news and not without just desert high commend the lord president provident wisdom in the say most important service to the State the same day they write these letter to the lord in England it may please your lordship the spanish fleet so long expect by the rebel here be now in the harbour of kinsale or cork as it may appear unto your lordship for a certainty by the copy of these enclose letter from sir Charles wilmott and the mayor of cork which be as much news as we have yet receive so as we can not judge whether this be the whole fleet set out of Spain or whether part thereof be come after to they or bind for any other harbour only we have some reason to think the weather fall out of late exceed stormy and tempestuous that all the ship can very hardly keep together and the report be the whole number be at least seventy we be now to be earnest suitor to your lordship to supply we with all thing needful for so weighty a action and so speedy as possible it may be the two thousand foot already as we conceive at Chester we now desire may present be lend to Waterford and neither to carlingford nor Dublin as I the deputy think fit in my last dispatch when I mean to have use they in the north two thousand more at the least have need come soon after unto cork if it be not invest before their come but if it be their land must then be at Waterford or Yoghall and with they three hundred horse will be as few as we conceive we have reason to demand and therefore expect both the one and the other so soon as may be also munition and victual must be send for ten thousand man to come likewise to Waterford unless your lordship hear from we to the contrary for if in those two kind we be not royal supply man and money will serve we to little purpose with all which we recommend to your lordship consideration whether it be not fit to send some part of her majesty navy to lie upon this coast aswell to assure the passage by sea as to attempt something upon the spanish ship thus have brief set down our request as spare as we may do the danger consider we think it not impertinent to acquaint your lordship with the cause of our meeting here and purpose we think fit upon the expectation of these foreign force before we hold it of any certainty to confer with the lord precedent of monster and to consult upon the general disposal of the force of this kingdom how to make the war upon their arrival which we can hardly do without be thorough inform by he of the state of that province and what mean of victual munition and other provision we shall find there if we shall draw the army thither or from thence be drive to make the war in Connaght where we find it will be of exceed great difficulty unless we may have good help out of monster for this purpose meet at this place upon Monday the one and twenty of this present the next day while we be in consultation come the first of these letter from the mayor of cork assure we of the discovery of the fleet near the old head of kinsale but whether friend or enemy he then know not but that be make certain by the rest of the letter that come since we present grow to this resolution that the precedent shall return with all speed possible though before he leave the province he take order to the uttermost that can be do in providence aswell to settle the same as to defend all place likely to be invade and we conclude that I the deputy shall draw forward as far as clommell to be near the chief brunt of the war and upon the present apprehension of all thing they to give direction to the rest of the kingdom and yet to omit no occasion against the invasion while the marshal draw up as many of the force to i as he can with best conveniency and expedition for since the two thousand suppose to be at Chester come not to carlingford and Dublyn in time to supply the company northward that they may have go on with their prosecution we have now resolve to leave no more in those part then be sufficient to keep the garrison place because we hold it to be to no purpose until her majesty send hither great force though we be still of opinion it be the best course to proceed there if her majesty will be please to enable we for otherwise it can be look for but that we shall go backward great in this business thus be confident your l ps. will be careful of we we take this to be sufficient upon this sudden since what be any way necessary or fit for we be to your lordship in your wisdom and experience best know and so we do most humble take leave with this assurance that we will leave nothing unperformed that may give true testimony to the world that we value our duty to our most gracious sovereign and tender the preservation of this her kingdom commit to our charge as we know we ought before our life and live and doubt not but to give her majesty a very good account of all our do from kilkenny this three and twenty of September 1601 your lordship most humble to command etc. sign by the lord deputy and council the four and twenty day his lordship write this follow letter to master secretary sir I do ever think that if any foreign force shall arrive it will be doubtful for i to lay my finger on any sound part of all this kingdom which if our supply have come in time to have leave the northern garrison strong we may in some good sort have provide for but now my resolution be this to bend myself as sudden as I can against these foreign force if we beat they let it not trouble you though you hear all Ireland do revolt for by the grace of God you shall have they all return present with halter about their neck if we do not all providence bestow on any other place be vain till I know more particular in how many place they have make their descent I can write much but for the present I apprehend a world of difficulty with as much comfort as ever poor man do because I have now a fair occasion to show how prodigal I will be of my life in any adventure that I shall find to be for the service of my dear mistress unto who I be confident God have give i life to do acceptable service which when I have do I will sing this day I expect to receive light and further ground to write more at large and be now ready with the precedent to take horse who fortune and i shall now be one I leave you to God continual blessing in haste kilkenny the four and twenty of September 1601. the same day master Martial be dispatch into the pale to draw the company thereabouts towards monster and to procure from the council at Dublyn all thing necessary for that business sir Henry Davers be send for the company about armagh and sir John Barkeley have direction to bring other company that be lay about the navan and the l. deputy the same night ride to Kiltenan a castle and dwell of the lord of Dunboyne be a great day journey where he be assure that the spaniard be land and enter into kinsale the five and twenty (@) lordship ride to clommell where sir Nicholas walsh one of the council come to (@) and there it be resolve his lordship shall go on to cork and so to proceed as there shall be cause the six and twenty his Lordp. ride to glonowre the lord (@) castle the seven and twenty his lordship ride from glonowre to cork accompany with the lord precedent sir Robert gardener and sir Nicholas walsh counsellor the eight and twenty day his lordship be advertise by a scot come from Lisbon that the spaniard send to kinsale be six thousand in number command by don jean del' Aguyla who have be general in Britain that one thousand of they scatter by tempest be since arrive at Baltemore that they be direct to kinsale with promise of great succour by the pretend earl of Desmona late take and send into England and by Florence mac carty who the lord precedent upon suspicion have late take and in like sort send prsoner into England that the spaniard give out that assoon as they can have horse from Tyrone and other irish rebel in which hope they have bring four hundred or as after be credible advertise 1600 saddle they will keep the field and therefore will not fortify at kinsale and that upon the revolt of this country the king of Spain mean from these part to invade England whereupon the same eight and twenty day the lord deputy resolve in counsel that letter shall be write into England that it be give out the spaniard in monster be six thousand and that of certain they be five thousand command by don jean del' aguila whereof three thousand be arrive in kinsale and the vice-admiral for don Diego de Brastino be admiral of the fleet with four other ship scatter by tempest be arrive at Baltemore that no irish of account have repair to they except some dependent of Florence mac carty of who imprisonment the spaniard have not hear before their land who be the persuader of their come to that port. that to keep rebel from join with they it behove we present to keep the field that it be requisite to send some of the queen ship who may prevent their supply and give safety to our supply both out of England and from coast to coast and may bring we to Carke artillery for battery with munition and victual likewise to write present for three hundred northern horse and for the two thousand foot at Chester and two thousand more to write for six piece of battery the big to be cannon for the field with carriage and bullet to certify the lord that artillery can not be bring from Dablyn because the irish ship have not mast and tackle strong enough to take they in and out beside that easterly and northerly wind only serve to bring they be rate at this season of the year and that the great piece in monster lie unmount on the ground and last to write for powder for five thousand shot and for six piece of battery which must be some sixty last and for fifty ton of lead with like quantity of match and five thousand pioneer tool the same day his lordship be by letter advertise that a in a soldier habit be dispatch from kinsale the four and twenty of September and pass through clommell name himself james Fleming and from thence go to Waterford where he abode few day and name himself Richard Galloway that he have bull from the pope with large indulgence to those who shall aid the spaniard send by the catholic king to give the irish liberty from the english tyranny and the exercise of the true old apostolic Roman religion and have authority to excommunicate those that shall by letter by plot or in person join with her majesty who the pope have excommunicate and thereby absolve all her subject from their oath of allegiance that every general vicar in each diocese have charge to keep this secret till the lord deputy be pass to cork when he assure they his lordship shall either in a general defection not be able to understand these proceed or hear thereof shall be so employ as he shall have no leisure to prevent they that he give out the spaniard at kinsale be 10000 beside 2000 disperse by tempest which be land at baltimore have treasure munition and victual for two year and that Tyrone will present come up to assist they at kinsale and to furnish they with horse which they only expect from he and have bring saddle and furniture for they last advice therein be give to his lordship to write to the corporate town and chief lord not to believe these fabulous report but to take advice not give out for fear of their defection but only for their good to continue loyal subject the nine and twenty his lordship with the lord President and the above name counsellor take some horse for guard and ride to view the town and harbour of kinsaile and the spaniard fleet that upon that view they may resolve of the fit place for our camp to sit down by they they find the spaniard possess of the town and the great part of their ship to have put to sea for Spain for of thirty four ship arrive there only twelve now remain in the harbour some of the other be late put out and then see under sail so as they see there be no more to be do till our force shall be arrive out of the north and Lemster and we enable from England to keep ourselves from break after we shall take the field the first of October his lordship and the counsel here write to the lord in England according to the project resolve on the eight and twenty of September Further beseech their lordship to pardon their earnest writing for munition and victual though great proportion of they be already send and that in respect the magazine former appoint for the best when the place of the spaniard descent be unknown be so far divide as we can not without great difficulty make use of they in these part and at this time when for the present the Spaniard be master of the sea and the queen force be draw towards kinsaile the rebel may easy intercept they by land but especial for that great use may be make of those provision in the very place where now they be if tirone come into monster with his force as no doubt he will namely the magazine at Lymricke will serve excellent for the prosecution former intend and after to be make in Connaght though by sea or land they can not be bring to cork without great difficulty and danger add that for the present the lord deputy be force to draw most of the force of the north into monster leave only the fort guard and so the pale be not able to defend itself against Tyrone whereas he hope to have be enable both to continue the prosecution in the north and also to besiege the spaniard at one and the same time whereof yet he do not altogether despair so as their lordship will speedy furnish such thing as be earnest desire by they for the good of the service be confident of opinion that the only way to make a speedy end of the rebellion and as quick a dispatch of the spaniard out of Ireland be to make the war round both in the north and in monster at one time also advertise that the spaniard as they for certain hear bring with they not only sixteen hundred saddle upon the rebel promise of horse but also great store of arm for the common people upon hope they have give they of their general revolt and humble pray their lordship that in regard our great strength and advantage consist in our horse they will cause a thousand quarter of oat to be speedy send for cork without which store our horse be like to starve within a short time and in case they approve the prosecution in the north to be continue without intermission than they will be please to send the like quantity of oat to be keep in store at carlingford last pray their lordship to send hither a master gunner with six cannoneer the second of October his lordship write this follow letter to master secretary sir I do think we shall find these force out of Spain to be above four thousand abundant provide with munition artillery and arm beside their own use to arm the country people great store of treasure and of all victual but flesh all the chief that be in rebellion and all the loose sword man will present take their part the lord that we have reclaim if we do not defend they from tirone must and will return unto he upon the first good countenance the spanish army shall make I fear i many will declare themselves for they but upon the first blow we shall receive from the which I hope God will preserve we I doubt there will fall out a general revolt the commander of the spanish army be one of the great soldier the king of Spain have the captain under he be most ancient man their band some out of Italy some from the tercerae and few bisonioes they be special well arm all their shot as I hear musket they have bring sixteen hundred saddle and arm for horseman of light shot whereof they make account to be provide in Ireland and so may they be as well as in any part of Christendom and likewise to have horse for their saddle but therein I think they will be deceive there be not yet come unto we any other force but such (@) only I find in this province upon the arrival of the first troop which I look for hourly we shall send you word of some good blow that will pass between we for I mean to dwell close by they by the grace of God to put they to it sir the king of Spain have now begin to invade her majesty kingdom if only to put Ireland in general commotion he have choose the worst place if to do that and to lay a sudden foundation for the war of England the best if he have be deceive in any expectation here the state of Spain must now make good the error and doubtless be engage to supply all defect the commodity that be offer unto her majesty be that she may soon prevent than Spain provide now as her majesty faithful workman I be bold to propound in my own task that it may please she to send present good part of her royal fleet and with they such provision for battery as we do write for and at the least so many horse and foot as by our letter we have sue for with victual and munition in abundance for they it will be fit that this winter there be a sharp war make in Ulster which will keep the Spaniard from any important succour and ruin for ever the traitor if the war be well follow if it be make by the several governor the effect will not be so great if you will have it perform thorough you must make one governor of all Ulster and the fit man that can be choose in England or Ireland be sir Arthur chichester if you resolve on that course from he you must continual receive his demand only of the three hundred horse we do write for it be good he have send he out of the north one hundred for foot if you send he out of England to supply the company at Loughfoyle and Knockefergus above our proportion it will be much better for armagh and those part shall receive from we this course I hope will soon make a end of the war in Ireland of Spain in Ireland and perchance of Spain for a long time with England I doubt not but you will conceive this action to be of no less importance than it be what goodly haven be in these part for ship how many fight man of the irish may be from hence by the king of Spain carry for a invasion of England the want of which two kind have be his chief impediment hitherto you well know believe sir out of my experience here if the king of Spain shall prevail in Ireland he may carry above ten thousand man from hence that join with his army will be of more use for the invasion of England than any that can be choose out of any part of christian doom and now sir that you know as I hope the worst I can dissemble how confident I be to beat these spanish don as well as ever I do our irish Macks and o and to make a perfect conclusion of the war of Ireland as soon as if this interruption have never happen if we have god blessing and the queen and those ordinary mean without the which none but infinite power can work I beseech the eternal God preserve her majesty and her kingdom and send i the happiness to kiss her royal hand with the conscience of have do she the service I desire and so sir I do wish you all happiness and will be ever from cork the 2 of October 1601 you sir most assure to do you service Mountioy the same day his lordship write another letter to master secretary as follow sir here be diverse worthy man very fit to have charge who have follow the war here as voluntary to their very great expense and look now by my mean to have command upon the come over of the next company if you send more than serve only for supply I have no mean to keep they from go thither to use the help of their friend and get they company there but by promise they any thing that I can do for they here for by that course I conceive I ease you of that trouble which their importunate suit will breed you and hold they here ready for any service upon the sudden think it no policy at this time to spare any that may give furtherance to the great work we have in hand if it will please you to do i that favour to procure that the company to come over may be appoint captain of my nomination I shall be able to satisfy those gentleman expectation who I be persuade will be fit for this employment than any that can be send from thence and they find their advancement here where they be to be tie to their task will in my judgement endeavour to deserve the best be in the eye of he that be the mean thereof which for the service sake chief I affect though I can be content sir to acknowledge unto you that I will glad have the world see that I be no less grace in my employment then my predecessor have be for this people do not little observe it and at this present especial I hold it a matter of that consequence as without it I shall be the less able to wield this great business with that success that otherwise I be hopeful of we have not here any of the queen pinniss whereof at this time there be great want at my come out of the north althoug the rebel in general do give out that they be out of hope of foreign succour this year I think in policy and to make we flow to call for supply yet Tyrlogh Henry do assure i upon his life that the spaniard will come and further tell i that one bath agent for Tyrone in Spain and since return to he be send into Scotland whence he be present to return whereupon I deliver a description of the man to captain button and will he to lie upon the coast to apprehend he assure myself that I shall have wrest out of he the certainty of all thing since that time I have hear nothing of that captain nor of the queen pinnace under his command I pray you sir let we have some of the queen ship with expedition for without they we shall not be able to convey any thing upon this coast from place to place and the way by land will be dangerous so sir I wish you all happiness the three of October his lordship and the counsel here write to the lord in England this follow letter it may please your lordship have serious consider of the great work we have now in hand we observe that beside the foreign enemy the Spaniard with who we be first to deal and the know traitor and rebel already in arm there be two other sort of people here which if we do not careful provide for they will soon adhere unto the rest and make their party so strong as in judgement we can see how we shall be well able to encounter it unless by good providence it be prevent which be the mark we aim at the one of these two sort be the subject who have land and good to take to for who we must provide defence else with his livelihood we be sure to loose he and therefore we will omit nothing that our mean will stretch to that may preserve cherish and content he the other sort be such as have no live nor any thing that will afford they maintenance and yet hitherto have not show themselves disloyal though all of they be swordman and many gentleman by descent and be able to draw after they many follower to this sort we hear for certain the spaniard make offer of great entertainment and if we shall not in some sort do the like we can in reason look but they must and will fall to their party we have therefore out of this necessity resolve to take as many of they into her majesty entertainment as we have any hope will true stick unto we be confident that we shall make good use of they against the Spaniard for we mean thorough to put they to it though if we shall fail in our expectation and find they cold or slack in serve with we yet will it be a great countenance to the service to show the person of so many man on our side where otherwise they will have be against we and of this we can assure your lordship that when they have serve our turn against the spaniard until we have free ourselves of they we can without danger case her majesty of that charge and will no long hold they in entertainment in the mean time they shall spend little of the queen victual but be pay of the new coin provide for themselves which may be with less oppression to the country then if in that sort they be not entertain for than they will spoil all and put out such as otherwise will continue in subjection of this course of we we humble desire your lordship approbation though we will be very spare to entertain more than shall be necessary and warrant to master treasurer to make they payment and hold we we beseech you excuse for resolve it before we acquaint your lordship there with all see we be enforce thereunto by necessity for the service sake since many of they be active and will otherwise have serve the enemy and we can not soon write unto your lordship of it and even so etc. the same day sir Benjamin Berry come to cork with his lordship guard which he command and with some other company for till this time his lordship have no part of the army with he but only the band of the monster list the nine day the company come to cork which sir Richard Wingfield the marshal have draw out of the pale and sir John Barkeley sericant mayor have draw from the frontier of lemster and Connaght the ten day be Saturday the company come to cork which sir Henry Davers have draw from armagh and the northern garrison and this day sir Richard Wingfield Martial and sir John Barkeley sergeant mayor be send with some horse and foot to view and choose a fit ground near kinsale where our army may sit down to besiege the town the next day some horse and foot be send out to keep the irish from sell victual to the spaniard the twelve two french man run from the spaniard to we who confess that three thousand spaniard land at the first in kinsale beside six hundred since artive in a great ship scatter from they by a tempest this day one advertise his lordship that under pretence of favour the spaniard descent he have speak with their general who inquire whether the l. deputy in person come to view kinsale and with what number to which he answer that he be there in person with four hundred foot lodge not far off out of sight and four troop of horse that he ask what soldier the lord deputy have to which he answer some eight thousand beside the daily arrival of other of the army in Lemster and the north what soldier be new and what weapon they have and what artillery the lord deputy have to which he answer with addition to our strength he say that the general presume by the contrary wind that they in England hear not of his arrival and though he tell he the english fleet be at Plymoth he seem not to believe it and make countenance that they shall have enough to do to defend the english coast from invasion and much insist upon the copper money the queen send with purpose to make the irish her slave but promise gold and silver from his mastor that he inquire to Tyrone and Odonnel seem to distaste their be so far off and the way to they be dangerous and his own want of horse and therefore pray this gentleman to certify tirrell and the lord of Leytrim that he expect Tyrone with horse and beef which he pray they to supply in the mean time both send he notice before they come add that himself have bread Rice pease and wine for eighteen month and store of treasure and that he inquire much after the strength of cork and the queen new fort there last he advertise that the ship return be fourteen of they six the king own of one thousand run the least in which be the admiral general saint liage and the great admiral of Castill don Diego Bruxero that the twelve remain be small and embarge or arcty to serve the king whereof some be irish. that the ship at Baltemore have 700 man that by his view there be 3000 in kinsale royal provide of all provision for war have many saddle for horse and that upon tyrones expect come they intend to take the field the thirteen it be resolve we shall present take the field though we have not as yet any provision fit for that purpose but that day and the two day follow we can not stir from cork by reason of extreme rain and foul weather neither artillery munition nor victual be yet come from Dublin yet it be think fit thus unprovided to take the field then by discovery of our want to give the irish opportunity and courage to join with the Spaniard chap. 2 of the besiege of the spaniard at kinsale with the delivery of the town to the lord deputy and their return into Spain in the same year 1601. the 16 day of October his lordship with the army rise from cork and encamp five mile short of kinsale at a place call owny buoy the 17 the army rise and march towards kinsale encamp within half a mile of the town under a hill call knock Robin where some few shot of the spaniard offer to disturb our sit down but be soon beat home we have at that time scarce so much powder as will serve for a good day fight neither have we any competent number of tool so as we can not entrench ourselves for these provision be not yet come from Dublin that day captain Morgan come out of England with one of the queen ship and our master gunner come from Waterford advertise that some ship of provision send from Dublin be come to that port where they be enforce to stay by a contrary wind be southerly the eighteen the army lie still and we view the fit place to encamp near the town but our artillery be not come we remove not and that night the spaniard make a sally much great than the former to disturb our camp but our man soon repel they without any loss to we the ninteenth we lie still expect provision and that day our man send to view the ground have some slight skirmish with the enemy and jean after profess that he never see any come more willing to the sword than our man do that night sir John Barkeley be appoint to give alarm to the town who do beat the spanish guard set without the town into their trench the next night after some sixteen hundred spaniard come to the top of the hill under which we lay either with purpose to cut off some of the scout or to attempt some thing on the camp but sir John Barkeley lie with a party of we not exceed three hundred discover they and skirmish with they kill some dead in the place take some arm and other spoil and hurt diverse and do beat they back to the town without the loss of any one of our man and only three hurt the one and twenty Cormock Mac Dermot a irish man chief of a country call ministry come with the rise out or soldier of his country to show they to the lord deputy who to the end the spaniard may see the mere irish serve on our side command they at their return to pass by the spanish trench make without the town on the top of the hill but lodge strong party out of the enemy fight to second they the irish at first go on well and do beat the spanish guard from their ground but according to their custom sudden fall off and so leave one of the lord precedent horseman engage who have charge two spaniard but sir William godolphin command the lord deputy troop when he see he in danger and unhorse do charge one way upon their gross and captain Henry Barkley cornet of the same troop charge another way at the same instant and drive their shot into the trench and so rescue the horseman with his horse come off with one man hurt and only one horse kill from the great number of spanish shot whereof four be leave dead in the place diverse carry off dead into the town and many hurt the two and twenty day captain button arrive at cork with the queen pinnace call the moon which waft other ship bring victual and munition from Dublyn and the same day come to the camp advertise that the same ship be come from Waterford towards cork that night his lordship send he back to bring his ship about to kinsale harbour and to take with he captain ward ship from oyster haven where it lay to guard the victual and munition we bring with we these two ship be command to annoy the castle of Rincoran seat close upon the harbour of kinsale and possess by the Spaniard but after they have spend many shot upon the castle without any great effect because their ordinance be small they lay still to keep the harbour that neither the castle nor the town may be relieve by water which be the chief end of their come the three and twenty the Dublyn ship arrive at cork and be direct to come present to oyster haven where we may unlade the artillery which can not be bring by land and other provision for the present use of the army the four and twenty day it be resolve we shall rise and encamp close by the town but the ship being not come about with the artillery and other necessary that day be spend in dispatch for England and by night captain blany and captain Flower be send out to lie with five hundred foot to entertain the spaniard which be draw out of the town but they come no further and so our man return this day his lordship and the counsel write to the lord in England this follow letter it may please your lordship since our last dispatch from cork which bare date the four of this present month we spend some time there expect the come of the old company out of the pale and northern part and hope to be supply with victual munition and other necessary from Dublyn without which we see it will be to little purpose to take the field but when we have stay there till the sixteenh and be not provide of munition none be come to we from Dublyn or from Lymricke whether we have likewise send to have some bring to we and want both victual and most of the provision belong necessary to so great a siege yet to invest the town where the spaniard be lodge from receive succour both of victual and of such as be dispose to join with they and withal to avoid the opinion which the country begin to conceive of our weakness because we do not draw into the field we resolve the sixteen day to rise and the next day do sit down within less than half a mile of the town keep continual guard round about the enemy we can assure your lordship that we do not think ourselves much strong if any thing at all in number then they be who army at their set to sea do bear the reputation of six thousand and we have cause to judge they because since our last letter to your lordship there arrive another ship at kinsale which bring five hundred man more unto they now to be above four thousand by the Pole in both these point of number in reputation or by pole they differ not much from we for it may please your lordship to consider that the whole force we can draw into this province leave the pale Connaght and the north provide for as it may appear by this enclose note they be in some measure do not exceed in list 7000 and of those we be enforce to leave some part upon the border towards Lymricke to be some stay to the whole country and it must in reason be think that our company general be weak in number see they have have no supply of a long time and that we desire two thousand to reinforce they beside that many be take out of they for necessary ward some be sick and many of the northern company lie yet hurt since the late great skirmish against Tyrone which they perform with good success but a little before they be send for to come hither we do assure expect that many will join with Tyrone if he only come up towards these part and almost all the swordman of this kingdom if we shall not keep the field and the countenance of be master thereof how ill provide soever we do find ourselves wherefore we most humble and earnest desire your lordship to hasten away at the least the full number of such supply of horse and foot as we do write for in our last and that it will please your lordship to believe from we that if the country shall join with Tyrone and make a defection our chief security will be in the horse we must receive out of England for the most of these here already be much weaken and haraze out with their continual employment in every service it may also please your lordship to consider that in a siege where four thousand such man as these spaniard be possess of any place whatsoever there will be necessary require royal provision and great number to force they neither can it be think but the sword and season of the year will continual waste our army so as we be enforce earnest to desire your lordship while this action be in hand to send we continual supply without which this army will not be able to subsist and although grieve with her majesty huge expense we be loath to propound for so many man as be conceive to be needful and profitable for the present prosecution of this dangerous war yet we be of opinion that the more man her majesty can present spare to be employ in this country the more safe and sudden end it will make of her charge and not without cause we be move to solicit your lordship to consider thereof since we now perceive that we have a army of old and discipline soldier before we of four thousand spaniard that assure expect a far great supply and much about twenty thousand fight man of a furious and warlike nation of the irish which we may just suspect will all declare themselves against we if by our supply and strength out of England they do not see we likely to prenuaile these provincial asleep few of Carbry only except appertain to Florence Mac carty do yet stand firm but no better than neutrality be to be expect from those which be best affect nor be it possible to discover their affection until Tyrone with the irish force do enter into the province who as the council at Dublin write be provide to come hither the supply from Spain be present expect if they shall arrive before our army be strengthen out of England or before this town of kinsale be take it must be think a general defection through out the kingdom wherein we may not except the town will ensue and then the war will be draw to a great length and the event doubtful if the queen ship do not in time come to kinsale our task will be very heavy with this small army to force so strong a enemy so well provide of all necessary for the war wherefore we humble beseech the send of they away which will not only give we a speedy course to win the town but also assure the coast for our supply and give a exceed stay to the country the enemy fear nothing more and the subject desire nothing so much as the arrival of her majesty fleet the sixty last of powder and six piece of battery with their necessary the victual and all thing else write for in our former letter we humble desire may present be dispatch hither and although so great a mass of victual as be needful can be send at a instant we desire it may be send as it can be provide and direct for the haven of cork what we shall be able to do till our supply come we can say but what we shall have reason to fear except they come in time your lordship may judge only we assure your lordship that her majesty with the help of God shall find we will omit nothing that be possible to be do nor shun any thing that may be suffer to do she the service we owe unto she if in the mean time by all our letter both to the council at Dublin and all other in this country to who we have occasion to write we give out these spaniard to be in number not three thousand in their mean scant and miserable in their person weak and sickly and in their hope dismay and amaze we hope your lordship will conceive we do that but for the countenance of our party and to keep as many as we can from fall from we on the other side don jean the spanish general have use many argument to move the irish to defection and among other which be very forceable and fearful unto their waver spirit he tell they that this be the first great action that the king his master have undertake and assure they he have protest that he will not receive scorn in make good his enterprise and that he will rather hazard the loss of his kingdom then of his honour in this enterprise the priest likewise to terrify the conscience threaten hell and damnation to those of the irish that do not assist they have bring bull for that purpose and send abroad indulgence to those that take their part these and such like policy as their offering of six shilling a day to every horseman that will serve they do so prevail with this barbarous nation as it be a wonder unto we that from present stagger they fall not to flat defection as they will soon do if they once discover they of ability to give we one blow before the come of our supply and mean which we be most earnest to solicit your lordship to hasten assure your lordship that nothing will more confirm the state of this kingdom than the arrival of her majesty fleet which we be resolve by the best judgement may be employ in these part to prevent the arrival of foreign succour yet in the mean time we will omit nothing that shall be feasable with the force we have neither have we be idle since our come hither have have continual skirmish whereof two especial be well perform by our man the first the twenty of this month when the enemy by night sally with more than a thousand foot to cut off a guard of horse we keep near the town and purpose to attempt something on our camp but three hundred of our man lead by sir John Barkley do encounter they and beat they back with loss of many of their man and some body leave in the field by who spoil our man be encourage and return with triumph the other the next day when Cormock Mac Dermot chief lord of Muskerie come to the camp to show we his rise out we will he to return by the spaniard trench that they may see the irish serve on our side against they where they entertain a good skirmish but soon fall off a horseman be engage and unhorse but sir William godolphin with my troop rescue he charge close to their trench in a way flank by two trench and fill with great number of shot yet return to our great marvel with little or no hurt have beat they from their strength and kill many of their man whereof they leave some behind they beside other we see they carry off from this beginning we hope God will so bless our just quarrel as short we shall have cause to inform you of better success we understand that Tyrone will present come hither which if he do your lordship can judge how weak we be to deal both with he and the spaniard the same day his lordship write this follow letter to master secretary sir what we desire and how our affair be dispose of you have by our general letter to my lord now I will desire that my unremovable affection may be hold as a conclusion so absolute grant that I may no more trouble you with any ceremony for you shall find that I will not value my life nor any fortune of this world to make you assure demonstration thereof when I shall have the happiness to have power and occasion to show it only now touch the point of my lord precedent come over to take from you any doubt that in my own particular I can not earnest concur with you I do protest on my christianity that I know no man in this kingdom that I can have be better please shall have be the deliverer of my affection and action than himself and by he unto you and from you may have deliver and receive much which I desire most to do neither do I know any who I conceive can have deliver more sufficient the present state of this kingdom nor propound to great purpose for her majesty service the course that will be fit for you in England to embrace but against my own private desire he have oppose his own peremptory distaste of the motion with this protestation to hate i if I shall urge it beside it seem to i against the public commodity in so weighty a cause to send away so worthy a instrument and deprive ourselves of the assistance we receive thereby at this time especial the stage of this great action be chief in his own province in the which the success of his government do best show what authority his judgement and presence do carry so that I conclude for your sake his own and i but especial for the public at this time he can well be spare from hence beside that he have vow to fall out with all if it be urge and although these spoil of ambition be of all other the most unwilling share by man of our profession yet I protest I be glad even in this great goal of honour to run equal with he and to participate with all his adventure this band of the honour we bear to you and mutual affection to each other have for chief knot the service of our dear sovereign there be no corruption that may be likely to dissolve it and therefore I hope it be tie by the hand of God and it shall not be in the power of man to loose it I be assure that you and I think the state of England can but conceive the importance of our work for now I act a between England and Spain and we that do play the game have least interest in the stake though we will win or loose our life to show that we do not play booty wherefore I hope you will not forget we for and let this only argument which I can confirm with many circumstance oppose itself against the counsel of those that will sell their birth rite in heaven itself to please their own envious and partial palate that the war of the low-countries be begin and have be maintain with few more natural spaniard then be arrive here already and that put arm and discipline into this people they be more warlike than any of his auxiliary sir I will trouble you no long be desirous to do somewhat worth the write God send we a easterly wind and unto you as much happiness as I do wish unto my own soul from the camp by kinsale this 24 of October 1601 you sir most assure for ever to do you service Mountioy the five and twenty the army be ready to rise but the weather fall out very foul direction be give not to dislodge four natural spaniard come this day to we from the enemy who the next day be send to cork this night sir John Barkeley go out with some three hundred foot have with he captain Flower captain Morris and captain Bostocke and fall into the spaniard trench and do beat they to the town fall into the gate with they and kill and hurt above twenty of the spaniard have but three hurt of our man hitherto we lodge in cabin so as it rain upon we in our bed and when we change our shirt the six and twenty the army dislodge and encamp on a hill on the north-side before kinsale call the hospital somewhat more than musket shoot from the town and there entrench strong when we fat down we discover that the spaniard have get a prey of two hundred or three hundred cow and many sheep which be in a island as it seem upon the southeast side of the town beyond the water which we can not pass but by go eight or nine mile about where there be a neck of land to go into it captain taffe be send with horse and foot use such expedition in that business as he attain the place before night and (@) hot skirmish recover the prey save only some twenty cow that the spaniard have kill although they be under the guard of a castle call castle nigh park which the spaniard have in possession the disposal of the whole army in Ireland the seven and twenty of October 1601. leave at Loughfoyle sir Henry Dockwra 50. sir John Bolles 50. horse 100 sir Hansard 100 captain George greame 100 captain Yeluerton 100 captain panton 100 captain cullom 100 captain hobby 100 captain Gown Haruy 100 captain Coote 100 foot 2000 horse bring from the north and the pale to kinsale the lord deputy troop 100 sir Henry Davers 100 master Martial 50. sir Christopher saint Laurence 25. sir Henry Harrington 25. sir Edward harbert 12. sir William Warren 25. sir Richard greame 50. sir Oliver saint john 25. sir Francis rush 12. captain George greame 12. horse 436. foot that sir John Barkeley bring from the border of Connaght to kinsale sir John Barkley 200. sir Arthur savage 150. sir Oliver saint john 200. sir John dowdall 100 captain Kingsmill 100 captain George Blount 100 captain Bosteck 100 foot 950. foot bring out of the pale by master Martial and from the northern garrison by sir Henry Davers to kinsale the lord deputy guard 200. master Martial 150. sir Benjamin Berry 150. sir William fortescue 150. sir james Fitz-piers 150. sir Thomas loftus 100 sir Henry Follyet 150. captain Edward blany 150. captain josias bodley 150. captain rotheram 150. captain Thomas roper 150. captain roe 150. captain trever 100 captain Ralph Constable 100 foot 2000 at kinsale horse 611. foot 6900. Totall of the whole army in Ireland horse 1198. foot 16000 of the six thousand nine hundred foot at kinsale in monster one company of one hundred be convert to the earl of desmonds use who be then keep in England and some be place upon the border of the province to be a stay to the country and all the old band call out of the north the pale and Connaght be very deficient in number have be long wear out in skirmish (@) and sickness without any supply late send out of England though much and often desire these six thousand nine hundred foot be distribute into regiment command by colonel as shall appear at the increase of the list the next month the seven and twenty day our artillery and provision send from Dublin be land at oyster haven our munition be bring into the camp and the front of the quarter that face the town and both the (@) of our (@) be more strong fortify and the camp be round about entrench and all those work perfect which can not be do the day before by reason of the foul weather now the spaniard hold the castle of Rincorane from their first land and because it command the harbour of kinsale so that our ship can not safe land our provision near the camp it be think fit to make the take thereof our first work to which purpose sir John Barkeley sir William godolphin and captain josias bodley trenchmaster be send to choose a fit place to plant our artillery against the castle the 28 day two coluering which have not be long use be make fit and the next day they be mount the spaniard be in the town four thousand strong and we have not many more in the camp by pole though our list be more that night the spaniard issue out of the town by water to relieve the castle but captain button ship do beat they back the thirty day the two culverin begin to batter the castle but one of they break in the caven in the mean time the spaniard give a alarm to our camp and draw a demi canon out of the town wherewith they play into the camp kill two with the first shot near the lord deputy tent shoot through the next tent of the paymaster wherein we his lordship secretary do lie break a barrel of the paymaster money with two barrel of the lord deputy bear in the next cabin and all the shot be make fall in the lord deputy quarter and near his own tent this night the spaniard attempt again to relieve the castle but sir Richard Porcy have the guard with the lord president regiment under his command do repulse they the one and thirty day the culverin batter the castle and that morning another culverin and a canon be plant they play without intermission which while we be busy attend 500 of their principal spaniard come out of kinsale with show to go to relieve Rincorran by land and draw towards a guard we keep between Rincorran and the town leave a great gross for their second under the wall under that colour to give a safe passage for their boat to the castle whereupon diverse break company out of the regiment in the camp be all in arm draw voluntary that way and sir Oliver saint john send out captain roe his lieutenant colonel and sir Arthur savage lievienant with one hundred man and see they likely to draw on a round skirmish he himself take thirty shot of his own company and go upto they where he find captain roe and those with he skirmish with the enemy shoot be two hundred and have another gross lie near the town to second they and see the spaniard come up close with their pike to give a charge he join with captain roe and encounter they do beat they back to their second make they to retire hasty the spaniard then play upon our man with shot from every house in that part of the town in this charge sir Oliver saint John receive many bush of the pike on his target and with one of they be slight hurt in the thigh but he kill a leader and a common soldier with his own hand the lord audley come up with his regiment be shoot through the thigh sir garret Haruy be hurt in the hand and have his horse kill under he captain buttlers lieutenant be slay and four other of our part sir arthur savage lieutenant be shoot through the body and fourteen other of our part wee hurt the enemy leave ten dead in the place beside their hurt man which we apparent see to be many and the next day hear to be seventy by one who see they bring to the house where their hurt man lay and who report that eight of they die that night likewise in this skitmish juan hortesse Contreres be take prisoner who have be sergeant mayor of the force in Britain and our man get from they diverse good rapier and very good arm all this while our 3 piece batter the castle till six of the clock at night when those of the castle do beat a drum which the lord precedent who the lord deputy have leave there when himself in the evening return to take care of the camp admit to come unto he with the drum come a irish man bear at cork and these in the name of the rest pray that with their arm bag and baggage they may depart to kinsale this the lord precedent refuse and say he will not conclude with any but the commander of the castle neither have commission to accept any composition but yield to her majesty mercy present they send another drum and a sergeant with he but the lord precedent refuse to speak with they at their return the commander himself be a Alfiero or ensign call Bartholomeo de Clarizo for the captain have his leg break come unto the lord precedent but insist on the condition to depart with arm bag and baggage to kinsale his offer be refuse after he be put safe into the castle we begin afresh the battery and they more hot than ever before bestow their volley of shot on we but the first of November at two of the clock in the morning when they find how the castle be weaken by the fury of our battery they do again bear a drum for a parley but we refuse it many of they attempt to escape under the rock close to the water side which our man perceive draw close up to the castle and hinder their escape the first of November early in the morning the lord precedent come to the camp and make relation of that night proceed to the lord deputy where it be determine that if they will render the castle and their arm upon promise of life to the spaniard only and promise to send they safe into Spain they shall be receive to mercy which be conclude because the speedy take of the castle be of importance to the more easy furnish we with all provision from that harbour and of reputation to our side as also because we can not enter the breach without loss of good man which we esteem precious be no more by pole in the camp than the spaniard in the town besiege by we by reason our company be very deficient in the number of the list have not be supply out of England of a long time and because this noble deal with the spaniard in the castle may induce those in kinsale to leave the town upon like composition when they feel the misery whereunto we hope ere long to bring they about one hour of the day the Alfiero send word to the lord precedent by that time return that he will quit all their arm and render the place so as they may be suffer thus unarm to go into kinsale which be refuse he entreat that himself alone may hold his arm and be put into kinsale which be also refuse he resolute resolve to bury himself in the castle his company see he desperate bend not to yield do threaten to cast he out of the breach so as they may be receive to mercy so as at last he consent to yield and that all his people shall be disarm in the castle which be commit to captain Roger Haruy then captain of the guard to see it do that the Alfiero himself shall wear his sword till he come to the lord precedent to who he shall render it up and this be do they be all bring prisoner into the camp and immediate send from thence to cork the spanish thus yield be in number fourscore and six and four woman who name I have but omit they for breultie beside a great multitude of irish churl woman and child but not any swordman for those be skilful in the way have all escape one Dermot Mac carty only except who be a pensioner to the king of Spain and heretofore a follower to Florence Mac carty also some thirty spaniard have be slay in the defence of this castle which be now yield to we those in kinsale not make one shot at our man the while but stand as man amaze the second of November find how much we have to do in take Rincorran castle with our weak provision it be conclude that all attempt against kinsale town be in vain till we be better furnish for such a business which notwithstanding we make daily countenance to take in hand this day we draw our ordinance from the castle into our camp the three day of November the spanish sergeant mayor in britauny take in a skirmish and the Alfero yield at Rincorran obtain licence to write to don jean and one of our drum have licence to carry their letter who stay in the town all the follow night and this day his lordship receive letter of supply send out of England whereupon he write to the counsel at Dublin and to sir Arthur chichester to make stay of certain company which late he have direct to be bring out of the north and the pale to the camp at kinsale touch the say supply her majesty write to the lord deputy this letter follow Elizabeth right trusty and well belove we greet you well upon such advertisement as we have receive from diverse place of a fleet dispatch from Spain with a good number of man of war to be land in that our realm in assistance of our rebcl there we have think good to send from hence some further force for increase of our army there to enable you the better to make head against they if they shall fortune to land wherefore we have cause to be levy here the number of two thousand man and appoint they to be embark by the twenty day of this month of October above the other two thousand late send unto you for this two thousand now levy because we can certain judge here whether you shall find most commodious for our service either to use they for the fill up of decay band there or to retain they in company or to employ some of they for fill up the decare of other company and to retain other some in band we have think good to leave the disposition of they to your discretion with the advice of our council and only to authorize you hereby that forasmuch as any part of they which you shall retain in several band will be above the number of our establishment for the payment whereof neither you nor our treasurer have any warrant that you may give warrantto our treasurer for the payment of the whole or any part of the say two thousand which you shall find necessary to retain in our pay in band several above the number of our establishment or any other former warrant limit the say payment to be make in lend and apparel as other our soldier there be pay and to begin from the day of their land there since the write of thus much to you concern the two thousand prepare we have receive advertisement of the land of the spaniard at kinsale whereupon we have add a further supply of three thousand man more to be send to you and for that it may be we shall have cause to increase or alter the number of our army as our service shall require we do therefore give you warrant to give order from time to time for the payment of all such number of man there either horse or foot above the number limit by our establishment as you shall be from our privy council here or six of they whereof our treasurer of England and our principal secretary to be two authorize to retain in our pay as our service shall require give under our signet at our manor of Richmond the four day of October in the forty thee year of our reign postscript of all these number two thousand shall be embark in
