part they take as while the power of these king be weaken by mutual war Italy in the mean time receive small or no damage true it be that Charles the five by subtle art and open force have almost subdue Germany distract by dissension of religion and have almost bring the free empire into the form of a subdue province till mauritius elector of Saxony obtain help of the king of France Henry the second who come with a great army to the confine of the empire profess himself the champion of the german liberty at which time mauritius besiege magdeburg with the emperor army receive that city into the protection of the empire and of himself and lest he may seem to deal perfidious with the emperor if he shall assail he with force under his own pay dismiss the whole army yet so as himself present entertain in his own pay the great part thereof willing to serve he and with these force he so speedy come to insprucke where the emperor than lie as his sudden repair make the emperor hasty fly out of the empire into Italy thus mauritius cause the captive prince of the reform religion to be set at liberty give peace to the reform religion and restore liberty to the oppress empire and how soever he cunning have advance himself and his posterity by the dejection of his own kinsman suffer for the reform religion and for the liberty of the empire yet he repair the public loss of his religion and of his country but they who more judicial observe the affair of this age confess that nothing have more keep the house of Austria from subdue the west than those of the same house for the foresay confident proceed of mauritius be cause by the distrust and jealousy between Charles the five and his brother Ferdinand spring from the follow cause namely that Charles the elder brother to the end that he may keep the empire in his own family have cause his brother Ferdinand at Colen in the year 1531 to be choose king of the roman so they call he that be choose in the emperor life to succeed he hope that when his son Philip shall come to age his brother for some increase of his patrimony will be induce to surrender his right in the empire but Ferdinand at this time have have large offer make he to resign the same can not be induce to do that wrong to his child and because he suspect that Charles the emperor may force he thereunto he be say to have glad bear the adverse fortune of his say brother and all trouble rise against he yea if man of experience may be believe to have himself encourage mauritius to the foresay attempt therefore Charles fail of his hope and for age and weariness of the world retire himself to a private life in a monastery of Spain in the year 1558 his brother Ferdinand take possession of the empire which remain to this day in his posterity the elector always use to respect the right of blood in choose the new emperor and under their poor estate and unwarlike mind the empire at this day languish like a spark lap in ash and the pope hold for god upon earth have no more fear the emperor authority but rather support it against the reform religion and the invasion of the turk the emperor always acknowledge this unprofitable servant of their progenitor for their benefactor and spiritual father the emperor rodolphus at this time live be of the house of Austria who pedigree I will set down the first family of the house of Austria give many emperor the empe rours pedigree to Germany but that be extinguish in Conradine the son of Frederick few year before rodolphus of Habspurg come to the empire who be the root of this second family of Austria of Habsburg of the house of Austria be choose emperor in the year 1273. Albert the first heir of the dukedom of Austria stiria and Carniola after his father have subdue the kingdom of Bohemia and join it to the empire be choose emperor and die in the year 1308. duke of Austria die in the year 1308. Frederick make duke of Suenia and Morania by the emperor gift die in the year 1330. Leopold duke of Austria Albert the second count of tyrol by the marriage of his son to the niece of the king of Bohemia die in the year 1359. Albert the three duke of austria die in the year 1395. Albert the five emperor and by marriage of the daughter of the emperor Sigismond make king of Hungaria and of Bohemia die in the year 1439. Frederick the three emperor die in the year 1493. Maximilian the first emperor after the death of Mathias king of Hungary recover that kingdom which he have usurp then retain to himself the right of succession yield it to and marry the daughter of Charles duke of Burgundy make that dukedom and all the province of netherlands hereditary to the house of Austria he die in the year 1519. Philip marry the daughter of Ferdinand king of Spain become heir to those kingdom and die young before his father in the year 1506. Charles the five emperor die in the year 1558. by his wife Isabel daughter to the king of Portugal Philip king of Spain this be the first family of the archduke of Austria to this day reign in Spain Philip king of Spain bear of Anne of Austria in the year 1578. two sister catherin-borne of Isabel of valoss wife to the duke of Sanoy and Isabel Clara Eugenia wife to archduke Albert and bear of Anne of Austria by Anne of Austria james die of nine year of age Ferdinand die a child Charles dentatus by Marie of Portugal by his father permission put to death by the inquisition 1568. Ferdinand die a infant two sister marry wife to the emperor Maximilian the second and jone wife to the king of Fortugall by jone his concubine don juan victor of the turk in naval fight die an. 1578. jone wife to the duke of Florence marry wife to the duke of Parma four daughter Elinora marry to Francis the first king of France die ann. 1558. Isabel wife to the king of Denmark die ann. 1525. marry wife to the king of Hungary and after govern netherlands die ann. 1558. Anne wife to the duke of Bavaria marry wife to the duke of Cleve Magdalen unmarried and Catherine wife to the duke of Mantua and after to the king of Poland Ferdinand of Ispruck so call of that city wherein he hold his court. he marry the daughter of the duke of Mantua by who he have some daughter but no heir male but by a citizen daughter of augsburg his wife he have two son this be the three family of the archduke call of ispruch the city wherein they live Charles marquis of Burgh Andrew a cardinal John die a child six sister Leonora wife to the duke of Mantua Barbara wife the duke of Margareta Ursula Helena and joanna Charles of gratz so call of that city where he hold his court. he be the four son of the emperor Ferdinand by Marie the daughter of the duke of Bavaria he beget twelve child and die in the year 1519 leave two son beside diverse daughter this be the four family of the archduke of Austria call gratz of that city wherein they hold their court. Ferdinand gratz Carolus Posthumus Margeret govern netherlands and die in the year 1530. Leopold the second duke of Austria die in the year 1386. Frederick proscribe in the counsel at Constantia die in the year 1440. Sigismond die in the year 1497. ernestus of iron die in the year 1435. Ladislaus Posthumus king of Bohemia under George his tutor and king of Hungary under John Huniades tutorage die in the year 1457. thus I have show that beside the branch of the house of Austria now reign the house of Austria in Spain there remain three branch thereof in Germany the first of the emperor rodolphus and his brother ernestus die in his life time Mathias and Maximilianus and Albertus whereof four live unmarried the five name Albertus have long be marry but have no child the second branch be that of Ferdinand of ispruch waa marry Philippina the daughter of a citizen in augsburg whereupon his kinsman difdain that her ignoble issue shall inherit with they force he to agree that the county of tyrol shall not descend upon his son whereupon his elder son by she name Charles possess only the city and territory of Burgh which be in his father power to give with title of the marquis of Burgh and the say county at the father death fall back to the emperor his second son Andrew cardinal of brixia beside the spiritual possession of that county have also the bishopric of Costnetz in Suevia but Ferdinand of his second wife daughter to the duke of Mantua have some daughter but no heir male the three branch be of Charles of gratz who beside his heir male leave eight daughter whereof one be now marry to Sigismond king of Poland by election and of Suecia by inheritance the second to the prince of Transiluania the three to Philip king of Spain the emperor by right of his own inheritance not of the empire be lord of many and large province namely king of Hungary king of Bohemia with the annex the emperor dominion most fertile province of moravia silesia and Lusatia also towards the Alps he have by inheritance many large province get by his progenitor as appear by his pedigree namely the archdukedome of Austria the province of styria Carinthia Carmola Tyroll and other large territory in Suevia and alsatia beside great jurisdiction among the sweitzer call the Grysons Ferdinand the emperor brother to the emperor Charles the five marry the sister Bohemia and heir of Lodovicus king of Hungary and Bohemia and after the unhappy death of Lodovicus kill in the field by the turk in the year 1526 be choose king of Bohemia which kingdom with the empire descend to his heir and this kingdom be exempt from the parliament and contribution of Germany by a privilege grant by Charles the four emperor and king of Bohemia of who the german complain as more respect Bohemia then the empire in which point he be less to be tax because howsoever that kingdom free elect their king yet the heir be therein always respect before any other and be a infant yet be common choose king with a tutor for his nonage the three state of baron knight and citizen choose the king but Ferdinand the emperor in his life time cause his son Maximilian to be choose King in like manner this emperor rodolphus be choose king of Bohemia and also king of Hungaria while his father live and howsoever he be unmarried have less care of his successor yet custom and the public good have such force as Bohemia seem hereditary to the house of Austria either for fear of so great a family border upon the kingdom or because they just triumph to have the emperor send at prage the cuecfe city of Bohemia especial since no prince out of that family be able to bear the burden of the empire if they observe the law bind the elector to choose a emperor among the prince bear in Germany as the say three state choose the king so they choose a viceroy for life to govern the kingdom at the king death and to be one of the elector as king of Bohemia at the choice of the emperor dead in the same person yet common before this time wherein the unmarried emperor neglect the succession the german be wont while the emperor live to choose his successor entitle king of the roman at this time the baron of Rosenburg be viceroy of Bohemia for life who hold his court near lintz upon the confine of Austria and be say to have the keep of the king crown in a castle call Carlstein touch Hungary it have the name of the people call the Hunns who under Hungary their king Geysa receive the christian religion his son Stephen be choose king in the year 1002 from who in order many king have be choose so as due respect be always have of the elder son to the decease who sometime refuse do stir up civil war king andrew about the year 1230 first give great privilege to the nobility which their king to this day have use to confirm as soon as they be elect king Vladislaus in the year 1490 first join the kingdom of Bohemia and Hungary together who son Lodovicus perish in the unhappy battle against the turk in the year 1526 at which time Ferdinand of the house of Austria brother to the emperor Charles the five and successor to he in the empire be choose king of Hungary as well by the covenant which the emperor Maximilian the first make with Mathias Huniades as by the right of his wife be sister and heir to the say Lodovicus and he cause his son Maximilian the second to be choose king in his life time as his son rodolphus at this time emperor be choose king ' while his father live and under they through civil dissension and the fearful neighbourhood of the great Turk great part of this kingdom have be subdue by that tyrant and for the rest the emperor rodolphus to the great reproach of the empire be force to send yearly tribute to Constantinople till the free city of Germany slack to pay this tribute the great Turk take that wish occasion to make war against the christian and find none weak to resist he then the emperor have in our age horrible waste Hungary and subdue the great part of that kingdom the say tribute be say to be seven ton of gold each three year as I have hear by grave and learned man but I know not how conversant in matter of State rodolphus the emperor be of a middle stature somewhat corpulent with a ruddy the emperor and his court. but sour countenance a short thick beard and brown colour hair at that time mourn for his dead sister he wear black garment of small price he be say to love solitariness and to exercise the art of alchumy and paint he be most easy of access and very affable so as every man speak to he with small reverence and in the chamber of presence the courtier and stranger give no reverence to the chair of estate the sword and the sceptre but stand by with their head cover yea lay their hand or lean upon the cushion without any ceremony of reverence he be esteem spare of speech and liberal in his nature so as he reward his courtier honourable though slow for want of money which make he not able to show any magnificence nothing be more common in every man mouth as well german as Bohemian then that he be much addict to the warfare of Venus bear in his body strange scar and privy maim thereof but abhor from the war of Mars at Vienna I do see ernestus and Mathias brother to the emperor eat at one table together for they admit all subject and stranger to come into the room where they eat at the time of dinner and supper before the archduke come in all stand with their head cover then the carver make himself ready to serve at the table lay his hat upon the chair of estate contrary to our english manner who give reverence to that chair though our prince be absent when the archduke sit down at table all the stander by bend their knee they both sit on one side with their back to the wall and each have a fool to stand by he one at the table end another on the opposite side to who with their own hand they give large to eat which they greedy devour the two Arch-duke do both together feed on spoonemeate for other dish like either of they each call for they by a beck or dumb sign and so refuse other but if any one dish like they both it be first set before ernestus and after before Mathias both have one taster but each have his cupbearer they speak not a word one to the other or to any attend and ernestus do swallow his drink Mathias do sip it ernestus be somewhat like the emperor his brother save that his hair be black and his countenance more warlike Mathias be very slender with a more effeminate face and a thin or no beard and whitish hair their apparel be nothing less than sumptuous these brother of the emperor have no possession of inheritance allot unto they but be content to have their expense bear by the emperor many pensioner live in the emperor court but few have diet and lodging therein the emperor have one hundred haschere to who he give for diet to each twelve rhenish gulden by the month and for apparel to each four and twenty gulden by the year he have one hundred for his guard call trabantoe of which each one have eight gulden by the month for his diet and if any one of they have serve ten year to he the emperor use to give a pension above his wage grant for life and to dispose they in monastery when they grow old and unfit for service ten haschere and twelve trabantoe attend each day and watch the night in the court who for that time have at the emperor charge plenty of bread and wine many gentleman have pension to keep horse to the number of some 1500 and for each horse they be allow ten gulden by the month but these stipend be pay but once in two year and then not full they keep not these horse at all time in full number but only when they hear that the payment be like to be make and because they be so pay the officer never muster they but at that time some few have diet and lodging in the court as 6 gentleman of the chamber whereof each have a pension of forty gulden by the month and six under they who have twenty gulden by the month likewise sixty trux who have each a pension of thirty gulden and sixty horseman call Hofdiener that be servant at court who be allow for each horse as I formet say ten rhenish gulden by the month and no man have allowance for more than three horse likewise a master of the wardrobe have twenty gulden and a controller have the like pension sixteen boy the son of gentleman be page to the emperor to who he give apparel and diet in the Court the very chief counsellor have yearly pension from the emperor he have three favourite a bohemian baron of the popell the lord of Firstemburg a German and Rumpf a gentleman of Ausirta who be in chief grace with he and be say to have a pension of five hundred dollar by the month and to have receive by gift in the space of one year eighty thousand gold gulden the wage and pension be very uncertain pay so as the courtier use diligent to observe when the revenue of any province be bring in that by such opportunity they may get part of the money due to they but when the emperor coffer be full these payment be easy obtain so as I have know forty thousand dollar distribute for wage and pension at one time the emperor have five stable and in one sixty heavy horse of Germany in the second twenty spanish genet and in the other three 60 foreign horse of the best race from Charles the great the western emperor be either appoint by the die the (@) ror election emperor testament or choose by the general consent of the prince in both which course the next heir be common prefer till the reign of Otho 3. in his time his kinsman Brenno a saxon be choose pope take the name of Gregory and he first institute the seven elector of the emperor which institution some attribute to pope Silnester but whether Gregory make this law or confirm it no doubt about the year 1002 the elector be establish about which time many tumult be at Rome between the emperor and the roman prince crescentius for the choice of the pope and the common opinion be that pope Gregory in the year 997 make this law of seven elector to choose the emperor and that pope Silvester restrain it to certain family and this institution seem to give great strength to the empire since the former sedition be thereby take away and it be likely these prince will choose a man of the great virtue and power but Charles the four choose emperor with condition not to meddle with Italy first obtain of the elector to choose his son to be Caesar in his life time and so make this institution of no effect all emp. after he chief labour as much as they can to make the empire hereditary by like mean and the successor thus choose in the life of the emp. be call king of the roman and after his death receive the crown be style emp. of the elector 3 be Churchmon and archbishop 3 be lay-prince of Germany and least by faction of six churchman and layman the voice shall be equal the the institution of the elector and diverse constitution of the empire concern the elector and other officer and the emperor himself king of Bohemia be add for the seven elector the archbishop of Trier chancellor for France sit before the emperor the archbishop of Mentz chancellor for Germany sit at the emperor right hand in all place but in the diocese of colon where he give place to the archbishop thereof the archbishop of Colon chancellor for Italy sit on the emperor right hand in his own diocese but on his left hand in all other place the k. of Bohemia arch-butler of the empire sit next the archbishop of Mentz on the right hand of the emperor the d. of Saxony the marshal of the empire carry the sword before the emperor sit on his left hand next the archbishop of colon. the count Palatine of the Rhine carry the first dish at the feast of the emp. coronation and sit on his right hand next the k. of Bohemia and the marquis of Brandeburg great chamberlain sit on the left hand of the emp. next to the d. of Saxony it be to be remember that for long time the emperor have be also king of Bohemia to the end that upon the death of the old emperor there shall not be want one to supply the place of the king of Bohemia at the election of the new emperor the bohemian have always a viceroy choose for life who not only supply that place but also govern Bohemia till the new emperor be choose and after receive for king at prage the emperor Charles the four make many law concern the emperor and the elector which law be all collect together and by the german call the golden bulla and it will not be impertinent to remember some of they it be decree that no elector shall lie in ambushment for another elector come to choose the emperor neither shall deny he safe conduct through his country under the pain of perjury and loss of his voice for that election under the same penalty that no man whosoever lie in wait to intercept the person or good of any elector that the archbishop of meut shall apdoint the day of the election by letter patent so as the elector or their deputy have full power may meet for that purpose at Franckfort upon the meinie within three month and if the archbishop fail to appoint the day yet that the elector uncalled shall meet there within that time that no elector nor depute shall enter the city attend with more than two hundred horseman nor above fifty of they arm that the elector or deputy call and not come or depart before the emperor be choose shall loose his voice for that time that the citizen of Franckfort if they protect not those that come to the election shall be proscribe and deprive of their privilege and good that no man be admit into the city beside the elector and their deputy and the horseman attend they that the next morning early after their entry mass be sing in the church of saint Bartholomew and that do the archbishop of Mentz at the altar give a oath to the elector in these word ay n. n. swear by the faith that I owe to God and the sacred empire that I will choose a temporal head of christian prince and give my voice without any covenant stipend reward or any such thing howsoever it may be call as God help i etc. that if they shall not agree of the election within thirty day they shall eat bread and water and shall not go out of the city till the election be finish that the great part be hold for a general (@) consent that the elector slack his come shall notwithstanding be admit he come before the election be finish that the person elect shall present swear in the royal name of king of the roman to the elector prince secular and spiritual and to all the member of the empire that he will confirm all privilege custom etc. and that after his coronation he shall swear the same in the name of emperor that a elector shall have his voice in the choice of himself to be emperor that the archbishop of Mentz shall ask the voice first of the archbishop of Trier then of the archbishop of colon then of the king of Bohemia then of the palatine then of the duke of Saxony then of the marquis of Brandeburg and last that these prince shall ask the voice of the archbishop of Mentz that the empire be vacant the count Palatine shall be of the empire in Suevia and franconia as well in judgement as in confer church-living gather of rent invest of vassal which invest notwithstanding be to be renew by the emperor when he be choose and alienation etc. that the duke of Saxony shall have the same right in his province that when the emperor must answer any cause he shall answer before the palatine so that be in the imperial Court that no man in the court shall sit above the elector that to a secular prince elector his elder lay son shall succeed or for want of son the first of the father line and if he be under age that the elder brother to the decease father shall be his tutor till he be eighteen year old and that this tutor for that time shall have all his right which he shall then restore to he and for want of heir male that the emperor shall give the electorship to who he will except the king of Bohemia who be to be choose by the bohemian that mine of metal find in the territory of any elector shall be proper to himself that the subject of the elector shall not be bind to answer the law out of their own province nor may appeal to any court but their lord except justice be deny in which case they shall only appeal to the chamber of the empire that the elector shall meet in some city once in the year where they shall have no feast to the end that the cause may be hear with more expedition that the privilege of city and university in any thing derogate from the right of the elector shall be revoke and make void notwithstanding the letter patent may except all eminency of person that the resignation of fee except they be personal make shall make the vassal infamous in denounce enmity to their lord that conventicle of city make to the prejudice of their lord shall be punish with loss of fame good and privilege that no citizen subject to prince and incorporate themselves in free city shall enjoy the privilege thereof except they dwell there under a great penalty to be impose on the city receive they with any other condition that the fee of the elector or officer of the empire shall not be divide by their heir that they who conspire the death of any elector shall be guilty of treason and their son deprive of their inheritance even from the mother side shall live infamous and they shall be note who make intercession to restore they to grace but that the daughter less dare for the weakness of the sex shall have part of the inheritance and that no enfranchisement of son or alienation of good shall frustrate this Law that all accessary shall be so punish only he that betray the conspiracy may be hold worthy of pardon also this penalty shall be of force against those that be dead if the crime be not know till after their death in solemn court that the emperor shall sit in his throne and the duke of Saxony at coronation and like feast lay a heap of oat as high as his horse saddle before the court gate shall with a silver measure of twelve mark price deliver oat to the chief equerry of the stable and then stick his staff in the oat shall depart and the vicemarshall shall distribute the rest of the oat that the three archbishop shall say grace at the emperor table and he of they who be chancellor of the place shall lay reverent the seal before the emperor which the emperor shall restore to he and that the staff of the chancelorship shall be worth 12 mark of silver that the marquis of Brandeburg sit upon his horse with a silver basin of 12 mark weight and a towel shall light from his horse and give water to the emperor that the count Palatine sit upon his horse with four dish of silver with meat each dish worth 3 mark shall light and set the dish on the table that the king of Bohemia sit upon his horse with a silver cup worth twelve mark fill with water and wine shall light and give it the emperor to drink the gentleman of falkenstein under-chamberlaine the gentleman of Norsemberg master of the kitchen and the gentleman of limburch vice-buttler or in their absence the ordinary officer of the court shall have the say horse Bason dish cup staff and measure and shall after wait at the emperor table that the emperor table be six foot high than any other table where he shall sit alone and the table of the empress shall be by his side three foot low the elector table shall be three foot low than that of the empress and all of equal height and three of they shall be on the emperor right hand three on his left hand and one before his face and each shall sit alone at his table when one elector have do his office he shall go and stand at his own table and so in order the rest till all have perform their office and then all seven shall sit down at one time the emperor shall be choose at Franckfort crown at Aquisgranum vulgar call ache and shall hold his first court at nurnberg except there be some lawful impediment the deputy of a elector absent howsoever he have his voice in choose the emperor yet at the say feast shall not sit at the elector table prince receive their fee shall pay sixty mark to the officer of the court except the elector who be not bind to give any thing but of free will since the officer be their substitute and the horse upon which the prince sit when he be invest in his fee shall be give to the marshal or to the vicemarshall the elector be presume to be germane and their son at the age of seven year shall be teach the grammar and the italian and selavonian tongue so as at 14 year age they may be skilful therein and be worthy assessor to the emperor these thing for this purpose take out of the golden bulla shall suffice touch the present general estate of the empire the emperor and his brother the general date of the empire be not much esteem among their own subject and have little or no authority in the rest of the empire the german confess that the house of Austria be most fit to bear the burden of the empire especial since no stranger may be emperor the law bind to choose a prince bear in Germany and because the empire have no principality belong to it nor any certain revenue but only some accustom subsidy which upon some occasion be of old grant by parliament and these occasion be take away the subsidy for they have also in latter time be discontinue so that the common affair be to be administer with the charge of the emperor private inheritance and last because they just fear if any other prince of Germany shall be choose emperor that the house of Austria have in a long line succeed in the empire and possess large dominion by inheritance will either altogether separate itself from the empire or at least their inheritance in Hungary Germany and Bohemia through mutual dissension between they and the emperor will be a prey to the turkish tyrant only keep back by the house of Austria according to the weak mean it have from invade Germany at this day but when the german do particular observe the person of the prince of the house of Austria they judge again none more unfit to bear up the empire and to defend it from the turk invasion and this common diffidence be infinite increase by the mutual jealousy of Germany there want not jealousy in the house of Austria between themselves be they not force to compound they by fear of the turk in general the gentleman fear the conspiracy of the common people lest after the example of the sweitzer they shall root out the gentry or at least yield either none or voluntary obedience at their own pleasure the prince fear the free city so as they dare not exact absolute obedience of the city subject to they lest they shall thereby be provoke to make league with the free city and so make themselves free and this cause alone make the prince less able to give strong help to the emperor if they be willing to do it again the free city fear the ambition of the neighbour prince for as most of the city of old subject to the emperor or to particular prince get their freedom in civil war by assist one of the party or else by privilege grant by favour or buy for money or else by open force of arm so they think it likely that the prince upon the change of the state of thing will omit no fit occasion to bring they again into subjection and the say prince do not only fear the say free city for combyne with their subject but have also mutual jealousy among themselves as well for inheritance as for the difference of religion last all and each of these state fear the power of the emperor lest he shall break the absolute power they have in their own territory or lest he shall by force of arm make they more obedient to himself or lest he shall oppress they in the cause of religion either of his own motion or by the instigation of the pope hence it be that he who dare not make war upon the emperor yet dare deny to help he and he that dare not deny help yet dare either fail in performance or by delay make it unprofitable beside that by nature the decree and counsel of many head be carry with less secrecy and be seldom execute with convenient speed and that for which many care each one neglect as Piato faith dispute against community also the emperor power be many other way weaken first that the german in the very war against the turk slow grant or plain refuse any contribution or subsidy and will little rejoice that the emperor shall have a great victory against the turk partly lest he shall turn his force upon the absolute prince or city of Germany partly lest the emperor then be as they open profess shall spend the money contribute in his private lust not in the public affair and last because the charge of the war shall be common but the profit of the conquest shall only be to the advancement of the house of Austria for which cause the prince and city use to deny contribution of money towards the turkish war and rather choose to send and maintain band of soldier in Hungary under their own pay for a set time and these band be so common send without order or mutual consent and so slow as when some of the band come to the army other band have serve out the appoint time desire leave to return home thus they seldom meet together to attempt any brave enterprise and while part of the force be expect the occasion of good adventure be lose second the emperor be more weak because the meeting of parliament which they call dytetaes require the expectance of some month beside the delay of counsel after the meeting and the contrariecy of opinion which must needs be great in mind so ill unite three because the german unwise think that the tyranny of the turk hang over they yet be a less and more remove evil than the jealousy of their private estate and fear to be oppress in the cause of religion last because the german think it not equal to be at public charge to recover the private city of the house of Austria from the turk these thing make the great power of Germany so weak that as the whole body pine away while the hand deny meat to the belly so not only the empire to the general shame of christian draw the last breath under the turkish tyranny while the disagree and sluggish christian prince deny help in this case to the house of Austria and oppose the weak branch of that house to the most powerful force of the turk but also it may just be fear lest other kingdom and the very name of christian shall be utter consume in this fire daily creep and increase upon we which God in his mercy forbid next to the say vassal to the emperor a king a palatine a duke a marquis the state of certain prince and three archbishop the seven elector of old be institute four duke of the empire namely the duke of Bavaria of Brunswick of Suevia and of Lorraine and four langrave and of each degree four whereof some be at this day extinguish and many other have since be create by diverse emperor in like sort of old be institute four metropolitan city of the empire namely augsburg call of the vandal for difference Aquisgranum vulgar ache Mentz and Lubeck of city bishop spiritual prince be of old twenty seven in number whereof some of bishop have secular dominion only by habit distinguish from secular prince but the churchman know no mean not content with tithe but scarce leave that portion to the layman have cause prince first to make law against inordinate joust to the church and then by other vanity provoke they to reform this abundance of their riches the impurity of their life and the falsehood of their doctrine so as at this day many bishopricke be in the hand of secular prince within their own dominion under the title of administrator in this sort to pass over the rest the elder son of the marquis of Brandeburg be in his father life time call the administrator of Holla not only the emperor but also many of secular prince prince of Germany as well secular as spiritual have kingly power in their own dominion and these absolute prince be so many in number as a passenger in each day journey shall observe one or two change of prince money and religion furthermore in free city here the patrician order there the common people and of free city other where both with mix power govern the city in such absolute freedom as most of the city have regal right of make peace or war of coin money and of like privilege but the plebeian among they prove they never so rich can have any high degree and their governement be with such equity equality and moderation as no degree be subject one to the other but all equal to the Law of these prince secular and spiritual and of the deputy for free city meet in of the dietaes parliament which they call ditetaes be the true image of the empire where they deliberate of great affair and impose contribution from which only the king of Bohemia be free by privilege grant from Charles the four emperor and king of Bohemia as I have former say the form of the commonwealth in the empire be aristocratical over which the of the empire commonwealth in general emperor shall be as head appoint the meeting with the consent of the prince and cause the decree to be put in execution but at this day the name of the emperor be become a mere title and his authority have no sinew so as he can neither call they if they think not good to come nor decree any thing if they be unwilling nor compel those that be refractory and the very prince be not constant to their own judgement if you respect the imminent danger from the turk nor active in their own motion concern the public cause but be diverse distract between fear to increase the suspect power of the emperor by help he or to stir up civil war to the ruin of the disunite state by make open opposition to his authority in the mean time nothing be more frequent with they then bold to refuse either appearance in the emperor court or obedience to any other of his commandment that be unpleasing to they and give i leave to say that myself there observe that a great prince of Germany for good respect nameless to who the emperor have engage certain city for money borrow of he when the emperor lend the money by ambassador desire restitution of the town not only refuse to restore the same but can not be induce to appear at prage by his substitute to compound this difference and it seem more strange to i that diverse other ambassador come to the city the same time have all audience before those from the emperor who stay long before they be admit to speak with the say prince the decline generosity of the prince of Austria and the fearful danger hang over they from the turk nourish this confidence in the prince of Germany and indeed the turkish war do so employ or rather bind the hand of the prince of Austria as be they never so valiant yet they shall be force rather to suffer any thing from these christian prince then by oppose they to be devour by infidel neither can the private calamity of Germany and the public misery of all christian in this point be sufficient bewail I say the private calamitic of Germany because the member be most strong if they be unite yet be without sinew thus disjoin and have no common force though in each part they be strong I say the public calamity of christian because howsoever the private prince of Germany be of great power yet the whole body of the empire be weak the daily victory of the turk threaten destruction not only to Germany but to the name of christian the duke of Florence of Savoy and of Mantua and all the prince of Italy who the pope have not draw to be his vassal the duke of Lorraine of Burgundy with diverse duke and earl of netherlands after a sort acknowledge the safe and far remove patronage of the emperor but they neither come to the parliament about the affair of the empire as not pertain to they nor contribute any money to uphold the dignity thereof except perhaps sometime in the common cause of the turkish war they lend the emperor some money which no doubt all other christian prince will no less do who have no bond of subjection the king of Denmark by a double bond of his kingdom and of the dukedom of holst the king of Swetia the canton of the sweitzer and the grison inhabit the snowy Alps be of old member of the empire but in time these feather pluck from the eagle have grow into new body and at this day do not at all acknowledge the emperor in Germany the tolles and tax be frequent as the number of absolute prince be the tax imposition and renenew great who impose they in their several territory upon all passenger and kind of merchandise or very small pack scholar of university only except who pass free for their person and good but above all other prince the elector of Saxony as shall be show in his due place seem best to have learn the art of shear his subject so as he not only imitate but be equal in this point to the prince of Italy Boterus relate that the emperor of his own hereditary dominion have the yearly rent of two thousand five hundred thousand crown and beside exact five hundred thousand crown ordinary and as much more by extraordinary mean man of good credit have affirm to i that the province of silesia alone subject to the emperor as king of Bohemia yield he each quarter of the year 60000 gold gulden or crown by which may be conjecture what he receive of his other large dominion yet silesia yield more than any one of the rest in respect that of the twelve dukedom therein contain eight be fall to the emperor for want of heires-maies the bishop of silesia be call the golden bishop and the same province have thirty abbey be most rich in that and all other respect at prage subject to the emperor as king of Bohemia I observe that every house pay he yearly three dollar but this burden equal impose on thatch house and stately palace seem unequal share the citizen agree among themselves of a more equal division thereof so as I remember that my host house purchase for three hundred dollar pay yearly to the emperor nine dollar beside other charge of maintain poor scholar of watch and the like impose upon each master of a family in each several parish for which he also pay two dollar yearly in the dominion of the emperor the brewer of beer for each brew pay six dollar to the emperor which tribute in one city of prage be say to pass five hundred dollar weekly also the emperor exact of his subject for each tun of wine draw a Doller and ten grosh for each bushel of corn buy in the market not the private corn of their own spend in their house one silver grosh these and like tribute be at first grant for certain year by consent of the three estate but prince know well to impose exaction and know not how to depose they the emperor give a city to the jew for their dwell at prage who be admit in no city of Germany except only at Franckfort where they have assign to they a street for their dwell of which jew upon all occasion he borrow money and many way shear those bloodsucker of christian the german impose great tax upon all foreign commodity bring into their haven and not only upon man person and upon commodity lade on beast to be distract from city to city but even upon small burden to be carry on a man shoulder as they pass through their fort or city which they use to build upon their confine to that purpose and only scholar of university be free from these frequent exaction for their body and good touch the revenue of the empire itself Boterus relate that it receive yearly seven thousand thousand crown or gold gulden and this revenue be of small moment for such great affair if he contain all the prince of Germany under this taxation since otherwise a communication of treasure can be expect from so disunite mind as they have he add that the free city of the empire yield a small yearly tribute to the emperor of fifteen thousand gulden it be well noun that those city of old custom maintain twenty thousand foot and four thousand horse for the emperor army when he go to be crown at Rome but this custom by long discontinuance be vanish since the emperor for many age have forbear this expedition the matter of great moment be the contribution which for the doubtful affair of the empire have be accustom to be grant by the three estate in parliament and these such as they be yet be more easy or hardly obtain of that free nation as the emperor have more or less reputation with they but that it may appear that the empire want not treasure the sinew of war let we gather by one particular example what may general be judge of this subsidy in the time of the emperor Maximilian the first the follow subsidy be grant in a diet or parliament at worm by consent of the estate for the use of the commonwealth and especial for the war against the turk which at that time much less press Germany than it do in these our day first it be decree that for four year next follow each person of any sex or quality howsoever possess through long and broad Germany or be worth by all mean 500 gold gulden shall yearly pay half a gold Gulden to this purpose and each one of less value shall pay a quarter of a gold Gulden and all jew as well man as woman and child shall pay yearly by the pole one gold gulden that prince and baron for decency yet of their free will shall contribute much more and that this collection shall be make not only in the private dominion of the emperor but in the private territory of all prince and the money first deliver to the superintendent or chief minister of God word and by they be convey to seven treasurer reside at Franckfort the first appoint by the emperor the second by the elector the three by other prince the four by the prelate the five by the earl and baron the six by the knight the seven by the free city all which be to take their oath for the faithful execution of this office after it be again decree in the diet hold at nurnberg that for the turkish war each 40 inhabitant reckon the husband wife and child for one person shall maintain one footman that man and maid servant shall give the six part of their yearly wage and each one have no wage shall pay a shilling of Germany that spiritual person man and woman that be nun as well as other shall for each forty gulden value pay one gold Gulden and in like sort the spiritual order of knight and namely those of saint John and all monastery and almshouse and whatsoever spiritual community shall give the like contribution except the four order of mendicant friar of which each five monastery be to maintain one footman that man and maidservant of spiritual person shall pay as much as those of the laity that no elector or prince shall maintain less than five hundred horse and each earl shall maintain one horseman that knight shall contribute according to their estate that the jew shall pay by the pole one gold Gulden yearly the rich pay for the poor that all preacher shall in the pulpit exhort man willing to give these contribution give hope that they shall be diminish according to the booty get by victory and that bishop shall make collection of this money and deliver it over to the counsellor of the state twenty noble man be at that time choose to have care of the commonwealth for matter of peace and war who in difficult accident be to call unto they the six elector the king of Bohemia in the emperor person not reckon and certain other prince and this must always be understand that these collection be make in Germany with great severity or strictness where he that dissemble his full wealth shall be force to repair all the damage the commonwealth have sustain thereby and shall be also deep fine when the fraud be make know which at least will appear at the death of each private man by his last will and testament so as these subsidy must needs be of great moment but the german in our day though ready to be devour by the jaw of the turkish tyrant yet for the abovenamed cause very unwilling grant these contribution yea for the very turkish war the german for the say mutual jealousy at this day in the great peace at their warlike provision in time of peace home yet live as in the time of a civil war at least in common fear of surprise so as almost in all city they have victual lay up in storehouse to bear a year siege and beside this public provision all householder be command to make their private provision before hand of dry fish corn and like thing to eat of fuel to burn and of all necessary to exercise their manual trade the city have watchman continual dwell with their family on the top of high steeple and tower who by sound of trumpet and by hang out flag of diverse colour one for horseman another for footman continual give warning what people approach to the town and in what number and beside these watchman be enjoin to sound their trumpet at certain hour of the day and night the very recreation of the citizen be no other then shoot in piece and crosebowe at mark in public house and thus they exercise themselves on holiday and at all idle time shoot for wager both private and public and for like reward and prize so as they must needs be thereby much better train up for war yet their footman in war do not so much use the piece as the pike and their horseman contrary to the custom of other nation be general arm with two short pistol not at all with lance to conclude if any man in this time of peace shoot piece within the wall of a city he shall no less then in a town of garrison be draw by the sergeant before the magistrate and be sure to pay a mulct for his error Caesar report that the Schwaben or people of Sueuta a great province in Germany their ward fare of old most part of upper Germany have be so call of old be most warlike yet at the first hear so fear the roman as some thought to leave their dwelling some make their last will and all mourn and be sad he report also that the half part of this people be employ and nourish in arm and the other half give themselves to husbandry and that so by yearly course they be one year husbandman another year soldier that none of they have any private field nor dwell in one place more than a year last that freedom in youth and hunt after they come to ripe year make they of huge stature many witness that the german of old in feast take counsel of peace and war think the vigour of the mind then to be most enlarge when they be warm with wine they be wont to promise their neighbour that they will overcome in fight or else die valiant and so be lead forth to the war with the people acclamation exhort they to valour and at their return be not praise except they show scar get in fight it be infamous for any of they to lose his shield so as many for that cause hang themselves for it be not lawful for they to be present at their sacrifice or counsel be ready to fight they call upon Hercules and their horseman use target and lance their footman dart their army lie compass with chariot and cart in stead of trench last in counsel they signify consent by shake of their spear and dissent by murmur at this day the horseman of Germany be vulgar call reytern that be their horseman at this day black horseman not only because they wear black apparel but also for that most of they have black horse and make their hand and face black by dress they and by black their boot wherein they be curious or else because custom have make blackness a ornament to they or else because they think this colour to make they most terrible to their enemy for the german use more to brawl then fight and rather to chide then fight themselves friend desire rather with fierce look to strike fear into their enemy then by conceal their strength to draw they to fight the best horse and horseman be of the territory of Brunswick Cleave and franconia but howsoever their horse be strong yet they have less courage because they be take from the plough and be of a heavy race neither the horse nor the horseman be arm so as both may easy be hurt by footman thus be light-horseman yet be they less fit for that service by reason of their heavy horse unapt to follow the enemy fly or to save themselves by speedy retreat and this have often be observe in their war against the turk have swift horse who they can neither overtake in flight nor escape from they when they pursue such and so heavy horse be throughout all Germany except Westphalia and those part where their wagon be draw with very little horse though perhaps they have great for service in war these horseman carry each of they two short pistol at their saddle with a sword and like short weapon but without any lance and their saddle be little such as be common use by passenger not such as our horseman use in war so as they may easy be cast from their horse and have the disadvantage be assail with horseman bear lance their footman be vulgar call Lantzknechten that be servant with lance their footman at this day and the best of they be those of tyrol Suevia and Westphalia common they be corpulent and of a dull or less fiery spirit yet be of great strength in fight a battle by reason of their strong member and the constant order they use in fight and they be arm with lance most fit for their strength rather than with caliver require nimbleness in charge and discharge in general the german willing hear themselves compare to bull for as their warfare in general at this day bull bear their horn on the ground with firm foot attend the assault of the dog so the german neither rush fierce on their enemy nor can easy be break by any charge the province of Germany be populous and the soldier be mercenary foreign prince common supply their army with they and for the faithfulness of the nation and the strength of their body the prince of France and Italy willing entertain they for the guard of their person the prince of Germany levy soldier by absolute command in their own war but only voluntary man be send to foreign war which they willing undertake out of all man general affection to the dissolute liberty of the war and because the german have ever be mercenary beside that the pleasant wine of France and Italy draw they to those war in our age the french have have civil war between the papist and protestant both part have often hire the german and they be for the most part lutheran and so hate both part as well the papist as the caluinist so I call they for distinction be so term by their common enemy though they follow neither Calvin nor Luther further than they agree with the word of God I say that they hate the papist and most of all the caluinist near to they in religion as the potter hate the potter and the beggar hate the beggar and each one his next neighbour more than any other and be blame for serve they they will free profess that it be all one to they to serve the one devil as the other so they call they both thus serve more for booty then for love they demean themselves so froward in those war as they much impair the old reputation of their nation in warfare the french I say have just no confidence in their own footman for the most part use the german as also the sweitzer in that service and find by experience that the firm and constant body of their foot be most fit to receive the loose wing of the french cheerful assault but soon drive back and that after the first fury of the french the body of the dutch foot like the among the roman stand firm and the great victory of the french at Ravenna against the spaniard and italian be in great part attribute to the german footman who receive the french foot and namely the guascon the best foot of France into their body when they be put to flight but they be most unfit to besiege strong fort and have be find no less unfit to defend they be besiege whether it be for that they be less serviceable in thing require witty resolution and fury in sudden assault then in a firm and constant strength or for that contrary to their old reputation they be not find able in this our age to bear hunger thirst cold and watch the necessary evil of a siege and it be certain that the netherlander use they in this kind as the course of their war consist especial in defend and assail fort have tax they with bitter jest on this behalf which I willing pass in silence desire more to express virtue then to impute vice yet the german have many very strong and well fortify city of which some be judge impregnable in which they place great hope of safety from the incursion of the polonian or of the turk for the polonian trust to their famous strength of horse brag that they despise the force of the german in open field and the turkish horse praise for swiftness seem not to fear the heavy horse of Germany sure though I do not think the german to degenerate from the valour of their old progenitor yet I have read the history and have hear the gentleman of France in our time much inveigh against they first that be in neutral or friend country far distant from the enemy they consume wine and victual as if they have be bear to no other end and spoil all man good but when the enemy draw near that not content with their former spoil they will then murinie for pay and refuse otherwise to fight when the prince have no present mean to satisfy they yea and for want of it will threaten to leave their party and go to the enemy bear no more affection to the one than the other second that in all army wherein their strength be predominant and especial upon the approach of the enemy they be prone to threaten and seditious demeanour three that the horse have give one assault without success can by no entreaty no reward no hope of victory be induce to give a second charge four that once put out of order and rout they can never be gather again together five that in the battle of Mountcontour by confuse fear they have almost expose themselves and the whole army to the sword and that in the next battle have the victory they spare neither man woman nor child but like bear rage against their yield suppliant still cry mountcontour mount contour for the word of revenge last that the levy of they be a excessive charge that they consume abundance of victual and especial wine and can bear with any want of the least of they and be a great burden to a army with their baggage touch victual I have hear the citizen of Vienna be themselves germane yet free profess that when the turk make a show to besiege they and encamp on one side of the town they suffer far great loss by the soldier receive into the town to help they then by the enemy spoil all abroad touch their baggage every footman have his wench that carry on she back a great pack and a brass pan while the soldier himself go empty carry nothing but his arm and at strasburg I do see certain troop of horse enter the town send from the marquis of Brandeburg to aid the citizen against the duke of Lorraine which horseman have a unspeakable number of cart to carry their arm and other necessary and upon each cart sit a cock which creature as most watchful the german have of most old custom use to carry with they to the war I can pass in silence the judgement of a italian well know though by i unnamed who because the german in our age have have some ill success in the war do attribute the same to the impurity of the reform religion process by they wherein he sophistical obtrude the false cause for the true not much unlike the old man record in our history who be ask for his age and experience what he think to be the cause of Goodwin sand near the mouth of the Thames answer that he think the build of Tenterton steeple be the cause thereof because no such sand be see till the time when it be build nothing be more manifest then that the german of the reform religion nothing yield or rather much excel the germane continue papist in all manual art liberal science and all endowment of nature which may clear be prove by one instance of the norenberger and sweitzer profess the reform religion who in all art and the military profession pass all other german whatsoever neither be I of the same italian opinion who to make the german active in war think they must have a italian or some foreign prince for their general which none in the world can less endure since they not only most willing hear read and obey the preacher author and superior of their own country but above all other nation singular in self-love do also despise all stranger compare with themselves though otherwise they be not unhospitall towards they they have one commendable custom proper to they with the sweitzer only namely that after a year or long warfare they return home uncorrupted with the dissolute liberty of the war and settle themselves to their manual trade and tillage of the ground the emperor Charles the five do lead against the turk a army of ninety thousand foot and thirty thousand horse and the emperor Maximilian the second do lead against the turk a army of one hundred thousand foot and thirty five thousand horse and in the civil war between the emperor Charles the five and the protestant beside the emperor army consist partly of german partly of italian and spaniard the protestant prince have of their own country man a army of eighty thousand foot and ten thousand horse and in all these army there be no complaint of any the least want of victual so as by these example it appear that the empire can levy and nourish a most powerful army and for better understanding of their warfar I will add the decree of the emperor and the elector in the expedition against the turk in the year 1500. Albert Palatine of the Rhine be confirm general of the empire and six counsellor be choose to assist he and it be further decree that the general shall not make war upon any without direction from the council of the empire then choose and consting of six spiritual and six temporal prince three abbot six choose by the people and eight choose by the free city that the soldier shall swear obedience to the general and he give like oath to the emperor and the empire that the general shall have the command of three hundred horse with eight gulden by the month allow for each horse that the general shall further have one thousand three hundred gulden by the month or more by consent of the council that each horseman shall have eight gulden by the month and each poorman four gulden that the general shall have twenty four for his guard with five gulden by the month for each of they that the general shall have pay for thirty two cart each cart draw with four horse and allow two horseman pay that the general happen to be take by the fortune of the war the empire shall ready pay his ransom and redeem he that no peace shall be make without the consent of the general last that the general shall depose this dignity when he shall be direct so to do by the council within three month if he be within the empire or within six month if he shall then be out of the confine of the empire to conclude he that shall particular visit and behold the armoury and storehouse for military provision as well of the prince as free city shall be force to wonder at the quantity variety and goodness thereof which if they be all under the command of one prince no two of the mighty king of Christendom may therein compare with he it remain brief to add something of the naval power of the german almost their naval power at this day all Germany be within land only the city upon the northern ocean and upon the baltike sea have any exercise of navigation and I do never read or hear that any of they do ever undertake any long and dangerous voyage by sea nor can their mariner be praise for their experience or boldness compare with the english and netherlander the city of dantzk which for agreement of tongue and manner I reckon among the city of Germany though it be in some sort annex to Poland howsoever it be famous for concourse of merchant and rich commodity yet not use to export they in their own ship but rather to sell they to stranger or to lade their ship and especial those of the hollander I can not understand that forty ship belong to that city among the other city Lubeck and hamburg be far more powerful in this kind than all the rest join together the haven of hamburg have common great number of ship and they say that more than six hundred ship do then belong to the city but they be vast and build only for burden be hold unfit for war the city of hamburg and the other city upon the northern ocean have long enjoy peace as neutral while all their neighbour have make war one with the other and none of the city except hamburg send out ship further than upon the coast it can be that the ship shall be strong arm at hamburg I do see a ship then build for a man of war of one thousand two hundred ton and among the other ship belong to that city the great be call the golden lion strong build and bear eighteen brass piece on each side which they name their admiral but our best sea man think they both more fit to defend the haven as fort then to make any fight at Sea in our age thirty seven ship of hamburg be lade by the fleming with dantzk rye for Spain where they have free traffic in the heat of the war between England France netherlands and Spain and of these ship six perish in the very go out of the ell by tempest while english and other ship safe put to sea and the rest despait of the voyage into Spain be unlade not long before my be there they have send some eight or ten ship into Spain whereof only one return in safety to hamburg the city Lubeck have a great number of ship than hamburg but they common trade within the baltic sea seldom trouble with war or pirate and their ship be only build for burden be slow of sail and unfit to fight at sea beside that for the foresay reason they carry few or no piece or other arm to conclude while I be at Lubeck a great ship of that city of one thousand four hundred tun call the eagle and lade with salt perish in the return from Spain whereupon I then hear our best seaman impute great ignorance to the german mariner of those city this shall suffice for their skill in navigation whereof I have former speak in the three book of this volume or part treat of the traffic of merchant in Germany touch their law and judicial course in general of old the magistrate of Germany be as captain of city who determine of civil cause at home and have public meeting yearly for that purpose most common in the month of May or at the time of the full and new moon they come arm to these meeting not all together but every man at his pleasure and as it please the multitude so they sit in judgement silence be command by the priest who have power to punish they then the prince or king or any eminent person in eloquence or in favour be hear to speak yet as persuade not command and if the speech please the people show consent by murmur or otherwise dissent by strike their spear together here they determine all controversy and choose new captain or governor they have a custom that if any man complain of another he shall make a supper for a hundred man who due examine the cause and if the plaintiff have the right the defendant pay the charge otherwise he escape free they give of free will to their prince of their cattle and corn as much as they think fit for his honour and necessity Tacitus write that the old magistrate of Germany do nothing unarm public or private and the german themselves confess that their old progenitor seldom try injury by law but common revenge they with fire and sword and that they shame not to take prey by stealth or source Quintilianus Varus appoint governor of Germany by the emperor Augustus do first appoint the judgement of scabines which in the Hebrew tongue signify a judge for he have former be governor of jury these scabines determine all controversy and to this day the german in most place so call their judge the low and upper Saxony have a provincial law yet determine also many cause by the civil Law the statute of the diot or parliament bind all but the statute of private prince only bind their own subject the great part of Germany be govern by the civil law and therefore the doctor of the civil law be much esteem among they and be counsellor of estate aswell to the emperor as to other prince which place they think unfit to be confer on any doctor of divinity yea the prince of Germany have this peculiar fashion that no son use his father old counsellor but rather new choose by himself the say doctor of the civil law have privilege by their degree to wear chain of gold about their neck and feather in their hat there be in Germany four kind of law give or rather four chief court of Iustice the first be that of the diet or parliament vulgar call (@) that be day of the kingdom which meeting by the law shall be make once in the year and last no less than a month at least no man have liberty to depart from they without leave of the council neither may the emperor or his son or the elect king of the roman make any war or league without consent of the same the second court be call landgericht that be the justice of the land wherein the chief man of each province be to be call together thrice in the year and be to sit three week to determine the chief affair of the province as the parliament handle the chief affay of the empire the three court be vulgar call Camergerichl that be the justice of the imperial chamber which be hold at spirit four time each year each time last forty day to determine the general cause of the empire the four court be the burgraves right by which debt by specialty be recover the kingdom of Bohemia have a provincial law derive from the law of Saxony and for that cause there be few student of the civil law but because the emperor have institute three chaunecry one for the law of Saxony which province lie upon the north side of the kingdom the second for the law of Bohemia the three for the civil law in respect of the emperor subject of Austria lie on the south side o. Bohemia for this cause there be many doctor of civil law and they also much esteem in the emperor court. if a bohemian have a cause in any court of the german he be try by the civil law or by the law of Saxony and if a german answer in the court of the bohemian he be try by the provincial law of Bohemia and the defendant draw the cause to his own Court moravia a province incorporate to Bohemia use the language and law of that kingdom in the old city of prage howsoever almost all speak dutch yet the law be give in the bohemian tongue by a statute late make silesia a province incorporate to Bohemia have the manner and language of Germany and justice be there administer by the law of Bohemia derive from the law of Saxony but for the great part by the civil Law general in Germany if a cause be receive into any court and the defendant escape to another city the magistrate of the place must send he back to answer the plaintiff his accuser the cause of the empire as I former say be handle in the imperial chamber the imperial chamber at spire and therefore it will not be amiss to relate some statute make in the imperial meeting which be collect into a book vulgar call Reichs that be the epitome or abstract of the kingdom but I will only set down brief some of the chief statute it be decree in the year 1556 that no subject of the elector nor any inhabitant or earl of their province shall appeal from they to this court of the imperial chamber the emperor Frederick the three in the year 1442 make these statute that no prince shall by arm right himself against another before justice have be deny to he in this imperial court. that the judge of the chamber shall be a prince or Barron and of sixteen assessor half shall be civil lawyer and half of the knightly order that the great part shall carry the cause and the voice be equal the judge voice shall cast it that the judge shall not be absent without leave of the assessor nor they without his leave and that without some great cause more than four of they shall not be absent at one time that in absence they shall have no voice that the chief judge be sick shall substitute a prince in his place who shall first take his oath the proctor and advocate shall take no more of their client than the judge shall appoint and shall swear to avoid slander and malice the notary shall execute the judgement in the name of the emperor appeal shall be of no force except they be make in order to the next superior court and so ascend all that belong to this chamber shall be free from all payment but not one of they shall either keep a inn or trade as a merchant the judge shall deliver over to the senate of the city those that be guilty of death by the same decree all fee for writing and process be set down so as the client swear poverty shall go free so as he swear to pay the fee when he shall be able further it be decree that the seat of this chamber or court shall not be change but by the consent of the imperial diet or parliament that the defendant hide themselves the prince or citizen to who they be subject shall swear upon a set day that they be not privy to any of their action or else shall satisfy all damage that the proctor shall speak nothing but to the purpose and for jest or impertinent thing in word or writing shall be punish by a mulct in money and by be put to silence in that cause by the emperor Charles the five in the diet at augsburg the year 1518 two new assessor be add and it be decree that Charles as emperor shall appoint the chief judge two assessor of the law and two gentleman assessor and as heir to his patrimony shall appoint two learned assessor that three gentleman assessor shall be name by the three secular elector three learned by the three spiritual elector and three gentleman with three learned by the common consent of the six community for the empire be divide into six community vulgar call Kreysen for the collection of tribute and like duty as other kingdom be divide into county and since that time in the year 1522 for the same purpose the empire be divide into ten community further it be decree that twenty two person shall with like equality be name yearly to visit this chamber or court. that no appeal shall be admit into this court under the value of fifty gulden and that the execution of judgement shall be do by the next magistrate and they not willing or not dare to do it shall be refer to the emperor at a parliament in the year 1522 it be decree that no stranger shall be appoint chief judge that for absence the pension shall be abate after the rate of the time and be distribute among the present that the judge shall swear to take no gift not to prolong cause and to do right without respect of person and that the proctor shall take no fee but such as be set down by statute at the parliament in the year 1555 it be decree that no assessor shall be of any other religion then of the roman or the confession of the protestant make at augsburg that one assessor shall not interrupt the speech of another nor shall rise to confer one with the other and that all speech of anger shall be punish and all be swear to keep secret the act of the council that advocate shall not be more than four and twenty in number that any man shall be admit to speak for himself first swear to avoid slander that this chamber or court shall be yearly visit upon the first of May by the archbishop of Mentz as substitute to the emperor by three other each choose by one of the elector by two prince one temporal the other spiritual and by one counsellor choose by each order namely one by the earl and one by each free city to who the complaint shall be present upon the first of March that no man shall forbid his subject to appeal to this court except they shall willing renounce the appeal but that all froward appeal for unjust cause shall be punish by pay charge and be fine and that no appeal shall be admit under the value of fifty gulden except those who have privilege to appeal for less sum and that no appeal be make for corporal punishment that the chamber shall be hold at spire till it be otherwise decree by parliament but that in time of famine or plague they may for the time choose another place that two brother shall not be the one a assessor the other a proctor that the judge shall meet three day in the week and eight of they at the least shall be present that execution of judgement shall first be require by letter of the court to which if the defendant shall not yield obedience he shall be cite to appear and shall be condemn in cost and the plaintiff shall be put in possession of his good and the defendant by the pope privilege grant to this court shall be excommunicate and then execution shall be desire from the magistrate of the community or in case the defendant be powerful it shall be desire from the emperor himself last that no appeal nor petition against the judgement of the chamber shall be admit and thus much brief write of the imperial chamber or court shall suffice only I will add that appeal be of old grant to the elector subject and at this day in some case and above a certain value be grant to the subject of prince and city and that in difficult cause the german often refer they to beiudge by the college of civil lawyer in the university but since prince and city weekly hold court of judgement so as execution be do before appeal can be make and since many city and prince have privilege against appeal grant to they from the emperor these appeal be many time and by diverse mean make void in this chamber the emperor himself may be accuse and many time a gentleman or any man of inferior condition have difference with a prince gain the cause against he and the great difference of prince wont to break into war use quiet to be compose in this Court the chief judge if he be earl or Barron have two thousand gulden yearly by the statute make in the year 1548 and have more if he be a prince a assessor if he be a earl or Barron have yearly by the same statute seven hundred gulden if he be a doctor of the civil law or a gentleman he have five hundred gulden and each advocate in exchequer cause have yearly three hundred gulden and by a statute in the year 1557 they receive for each Gulden 77 creitzer for better of their pension whereas former each Gulden be value at sixteen Batzen or sixty four creitzer touch capital judgement by the civil law in most heinous offence capital judgement the affection be punish though it take no effect yet in common custom and after the form of the statute of Italy he that have a mind to kill be not punish except he do kill the old law of Saxony respect the fact not the will but of late the elector of Saxony have make a statute which be yet in vigour that he that provoke a man to fight or threaten death to he shall die though he never assail he the german hold it reproachful to apprehend any malefactor which be only do by the sergeant of the hangman disgraceful family myself observe that a young man kinsman to the consul or mayor of a city have kill a gentleman remain two hour in the city and then flee without any stop by the sergeant who notwithstanding do afterward for fashion sake pursue he some few hour yet I must needs confess that the german be general most severe in justice
