have no bishop but the revenue of bishop the bishopricke be either conucrt to godly use or possess by the prince under the title of administrator and in like manner the revenue of monastery for the most part be employ to maintain preacher and to other godly use but in some place they still permit monk and nun I mean person live single but not tie with papistical vow for the education of their child and the nourish of the poor in each city and each church of the city many minister or preacher serve who have no tithe but only live upon pension common small and not much unequal for minister common have one or two hundred gulden and the superintendant one or two thousand gulden by the year beside wood for fire and corn and some like necessary for food these superintendant be instead of bishop to oversee the clergy but be not distinguish in habit or title of dignity from the other minister yet to they as chief in virtue and learning as well the minister as all other degree yield due reverence and in all ecclesiastical couse they have great authority but otherwise Germany have many rich and potent bishop of who general mention be make in the chapter of proverb and particular in this chapter much have be say of the three spiritual elector the husbandman in Germany be not so base as the french and italian or the husbandman slave of other kingdom but much more miserable and poor than the english husbandman yet those of Prussia a fat and fertile country come necrest to the english in riches and good fare the other be hire by gentleman to plough their ground give their service at low rate and pay so great rent to their lord as they have scarce mean to cover nakedness with poor clothes and to feed themselves with ill smell colewort and like meat in moravia incorporate to Bohemia and lie between it and Polonia the husbandman be mere slave and at my be there I hear that the baron of Promnetz have be late in Italy do make free a slave of he who be there a apothecary and give he a present also I understand by discourse that the marquis of Anspach in Germany have many mere slave for his husbandman but all other in Germany be free howsoever without doubt they be great oppress not only by the gentry but also by the churchman so as we find in late history that the bawren or clown in the year 1502 make a rebellion perhaps with the mind after the example of the sweitzer to get liberty by the sword but yet pretend only revenge upon bishop and churchman proverbial say that they will not suffer they to draw breath and it be probable that the neighbourhood of the sweitzer who root out their nobleman and get liberty by the sword make the gentleman of Germany less cruel towards the poor clown for either upon that cause or for the fertility of the country no doubt the clown in Suevia and place near Sweitzerland live much better than in any other part as likewise in place near Denmark and Poland admit slave general the poor people be more oppress than any where else through Germany in Bohemia the high degree be that of baron and the gentleman have the same the degree in Bohemia privilege with they all other in town and field be mere slave except city immediate subject to the emperor as king of Bohemia where many be either emancipate for money or find more clemency under the yoke of a german prince for in land belong to the baron and gentleman the king have no tribute but all be subject to the lord with absolute power of life and death as likewise the king have his land and some thirty city in like sort subject to he and howsoever the gentleman do not common exercise this power against the people leave the german shall repute they tyrant yet with wonder I do hear at prage that a baron have late hang one of his slave for steal of a fish it be free for a gentleman to hang any of his slave for go into strange country without be make free if he can apprehend he many time they give they leave to go into foreign part to learn manuary art but they call they home at pleasure and when they come back make they work for the lord behoof they take their daughter for maid servant and son for household servant at pleasure and these poor slave can leave their child nothing by last will and Testarnent but all their good in life and at death belong to the lord and they will find they be they never so secret hide in the province of moravia incorporate to Bohemia I have former say that the gentleman have like privilege and absolute power over their subject be all bear slave and in Germany that the marquis of Anspach have like bear slave and I shall in due place show that in Denmark and Poland the people be mere slave so as the gentleman and lord reckon not their estate by yearly rent but by the number of their bawren or clown who be all slave in Bohemia the good of condemn person fall to the lord of the fee among the baron the baron of Rosenburg be chief who for life be choose viceroy and dwell upon the confine of Austria be say to have the yearly rent of eighty thousand dollar but in respect he have no son to succeed he he be less esteem especial himself be decrepit and his brother also old and without probable hope of issue the second family of the baron be that of the popel have many branch and plenty of heir one of they be at that time in great grace with the emperor and the whole family for the issue be much esteem of the people and state of the kingdom in Bohemia as in Poland gentleman can be judge but at four meeting in the year and then be try by gentleman so as the accuser be weary with delay the offender be common free but man of inferior condition be daily judge and sudden try the bohemian give great title to gentleman by writing and in salute than the german where notwithstanding as appear in the due place there be great and undecent flattery by word among all degree I do not observe or read that the bohemian have any military or civil order or degree of knight as the english have the hussite have change nothing in religion save only the communicate of the lord supper in both kind with some other small matter yet I do not hear that they have any bishop and I be sure that the bishopric of prage have then be long void they and all of the reform religion in Bohemia send their minister to Wittenberg a university in Saxony for receive of order with imposition of hand from the Lutheran Superintendant and the minister of that place chap. 4 of the particular commonwealth as well of the prince of Germany as of the free city such of both as have absolute power of life and death it remain to add something of private prince court the prince of the empire and free city and the government of the free city and since I have former say that these prince and city have absolute power of life and death be many in number and that according to the number of the prince the place also where tax and imposition be exact be no less frequent as well for subject as stranger pass by both for person and for ware and that they who deceive the prince in any such kind never escape unpunished now to avoid tediousness I will only mention the chief prince and city by which conjecture may be make of the rest and this I will do brief without any repetition of thing former set down touch the elector I have former relate the principal law of the golden bulla the duke of Saxony be one of these elector many way powerful and he derive his pedigree the duke of Saxony elector from witikind a famous duke of the german in the time of the emperor Charles the great who force he to lay aside the name of king permit he the title of a duke and to become christian in the year 805. witikynd the second Fredrick Frederick invest marquis of mizzen by the emperor Henry the first he die in the year 925. bruno Dittimare Christian invest marquis of Lusatia by the emperor Otho the first Theodorike die in the year 1034. Henry marquis of mizzen and Lusatia die in the year 1106. timo. Conrade the great die in the year 1150. Otho the rich build Friburg where he have find mine of silver and die in the year 1189. Theodorike be poison by the citizen of leipzig in the year 1220. Henry by right of inheritance become langrave of Thuring and die 6287. in right line from Henry descend Frederick who choose emperor yield the empire to his competitor the emperor Charles the four take money for give up his right and he die in the year 1349. in right line be Frederick the warlike who overcome the bohemian rebel against the emperor receive the scholar of prage to study at leipzig restrain the title of duke of Saxony to family which after the emperor of Saxony have be confuse usurp and last appropriate the title of elector to his family he die in the year 1423. Frederick the gentle die in the year 1464. ernestus the elector die in the year 1486. the elector Fredrick the wise who put the empire from himself and choose Charles the five he do find the university at Wittenberg and die 1525. John elector exhibit the eformed confession at augsburg and die 1533. John Fredrick for the reform religion depose from the electorship by the emperor Charles the five he marry Sibyl daughter to the duke of juliar and die 1554 John Fredrick proscribe by the empire and prosecute by Augustus elector of Saxony in the emperor name be take prisoner by he at the take and raze of Gotha John William serve the king of France in those civil war and die 1573. the last elector of this branch Albert the stout duke of Saxony die in the year 1500. George of leipzig call the popish be duke of Saxony and die in the year 1539. Henry duke of Saxony make governor of Friesland by his father be there in danger to be put to death have not his father come to deliver he he die in the year 1541. the first elector of this branch mauritius make elector by the emperor Charles the 5 be bear 1521 die 1553. Augustus elector marry Anne daughter to the k. of Denmark and die 1586 eight boy and three girl die Christian the elector marry the daughter to the elector of Brandeburg and die 1591. Elizabeth marry to Casimire administrator of the electorship of the palatinate Dorothy marry to the duke of Brunswick and Anne to John Casimire duke of coburg this be the seventeen duke of Saxony and the first elector of Saxony in his family witikynd the three of who be the king of France the prince bear of these three family be duke of upper Saxony for there be also poor duke of low Saxony as one reside at Angria while I live at leipzig Christian the elector of Saxony die 1591. who uncle by the father side mauritius be the first elector of that family for the emperor Charles the five make war against John Fredrick then duke and elector of Saxony and against the Langrave of Hessen as rebel to the empire but indeed with purpose to suppress these chief defender of the reform religion and to bring the free empire of Germany under the spanish yoke he cunning warn mauritius as next heir to seize the land of John Fredrick or otherwise they shall fall to he that take possession of they whereupon mauritius though he profess the reform religion which now have great need of his help yet invade his kinsman land under a fair pretext that he take they lest the emperor shall alienate they to stranger profess that he will restore they to his kinsman when he shall be reconcile to the emperor but such be the power of ambition as in the end he do nothing less but further receive the title of elector take from John Fredrick and his child and confer upon he and his heir male by the emperor the report be that Luther see mauritius bring up in the court of the elector John Fredrick foretell the elector that he shall one day confess he have nourish a serpent in his bosom true it be that mauritius short after restore the cause of religion in like sort deceive the emperor hope by make a league with the king of France but ever since the posterity of mauritius have be jealous of the heir to John Fredrick and have glad take all occasion to suppress they whereupon Augustus succeed his brother mauritius be easy induce by virtue of his office as arch-marshal of the empire to prosecute with fire and sword John Fredrick the elder son of the say John Fredrick who the empire have proscribe at which time he besiege he in Gotha a strong fort which he take and raze to the ground coin dollar in memory of that victory with this inscription Gotha take and the proscribe enemy of the empire therein besiege either take or put to flight in the year 1567. Augustus elector of Saxony coin these and it be not unlikely that Christian son to Augustus especial for fear of this family fortify dresden with so great cost and art howsoever the common people think it rather do because he affect to be choose emperor at the next vacation of this family thus prosecute and depose from the electorship be the two duke of Saxony the one of coburg the other of Wineberg so call of the city wherein they dwell and the duke of coburg have be proscribe by the empire and never restore the duke of Wineberg though more remove kinsman yet be make administrator of the electorship with title of elector as tutor to the son of christian and his two brother who be bring up by he in the court at dresden under their mother the widow to christian be of the house of Brandeburg so as be not the german nature honest and peaceable have not the power of the elector of Brandeburg stand for the pupil it be then think that the wrong family have great mean of revenge this example make i think that it be far more safe to make the next kinsman on the mother side tutor who can have no profit but rather loss by the death of the pupil than the next kinsman by the father side be his heir the duke of coburg and of Wineberg be duke of Saxony by right of blood and of possession therein but the family of the elector have nothing either in upper or low Saxony but only Wittenberg belong to the electorship which be confer upon they by the emperor Charles the five the elector hold his court at dresden in the province of mizzen touch Christian the elector he be repute to be much give to hunt to be prone to anger not to be solicit by petition but at some fit time to affect solitariness and little to be see of the people hardly to admit stranger to his presence at any time much less when he sit at the table to eat contrary to the use of the prince of the house of Austria to have skill in the art of goldsmith and to spare no charge in keep brave horse and no doubt he be so carry away with this last delight as he will take in gift from his very enemy any beautiful thing belong to the stable and while I be at Wittenberg a scholar have speak some word that he love horse better than scholar be send to dresden and there whip about the street beyond measure he be give to large drink in plain term to drunkenness and that of the most strong wine so as this intemperance be think the cause of his untimely death and for these drink game he have certain fair chamber over his stable something distant from his lodging of his court which be appropriate to festival solace as soon as he be make elector he present ordain the new judge for the saxon law vulgar call Schoppenstuel and the consistory in the year 1586 he have a meeting at Lubeck with the king of Denmark and the elector of Brandeburg in the year 1589 at Naumberg he renew the hereditary league between his family and the neighbour prince namely the elector of Brandeburg his elder son (@) Fredrick then call administrator of hall the three brother a William Lodwick and George langrave of Hessen Fredrick William duke of Saxony for one man have often time two name in baptism John duke of Saxony for the title be common to young brother and house of one family with the elder John Casimire tutor to his nephew the elector Palatine John Ernest duke of Saxony christian prince of anhalt Wolfang and Philip duke of Grubenhagen and to knit his friend love more firm to he I have say that he do institute a order of knighthood call the golden fellowship he have for his counsel his officer of court and some doctor of the civil law and among they Crellius doctor of the civil law and the master of his game or hunt who name I have forget be in special grace with he for the prince of Germany admit no physician nor divine to their counsel as have care of the body and soul not of the worldly estate neither do any young prince keep their father counsellor but such as serve they in their father life time Mysen Voitland and part of Thuring province subject to the elector have fertile field frequent city many castle proper to the elector innumerable village and near Friburg rich mine of silver as I have show in the first volume or part where I treat of my journey through these part but howsoever these province excel in these thing yet because they be of no great circuit the elector be not so powerful in the number of vassal as in yearly revenue so as at a public meeting he have no more than some two thousand vassal when the elector of Brandeburg have eight thousand who notwithstanding be far inferior to he in treasure and warlike power he then fortify the city of dresden as a fort and so strong as it be think impregnable by force and all the citizen be bind to have corn and all necessary for the food of their family for six month always lay up in store and in time of that secure peace yet the wall be furnish with artillery as if a army have line before the city and in time of divine service the street be chain and guard of soldier be set in the market place and other part of the city so as nothing can be add in time of the great war the elector have in the city three hundred garrison soldier whereof those that be citizen have three gulden and the old soldier six gulden by the month the captain have the pay for eight and the lieutenant for two horse each horse at twelve gulden by the month the ensign have sixteen gulden by the month four corporal or campe-master have each ten gulden the scout-master ten gulden and the quartermaster eight gulden he give honourable stipend to four great captain who live at home but be bind to serve he when he shall call they his court be no less magnifical wherein he have three duke for his pensioner namely christian prince of anhalt John d. of Winbrooke both young brother and the duke of Desh who dukedom lie upon the confine of Hungary and to each of these he give the pay of twenty horse each horse at twelve gulden the month he have also in his court three earl bastian stick a bohemian Philip count of hollock and one of the count of Mansfeild and to each of they he give the like pay for twelve horse he have also in his court five baron namely two cousin baron of Zantzke in Bohemia the baron of the baron of shinck and the baron of done and to the four first he give like pay for ten and to the last for twelve horse he have in his court twenty young gentleman who carry his lance and helmet vulgar call Spissyongen youth of the spear to who he give yearly coat of velvet and all necessary and to each of they he give a chain of gold to wear he have twelve gentleman of his chamber and to each of they he give a chain of gold his diet in court and like pay for ten horse he have sixteen youth of his chamber and to six of the elder yet not bear arm he give each like pay for two horse and the other ten he maintain with all necessary he have fifty pensioner to wait at his table vulgar call Drnckses and these do ride before he and to each of they he give his diet in the court and like pay for three horse he have twelve sexhsruss and to each of they he give like pay for six horse he have fifty audlepursen so call of a short piece they carry in english we call they calbiner and to each of they he give the pay of one horse apparel twice in the year and two hundred gouldens yearly stipend these as all other degree have their captain and lieutenant and each three night by turn they do watch at the door of the elector chamber have no diet in court but only the night of their watch both live otherwise and lie in the city he have fifty einspauner with a captain and lieutenant who do ride as scout far before the elector and look to the safety of the way each of which have pay for one horse he have sixteen trumpeter whereof three do ride always with the elector and two drum beat a drum of brass vulgar call kettell Drummern and each ride have sixteen gulden by the month out of which they keep each man his horse and each stay at home have ten gulden monthly stipend and all of they at solemn feast be apparel by the elector he have of his guard one hundred vulgar call trabantoe whereof the gentleman have eight the rest six gulden monthly and the gentleman keep watch at the door of the elector chamber carry holbeard and the rest keep watch at the gate of the court arm with musket and yearly they be apparel he have three chaplain whereof one be always to be at the side of the elector he have sixteen singingman whereof ten be man have each of they 400 dollar stipend and six be boy have some 100 dollar for maintenance he have 18 musician of diverse nation whereof each have some 140 dollar yearly stipend he have two tumbler or vaulter one a english man the other a italian with the like or somewhat great stipend he have eight french and two dutch to run by his stirrup or the side of his coach whereof each have some 100 dollar stipend and apparel beside extraordinary gift the duke stable may not be omit be more magnifical than any I do ever see in the world whereof I have at large speak in the first part write of my journey through dresden for therein I do see one hundred thirty six foreign horse of the brave race beside two hundred horse keep in other stable for draw of coach and like use and in this chief stable a boy and a man be keep to attend each horse the man have for diet thirty grosh weekly the boy twenty four that be a Doller and the man for yearly wage have also sixteen dollar beside apparel twice in the year and boot both to man and boy it can be express at least this be not the fit place to write how sumptuous and curious all thing be prepare for the horse and their keepet a gentleman of special account be overseet of this stable and have a great stipend for his care thereof he have eight Leibknechten that be servant for the body who do lead the horse for the elector saddle whereof each have the monthly pay for two horse and three hundred gulden yearly stipend he have four rider whereof each have two hundred dollar yearly stipend and apparel one chief and two inferior horseleech and smith four armourer to polish the arm for tilt three sadler two cutler to polish the sword two feathermaker and two porter of the stable have each of they one hundred gulden yearly stipend and apparel twice in the year beside the elector Christian have a kingly armoury or arsonall for artillery and munition of war which they say have furniture for a army of eighty thousand man oversee by a captain or master of the ordinance his lieutenant and three captain of the watch who have no small stipend beside fifty gunner who have each of they six gulden by the month with yearly apparel but when I be at dresden this armoury be much unfurnished by aid new send into France to king Henry the four at the instance of his ambassador the earl of Tarine these aid though send with the consent of the foresay prince confederate yet be levy as at the charge of the king of France and as voluntary man because the prince be bind upon pain to lose their fee and by the covenant of the peace give to the confession of augsburg not to undertake any waire without the emperor knowledge which bond be often break the prince of Germany administer all as absolute prince only with consent of their confederate but I pass over this and return to the matter in hand the foresay so many and so great stipend be most ready pay without delay out of the exchequer call the silver chamber monthly or yearly as they do grow due and all the pensioner aforesaid do keep the horse in the city for which they have pay to which if you add the 136 horse of the chief stable and the 200 keep by the d. in other stable you shall find that dresden be never without a 1000 horse of service for any sudden event and the number be not less of the horse which the elector keep in his castle not far from the city so as he have ever as it be in a moment ready 2000 horse for all occasion this christian elector of Saxony be say to impose most heavy exaction upon his subject no less than the italian prince who place all their confidence in their treasure none at all in the love of their subject or then the netherlander who for fear to become slave to the Spaniard bear untollerable exaction the country people about dresden cry that they be no less oppress than the jew in Egypt be daily force to labour at their own charge in fortify the city and many complain that the red dear wild boar and like beast destroy their field for I say that the duke be much delight in hunt which be also forbid to all even the best gentleman no man dare so much as to drive the beast out of their pasture and corn he that set a dog on they be subject to great penalty and he that kill one of they be guilty of death but nothing do more cause the duke to be malign then that he have leave the position of Luther in religion and careful endeavour to establish those of Calvin as shall be show in due place his subject be wont to pay for several good as a sheep a cow and the like a yearly tribute but of late it have be decree by the 3 state that after the value of good each man for 60 grosh shall pay two fenning yearly I mean as well movable good namely ware and ready money as house land and all unmoveable good and that not according to the yearly value but yearly according to the value at which they be or may be buy or sold. neither can any man dissemble his wealth since that deceit will appear at least upon the last Will and testament and once find use to be punish with repair the loss and a great fine this tribute be at first grant only for 6 year but those end the term be renew and so it continue for ever and this tribute alone be say to yield yearly 600000 gulden but the chief revenue of the elector be by the imposition upon beer which as I have former say that people drink in great excess and they say that this tribute also at first be impose only for certain year but the elector mean nothing less than to ease they of this burden of late there have be a paper set by some merry lad upon the court gate contain these word in the dutch tongue i gutten and der hat auffgehebt dab bearstewer wort gotte berestewer wheren in that be I wish long life may he befall and not one good day therewithal and hell-fier after his life here who first do raise this tax of bear postscript the word of God and the tax of bear last for ever and ever the brewer pay tribute according to the value of the brew not according to the gain they make namely some eight part for one kind of bear some five part for another kind in most place at Wittenberg I observe that for one brew of some 48 bushel of malt worth some 48 gulden the duke treasurer receive 8 gulden this treasurer do four time yearly view the brew vessel and number the student of Wittenberg to prevent any defraud of tribute for howsoever in all these part they drink large yet at Wittenberg in respect of the great number of student and at leipzig for the same cause and in respect of a great fair this tribute grow to a high rate then in other city yet the city Torge though less in circuit then these only exceed these and all other in yield this tribute because the bear thereof be so famous good as it be in great quantity transport to other city of these province where the better sort most common drink it and no other so as that city alone yield one year with another seventeen thousand gold gulden for tribute of bear the same city make yearly seven thousand woollen clothes each cloth thirty two else long and worth some fourteen dollar yet for each cloth they pay only one silver grosh whereby it appear that the tribute of cloth and like commodity be light esteem as of less importance than the transcendent traffic of bear Torge likewise yearly pay to the elector 500 dollar for the fish of a lake near the city which once in 3 year be say to yield 5000 dollar to the city one sole province yet much inhabit and very fertile namely mizzen be say one year with another to yield 1800000 dollar for all tribute and half part thereof only for bear the mine of silver be of great importance which by the law belong to the elector in their province not to the emperor and this elector have many of these mines-name those of Friburg those of Scheneberg those of Anneberg and those of the valley of joachim of all which I have write at large in the geographical description and no doubt this elector be potent in treasure so as how soever he be inferior in dignity to the elector Palatine yet he be most powerful of all the elector among the wall city subject to he not to speak of the town castle and pleasant village leipzig be next to dresden to which it only yield for the fortification and the elector Court leipzig give the law to the upper territory as Wittenburg do to the low and both be adorn by be university but at leipzig the scabines sit judge of great authority for the law of Saxony be in number seven namely three senator of the city and four doctor of the civil Law but Wittenberg have not the right of the sword to execute malefactor which the elector Augustus they say translate to leipzig because the judge obstinate deny he power to pardon malefactor or to moderate the Law so as when any man be capital accuse at Wittenberg the cause be first refer to the scabines at leipzig who find he guilty give power to the senator of Wittenberg to pronounce sentence and do execution Wittenberg be no fair city but a famous university and at this time have a great many of student and it be not subject to the duke as inheritance from his progenitor but as he be elector for to the electorship it proper belong beside the great tribute it pale for bear it also yield yearly to the duke 1500 gold gulden for the bridge build over the ell here as in all other place lime and brick be sell in the duke name and to his use as well leipzig as Wittenberg in difficult case ask counsel for the civil law of their own and if need be of foreign university where the doctor of the civil law in the name of the faculty write down their judgement in the case propound these doctor be also advocate whereof there be twenty two at this time at leipzig and because this profession be much esteem the german willing apply themselves to the study thereof the count Palatine of the Rhine by old institution be chief among the temporal the count Palatine of the Rhine elector and the duke of Bavaria elector and be of the same family of which the duke of Bavaria descend the pedigree of they both be derive from the emperor Charles the great Otho the elder brother Palatine of Wirtelbach upon the proscription of the duke of Bavaria have that dukedom confer on he in fee by the emperor in the year 1180. from his young brother descend the count of psalm now live but from the say Otho the elder brother be descend both the palatine elector and the duke of Bavaria now live Lodowick duke of Bavaria who die in the year 1231 receive the palatinate of the Rhine in fee from the emperor Frederick the second Otho the four succeed he in the dukedom of Bavaria and the palatinate of the Rhine and be the first elector of this family who die in the year 1253. his son Lodowick the severe elector Palatine and duke of Bavaria make rodolphus of Habsburg emperor who be the first emperor of the house of Austria he marry this emperor daughter and die in the year 1294 leave two son who divide the inheritance as follow from this descend the count palatine and elector the elder brother be count Palatine of the Rhine and elector who die 1319. rupert Palatine of the Rhine elector found the university in Heidelberg in the year 1346. rupert elector and emperor die in the year 1410. Lodowick count Palatine and elector the elector Fredrick the second descend from he free Vienna from the siege of the turk and die 1556. Otho enrich his nephew die 1559 without heir male and so the electorship fall to the duke of Zweybruck Stephen duke of Zweybruck Fredrick Palatine John the first John the second Fredrick the three succeed Otho enrich in the electorship and die 1576. Lodowick the four elector Palatine marry the daughter of the Langrave of Hessen and die 1583. Fredrick the four elector than pupil to John Casimire his uncle the first house of the palatine and elector in two branch Christian his sole sister John casunire be tutor to his nephew and elector in his nonage and marry Elizabeth sister to christian duke of Saxony and die 1592. Dorothea his sole daughter Elizabeth marry to John Fredrick duke of Saxony call of coburg Susan Dorothy marry to John William duke of Saxony call of Wineberg Anna Maria marry to Philip the 2 langrave of marry to the count of nassawe Richard d. of hunneseruck live when I write this the 2 brach of the first house Lodowick the black Alexander Lodowick wolfgang d. of Sweybruck marry the daughter to the Langrave of Hessen and die in the french war 1569. Philip Lodowick marry the daughter to the duke of julice of three daughter one marry to Fredrick William duke of Saxony wolfgang bear 1578. Augustus bear 1582. John Fredrick bear 1587. John marry to another daughter of the duke of julce two son and two daughter the second house of the countess palatine in four branch then live the last branch be multiply from Philip Lodowick be then child Fredrick marry the daughter of the duke of Lignic two twin bear 1591. Otho enrich marry the daughter to the duke of Wirterberg he have both son and daughter eight sister partly dead partly live then rupert of rupert be George bear of the daughter to Gustanus king of Swetia who then be live he have 3 son and diverse daughter a five branch of the second house of the countess palatine thus of the elder son to Lodowick the severe descend two house in many branch of the countess palatine whereof the chief and first have the electorship and of Lodowick the emperor the second son to Lodowick the severe descend the duke of Bavaria as follow Lodowick the young brother be d. of Bavaria and be make emperor who die 1347. the duke of Bavaria Lodowick the emperor have two son Stephen duke of Bavaria who die 1392. Fredrick duke of Bavaria die 1404. George the rich found the university of Ingolstat and build the college of saint George and die 1503. elizabeth his daughter be marry to rupert count Palatine and to rupert George by his last will give the dukedom of Bavaria but the emperor Maximivan will not confirm this gift as injurious to the next heir in this pedigree whence rise the war of Bavaria John of Monach duke of Bavaria die 1397. Albert the three refuse to be choose king of Bohemia and die 1460. Albert the four bring the channon of 2 monastery to Monach and that of his own authority for which he hardly escape the proscription of the empire and to he the emperor adjudge the inheritance of George against the palatine who have marry the daughter of George and be by his last will make his heir he die 1508. Albert the 5 build 3 college for the jesuit he marry Anna daughter to the emperor Ferdinand and die in the year 1579. william bear 1548 marry Rinata daughter to Francis duke of Lorraine in the year 1568. Maximilian d. of Bavaria Philip bishop of Ratisbona Ferdinand a of Colen and cannon of Trier one sister Maximiliana Ferdinand bear in the year 1550. ernestus bishop of liege after archbishop and elector of colon bear in the year 1554. Maria Maximilian bear 1552. maria bear 1553 and marry to Charles archduke of Austria 1572. William make war against the duke of Wirteberg and die in the year 1577. Sibyl marry to Lodowick the four elector Palatine die in the year 1511. Sabina marry to Vlrich duke of Wirteberg die in the year 1564. sidonia marry to philibert marquis of baden William count of Holland by right of his wife of he descend the count of Holland from this Lodowick descend the duke of Bavaria it be covenant and stand agree between the house of the elector pallatines and the house of the duke of Bavaria that upon want of heir male one of they shall succeed the other and when the daughter to the duke of Bavaria in the time of the emperor Maximilian oppose herself to this contract she be force to yield to it by the emperor I return to the elector palatine the foresay Frederick the four Palatine and elector be under age have duke John Casimire his father brother for his tutor the elector Palatine of the Rhine who at his brother death besiege the city of Colen in the name of the bishop who they have drive out for be marry this Casimire in his brother life-time have a noble inheritance beyond the Rhine to he and his heir and then hear of his brother death haste to Heidelberg where he bring the people to obedience who will not have he administrator because he profess the reform religion after Calvin doctrine not after that of Luther and he present send back the emperor ambassador who be come thither about that controversy refuse to yield his right in the tutorage of his nephew which he defend in the imperial chamber at spire after he bring up his nephew wise and religious appoint he his diet apart with his teacher and the steward of his court to who table one professor of the university be daily invite who have charge to propound a question to the prince out of the history and controversy of religion and the prince do not present make answer except it be in a common subject but ask time to consider of it and consult apart with his teacher after some half hover return to give his answer thus by daily practice the chief accident of history and controversy of religion be make familiar to he the city Heidelberg sometime hold in fee from the bishop of Wormz be in time beautify with building and a university and become the seat of the elector the say elector Fredrick the four be a pupil be after the foresay manner bring up in the reform religion according to the doctrine of Caluine but in the mean time Richard the duke of hunnesruck his next heir if he shall die without issue male do obstinate follow the reform doctrine of Luther and so do the rest of his kinsman the duke of Zweybruck their town be so call of the two bridge except the second brother of they who consent in religion with the elector this elector Frederick the four marry the daughter to the prince of Orange by his wife of the french family Chastillion his court be not great nor any way comparable to that of the elector of Sexony for he have scarce thirty gentleman to attend he and to they he give no more than some twenty five gulden for stipend which they spend upon their servant that attend they and keep their horse and he have no more than eight yeoman for the guard of his body wine be spare draw and all expense make with great frugality but the fame of this elector wisdom and affability make he much esteem of stranger and while he converse with his citizen often come to the public place for exercise of the piece and crossbow and be easy of access yet carry himself like a grave and noble prince he become dear to his subject of who he exact moderate tribute for their land house money and good and some two small fenning for each moss or measure of wine in five place upon the Rhine he exact imposition or tax which one year with another yield some twelve or sixteen thousand french crown and they say that he receive yearly some fifty or sixty thousand crown by the silver mine of Anneberg beside extraordinary subsidy which his subject use to grant he upon occasion of war or like necessity of the commonwealth and I remember when the citizen of strasburg his neighbour make war with the brother of the duke of Lorraine about their bishopric so as the palatine be force to levy soldier for defence of his people from the rapine of both army that he impose a extraordinary subsidy upon his subject of a quarter of a Doller for each hundred dollar any man possess in movable or unmoveable good the margrave or marquis of Brandeburg be by the first institution the last of the the margrave of Brandeburg elector elector but more powerful than any of they in the number of vassal and his territory be much large than those of the elector of Saxony but his revenue be nothing so great he hold his court at berlin some twelve german mile distant from Wittenberg in Saxony his pedigree be derive from Peter a roman patrician of the ancient blood of the camill who banish by pope Gregory the seven have land give he in Suevia by the emperor Henry the four and build the castle of Zagaroll and about the year 1120 have a son call burchard burchard count of zoller his castle so call Frederick the first race of the burgrave of nurnberg be extinct be invest 1273 of that dignity by the emperor who sister be mother to Frederick Frederick the second Burgrave of nurnberg die in the year 1330. Frederick Burgrave of nurnberg have the marquisate of Brandeburg confer on he by the emperor Sigismond and also the electorship in the year 1427 and he sell the castle and burgraveship of nurnberg to the city of nurnberg he die in the year 1440. John marquis of Brandeburg at his father commandment yield the electorship to his second brother and die in the year 1464. Frederick marquis and elector go into Palestine do yield the electorship to his brother Albert and die in the year 1470. Albert marquis and elector call the Achilles of Germany overcome the citizen of nurnberg in eight battle and in the nine be overcome promise they peace he first make league with the elector of Saxony and the langrave of Hessen and die sudden in a bath in the year 1486. by his first wife daughter to the marquis of baden he have John marquis and elector common call the Cicero of Germany he die 1499. and have two sister by his mother joachim the first elector found the university at Franckfort upon Viadrus in the year 1506 he marry Elizabeth daughter to the king of Denmark and die 1535. joachim the second elector for kill a Turk have a military girdle of Charles the five to who part he be firm and obtain life for the captive elector of Saxony he die 1571. by Magdalen daugh. to geo. the beard d. of Saxony johannes georgius the elector then live bear in the year 1525. by his first wife Sophia as some say daughter to the count of Barba he have joachim Frederick bear 1546 heir to the electorship at this time administrator of the Archbishopk. of Holla he marry one of the house of Brandeburg in the year 1570 and if I be not deceive have at this time a second wife the daughter of the duke of Wirteberg John Sigismond bear 1572. Anna Catherina bear 1575. george bear 1577. Augustus bear 1580 cannon of strasburg Albert Fredrick bear 1581. joachim a twin bear 1582. Ernest a twin bear 1582. Christian wilhelm bear 1588. by Sabina daughter to George marquis of Brandeburg marry 1547 and die 1574. he have three daughter Ermund marry to John Frederick d. of Pomern Anna Maria marry to the elder brother d. of Pomern and Sophia marry to christian elector of Saxony 1582. by Elionora daughter to the prince of anhalt marry 1577 at the fifty three year of his age and fourteen of her age he have three son christian and joachim Ernest and a three who name I know not and in the year 1592. when he be 67 year old he have a daughter beside two other daughter former beget Barbara marry to the d. of Bregan in Stlesia Elizabetha magdalena marry to Otho d. of Luneburg marry to julius duke of Brunswick Sophia marry to the baron of Rosenburg viceroy in Bohemia 1564. John league with the protestant yet serve the emperor at his brother persuasion but after join with mauritius elector of Saxony against the emperor he die 1570. five sister Anne marry to the duke of Meckelburg elizabeth to the duke of Brunswick margaret to the duke of Pomern Elizabeth to George marquis of Brandeburg and Catherine to be Anna marry to the king of Denmark Frederick the first die 1521. Ursula marry to the duke of pomerania another Ursula to the duke of Meckelburg Albert archbishop and elector of Mentz and cardinal make the war of religion which Lodowick elector Palatine appease he die 1545. by his second wife Anne dauphin to frederick the second elector of Saxony he have Frederick the five marquis of Brandeburg in franconia and Voytland he marry the daughter of Casimire king of Poland and die in the year 1536. he have five sister by his mother three marry two nun Casimire marry the daughter to the d. of Banaria he die 1577. Albert call the Alcibiades of Germany most warlike be proscribe by the empire and die in banishment in the year 1557. marry marry to Frederick elector Palatine die 1567. George give the confession of religion at augsburg geo. fred. recover Prustia from the k. of Poland and take it in fee 1578. he marry Elizabeth of Brandeburg 1558 and Sophia daughter to the d. of Brunswick 1579. he have five sister William bishop of Regenspurg die 1563. Albert Mr. of the teutonike order be overcome by the king of Poland be make d. of Prussia the order be extinguish and found the university at konigsberg he die 1568. by the daughter of the duke of Brunswick he have Albert Frederick bear 1553 say to be frantic so as George Frederick his uncle son govern the dukedom of Prussia he be at this time live by the daughter of the d. of julec he have some daughter how the tentonike order be extinguish and of the succession in Prussia be former speak in this chapter and in the geographical description of Germany by Dorothy queen of Denmark he have Anna Sophia marry to the duke of Meckelburg and she die 1591. beside male and female die young five sister all marry the first marquis and first elector of this family Conrade divide the principality with his brother govern at nurnberg and four of his posterity be great commander of the order of the teutonike knight Eitel Frederick count of zoller hitherto I have speak of the temporal elector the fecond among the spiritual elector be the archbishop of Mentz which seat when I pass through Germany the spiritual elector be possess by wolfgang of the noble family of Dalberg and all his kinsman dwell here Heidelberg be of the reform religion after the doctrine of Luther and therefore less esteem he who notwithstanding be think no enemy to the reform religion but rather willing to permit it do he not fear the opposition of the chapter for Gebhard truchsesse archbishop of Colen and elector have late be depose and another place in that seat because he marry Agnes countess of Mansfield with who at that time he live be make a cannon at strasburg for that city have abolish the roman religion yet keep the place of cannon without any bond of superstition and use to bestow they only upon prince and gentleman of the reform religion and in this city he then live a quiet life after he have in vain try by force of arm to regain that archbishopric the three spiritual elector but first by institution be the archbishop of Trier a city seat beyond the Rhine upon the confine of France which seat when I pass through Germany be possess by John if I mistake not his name of the noble family of schonburg and whereas the other elector dwell in the city whereof they be name for the most part his continual abode be at the castle Erbrotsteine seat near the Rhine some half day journey from Trier all these arch-bishop have not only spiritual but also temporal power in all their territory the family of the langrave of Hessen be derive from Lambert count of Hannow the langrave of Hessen who die in the year 1015. of his first branch come the margrave of berg and also the baron of Grimberg of the second branch come the langrave of Hessen who progenitor Lodowick call the gentle be choose emperor refuse that burden and die in the year 1458. Philip unite to the protestant prince in the league of Smalcald and join his force with the elector of Saxony against the emperor Charles the five be persuade by his friend when the elector army be break to yield himself to the emperor by who he be keep prisoner for a time contrary to promise he found the university at Marpurg and die in the year 1567. the say Philip marry the daughter to the duke of Meckelburg and by she have the follow issue william bear 1532 hold his court at Cassiles have half the inheritance he deliver his father out of prison and marry Sabina daughter to the d. of Wirteberg and die not long before I pass through Germany mauritius a most noble young prince Langrave of Cassiles upon his father late death Anna Maria marry to Lodowick count of nassawe 1589. then asleep Virgin Sophia then a Virgin Lodowick bear 1537 hold his court at Marpurg and have a four part of his father inheritance he first marry heduige daughter to the d. of Wirteberg and after Mary of Mansfield both barren and at this time he live but without any child Agnes marry to mauritius elector of Saxony 1541 and after to John Frederick d. of Saxony george bear 1547 hold his court at Dormstat and have a four part of his father inheritance and marry Magdelen daughter to Bernard count of Lipp and she be dead but he then live Lodowick old his father yet live Philip John George two sister christian and Elizabeth Anna marry to the duke of Zweybruck die 1581. Barbara marry to George earl of Mompelgard Elizabeth marry to Lodowick the four elector Palatine christina marry to adolphus of the royal blood of Denmark his sister Elizabeth be marry to John duke of Saxony the foresay William langrave of Hessen of his chief city call the Langrave of Cassiles have in division with his brother half his father inheritance the other half be divide between his two brother and since that time I hear that his brother Lodowick of Marpurg be dead without issue and that his four part of this inheritance be return to mauritius elder son to William yet because mauritius be addict to the reform religion after the doctrine of Calvin which he and his courtier with many subject profess how soever he have not yet make any general alteration whereas his uncle Lodowick persist in the doctrine of Luther I remember the common speech in the land of Hessen that Lodowick have threaten his nephew mauritius to disinherit he and give his land to the child of his brother George of Dormstatt if he make any general alteration in religion I have former say that the dignity of the empire decay many principality be give in fee and the lord thereof become absolute prince at that time many great city be immediate subject to the empire whereof many be at sundry time after engage for money to the say prince at last the power of the empire be more fall by many civil war raise by the pope to confirm their usurp power over the emperor these city with money buy their liberty partly of the emperor partly of the say prince from which time these city be call imperial and have freedom with absolute power become daily more and more beautify with building and strong by fortification yet some city still subject to diverse prince yield not to they in beauty and strength as dresden and leipzig subject to the elector of Saxony Monach and Ingolstat subject to the duke of Bavaria and Breslaw the chief city of silesia a province join to the kingdom of Bohemia the emperor at his election swear that he will maintain these city in their freedom and not suffer they to be draw back to the subjection of the empire or the say prince also I have former speak of the many and just suspicion between the emperor the prince and these free city which it be needless to repeat of old the great city of the empire be ninety six in number but many of they have since be alienate to the prince of netherlands or unite by league to the canton of Sweitzerland so at this day there remain only sixty free city of the empire of the commonwealth of these city it shall suffice in general to have say that the government be very moderate and equal the patritian live upon their revenue as gentleman the plebeian intend traffic and shopkeeping and be they never so rich never so wise can never become patritian but still keep their own rank as all other order do and the artisan so they keep the law which bind the high as well as they be secure from the injury of any great man in civil cause they judge not after strict law but according to equity and without delay but more easy to conjecture of all in general it will not be amiss particular to observe the government of some few and because nurnberg be one of the chief I will begin with it the margrave the city of nurnberg of Brandeburg be of old burgrave of nurnberg till Frederick the four about the year 1414 sell that his right and the castle of nurnberg to the citizen thereof Albert his son call the Achilles of Germany for some duty deny to he make war upon the city draw seventeen prince to take his part as the other free city assist nurnberg at this day the margrave of Anspath be of that family call himself Burgrave of nurnberg but have only the bare title without any command in the city yet because his land lie on some side under the very wall thereof the citizen repute he a dangerous neighbour the common report be that this margrave have late sell to the city a great wood grow very near the wall thereof and that short after he be at variance with they as if he have sell only the wood and not the soil so as if vulgar speech may be believe they be force again to buy the ground and yet he have not renounce his right of hunt therein which he challenge proper to himself give i leave to digress so much from my purpose as to say that the neighbourhood of this margrave be no less suspect by the free city Wasenburg not far distant where upon a mountain in his own ground hang over the city he have build a strong castle and because all the street of that little city lie open to it the citizen when first he begin to build complain to the emperor of that wrong and obtain letter to command the margrave to build no further but he not only disobey those letter but build the same with more speed and strength now I return to nurnberg the commonwealth whereof be aristocratical the great counsel have no set number but common consist of some three hundred person whereof many be patrician live honourable upon their rent as gentleman other be merchant and some few artisan of the best and rich workman the senate refer to this counsel the imposition of tribute and the decree of peace and war which subject of counsel be rare this counsel be seldom call together but the authority of they be so great as the seal of any two of they set to any last testament serve in steed of seven witness require by the civil Law out of this great counsel the new senate be yearly choose and when the time of election be at hand this great counsel name a consul and a scabine of the gentleman call ancient or out of the chief of the next order and in like sort the old senate of the year past name three of the ancient gentleman these five be call the elector of the new senate and as soon as they be choose all magistracy cease then these elector be swear be shut up into a chamber whence they come not forth till they have choose twenty six consul and scabine of each thirteen then they choose the rest of the new senate and assoon as they be choose they name among themselves those that be call ancient which be common the same man except some be put in the place of they that be dead for it be a disgrace to be put from that dignity this election be make in one day and the senate consist of forty person whereof thirty four be patrician or gentleman and so the government be especial in the hand of the gentleman as a thing whereof they hold the common people to be uncapable of these gentleman be (@) the seven man and the senate of the ancient as also the choose captain and treasurer to be a doctor of the civil law make a gentleman or any other to be uncapable of a senator place but when in dificult case they need the advice of doctor they send two senator to consult with they who relate their judgement to the senate for this cause and because all judgement be according to equity not after the strict law there be fowe doctor in that city neither have they many advocate the senate give stipend onlyto four who plead all cause yet the city entertain some doctor to advise they at I former say and to assist they in judgement exhibit the cause in writing as also to be ambassador to the say 34 gentleman 8 plebeian be add which make the say senate and these plebeian have free voice but be remove from secret counsel and have liberty to be absent seldom meet with the senate except they be call so as the common people have little or no authority and be keep under in so much as meeting except funeral and like ceremony and walk by night be forbid yet they have their privilege inviolable keep and live in great liberty under a most equal government of these gentleman govern the city they have as I have hear twenty eight honourable family or there about and of the say thirty four gentleman of the senate eight be call the ancient who like old soldier be free from service the other twenty six diligent attend the public affair with capital and civil judgement and one of they be choose to entertain passenger worthy of honour by present wine to they in name of the senate and also to call the senate together to propound the cause upon which they deliberate to ask their voice and to do many like duty these twenty six gentleman be divide into thirteen consul and thirteen scabine and these scabines judge capital cause first examine by the whole senate as the consul judge civil cause and they so divide the year between they as each of they for a month be consul or scabine out of they be choose seven man who have the great authority and determine all secret of state and to they the treasurer make account and howsoever two of one family may be senator yet two of one family can be of these seven man three of these seven be choose captain who have the keep of the armoury and the key of the gate and upon any tumult all fly to they and yield they obedience two of these captain be treasurer where of the chief have the first place in all assembly to these treasurer one of the plebeian be add to oversee the expense of the treasure and two of the best sort of the plebeian be clerk of the exchequer but only the two chief treasurer disburse and lay up all money they have in all public counsel two chauncellor whereof one always attend the counsel of seven man and these chauncellor write the decree of counsel receive and read write and send all letter be as secretary and they have six clerk to write under they all the senator have their several stipend out of the common treasure each of the seven man have yearly five hundred gulden beside gainful office as the keep of the seal and each treasurer have eight hundred gulden and each chancellor two hundred gulden yearly in judgement they do not much use the plead of proctor or advocate but use to judge summary upon oath or to appoint arbiter to compound controversy but among the court of judgement one be of five man from who there be no appeal yet they refer the great cause to the senate the second court be of eight man and have two tribunal where the cause of citizen be determine which exceed not the value of thirty two crown and these two tribunal in great cause be unite and have three or four doctor appoint by the senate to advise they for only the scabines judge and from these tribunal appeal be grant to the senate if the cause exceed the value of five hundred crown these choose a judge to see their decree put in execution and to see capital offender execute they appoint a judge for the village and territory subject to the city for who assistance the senate choose some out of the great counsel these weekly give the law to the village and country people and by the exercise of this office the judge be enable for the office of Scabines also they choose a judge to have care of the fair and market who set the price of bread flesh and all thing there sell and he have four senator to assist he in weekly inquire after the work of artificer that they sell no unperfect work nor use any fraud of the senator three be choose supreme tutor for pupil and widow who divide inheritance see that all testament be perform and appoint new tutor in case the old be dead suspect or absent these supreme tutor provide that the money of pupil be put forth to use and that the profit return to the pupil they receive the account of the tutor and provide that the pupil be religious and honest bring up one senator be set over each church monastery and alm house to see the revenue well administer and to promote the cause thereunto belong five governor be set over the territory without the wall among which the chancellor have yearly one hundred crown each of the rest twenty five crown for stipend in time of war they choose seven senator who take upon they the care to provide all necessary for the same I understand there that not long before they have number in the city twenty two thousand artificer servant and people of inferior rank and that the last subsidy impose in time of war be one gold Gulden in the hundred of every man movable and unmovable good and one gold Gulden by the pole for all such as have neither inheritance nor art to live upon augsburg be one of the imperial city vulgar Reichs statt and in the year Angsburg 1364. the senate consist of two patrician consul and of ten merchant and seven artisan with power of tribune all yearly choose the emperor Charles the 4 give the city new privillge and confirm the old because the citizen swear obedience to his son and the emperor Sigismond confirm and increase the same when the emperor Charles the five hold a parliament in this city as many parliament have be hold there the old honour be restore to the patritian and the plebeian tribune be take away two advocate be set in their room two gentleman consul at this day govern the city with six judge for criminal cause whereof three be gentleman two citizen one plebeian these be choose by the great senate consist of those three order but in cause of religion the city be subject to the jurisdiction of the bishop of till this city have many noble and rich merchant whereof many have privilege of baron and some of earl and among they the chief family be of the Fuggari famous know be at this time both boy and man some thirty in number and the chief of they be mark of the fug who have marry the daughter to the earl of Schwartzenburg and be much delight in the gather of antiquity with much courtesy use to show the same to such passenger as take pleasure therein three cousin of this family have great and large but disperse scatter possession beside that they be rich in treasure for supply whereof the emperor Charles the five and his son Philip king of Spain often make use of they engage to they the imposition and custom of haven for ready money and give they great privilege of traffic in which kind the say king of Spain so oblige they to he as the heart be always where the treasure be he make they no less obsequious to he then subject so difficult a thing be it for covetous merchant to preserve their liberty great jealousy be between this city and the duke of Bavaria who territory extend to the very wall of the city and I remember at my last passage through augsburg this duke attempt to stop the course of water from the city whereupon the citizen send out soldier to beat back the duke workman but the controversy be soon after appease and come not to blow they perpetual even in time of peace keep some five hundred soldier in the city who dwell in a street by themselves and the city be seat upon the mouth of the Alps lead into Italy and the citizen be diligent in traffic it can be that it shall not abound in riches augsburg in the foresay parliament hold there after charles the five have overcome the protestant prince be say to have buy their peace of the emperor with 3000 gold gulden I know not for what cause they be severe towards stranger but I observe that they have a law forbid stranger to dwell in the city allow they only a short time of abode and during the same curious observe what business they have strasburg be also a free city of the empire and as the rest govern by a senate strasburg yearly choose for howsoever it be one of the city league with the canton of Sweitzerland yet it be still number among the free imperial city and it be stately build and rich in treasure for so it must needs be since the ordinary tribute and tax be so great as I have hear the citizen profess that they yearly pay one dolor in a thousand for the value of their movable and also unmoveable good wherein the full value of land not the yearly rent be reckon and that if any fraud be detect in the last testament or otherwise the heir or the party offend if he live be deep fine for the same while I pass through the city they have begin a war with the duke of Lorraine about the choice of their bishop which war they have unprovident denounce before they have levy soldier or make provision to make it so as their territory be expose to many oppression before they can gather troop to defend they and offend the enemy and it be vulgar report that they can deliberate of nothing in counsel so secret as it be not present make know to the enemy the imperial city Franckfort be famous for the two yearly mart one at Midlent Franckfort the other at the midst of September at which time all neighbour prince keep horseman to guard the merchant pass that way to which horseman I remember that each passenger give 6 creitzer either of duty or in courtesy for his person also this city be famous for another privilege contain in the law of the golden bulla namely that all emperor must be choose there and in case two emperor be choose the same law define that if one of they shall besiege the city and there expect his enemy half a month and if in that time he come not to break the siege than it shall be free for the city to receive the first as have the victory for of old custom the new choose emperor keep their coronation feast in this city with great magnificence which be last keep as they say by Maximilian the second at which time among other solemnity they roast a ox in the midst of the field for the people and when the marshal of the court have cut a piece as for the emperor the rest of the ox be in a moment rend in piece by the common people I must make at least some mention of the city lie upon the sea of Germany towards the north whereof most be not only call free because they be imperial city but by the same name though in diverse signification be call Hans steten that be free city in respect of the privilege of traffic grant to they of old in the neighbour country among these Lubeck be the chief of the neighbour city join in league for common defence whither the senator of all the other city come once in the year to Lubeck consult of public affair the territory of the city reach not above a german mile but after some few mile distance there be a certain town which belong to Lubeck and hamburg by common right be engage to they for money by the duke of lower Saxony of who they after buy the rest of his inheritance this town for six year space be wont to be keep by those of Lubeck appoint the governor and receive the rent which time end those of hamburg be wont to have it in like sort for six year and so by turn they be wont to enjoy it Lubeck of old have a duke till it be subject to the empire by the emperor Frederick the first after who death it become subject to their duke again and after five year become subject to the dane but by the help of Frederick the second it free itself from the dane in the year 1226 and after by favour of the emperor obtain freedom and absolute power both Lubeck and hamburg be say of old to have acknowledge the king of Denmark but at last expel the king proctor they become free and submit themselves to the defence of the empire for which cause to this day they wary observe the action of the king of Denmark and live in fear and suspicion of their attempt and howsoever they have freedom and absolute power yet they be careful to have the favour of the king of Denmark because they have power to hinder their traffic in the baltike sea yet sometime league with the neighbour city which in the common cause of freedom be easy draw to give mutual aid they have make war against the king of Denmark with good success Lubeck be commend for just government not to speak of their hospitality very fair and uniform building and the very pleasant seat of the town it be govern by the civil law and by statute make by the senate as also some make by the consent of the confederate city no appeal to university or to the chamber of the empire be admit except the cause be above the value of five hundred dollar they late make sumptuary law restrain the number of guest and dish in feast with penalty according to the excess the citizen yearly choose twenty new senator and this (@) choose of their number four consul with a judge skilful in the civil law these magistrate define all civil and criminal cause the whole senate first examine they and judgement be give by common consent with the door shut but when any capital judgement be to be execute at the day appoint to the malefactor and the very hour he be to die the hangman pronounce the sentence in the market place the consul take the high place by turn one in the morning the other in the afternoon at which time they also by turn hear ambassador and receive complaint many office be divide among the senator two gather the rent other have care of the wine which be sell in a public house to public use no private man be allow to make that gain other oversee the building that they be uniform and strong build and free from danger of fire and likewise the fortification of the city four sergeant attire in red gown attend the senate and summon man to appear beside twelve inferior sergeant and they neither carry sword nor any mace before the magistrate but follow they in the street like servant they do not imprison any debtor or light offender but only summon such to appear before the magistrate and declare to they the fine impose for not appear but they apprehend capital offender and prevent their escape by flight it be not lawful for a creditor to put his debtor in prison but after a set time and with caution prescribe in the law of Saxony wherein notwithstanding they of Lubeck so favour stranger as they only have right in this kind with expedition and have a proper tribunal or seat of judgement for themselves only yet herein they seem not favourable to stranger in that they permit they not to dwell in the city otherwise they do as the common use be to keep all commodity in the hand of citizen not to be sell to stranger but by a citizen especial since without the help of stranger they have their own ship to bring in and carry out all commodity hamburg be in like sort govern but I can so much commend they for hospitality hamburg be rude to all stranger and malicious to englishman above other for no other cause then for that our merchant leave that city seat themselves at Stoade so as it be not safe for any stranger much less for a Englishman to walk abroad after dinner when the common people be general heat with drink and the very justice be herein common tax not that they punish whoredom which no good man will disallow but that they permit whore in great multitude and yet favour the knavery of the sergeant who combine with the whore entrap man in their house so as not only the whore and sergeant make profit thereby but the very magistrate be just suspect to approve this course for their own gain Brunswick a imperial city worthy to be number among the chief and so call as the village of bruno be not far distant from hamburg and seat in the centre Brunswick of Saxony be of old as they say the metropolitan city thereof it consist of five city gather into one whereof each have his several privilege and they be thus seat Alstatt be the part on the west side newstatt on the north side imsacke the part towards the east Imhagen and altweg build first of all the rest be the part towards the south and howsoever all these have each their several senator and privilege yet all of they joint make the city of Brunswick live under one common law and government the senator of each by yearly course govern the whole body of that commonwealth for howsoever ten consul be yearly choose two of each city yet to the two consul of that city which by course be to govern for the year the other eight as inferior and much more all the senator of the five city yield for the time great reverence in the senate and all meeting and great obedience in all thing command one senate house be common to all the five city yet each of they have also a private senate-house the form of the public government be democratical or popular they live in such fear of the duke of Brunswick leave he shall take away their liberty as they have not only fortify the town very strong against assault or siege but also willing employ their citizen in foreign war as hire soldier insomuch as no man be make free who have not first serve one or two year in the war the duke of Brunswick and of Luneburg derive their pedigree from one root namely the duke of Brunswick and of Luneburg from the old family of the duke of Bavaria for Henry call the lion d. of Bavaria who be duke and elector of Saxony also command a most ample territory be proscribe by the emperor and for a time live as a banish man in England the dukedom of Bavaria be by the emperor give in fee to the palatine of the Rhine and so pass to a new family this Henry the lion die in Brunswick about the year 1195. his elder son Otho the four be choose emperor be overcome by his competitor and be again raise to that dignity be put from it by Fredrick the second and die 1218. Otho his brother son be force to yield the dukedom of Saxony to the elector of Saxony Albert the second and the emperor Frederick the second cast his garrison out of Brunswick with the help of the citizen and make Brunswick a free imperial city but at last the emperor upon the submission of Otho forgive he and create he duke of Brunswick and Luneburg this Otho die in the year 1252 from who this house be subdivide into the follow family from Henry the wonderful descend the lord of ember and Grobenbagen from Albert the fat die in the year 1318 descend the duke of Brunswick and of Luneburg as follow Torquatus heir of both dukedom who garrison the citizen of Luneburg cast out of the castle Calkberg leave three son Frederick choose emperor against Wenceslaus kill by treachetie in the year 1400. Bernard after the kill of his brother yield the d. of Brunswick to his nephew William and retain the d. of Luneburg die 1434. from Bernard descend Otho who exhibit the reform confession at Augtburg and die 1549. Otho lord of Harburg have to his first wife the daughter to the earl of Schwartzenburg and with the second wife daughter to the earl of Emden he then live when I pass though Germany Otho have by his first wife two son Otho enrich bear 1555 and John Frederick bear 1557 and one daughter Elizabeth bear 1553 and marry to the king of Suecia and by his second wife six son william bear 1564. Euno bear 1565. christopher bear 1570. Otho bear 1572. John bear 1573. frederick bear 1578. and three daughter Anna Margarita bear 1567. bear 1569. Catherina Sophia bear 1577. the first house of the duke of Luneburg and Brunswick for the title be common to al. Anne bear 1526. and Ernest who reform religion and die 1546 bury at Cella Henry marry the daughter of the d. of the low Saxony dwell at Angria Henry have three son julius ernestus bear 1571. Francis cannon of strasburg bear 1572. and Augustus bear 1579. and two daughter Sibilla Elizabetha bear 1576. and sidonia bear 1577. the second branch likewise duke of Luneburg and Brunswick William in the year 1561 marry Dorothy daughter to christian king of Denmark William have seven son Ernest bear 1564. Christian bear 1561. Augustus bear 1568. frederick bear 1574. Magnus bear 1577 than rector of the university of jena george bear 1582. John bear 1583. and seven daughter Sophia marry to George marquis of Brandeburg 1579. elizabeth to the count of hohelm 1585. doroty bear 1570. Clara 1571. margaret 1573. marry 1575. and Sibilla 1584. the three branch with the same title Francis of the reform religion leave two daughter no heir male and die 1549. Henry die before his brother Frederick William the victorious at the death of his uncle Frederick possess the dukedom of Brunswick which his uncle Bernard do yield to he he die 1482. duke of Brunswick from William the victorious descend Henry who twice in vain besiege Brunswick aid by other free city he die 1514. Henry make captive by the elector of Saxony 1545 twice besiege Brunswick he have two sister and four brother Ericus Christopher bishop of verden Francis and George archbishop of breme the elder marry Mary daughter to the duke of Wirtenberg and die 1568. julius his two elder brother be kill leave his priesthood reform religion after Luther doctrine found a university at Helmstat and call it julia marry daughter to joachim the second elector of Brandeburg and die in the year 1589. Henry julius bear 1562 administrator of two bishopric of Halberstat and mind first marry Dorothy daughter to Augustus elector of Saxony than Elizabeth daughter to Fredrick king of Denmark yet live by his first wife he have Dorothy bear 1587. by the second Fredrick vdalrike bear 1591. three brother philip Sigismond bishop of verden bear 1568. joachim Carolus bear 1573. julius Augustus bear 1578. five sister Sophia marry to Ernest duke of Pomeru marry to Francis d. of low Saxony 1582. elizabeth to the count of Schaumberg Dorothy bear 1577. 1580. the second house of the duke of Brunswick more powerful than all the former join he have four sister Catherine marry to John marquis of Brandeburg margaret to the d. of munsterberg Clara first a abbess then marry to Philip lord of Grachenhage and Mary a abbess and Ericus call the popish Ericus call also the popish marry his daughter to John prince Dauria of Genoa in Italy he have a base son who die without issue and lie bury with his father at Pavia in Italy the duke of Brunswick keep his court at a strong castle within the little city less than a german mile distant from Brunswick of which city he bear the duke of Brunswick the title in respect it of old belong to his progenitor in which kind he be also call duke of Luneburg to which he have right of succession and Purgrave of nurnberg which title have be long extinct not that he have any least power over the city or so much as a house therein who the citizen rather wish many mile remove from they I have say that Henry julius duke of Brunswick have three brother and that the elder of they be bishop of verden but when myself pass that way I understand that of these three young brother the elder be Bishop of Osenburg the next cannon of strasburg and that the young be a student in the university of Helmstatt found by his father and it be worth observation that the duke himself be administrator of two bishopricke I have show that the city of Brunswick get their liberty by the sword in the time of duke Otho and with the aid of the emperor Frederick the second and as they gain it by arm so they maintain it have be often besiege by the duke and to this day bear
