up the same against the duke with who they cease not to expostulate that they usurp the title of their city and not long before my passage that way when at the marriage of the duke with the sister to the king of Denmark the citizen of Brunswick discharge some great piece of artillery in honour of the marriage yet so great be the jealousy between the duke and they as he take it in ill part and shake his head for anger say it be do in ostentation of their strength and as the threaten of enemy rather than the triumph of friend and the senator of Brunswick though invite to the marriage yet will not come thither neither do they willing suffer the duke to come into their city and not long before when the duke for pleasure disguise himself as a carman and drive a cart of wood into the city to be sell there the chief consul have notice thereof command the gate to be shut and the street to be chain and when the duke have long sit upon the cart with some annoyance by reason of rain and find he can not go back and think it dangerous to go into any private house at last the consul buy his wood and so draw the duke to his house where he entertain he honourable yet remember he that he have put himself in needle danger know the ill affection of the common people towards he and then send for the duke servante to attend he lead he out of the city with honour Luneburg a free city of the empire be strong fortify and stately build but have no less jealousy with the neighbour duke bear the title of the city yet they neither the city and the duke of Luneburg dwell in the city nor meddle with the government thereof but abide at Cella and at sethern some twelve mile distant and in other city of that province according to the division of their inheritance between they the citizen of Luneburg know that of old they buy their liberty of these duke and that to this day they pay they some tribute just fear lest the duke or his brother and cousin be many in number shall practice any thing against their liberty or at least be poor shall seek new occasion to extort money from they the duke of old have a strong castle build upon a mountain hang over the city vulgar call Kalckberg that be the mountain of chalk but the butcher of the city at a shrovetide make show of muster in arm take this castle for which service to the commonwealth the butcher to this day have the keep thereof but the citizen be so suspicious lest it shall be betray to the duke for money as if any of they who keep it do at any time go forth of it into the city or to any other place they be no more permit to enter the same upon the further shore of the baltike sea towards the north-east the free city Dantzke of Dantzke be seat it be free in respect of privilege for traffic and for that it be govern by the senator and the law of the city yet not number among the imperial city because it in some sort acknowledge the king of Poland and pay some covenant tribute to his minister reside in the city in the time of Stephen Bathory prince of Transiluania and king of Poland this city be by he besiege and force to pay these tribute whereupon the citizen to pay they without their own prejudice double all imposition upon stranger trade there the consul of the city there as in all Germany be vulgar call burgomaster and he judge all civil and criminal cause but appeal be grant from he to the college of civil lawyer and from they to the senate of the city and in some cause from the senate to the king of Poland this city consist of three city vulgar call Reichstat Furstat and Altstatt that be the city of the kingdom the fore city and the old city and each have his gate and his senate and the consul may be choose out of either senate so as for the time of his office he reside in the chief city call Reichstatt here be the further limit of the empire towards the north and the east and from hence towards the west isle the shore of the baltic sea and of the german ocean upon which be seat Stetinum Meckleburg Lubeck hamburg and bream imperial city and free as weli for privilege of traffic in neighbour kingdom as for absolute government at home and in east-freesland for west-freesland belong to netherlands the further limit of the empire towards the west and north end in the city of Emden this city have his count bear title of the city and of late he keep his court Emden therein but the citizen profess the reform doctrine of Calvin and the count attempt to force they to the profession of Luther doctrine not long before I pass that way the citizen expel the count and give oath to the senator of the city to obey they to be ready in arm for the defence of the city and not to remove their dwelling from thence without leave of the senate and the citizen be thus confident because the city lie upon the confine of the netherlander who maintain man of war in that inland sea and upon the river pass by the wall of the city to stop any passage and to defend the ship of merchant force in those flat to expect the return of the tide and general it be think that Emden will join in league with the unite province of netherlands but as yet it remain under the empire not free by privilege nor yet subject to the count of Emden this count at this time have two son by the daughter of the king of Suelia and howsoever his young brother by the love of his mother have obtain the best part of the inheritance yet he have no child nor be think likely to have any so as no doubt be then make but that the whole inheritance will after his death return to the child of the elder brother and these thing shall suffice brief write in general of the commonwealth of Germany and in particular of the absolute dominion under the empire chap. v. of the commonwealth of Sweitzerland according to the diverse subject of the former chapter the sweitzer derive of diverse people and among other of the Schwalen and friselander howsoever they be german both in language and manner yet because the old gall give they a seat at the foot of the Alps they be account gall or french till the time of julius caesar. in the commentary of Caesar we find their commonwealth divide into four community whereof one namely that of Zurech have not long before overthrow the army of Lucius Cassius consul of Rome about this time the sweitzer weary of their barren seat attempt to seek a new place of dwell have not Caesar overcome they and contain they at home and from that time to the day of Charles the great and so long as his race possess the renew empire of the west they be still esteem gall but when that imperial dignity fall to the german they become subject to those emperor and be number among the german have the same manner law and custom with they as at this day they have the emperor govern this nation by governor vulgar call Reichs vogt till at last the commonwealth of the sweitzer be sever from that of the german and make a free state which in the age before we have get great reputation and here it be worthy to be observe that the ambition of pope and their devilish tyranny over the emperor not only after some eight hundred year from Christ cause almost all the garboil of state whereof we have hear or read or which with our own eye we have see but in particular be the chief yea sole cause of rend this strong member from the body of the empire Sweitzerland about that time abound with noble family and they the governor favour to increase their own power whereupon they oppress the common people and provoke their extreme hatred so as they be force to combine themselves in mutual league against this oppression yet the common people have never dare to oppose themselves to the gentry if the empire have enjoy peace but when the bishop of Rome often cast out their spiritual thunderbolt I mean excommunication against the emperor and aswell absolve all subject from the oath of allegiance as heartried ambitious traitor to be competitor against the emperor yea stir up their kinsman and their very child to make civil war with they in this confuse anarchy a patron be not want to the most wicked person to defend he so he will follow his party hence it come that when the emperor Frederick the second in the year 1240 receive the common people of Sweitzerland into his protection against the gentleman they likewise as the client of monastery follow the pope party whereupon the people of Zurech the and of who the whole nation be call Sweitzers be three community first in the year 1251 make a league for three year against the gentleman lie in wait to entrap they and after by little and little they make more firm and perpetual league for defence of absolute liberty and serve diverse emperor in the Parall tumult from time to time obtain great privilege then they draw other community and neighbour city to be partner of their league final after they have root out the family of gentleman and have by conspiracy castout the governor of the archduke of Austria to who in the lordship of Sweitzerland be fall they in process of time attain this absolute state which we see they enjoy at this day for the foresay strife continue between the emperor and the pope and Lodowick of Bavaria contend for the empire with Frederick of the house of Austria the sweitzer take part with Lodowick who express ratify the freedom or liberty of then commonwealth and that consist of three part the community vulgar call canton the four part of the commonwealth fellow or confederate and the stipendiary city or governement to which Semler add the forraigue league for a four part the community be vulgar call ort and by the italian canton of the thirteen canton sweitzer as we will hereafter call they and they be thirteen in number among these canton the come from the taurisci the come from the cimbri the of the race of banish roman about the year 1308 make a mutual league for ten year and have overcome in battle Leopold archduke of Austria in the year 1315 make this league perpetual the four canton be much wrong while it be under the patronage of the house of Austria to avoid tyranny in that confusion of the empire do in the year 1332 join itself in perpetual league Zurech a free city of the empire be in like sort receive for the 5 Canton when these canton make war upon Glarona to have the possession of a country most commodious for the common good all the people so hate the governor appoint by the house of Austria and so desire freedom as they yield up themselves to the canton and so Glarona the foresay year become the six canton likewise when the canton Zurech make war upon zug a town possess by gentleman subject to the house of Austria whence they be much annoy the citizen be forsake by the gentleman yield themselves and be receive into the league become the seven canton (@) a free city of the empire and under the power of the governor have find the faithful love of the canton in the say confusion of the empire do in the year 1352 make a perpetual league with the three first name canton wherein notwithstanding Zurech and be contain the three canton be bind to those two for the succour of bern and be bind to bern for the succour of the two canton and so bern become the eight Canton the town Friburg subject to the house of Austria be many way oppress in the say confusion of the empire make league with bern and when the house of Austria upon pretence to visit they do for that cause spoil they of their good they in the year 1481 after the end of the Burgundian war become the nine canton Solothara a free city of the empire have make a perpetual league with berne in the year 1351 and after in the year 1481 be receive for the ten canton Bizila free city of the empire have in the year 1327 make a perpetual league with the 3 first canton and after provoke by many injury of the house of Austria do in the year 1501 make a perpetual league with all the canton and so be receive for the eleven canton (@) a imperial city first sell or engage to the house of Austria and again unite to the empire when the duke of Austria be proscribe in the council of Costnetz because the emperor Frederick the three in the year 1454 exact a oath of the citizen as duke of austria not as emperor they first make a league for certain year with six canton and after at the end of the suenian war make a perpetual league in the year 1501 with all the canton and so become the twelve canton Abbatiscella vulgar call Apinzill contain eight village after it have obtain freedom from the abbot of saint Gallus by arm and money have war with the house of Austria about the year 1408 at which time it make a league with the canton and after provoke by the injury of the say abbot in the year 1452 it make a perpetual league with the seven first canton and at last in the year 1513 become the thirteen canton among the fellow in league first the abbot of saint Gallus in the year 1451 fellow in league obtain to be make a citizen of the four canton zurech Suitia and Glarona which right all the abbot renew and promise that all their possession shall lie open to the say canton and that in controversy they will rest in the judgement of the canton and by another agreement each of these canton appoint a captain to be assessor to the abbot in judgement and the canton have half of all mulct or fine impose and the subject of the abbot be bind to serve they in war and howsoever some of the canton at this day be of the reform religion yet they send a captain according to the covenant and defend all the right of the monastery in the second place be the town of saint Gallus which have buy freedom from all right of the abbot and of the empire make a perpectuall league with six canton Zurech bern Suitia Zug and Glarona which be confirm in the year 1454. in the three place be the call grison by the itallian divide into three league the first be call the upper league consist of nintene community and be make with the seven old canton in the year 1407. the second in respect of the bishopric of Chur be call the league of the house of God consist of nineteen community whereof two use the language of Germany the rest the language of the country be corrupt italian which the year follow join in league with the canton the three league call the ten judgement or jurisdiction and consist of ten community join in the league with the canton in the year 1498 and at the same time the house of Austria prepare war against the rhetian they all joint make a perpetual league of fellowship with all the canton in the four place the seven ten of the valesian and the bishop of sedune earl of Valesia for the controversy of religion in our time make a perpetual league of fellowship with seven canton of the roman religion Suitia Vnderualdia Tugium vulgar zug Friburg and Solodurum vulgar solothurne the town Rotevilla in the five place make a perpetual league of fellowship with all the canton in the year 1519 but because it be seat in Germany out of the confine of mountainous Sweitzerland caution be make that without the consent of the canton they shall make no war nor give any aid and if war be make upon they in case the enemy consent they shall rest in the judgement which the canton shall hold just and equal and that they shall make no league without the consent of the canton and in time of civil war shall follow the great part of they in the six place Mulhusium of old a imperial city be incorporate to the city of Bazill in the year 1506 and after nine year make a perpetual league of fellowship with all the canton in the seven place the town Bienna or enjoy all privilege under the bishop of (@) in the year 1303 make a more firm league with bern in the year 1352. in the eight place be Geneva which give all right and keep all old covenant with the bishop thereof till he sell the same to the duke of Savoy after that time this city make diverse league with the canton for certain year and at last covenant the right of citizen with the canton of bern and be assail for religion confirm the same more strict in the year 1536 since which time some motion have be make to unite Geneva with the canton in public league but hitherto it can not be effect in the nine and last place be the town neocomum with the county thereof which the sweitzer take in the war against the king of France Lewis the twelve and because it belong to the duke of Longoville in France his widow in the year 1529 obtain to have it restore to she upon certain condition yet still it have league of fellowship with the canton of bern Lucern Friburg and solothurn and the lord of the county have a particular league with the canton of bern. touch the people govern by the sweitzer in common they be five stipendiary stipendiary city and governement city and nine governement the city be so call because they serve the sweitzer in war at their stipend and have their own magistrate yet be subject to the canton and rule by their statute these city of old subject to the house of Austria become subject to the canton upon condition that keep their privilege they shall obey they in the same manner as former they do the house of Austria the sweitzer take baden Brimigart and when the duke of Austria be proscribe and after the emperor engage those city to the canton of Zurech which make other 7 canton partner of that engagement namely Suitia Vnderualdia Vria Glarona and berna the sweitzer take Rapersuilla in the year 1458 be receive into the city and help by those of their faction and they take the five city Frawenfield in the year 1460 when the duke of Austria be excommune upon the pope command well please to they among the government that of baden be subject to the foresay eight canton the second of Turg be subject to all the same canton except bern but the judgement and fine belong to the ten old canton the three of the free province be give to the sweitzer in fee from the emperor when the say duke of Austria be proscribe and it be subject to the same canton except bern but the governor dwell not among they only use to come to they for the judgement of cause the four be the country of the sarunetes sell by their earl to the say canton except bern in the year 1483. the fist of the Rhegusei be sell to the canton Apenzill in the year 1460 and the canton draw it to common subjection when Apenzill be admit into the number of the canton so as Apenzill also be partner in that government in the last place be the four government beyond the Alps seat in Italy namely the town Lucanum the Locarnenses the medrisians and the middle valley which the duke of Milan give to the canton for a reward upon the cast out of the french in the year 1513 and yet the king of France Francis the first after upon the cast out of that duke confirm this gift to the canton to these be add the town sell to the canton Vria Suitia and Vnderualdia in the year 1422 the country whereof be divide into three government command by the say three canton by course or turn touch foreign league among those make for certain year pope sixtus foreign league for (@) namely the papal league in the year 1478 make league with the sweitzer and give they large spiritual indulgence likewise at the end of the sweitzer league with the king of France Lewis the twelve in the year 1509 pope julius the second in the year 1510 make league with the sweitzer but the soldier levy under the pay of pope julius perceive that he deal not direct and true with they employ they to expel the king of France out of Milan who he have hire under pretence to defend the church against the duke of Ferrara they can not contain themselves from return sudden into their country and be dismiss without pay they cease not with many threaten to storm against the pope yet in the year 1511 the same pope julius be overcome by the french he call the sweitzer again to his aid who send he a army of 20000 foot at which time the sweitzer be offend with the french cast they out of Milan whereupon pope julius give to this commonwealth the title of the defender of the church and diverse banner charge with diverse image and a cap for sign of liberty with a sword also Maximilianus Sfortia by their aid be then put into the possession of the dukedom of Milan make league with the sweitzer and give they the foresay government in Italy also pope Leo the ten in the year 1515 join himself to the league make between the emperor Maximilian and Sfortia duke of Milan and the sweitzer against the king of France last pope Clement the eight sit in the chair of Rome when I pass through Italy have also league with the sweitzer but I must come to the hereditary foreign league which only and no other can true be call part of the commonwealth among the chief of they be that of Milan and not to speak of the ancient league which some of the canton have with the insubres old inhabitant of Lombardy foreign hereditary league as that of Milan Galeacius duke of Milan in the year 1466 make a league with eight canton wherein mention be make of the say league with the insubres and he grant to the that they shall possess the Lepontian valley for which they be to send unto the duke yearly four hawk and a crossbow moreover he grant to the eight canton that in his dukedom they shall be free from all imposition and tax afterward these and other head of that league be confirm and renew by the duke his successor and Ferdinand Gonzaga in the name of the emperor Charles the five confirm the same in the year 1551. and among other head of that league it be covenant that it shall be free for the sweitzer to buy corn yet in time of dearth they may carry none out of the dukedom only some 200 bushel shall in that case be sell to they as friend also caution be insert about the buy and carry of salt and that they shall free pass to and fro without safe conduct except those time in which Sweitzerland shall be infect with the plague and that they shall be free from imposition in all place except the city of Milan where they shall pay custom at the gate last that the sweitzer thus privilege shall not take any other to be partner in traffic with they to the prejudice of the dukedom and that all such shall be exclude from these privilege as have flee out of the dukedom whether they live in Sweitzerland or else where and this league be make to continue four year after the death of the emperor Charles the five and this time expire howsoever the league be not for a time renew yet the sweitzer enjoy all these privilege this hereditary league descend to the king of Spain who succeed Charles the five in the say duchy of Milan and all his possession in Italy the sweitzer have have sharp war with the house of Burgundy and long continue war with the house of Austria which at last be end in league and the Burgundian and austrian league friendship the first war of Burgundy begin in the year 1474 the house of Austria use the pride and ambition of the duke of Burgundy to keep down the sweitzer with who themselves have often fight with no good success for the cause of the war spring from certain country to this end engage by Sigismond duke of Austria to charles duke of Burgundy whence the desire and expect jealousy grow between they which Lewis the 2 king of France do nourish bear a spleen to duke Charles and for that cause do furnish the sweitzer with money to make that war and Sigismond duke of Austria more to encourage the sweitzer make league with they against the duke of Burgundy also the emperor Frederick of the house of Austria lead a army against the say duke do stir up the sweitzer to assail he but when they have with good success pierce into Burgundy the emperor make peace with the say duke wherein the sweitzer be not contain so as the duke turn all his force upon they and not to speak of light skirmish and fight the main business be try between they in three battle wherein the duke himself be in person first at the town Granson where the sweitzer have the victory but they have no horse which can not so soon be send to they from their confederate and the horse of the duke defend the foot in their flight few of the burgundian be kill there second they fight at Morat where they write that 26000 of the burgundian be slay and to this day huge heap of dead bone lie in that place to witness that great overthrow the three battle be at Nancy a city of lorain where Charles duke of Burgundy besiege Renatus duke of Lorraine confederate with the sweitzer and then 8000 sweitzer and 3000 of their confederate send to help they overcome the duke of Burgundy and himself be kill (@) his death give a end to that war in the year 1477. after the house of Austria have make many war and league for year with the sweitzer at last Sigismond duke of Austria before the Burgundian war make a hereditary league with they lewis the french king mediate the same that he may draw the sweitzer to make war with Charles the proud duke of Burgundy by this league it be agree that any controversy fall they shall be put to arbiter both part bind themselves to stand to their judgement that all old league always preserve they shall serve the duke of Austria in his war upon the same pay they have at home serve the State on the other side that the possessor shall hold all place without call into question for they that neither part shall join in league the subject of the other or make they free of their city that neither part shall burden the other with custom or imposition at this time in the year 1474 many neighbour prince city and bishop do join themselves in league for year but the foresay league with the house of Austria cease in the time of the emperor Maximilian the first who make league with the prince and city of Germany against the sweitzer call the great league of Sucura that war end Maximilian in the year 1511 renew the former league join therein the house of Burgundy and his grandchild Charles after make emperor the five of that name so unite all the thirteen canton with both those house and he promise in the name of Charles that he shall yearly pay in the town of Zurech two hundred gold crown to each canton for a testimony of love and for the abbot of saint Gallus and the town and beside for Apenzill he shall yearly the german emperor renew the league of Austria give each of they one hundred crown and that he shall confirm this league this be do in the year 1543 wherein Charles the emperor renew the league of Burgundy and his brother Ferdinand succeed he in the empire renew the league of the house of Austria at this time whereof I write the ambassador of the king of Spain reside at Philip king of Spain renew the league of Burgundy and Milan Frihurg and Philip king of Spain son to Charles have as I understand by Sweitzers of good credit renew the league of Burgundy and Milan with the canton of the roman religion to last five year after his death and this be do in the church of Milan where the ambassador be say to have hang up their shield in memory thereof and to have give a thousand gold crown to the beautify of the church at which time the king of Spain divide twelve thousand gold crown between the ambassador beside the charge pass three thousand crown but the ambassador of solothurn withdraw themselves from this league because the king of France be indebt to they which debt the king of Spain refuse to pay by this league they be mutual bind to aid each other with one thousand two hundred foot and the king of Spain promise yearly pension in general to the canton and in particular to diverse chief man and captain for the sweitzer use to make no league without profit since the neighbour prince grow of opinion that they can not make war except their army be strengthen with a firm body of sweitzer not only solothurn renounce the say league but also the canton of the reform religion partly not to do any thing against their league with France partly leave they shall take part with a king who they judge most ambition and a great enemy to the reform religion howsoever he cover that hatred and partly lest they shall advance the house of Austria just suspect by they who victory may turn to their ruin and at the same time the canton and fellow in league be of the reform religion after the doctrine of Caluine make a league for defence of religion among themselves and with strasburg a neighbour free city of Germany be of the reform religion after the doctrine of Luther the duke of Savoy have his ambassador reside at where the pope ambassador also reside of who league for year we former speak the old the league of Savoy allobroges now call Savoyans have old league with the canton of bern Friburg and solothurn but Charles duke of Savoy in the year 1512 make a league for twenty five year with all the canton by which among other thing it be covenant that the duke shall aid the sweitzer with six hundred or more horse at his own charge so he be not distract with war at home and that the sweitzer shall aid the duke with six thousand foot for any war in his own country to who the duke shall pay each man six Frankes by the month but he shall not employ they to fight at sea nor lead they beyond the sea but only to defend his own country and the confine thereof and it be covenant that during this league the duke shall yearly pay at bern two hundred gold crown to each canton when this league be expire duke Charles put out of his dukedom by the french king Francis the first follow the emperor Charles the five and the renew of this league be intermit but the king of France restore Philebert his son to the dukedom this duke in the year 1560 make a new and perpetual league with six canton namely Suitia Vria Vnderualdia Zug and Solothurne and after the rest of the canton upon like condition renew the old league with this duke only in this last league no mention be make of mutual aid covenant by the former league the french ambassador reside at solothurn who of old use to reside at Bazil the french league and the league of the french king with the sweitzer be of far great moment than any of the rest the first of the french that make war with the sweitzer be Lewis the french king son after the eleven king of that name who lead a army to assist pope Eugenius in dissolve the council at Bazill be persuade by the emperor Frederick to assail the sweitzer but a small number of they possess straight passage do so annoy his army as he soon retire he make peaco with the sweitzer in the year 1450 and have try their strength make league with they for ten year his son Charles the eight in the year 1483 renew this league and use the sweitzer in his war with the duke of Brittany and for the kingdom of Naples Lewis the twelve after the league for year be expire renounce the payment of all public or private pension wherewith the sweitzer be so great offend as after they refuse to renew that league with he and join in league with the pope and the duke of Milan against he so as by their aid he be in the year 1512 cast out of the dukedom of Milan the french king Francis the first fight with the sweitzer join against he in league with the emperor Maximilian pope Leo the ten and Sfortia duke of Milan for howsoever the sweitzer suspect the proceed of their confederate and purpose to return home yet the praetorian Sweitzers of the duke of Milan assail the french the rest of the sweitzer though call home yet lest they shall seem to forsake their companion join with the praetorian sweitzer and so by art and cunning draw to fight give the french a notable overthrow at which time the sweitzer have the great army they ever bring into the field be 31000 foot but the french king Francis the next day fight again with the sweitzer overthrow they yet so as the retreat as they write be nothing like a flight and so the king cast Sfortia out of the dukedom of Milan recover the same after this prosperous success the french king seek nothing more than to be reconcile and join in league with the sweitzer he have overcome which he do the league consist of 13 head 1. they covenant for take away all injury and controversy 2. for free of captive 3. how the sweitzer may plead any cause in judgement against the King 4 that all shall enjoy the benefit thereof be bear within the confine of Sweitzerland and speak the dutch tongue 5. privilege be confirm to the merchant of Sweitzerland 6 for charge in the siege of Dyiune and in Italy the king covenant to pay they a great sum of money by yearly portion 7. it be agree that all controversy shall be determine by course there set down not by war 8. that neither part shall give passage to the enemy of the other 9 that merchant and all subject on both part shall free pass not offend with reproach or oppress with imposition 10. that the king shall yearly pay to each canton 2000 frank and to the abbot of s. Gallus and his subject and to those of Toggenburg 600 Frankes and to the city of s. Gallus 400 to the mulhusians 400 to the gruerians 600 to the valisians 2000 and to the grison the pension give by Lewis the 12 and moreover yearly 2000 frank but howsoever the rhetian or grison by this league serve the king in his war with the sweitzer yet Semler witness that they serve several under their own captain in the 11 article all immunity in the dukedom of Milan be confirm to the Bilitianenses the inhabitant of the middle valley the Luganenses and the Locarnenses 12. choice be give to the sweitzer to retain the castle they have or to take money for they last it be agree and covenant that the league shall be peripetuall and not be break upon any fraudulent pretence in this league the king except all his confederate and the sweitzer except pope Leo the 10 the emperor Maximilian the empire and the house of Austria and all old league so as if the king shall make war upon any of these in their own country it may be free to the sweitzer to observe their league with they but if any of they assail the king in his own kingdom the sweitzer shall not permit any of their subject to serve they but shall call they home this league be make at Friburg in the year 1516 the month of November and upon the day of saint Andrew and the king rest not till after five year since this peace be make he league himself more strict at with all the canton that of Zurech only except and with all their fellow in league of which league I will brief relate some head add to the former namely that if any man shall make war upon the king in France or in the dukedom of Milan the king at his pleasure may levy in Sweitzerland a army of six thousand at the least or sixteen thousand foot at the most except the senate shall grant a great number that the king may choose the captain and the senate without delay shall permit they to march within ten day and not recall they till the war shall be end if the king shall please so long to use they that by the same right and under the same condition the king make war upon any may free levy soldier but with this caution that the sweitzer trouble with war at home shall be free from these covenant it be further caution that the king shall not divide the army of the sweitzer into diverse place or fort but shall keep it vait in one body that he shall not use it for any fight at Sea that they shall receive pay the same day they shall march out of their country and be they never so soon send back yet three month pay shall be present due unto they and that the first month pay shall be give they within the confine of Sweitzerland that the king to aid the sweitzer have any war shall send they two hundred arm horse and twelve great piece of ordinance with all furniture namely six batter piece and six middle piece and beside towards the charge of their war shall each three month pay a certain sum of money at lion and if the sweitzer shall choose rather to have money in stead of the arm horse the king shall further pay they two thousand crown each three month that if in time of war the sweitzer shall be forbid to buy salt in other place they may buy and bring salt out of France that neither part shall make the subject of the other free of their city or receive they into patronage that the king to declare his good will towards the sweitzer shall beside the two thousand francke promise by the former league to each canton pay yearly one thousand francke more to each of they during this league and moreover shall beside the former pension give to their confederate yearly half as much more in this league the king except pope Leo the ten the emperor the king of England Scotland and Denmark with other prince and the sweitzer except the pope the emperor the house of Austria the house of Medici the d. of Sanoy and some other but if these so except shall make war upon either part within their territory that aid shall be send mutual without any respect this league be make to last three year after the death of the french king Francis the first and be renew by his son Henry the second at Solotburn in the year 1549 and by all the canton except Zurech and bern and be after renew by Charles the nine and the succeed king but in the league make with the successor of Francis the first caution be insert that the sweitzer shall not serve the king in any war for the recovery of any part of the dukedom of Milan but if the king shall recover it with any other army than they shall aid he to defend his possession as former and whereas the canton of Zurech and bern refuse to join in the league make with Francis the first and Henry the second these reason thereof be then allege first because the canton of Zurech be then alienate from the french by the cardinal of Sedon second because a notable preacher of the reform religion do in many sermon sharp inveigh against mercenary warfare three because this league much displease the military man of Sweitzerland in that the senate have no liberty to look into the cause of the war in that the soldier and captain be not to be choose by the sweitzer but by the king at his pleasure in that the large profit of the league redound to few in that the arm horse to be send by the king be of no use to the sweitzer war common make in mountainous place and craggy passage last because it seem a point of great inconstancy that the sweitzer who late when the french king Francis and charles the decease emperor grandchild be competitor for the empire have write to the elector that they will yield no obedience to the french king in case he be choose shall so sudden change their mind and make a more strict league with the french but the great part be of a contrary judgement because soldier be not bind curious to inquire after the cause of war for which only the king in his conscience be bind to give account and because their barren country be also populous be most fit for a mercenary war and that military experience be thereby to be retain and gain by which and like reason they persuade the necessity of this league thus have I according to the description of Sembler brief show that the sweitzer of the sweitzer commonwealth in general commonwealth consist of three part at home not to speak of the foreign league namely of the canton of the fellow in league and of the stipendiary city and prefecture or government each community be vulgar call ort and the italian call they canton whereof I have say that there be thirteen in number namely Suitia vulgar schwei whereof the rest have the name of sweitzer Vria Vnderualdia vulgar zurech Glarona Tugium vulgar zug berna Friburgum vulgar solothurn Basilea vulgar bazill vulgar Shafhusen and Abbatiscella vulgar Apenzill I have say that the fellow in league be the abbot and town of saint Gallus the rhetian or grison the bishop of Sedun the valcsians and the town Rotavile and bipenue and the governement be Turgea that of baden of the Rhegusci of the sarunetes of the free province the the Locarnenses the inhabitant of the middle valley and the Bilitionenses that of Turgea be subject to the seven old canton yet bern Friburg and solothurn have also their right in capital cause that of Raden the sarunetes the Rhegusci and the free province be subject likewise to the seven old canton only bern have be admit partner in that of baden and Apenzill in that of the Rhegusci the four italian governement be equal subject to all the canton except Apenzill and the Bilitionenses be subject to the three old canton all these join have these city and town Zurech bern Lucern Zug Bazill Friburg solothurn Schafhusen the town of s. Gallus Chur of the grison Sedun of the valesian Rotevil Mulhuse and bipenne all the rest dwell in village among the canton Bazill of the rauraci Schafhusen of Germany Glarona in part of the grison Vria in part of the be seat out of the old confine of Sweitzerland and so be all the fellow in league except the abbot and town of s. Gallus and the town among these the old nation of the rhetian now call grison be of old call and veragri and Rotevile be a city of Germany and mulhuse of the in France among the governement the Rhegusci and the sarunetes be of the old rhetian and the Luganenses the Locarnenses the and the inhabitant of the middle valley and the be of the and italian nation which tongue they speak many doubt to number these confederate among commonwealth since each of they be no otherwise tie to the decree of the other then by free consent as all private society be whereas in a commonwealth the great part bind all yet because they have one common council and most of the province be rule thereby because war and peace be make by common consent and they live almost under the same law and custom and be unite strict in perpetual league Semler conclude that this society come near to the form of a commonwealth for whereas some hate the nation object Anarchy to they and say they get freedom by kill the gentleman and other interpret it more mild and confess the oppression of the gentleman yet judge the revenge to have exceed all measure the truth thereof will appear by the history of Semler and other show that great part of the gentry be extinguish by the house of Austria therefore it must be a mix commonwealth if such it may be call be neither a monarchy of one just king aristocraty of just great man nor democrity a popular state much less any of the corrupt commonwealth call tyranny oligarchy and anarchy that be the tyranny of a king or of noble man or a confuse state the equity of the government show that it much differ from they the the Glaronenses dwell scatter and zug though it be a town govern all with the consent of the people Zurech bern Lucern Bazill Friburg solothurn and Schafhusen be govern by the chief man but since the magistrate be choose by the people some of these city be more some less aristocratical or popular the ambassador send to public meeting have aristocratical form but since they be choose by the people with limit power it may seem popular and it be not unequal that the people have settle freedom with their danger shall be partner of their government but in the mean time the sweitzer avoid as much as they can the discommodity of a popular state while none but the best and most wise be send to the meeting and howsoever their power be limit yet when they return they so relate all thing to the people as they easy understand they and give consent thereunto but to make the strict union of these confederate more apparent it will not be amiss to peruse some of the head of the league between the eight old canton therein first be caution of send mutual aid so as first in public counsel the justness of the war may be examine then the aid be not to be require of all but of each particular canton according to their mutual league Zurech by old league have right to require aid of the first six canton and by a new league also of bern. and bern require aid of the three first canton and they of bern. require aid of five canton the three first canton require mutual aid of one another and of all the rest Zug and be bind to aid the five canton Glarona require aid of the three old canton and Zurech thus while one canton call the next join to it in league howsoever each one can require aid of all or each one by right of league yet in any common danger all the canton bring their force be call of some one or more canton in league with they beside that they have wise decree that in sudden danger all shall bring succour whether they be call or no. they which be call to give aid by virtue of any league serve at their own cost without any pay between bern and the three old canton it be decree that if the aid be send beyond certain bound than they which call they shall give they pay and in like sort certain bound of send aid be limit between all the rest of the canton in their several league with all condition express in any siege the canton which cause it be bind to find many necessary but if the cause be public all provision be make at the public charge the four old canton and Glarona can make any new league which be free to the rest always preserve the old league which they also may increase or diminish by common consent it be decree that every five or ten year this league shall be renew by word or writing or if need be by oath reach bern Vria Suitia and Vnderualdia in this league except the right of the roman empire and zug the right of the duke of Austria Glarona the right of their lawful magistrate and each canton the right of old league when the say eight canton receive the other five into their number beside the foresay head it be decree among other thing in their league that the five last canton howsoever wrong shall make no war without the consent of the eight old canton and in like sort that they shall make no league without their consent neither in time of war shall refuse good condition of peace and last it be decree that without great cause no war shall be make in place out of the mountain and difficult passage of that province where they can not fight with advantage the thirteen canton have that privilege that they deliberate and determine the affair of the commonwealth in public meeting by voice and govern by equal right the government get joint by they and have equal part in all booty the great senate be when all the ambassador that be choose burgess of the canton and fellow in league be call together which be seldom do but in the cause of make war or peace only the ambassador of the thirteen canton be common call to counsel all ambassador have equal right in give voice but two or more be send from one Canton have but one voice in cause concern the governement belong to seven or eight or 12 canton only the ambassador or burgess or state of those canton meet to who the government belong and so the burgess of all other several for thing belong to themselves but where the cause concern the public state the full senate of all the canton be call to the meeting since the late difference of religion new and particular meeting have be institute the canton of the roman religion Vria Suitia Vnderualdia and zug join in a more strict league do often meet together and when any man name the five canton simple they mean they not the hue old canton howsoever name the three seven or eight canton they be take according to the time of their enter into league and sometime the canton of Frihurg and solothurn be also of the roman religion come to the meeting of the say five canton great part of the citizen of Glarona and Apenzill be of the reform religion and the four city chief of the canton namely Zurech bern Bazill and Schafhusen have altogether cast off the roman religion and have particular meeting but not often yet when I pass through this province I understand that Glarona be altogether of the reform religion and that Apenzill be number among the canton of the roman religion the great senate determine of war peace and league each have freedom to refuse any league likewise of make law of send receive and answer ambassador of government of distribute gainful office of difficult cause refer to the senate by governor and of appeal make from governor to the senate ambassador or burgess in place of judge be send about the month of june to hear the cause of the italian government from who they may appeal to the senate and these appeal as all other be determine by the senate in the meeting at baden where also they deliberate of custom and imposition and the revenue and if need be of punish the governor or displace they in which case the canton which send that governor appoint another the city Zurech chief of the canton have the first place not by antiquity but dignity and of old custom have the high authority to call the senate together signify to each canton by letter the cause and the time of each meeting yet if any canton think it for the public good to have a extraordinary meeting they write to Zurech to appoint the same or if the cause admit no delay they meet uncalled most common the general meeting be at Lucern Zurech Bremogart and baden but more common in these day and almost continual they be at baden in respect of the commodity of the house and inn the pleasant situation and famous medicinal bath and because it be seat in the centre of Sweitzerland and be subject to the 8 old canton the canton of the roman religion common have their particular meeting at sometime at Bockenried of the or brame of the and be call together by the canton of Lucern and the canton of the reform religion have their particular meeting common at Arowike under bern sometime at Bazil and be call together by the canton of Zurech foreign ambassador require of Zurech to have audience in the senate but the peculiar meeing for french cause be call by the french ambassador as often as he will at solothurn where he reside or at Lucern and other ambassador shall not be deny extraordinary meeting so they pay the expense as the french ambassador do the full senate yearly meet about September at baden about which time I say that burgess in place of judge be send to hear the cause of the italian government and in this first meeting the great cause be not determine either because the ambassador or burgess or state have not full power or for other cause but another meeting be there appoint and howsoever this senate be only call for public cause yet those be end they use to hear private cause also assoon as the say burgess or state at the appoint day come to the city the burgess of Zurech send the vice-governor of baden to salute they and to acquaint they with the time of meeting then they sit down in the court first the burgess of Zurech in a place raise high than the rest 2. those of bern three those of as chief though not in antiquity yet in dignity and after the rest according to the antiquity of their canton the burgess of Zurech first make a oration and propound the cause upon which they be to consult add what his canton have command he in each particular and then the rest speak in order according to the direction give they at home the under governor of baden of what canton soever he be ask and number the voice the peculiar meeting of particular canton and those for french affair have no set time each canton have public magistrate vulgar call Vmbgelten who administer the imposition upon wine and corn and gather they by their deputy they pay the tribute tribute only for that wine which be sell in tavern and for that corn which be export or use by baker for otherwise the citizen pay not for wine and corn bring their private house and spend therein and I have observe that they pay in some place the value of 24 measure tribute for a vessel of wine contain ninety six measure the salt which be bring in be only sell by the senate of each city or canton and I understand by discourse that the citizen may not buy salt or take it of gift out of the city particular at Schafhusen the custom be great especial for salt in respect that the water of the Rhine have a great fall from a rock so as all ship must be unlade before they can pass by that city in general the sweitzer especial want wine corn and salt as may appear by the covenant of their foreign league and otherwise the tribute be small which can be impose upon such a free nation concern their law I have former say that the senerall canton be not bind one to the decree of the other except they free consent thereunto yet that the law they all have one common council and almost all have the same common law and custom which they inviolable keep they long suffer the governor of the empire to be over they in capital cause though with prejudice to their freedom till at last in the suevian war about the year 1499 the judgement of capital cause be grant to they by the emperor among the condition of peace whereupon the ten old canton who make this war have equal right of capital judgement in the stipendiary city and government with the canton to who they be subject though get before they enter into the common league howsoever they have no right in the civil cause nor any other command over they in the old league beside the article concern union many law for the public good be contain and establish such be that of the old league between the eight first canton wherein they set down caution for peaceable determine of public controversy between the canton and thereby two canton be at strife be to choose two honest man who give their oath to make a equal composition between they and the rest of the canton be to add one arbiter to they and in case one of the canton consent the other refuse to stand to their judgement all the rest be to help the canton consent thereunto and in the league of the five last canton as in all other they just give curious cantion for take away all controversy and especial labour to effect that they break not out into civil war in which case they shall be diverse distract according to their diverse combination and league among themselves therefore of old when the abbot of saint Gallus attempt to remove the trade of clothing and the holy relic the superstitious worship whereof bring great profit from Apenzill to rosake where the abbot have absolute command and this matter draw they to arm wherein the abbot call the four canton his confederate and Apenzill the six canton with who it have league to give they aid according to their mutual league the say canton thus call to aid both part earnest endeavour to make peace whereby they preserve the commonwealth for if they shall not alwaiea careful so do in like occasion many time the dissension of one or two canton may draw all the rest into a pernicious civil war in the foresay league between the eight old canton and in the Stantian transaction in the year 1481 law be establish that he who kill any confederate vulgar call eidgenossen that be injoyer of the oath shall be behead except he have sufficient witnessea that he do it to save his life and in case of flight he be banish by one canton shall also be banish by all the rest and that he shall be judge guilty of the crime who shall help he and that sentence shall be give upon he in the canton where the crime be commit that there shall be no general meeting of the people without consent of the magistrate that none of the canton shall support any disobedient subject of another canton but shall force they to obedience that a lay person shall not use the help of a ecclesiastical judge but in cause of matrimony and manifest usury which be refer to ecclesiastical judgement that pledge or gauge be not take at private man pleasure but with consent of the judge that cause be judge in the canton wherein the act be do and sentence be give without fraud or deceit and that every man be content and rest satisfy in the judgement law and custom of another canton that all booty in war be divide among the canton according to the number of soldier which each of they send but that town tribute and like thing gain by war shall be under the common command of all the canton of which commodity the subject of stipendiary city and fellow in league shall have no part though their force be join in the same war with the force of the canton howsoever they be to have part in the division of all other booty in like sort the league of Schafhusen with the canton of the sweitzer determine how debt be to be recover and what law be to be use in such suit and that no league be make by one without the privity and against the will of the rest and that the old league be ever most respect the commonwealth be administer with great equity yet with no less severity of justice than the german use and howsoever all the country lie within mountain and wood yet the high way for passenger be no where more safe from thief so as it be there proverbial say that you may carry gold in the palm of your hand for all crime be severe punish without all respect of person the scope and butt whereat all their league aim be that every man may peaceable enjoy his own and that the best man among they may in public counsel examine the cause of war that they be just and lawful to the end they may never rash make war upon any and because the common people be burden with debt be more prone to sedition curious order be set down in their league for the manner of exact debt and take pledge neither give liberty of oppression to the creditor nor permit fraud to the debtor also because military man and such as drink in excess be prone to brawl and blow most heavy penalty be thereby instict upon such as be author of injury duel and the league make not more frequent mention of any other thing then of reproach for which they prescribe such good remedy and real satisfaction not pass over the least injury of the poor man as among the very soldier yea half drunken there very seldom happen any murder wherein I can wish that our inferior magistrate will apply themselves and our law be accommodate to the sweitzer government for the english being most impatient of reproach and the law give ridiculous satisfaction for injury by word and all wrong except maim it have be account a disgraceful course to seek remedy that way and most injury have common be revenge by the sword in single combat but in Sweitzerland all stander by be bind to keep the peace and compose the strife and if they who strive be remember of the law do not obey they use to punish they most severe and if any kill another he be sure to be behead except he escape by flight in which case he shall be banish by his own and all other canton or except he can prove by witness that he kill he in defence of himself and such be the fame of the sweitzer sincere justice as many stranger their neighbour judgement desire to have their controversy end after the manner of the sweitzer and by they when cause be to be plead before the senate most man plead their own cause some use lawyer bring by they from home or sound there by chance and the cause be not determine after the civil but after the provincial law or according to that which seem good and equal and by the statute and custom of each canton they have no quirk or obscurity to protract judgement and they think it better sometime to err in a doubtful cause then to follow the lawyer judge according to law not equity and so make the suit perpetual in the governement all controversy be determine by the governor and judge of the place yet so as appeal be grant from they to the common senate in private canton cause be judge by the senator and judge of each canton yet they have some public judgement namely when the canton have any controversy one with another or a private man with a canton for which case they have many caution in their league and at this day they be determine after this manner each part choose two judge of his own citizen who be absolve in that case from their oath give to their own canton and then they be swear that they will consider of the controversy according to that which seem good and equal and that they will faithful endeavour to compose it at least so as it shall be decide by law not by arm and in the old league certain place be appoint in which these judgement be handle the 7 canton common meet for they in the monastery of the Heremites within their own confine and so other league in other appoint place the judge and burgess of those canton with which those that have controversy have more strict league determine these cause if the first arbiter can compose they and both part be bind to rest in the judgement of the great part and if the voice be equal on each part as many time it fall out a new judge or arbiter be choose who do not give a new judgement of his own but approve one of the judgement give by the equal voice of the say burgess and this arbiter be choose by those burgess and so he be a citizen of any one canton it be not require that he shall be of either of the canton to who the cause belong thus if bern be plaintiff against the 3 canton 16 man be choose by the canton out of which bern choose one to be arbiter but if the canton be plaintiff against bern they choose a arbiter out of the lesser senate of bern. likewise in controversy between Zurech and bern the plaintiff choose a arbiter out of the senate of the other city to conclude in all judgement public and private they use such integrity as this simplicity of their judgement disallow by subtle politician happy succeed in all occasion and so they retain their old virtue be like ever so to succeed in most of the canton namely at Zurech Basil and Schafhusen no bastard may bear public office nor be a senator or judge which law be common to the sweitzer with the german first institute to restrain fornication and to preserve the dignity of marriage in some place he must have be a citizen ten year in other place twenty year who be choose to be of the common counsel and at Zurech no stranger be ever choose to be a senator or judge and by common law no homicide adulterer or infamous person for any crime may be of the senate in all the canton they be no less careful to prevent damage by fire then to keep out their enemy for which cause they hire watchman to walk the street by night and belman to tell the hour and in some place as the town of saint Gallus they have nightly thirty two watchman and choose citizen to visit the chimney and oven that they be free from danger of fire in other canton they have public officer who in any such chance see that all thing be do in order and that no tumult be raise upon such occasion to which end they appoint some to quench the fire and draw other in arm to defend the wall and the gate and at Zurech able young man be yearly choose to be ready for the quench of any such casual fire in the law of retribution a arm for a arm a leg for a leg be in many case observe where he that kill a citizen be the cause never so just as repel force by force shall die if he be take or be perpetual banish if he escape by flight yet when he have satisfy the kinsman of he be kill he be permit to return from banishment and in all the canton where they dwell in village he that kill a man in his defence shall be banish and his own senate can permit his return which can only be obtain from the great and public senate and in the same canton no land may be engage to any stranger neither may any stranger buy any possession but only a house and a garden for herb and if any man often offend in drunkenness he be imprison and may drink no wine for a year till he have procure pardon of the public senate which i think shall easy be grant he by judge guilty of the same fault except they mean quarrel and like offence not simple drink which I think probable because general the Sweitzers drink as stiff as those of the upper part of Germany in the same canton matrimonial cause be refer to the consistory of the bishop of Costnetz but all adultery be punish by the senate at home common with the loss of good sometime with a fine of ten pound that be ten dollar with they the public edict be yearly in these canton confirm or abrogate by the voice of the common people and in the town of Friburg and the territory if a debtor pay not his debt the creditor send certain servant and horse to the public inn the charge whereof be pay by the debtor till he satisfy his creditor beside in any controversy if surety be thrice demand of any man and he bring not in surety or caution he be punish with banishment and the same punishment be inflict on they who violate the command of keep the peace and who without just cause take part with either of they that be at variance in general for the gentry of the whole province mention have be and be after to be make that the same be extinguish so as it be in vain to seek for any knightly order among these man who howsoever they be military man yet universal be citizen or of common plebeian rank they take to themselves coat of arm devise by themselves and trick after their own fancy yet not with open helmet as gentleman bear they but with close helmet after the manner use by the citizen in Germany and their law of inheritance and the dowry of wife do come near to those of Germany the civil law if I be not deceive pass with they into provincial law and custom by which they be govern upon the old and long continue use of they in one particular example I observe that the young brother in the division of his father inheritance first choose his part and have liberty to buy the part of his brother if he will and not otherwise but I shall have occasion to speak of the common law more at large in the discourse of the several commonwealth among they the league which the sweitzer have with foreign prince do manifest show that they profess mercenary arm no less yea much more than the german for their warfare whereas the german be hire for present service in time of war these man beside that pay must have ample pension in time of peace as their league with France especial show in this they differ that the sweitzer only send aid of foot but the german be hire both horse and foot and both these nation have one commendable property that after their service one or more year in the war peace be make they return home nothing corrupt with military licentiousness and round fall to the plough or any other their trade of life by the same league it appear that they will not serve in any sea-fight nor in the defence or take of fort neither will have their force divide as if they repute the strong body of their band only fit to fight in a pitch field and to defend the great ordinance and carriage neither use they to fortify their own city except few which of old be fortify and after receive into the number of the canton brag with the lacedaemonian that valiant breast be brazen wall in the time of julius Caesar we read that this nation be populous and weary of the barren soil wherein they dwell resolve to seek a new seat but be soon restrain and keep at home by the arm of caesar. from that time we read of no great warlike exploit do by they till they lay the first foundation of their commonwealth by mutual league the first perpetual league make between the three first canton be in the year 1315 from which time the rest of the sweitzer have long be subject to the house of Austria begin by part to rebel against that house and to win their liberty by the sword but all their war be at home long continue against the say house and at last break out against the duke of Burgundy upon their confine till the year 1477 when in the three battle the duke of Burgundy be slay and so that war end at which time only eight canton be unite in perpetual league the other five canton be after unite at several time from the year 1481 to the year 1513 when the 13 and last canton be unite to the rest in perpetual league touch their foreign war the first league they make for year be in the year 1478 and the second in the year 1510 with two pope the first perpetual foreign league they make be with the duke of Milan in the year 1466 wherein mention be make of former league with the insubres but we read no effect of war produce by they and the first perpetual league they have with France be in the year 1483 when Charles the eight make war in Italy for the kingdom of Naples about which time the sweitzer arm begin to be know in foreign part Guicciardine the famous writer of those italian war among the action of the year 1500 say that the sweitzer hire by Lodowick Sforza duke of Milan fight well on his side at the take of Novara but after that their captain be corrupt to betray he by the captain of other sweitzer serve the french king whereupen they provoke the multitude to mutiny for pay but the duke appease they by love word by present pay in good part and promise of the rest upon the come of money from Milan daily expect that the captain of the duke sweitzer conspire with the sweitzer of the french king to make the french present draw to novaria which do the duke prepare to fight but the captain of his sweitzer answer he that without special authority from their magistrate they will not fight against their kinsman and countryman on the french side and that so the sweitzer serve the duke upon their captain instigation mingle themselves with the swisser on the french side as if they have be both of one army say they will depart home and that the duke can with no prayer nor promise move their barbarous treachery to stand with he in this distress nor so much as to conduct he to a safe place only grant he to march in their band on foot disguise like a sweitzer in which disguise take of force he with some of his chief friend be take by the french move compassion of all man towards he and detestation of their treachery and this author leave it in doubt whether they be find out in this disguise by the french spy or rather vile betray Semler a famous writer of the sweitzer nation think that soldier in general may be excuse who be in a town unfortify and have other just cause as disability to withstand the enemy shall make peace and return home but grant this fact to be unexcusable yet whether it be do by the captain or by the common soldier or by both and that on both side he think it a great wrong to impute the same to the whole nation especial those soldier be levy secret and without leave of the magistrate the foresay author Guicciardine in the action of the year 1511 write of the sweitzer to this effect the sweitzer of old call helvetian inhabit the high place of the mountain jura man fierce by nature clown and by reason of the barren soil rather crasier then ploughman of old they be subject to the prince of Austria but cast off their yoke have long be free live after their own law and yield no sign of obedience to the emperor or any other prince divide into thirteen canton whereof each be govern by their own magistrate law and custom the name of this so wild and uncivil nation have get honour by concord and the glory of arm for be fierce by nature and train in warlike discipline and keep their order or rank they have not only with valour defend their country but in foreign part have exercise arm with high praise which no doubt have be great if they have fight to enlarge their own empire and not for wage to enlarge the empire of other and if noble they have propound to themselves other end then the gain of money by the love whereof be make abject they have lose the occasion to become fearful to all Italy for since they never come out of their confine but as mercenary man they have have no public fruit of their victory but by covetousness have become intolerable in exaction where they overcome and in demand with other man yea at home froward and obstinate in traffic and in follow their command under who pay they serve in war their chief man have pension of prince to favour they in their public meeting and so public counsel be refer to private profit they be apt to be corrupt and by degree fall at discord among themselves with great lessen of the reputation they have get among stranger he add that the sweitzer at the pope instigation arm against the french in Milan as if it be only the act of Suitia and Friburg who pretend offence against the french for a messenger of they kill by they and that the french king for spare a small addition to their pension neglect to reconcile himself to they and so lose their friendship which after he will have redeem with great treasure hope that either they will not arm against he or if they do that have no horse nor artillery they can do he small hurt the same Guicciardine in the action of the year 1513 witness that the sweitzer have then get great reputation by the terror of their arm and that it seem then that their state or burgess and soldier begin to carry themselves no more as graser or mercenary man but as senator and subject of a well order commonwealth and that they now sway all affair almost all christian prince have their ambassador with they by pension and great reward seek to have league with they and to be serve by they in their war but that hereupon they grow proud and remember that by their arm the french king Charles the eight have get the kingdom of Naples and Lewis the twelve the dukedom of Milan with the city and state of Genoa and victory against the venetian they begin to proceed insolent in the affair with prince that the french king Francis the first than woo they and to have audience give they the fort of Lugana and of Lugarna with such indignity do prince then seek their friendship yet that he can not obtain his demand but that they rather choose upon ample condition of profit to assist the duke of Milan also in the action of the year 1516 when the emperor join with other prince in league against the king of France he write that the sweitzer according to their league serve both on the emperor and the french king side and that the emperor know the hatred that nation bear to the house of Austria fear lest the sweitzer on his own part shall serve he as they serve the duke of Milan at novaria think it more probable in that he want money to content they whereof the french king have plenty and that he fear this the more because their general captain have with much insolency demand pay for they and that hereupon the emperor retire with his army the Sweitzers not follow he but stay at Lodi which after they sack and so return home of the other side he write that only some few of the sweitzer be at first come to the french party who profess to be ready to defend Milan but that they will in no wise fight against their countryman on the other side that the french complain of the slow come of the rest and at first doubt lest they shall not come and when they come fear no less lest they shall conspire with their countryman serve the emperor or leave upon pretence of their magistrate command they shall sudden leave they and return home that the french just complain thus of their slow come purposely use to be affect by they and continue to doubt of their faith especial because they have always say that they will not fight with their country man and to fear as before lest the canton shall recall their man from serve the french which fear after increase when they see two thousand of they already return home and doubt that the rest will follow also in the action of the year 1526 he write that the french king make request to have a great leafy of sweitzer hope they will ready serve he the rather to blot out their ignominy in the battle of Pavia but that this nation which not long before by their fierce nature have opportunity much to increase their state have now no more either desire of glory or care of the commonwealth but with incredible covetousness make it their last end to return home lade with money manage the war like merchant and use the necessity of prince to their profit like mercenary corrupt man do all thing to that end in their public meeting and that the private captain according to the necessity of prince stand upon high term make most impudent and intolerable demand that the french king require aid of they according to his league they after their accustom manner make long consultation and in the end answer that they will send no aid except the king first pay they all pension due in be a great sum and not sudden to be provide which their delay be very hurtful to the king make his army long time lie idle by the premise we may gather that the sweitzer arm be first make know to foreign part about the year 1483 that they increase in reputation to the year 1513 when they attain to the height of their glory which fall in few year by the foresay jealousy and covetous practice and no marvel for their league and levy be make with huge expense their band be great consumer of victual and waster of the country they pass they make frequent and great mutiny for pay they have league with the emperor as possess the archdukedome of Austria with the king of Spain as Arch-duke of Austria by title as heir to the duke of Burgundy and conqueror of the dukedom of Milan and with the king of France upon ample pension now all the war of those time have be manage by these prince and the sweitzer by league serve on all side since they will not fight against their countryman small trust can be place in their auxiliary band if any man speak of the king of England he do not in those time lead any army into the continent but associate with one of the king of Spain or France or with the emperor in which case the sweitzer serve upon the same condition on both side and if any of their confederate shall make war with the king of England at home they shall have no use of sweitzer who condition in their league not to be send beyond the sea nor to be employ in naval fight if any man speak of the war in netherlands the sweitzer will be find no less unprofitable to their confederate those war consist in take and defend strong place and the sweitzer covenant in their league not to have their body divide nor to serve in that kind and in truth since all the rage of late war common lie in defend and assail fort and set battle be rare fight it may seem strange they shall thus divide themselves from the common danger of the army in which they serve and all these thing consider I find not what use their confederate can make of they but only in civil war against their own subject with who the sweitzer have no league for the rest as we read of some indian who light one candle to the devil leave he shall hurt they and another to God that he may do they good so I think prince still entertain their expensive league rather leave their enemy shall be strengthen by their entire aid then for any profit themselves can reap thereby the sweitzer have no horse which be of no use in the mountain and craggy place of their country but when they make their own war out of their own confine their confederate be by league bind to supply they therewith and if the war be not their own their confederate only expect auxiliary band of foot from they the roman Boterus write that in his judgement the sweitzer can make six score thousand foot for the defence of their own country no doubt that nation be very populous but the great army we ever read they to have carry out of Sweitzerland be that of thirty one thousand when they join with the pope Leo the ten the emperor Maximilian and Sfortia duke of Milan be confederate against the king of France nature and necessity have frame they to the war for a mountainous region and woody as of old it be be still somewhat barren and laborious to the husbandman breed a rude people patient of hardness and of warlike disposition and as tall tree and large cattle so strong body of man so as they seem to be bear soldier necessity likewise force they to arm when the gentleman and prince oppress they and they have no mean of liberty but arm wherein long use have make they expert and their very law and custom be fit to the war all citizen and plebeian use and be command continual to wear their sword all their
