several exercise have a reference to the war as shoot with musket at butt which they practise for wager both in city and village leap cast of stone wrestle fence swim continual hunt wherein they pursue bear wild boar and lynx a kind of wolf the shoot of the boy in bow the use of drum in stead of music even at feast of marriage where the bridegroom be think most honour who be meet by his friend with most shot and pike all private man be bind to have their arm sit for war and there with be common arm though many time the worst furnish be supply out of the armoury of the city their kind of arm be musket calciver ashen pike 18 foot long halberd long two-handed sword which they carry on their shoulder and with they they defeat the burgundian come to hand stroke with they and another long sword gift to their side with a dagger very heavy the hafte of silver or guild and armour of solid steel for breast and back but the poor sort have only helmet of iron and thick leather pelt in stead of armour and some in stead of armour wear coat of quilt taffata wrought with aglet-hole they who will appear brave than the rest carry feather white or of some other colour common near the colour of their own banner each wear a right corner cross upon his arm which be the military badge of the sweitzer all follow the colour and banner of their own canton and use drum trumpet and bagpipe and a man can hardly distinguish between the beat of the drum of the sweitzer and german save that the former march be more grave and slow and not so tumultuous as that of the german the blow a horn of a wild hart which they call the bul. the have the like but those of use a horn of brass no man that can wear arm be excuse from war at home and no doubt their foot be of great force to fight within their mountain and keep themselves from tyranny of stranger howsoever they have not so much strength when with the snail they come out of their house man choose in peace be train for the war but in foreign expedition one man choose another that be acquaint and friend they may stick close together and when they be to march the law command they to lay aside all private quarrel so as they may more true be call brother than the landtznechts or foot of Germany who call themselves brother yet bring home more wound and scar from their private quarrel then from the enemy it be a capital crime with the sweitzer to fall to the spoil before the enemy be full overcome the public spoil as artillery castle country and tribute or any revenue belong equal to all the canton though some of they set forth five time more man than other yet extraordinary reward be give to the best deserve canton and private man they just give all protection to those that bring victual to the campe. they have a old law always to spare holy place and the sex of woman except such woman as give weapon to their enemy or by cast down stone and like help do hurt unto their army they boast that their fouresquared body of foot be the best form of battle to resist horseman and that thereby they have no horse do overthrow the french horse at novaria and when for want of artillery and by the great number of the french they be beat by they at Marignano yet that they retire in a close body and good array to Milan so as they can not be just say to fly it remain to speak something but brief of the government of the particular canton particular commonwealth because they have absolute power within themselves among they such as have no town but dwell in village call the head of their counsel Ammans and the chief power be in the common people such be Urania Suitia Vnderueldia Tugium Glarona 13 canton in three farm again some have town or city which command the canton and the same especial those that be build by prince or be subject to they be govern aristocratical by chief man namely a senate choose out of all the citizen and call their chief magistrate Scultet vulgar Schuldte Hessen such be bern Friburg solothurn three other town or city be divide into tribe or company and the senator be choose out of these tribe by the voice of the people whereof the chief be call Burgomeister such be Zurech Basill and Schafhusen among those of the first form dwell in village I name Glarona Abatifcella the six town and village of the first form vulgar Apenzill and vulgar zug for howsoever they have town yet the territory or canton be not command by the town have only equal right with all the inhabitant of the country all town and village of this form whereof I name six have a precedent of their counsel call amman that be amptman signify a man of Office the be divide into ten part call ten by the vulgar name the be divide into four part call quarter the be part with a wood of Oakes and thereby be divide into the upper and low and the whole canton have the name of the low as dwell under the wood and be their chief village zug for the town consist of two and for the county of three convent or meeting Glarona consist of fifteen tagwan signify a day tillage Apenzill as well town as country consist of twelve Roden whereof the six inward be of old under the abbot and the six outward be out of his territory either free or subject to private gentleman out of each of these convent or part the senator of the whole canton be choose in equal number be in most of they threescore in number beside those who have have public honour remain perpetual senator have forty five senator that be nine of each convent the town be take for two convent Apenzill have 144 senator namely twelve for each convent in weighty affair for which it seem not good to call the people together the counsel of senator in most place be double or treble each senator choose one or two assessor but only citizen be capable of this dignity and it be much more difficult to obtain freedom of be a citizen with these canton then with the city the high power be in the general meeting of the people to which all be admit of fourteen or sixteen year age and they meet in the midst of the territory or in the chief village of the canton and there be first choose the amman in most place for two year and out of all the people of what part or convent soever he be but at Apenzill he must remove his dwell to the town where public counsel use to be hold and there abide during his office and at Zug he be choose out of the convent by order and course and for the time of his office must dwell in the city next to the amman they choose his deputy call statthalter than the treasurer call Seckelmeister that be master of the purse than the scribe or clerk and other officer in order and this be peculiar to these canton in the seek of any public office that they who seek it be themselves present at the give of voice and themselves their parent and child give voice in their election which be give by lift up the hand from a high place and in case of doubt be number by the Pole the senator be not choose by the whole assembly but each by the inhabitant of his own convent or part beside this public meeting other meeting use to be appoint upon extraordinary occasion namely when ambassador be to be send or any decree be to be make of league peace or war beside the two counsel of all the people and of choose senator most of the canton have a privy counsel of few man thus the have a privy counsel of one senator and one amman choose of each convent or part and this counsel govern the public rent and expense they have two court of judgement one of nine man in which the amman be precedent and that determine the weighty cause of inheritance of defamation and injury the other of seven man in which the Ammans deputy be precedent and that determine civil cause of debt and contract the or canton of Urania have the same course where the court of fifteen man in which the amman be precedent determine civil controversy of great moment and the court of seven man in which the Ammans deputy be precedent judge of debt under the value of threescore pound the have one court of judgement at and another at Sarna and each have a amman for precedent the town or city of zug beside the public counsel of the canton have his proper senate and magistrate or judge in the canton of Glarona the indiciall court of nine man determine of inheritance defamation and injury and that of five man judge debt but only in the two month of May and September judgement be exercise by the judge yearly choose at the general meeting of the canton the canton of Apenzill have two court of judgement one of twenty four man two of each convent or part wherein fine be impose and defamation and injury be judge the other of twelve man call the swear court of judgement because it judge of doubtful controversy and such as be try upon oath and this also observe the breach of statute and determine what cause be to be propound before the senate and this office be perpetual of consistory and matrimonial and spiritual cause handle in other court I shall speak hereafter in the chapter of religion capital cause almost in all these canton be judge by the senate or public counsel and that common double the amman of the canton or his deputy be precedent at zug assessor out of each convent or part be associate to the senate and they sit in a public place where all man may behold the judge and hear their sentence for the court of judgement in the prefecture or governement common a deputy governor and assessor be choose of the inhabitant to join with the governor and they determine as well of civil as criminal cause and these governor in some place be choose for three year some village have municipal right under the canton and there they choose magistrate out of their own village yet they yearly crave this privilege at the public meeting and it be grant they as a singular favour and some of these village have also their peculiar banner and ensign but they bear they not where the great and common banner of the canton be display in the second place be the canton as former be show over which the town four town of the second form command not divide into tribe or company namely bern Lucern Friburg and solothurn in which it be forbid by the law that they shall be divide into tribe but the artisan have their college or Halles not for the choose of magistrate but for order of the art and these they call geselscafften that be society or fellowship not tribe or company which be vulgar call zunfften in these canton the chief magistrate be vulgar call schuldthessen that be set over debt who I may call consul and they have two counsel the great and the less the great at bern have the name of two hundred though they be more in number and the lesser be of twenty six man at Lucern the great be of one hundred man and each half year eighteen govern the commonwealth by course at bern when they choose the senate the four ensign of the city take to they sixteen out of all the citizen and these twenty man with the consul choose the great senate first they inquire after those two hundred that be of the senate and if any of they have disgrace his dignity by any ill act they put he out of his place and towards easter they choose new senator into the place of they and of such as be dead and the sergeant make this election know to they that be choose all the magistrate be choose and then all the citizen have feast in their several college or Halles and after dinner they pass the time in game and diverse exercise then the consul with the twenty man call the elector do meet again and choose the senator of the lesser counsel who the day follow be approve by the great counsel at Lucern twice each year they make election of senator into the great or lesser counsel if any place be void namely about each solstice of the year and the senator be choose by those of the great counsel who govern the last half year the consul be choose by the common voice of both the counsel and these consul be choose for a year at Lucern for two year at bern though for form yearly voice be give and so they may seem to be new choose at bern the chief authority next to the consul be give to the four ensign vulgar call Venner which be choose of the four college or hals of the smith shoemaker baker and butcher and the city be divide into four part each part commit to one of the ensign to visit their arm and oversee all military duty and they execute this place four year though for form they yearly resign their banner up to the senate and if in the mean time any one of they die another be set in his place to fulfil the rest of the year as deputy to his predecessor and then exercise the place four year more for himself in all the city of Sweitzerland the treasurer or tribune of the exchequer be of great reputation who exercise that office not for any set time but so long as the senate will and themselves like at bern the consul the ensign and the treasurer add one senator of the counsel of 200 make the privy counsel to which all secret be first bring in these canton as I say the magistrate be choose by public voice and so be the officer but the place of less dignity as sergeant and watchman be bestow by the lesser counsel it be peculiar to those of bern that they admit no man into the lesser counsel who be not bear in the city and of old they admit not the very son of senator into that counsel if they be bear out of the city but in these day for the public good the son of the absent be as if they be bear in the city but into the great counsel they admit those that be bear out of the city so they be the child of sweitzer or any confederate for stranger be also exclude from be of the senate of 200. all bastard or infamonus person be exclude from be of any senate at all the canton of bern have three court of judgement the judge of they be choose by the ensign and treasurer and confirm by the lesser counsel the first be call the outward court in which the consul be precedent but almost continual the chief apparater or sergeant supply his place and he have twelve assessor or assistant whereof one be the last choose ensign and another be choose of the lesser counsel the rest be ten be choose out of the great counsel or senate and to they be add one clerk and two appariter this court judge of debt of lesser injury as ill word and light blow and appeal be grant from it to the lesser senate from that to the sixty man be senator choose out of both the senate and from they to the great counsel and these judge meet daily Tuesday only except on which they have a market the second court of judgement hear the appeal make from those of the canton which dwell in the territory of Savoy whereupon it be call Das Weltsch gericht that be the stranger justice in which the treasurer of the french revenue be precedent and he have ten assessor but only Losanna though of the same territory be exempt from this judgement whether once every two year a judge be send with certain assessor to hear their appeal a three court of judgement call the consistory belong to the discourse of religion the canton of have two court of judgement one call Das wuchengevicht that be the weekly justice which determine of debt and contract the other of nine man in which injury and reproach be punish in the canton of bern and Lucern capital crime be not try by any special judge but both the senate sit and give sentence upon they at bern after sentence be give in the senate the consul set in the public tribunal where the clerk read in write the prisoner confession and the sentence of the senate which do the consul command the hangman to do execution and the prisoner to be deliver to his hand and at the senate judge all capital crime not only for the canton but also for the presecture or governement and all execution be do within the city whereas bern appoint special judge for capital crime in the governement the governor be precedent but their sentence there give may be change or mitigate by the senate of bern which command over large governement than any other canton at Friburg the great senate consist of two hundred senator which manage all public affairee and thing of great moment the lesser counsel or senate be of twenty four man and judge of citizen cause and appeal make by the subject and the consul be precedent of both senate who be choose by all the people for 3 year upon the day of s. John Baptist as the choise of senator be yearly make upon the Sunday next before that feast the four ensign be next to the consul and be set over the 4 part of the city and howsoever they be not senator of the lesser senate yet they be always present at their meeting in the name of the people and they perform this office for three year be choose by both the senate as most of the magistrate be likewise choose by they the treasurer be next in dignity who oversee the treasure and the building and hold his office for three year but yield account twice every year to the senate they have four chief clerk or secretary and the office of the chief apparater exercise by one man for three year be honourable here as at bern for common he assist the consul and when the senate meet stand at the door take the senator voice and have the care of captive at Friburg they have a court of justice call the city court which judge the citizen cause take the examination of captive and put the accuse to the rack or torment but after refer all to the senate they have another court of justice for the country wherein the cause of subject dwell out of the city be determine in both court be two of the lesser senate and eight judge of the great senate choose for three year and they meet thrice every week and appeal be admit from they to the lesser senate also twelve judge choose out of both the senate determine the appeal of the prefecture or governement meet once every month for that purpose and from they there be no appeal the governor be choose by both the senate and hold that office for five year but give account yearly before the lesser senate and they judge capital crime in their governement but the senate have power to change mitigate or approve their sentence as they judge meet in the three place it remain to speak of the three form of government in the three city of the three form three city distribute into tribe or company namely Zurech Bazill and schafbusen wherein the state be divide into two order of the noble and plebeian they have a peculiar society of those call noble which be vulgar call Eingeselschafft and at Zurech Etn Constaffell but Bazill for the great number of they have two society which have the chief authority the consul be choose of one and the tribune next in dignity choose of the other till the nobility be remove from government or rather free give it over for these gentleman first join with the house of Austria and be after banish with they till the year 1501 a perpetual league be make with the house of Austria and the gentleman return from banishment but hate the common people leave the city to dwell in their castle whereupon their authority be much diminish and that which remain they utter lose in the year 1529 when they leave the city and oppose themselves to the reformation of religion decree by the senate yet the say two society in name and their public house of the society and the private house in their possession remain to they at this day but none of the gentleman be choose into the senate be exclude by the common consent of the citizen from the government of the commonwealth which they willing forsake so as the gentleman have real no peculiar society only some few of they dwell continual in the city be number in the four chief tribe or company of the citizen and in they be choose into the senate as citizen and these four company be call the company of the lord or gentleman at Zurech they have a peculiar society of gentleman which have this privilege that half as many more be choose into the senate out of it as out of any other tribe and in this very society of gentleman there be difference among themselves for the old family have a peculiar society and a private stoave wherein they only meet and many citizen be join to the whole society who neither exercise any art nor trade of merchandise and because porter and the base sort must be number in some tribe or company all these for occasion of war be number in this society of the gentleman call Constaffel and under the same they serve in the war yea and give their voice in the choice of the master of the society who be one of the senate also at Schafhusen the gentleman have a peculiar society but in all these city the people be divide into tribe or company vulgar call Zunft whereas the gentleman society be call Geselschafft or Constaffell at there be 15 tribe whereof 4 be call the tribe of the lord or gentleman namely of the merchant of the goldsmith of the vintner of the apothecary and silkeman the most populous of all other and the other eleven be plebeian tribe of all kind of artisan Zurech have twelve tribe for the weaver of woollen cloth be few be number among the dyer Schafhusen have but eleven tribe wherein sometime artisan of diverse art be join in one tribe but each art have his peculiar hall and these be call the divide tribe and they meet in their peculiar Halles when they consult of any thing concern their private art but they meet in the common hall of the tribe for cause touch the commonwealth as the choice of senator or master of each tribe in the say city be two counsel the great when many meet in the name of the people to consult of weighty cause belong to the commonwealth and the lesser which daily sit in judgement at Zurech the great counsel or senate be of 200 man and the lesser of 50. at Basil the great be of 244 the lesser of 64. at Schafhusen the great be of 86 the lesser of 26 senator to these add two consul the head or precedent of public counsel in each of these city and this be common to all these city that each tribe have two master choose for half or a whole year which time end other succeed in that place yet common he that be master the last half year be choose again except there be some impediment the lesser senate be divide into new and old and that be call the old whereof the senator have serve half a year and these be not always call to the meeting for some business only belong to the new senate at Zurech the two senate be change each half year and the old senate at the half year end choose the new but at Basil and Schafhusen they remain in office a whole year and the master of the tribe be choose by their own tribe and confirm by the great senate but they be confirm by the old senate at basil. the voice be open take at Zurech but secret at Schafhusen for certain man be set over the election in who ear they give their voice soft whisper the lesser counsel or senate meet common thrice or four time each week the consul be precedent of both senate and be choose by the great senate for half a year and in some place for a year the tribune be join with the consul for head and precedent of the senate and at Basil nine other be join to they who make the counsel of thirteen to who the more weighty affair be refer to consider of they before they be propound to the whole senate Zurech have a peculiar counsel which may be call the exchequer court consist of eight man choose four out of each senate and to they all exchequer account be refer two clerk or secretary be present at public counsel with assistant join to they if need require and the office of these secretary especial of the chief be honourable and gainful and not easy confer on any but a patrician because they must have full knowledge of the law custom privilege and all secret of the commonwealth Zurech have two public court of judgement or justice one of eight judge choose out of the lesser counsel or senate who determine civil cause debt and the like and from they there be no appeal but themselves refer the most difficult matter to the senate the other determine the cause of the revenue Basil have two court of justice in the great town and a three in the lesser town the great court consist of ten judge who be partly take out of the senate partly out of the people and they determine civil and criminal cause but the burgomaster or mayor be precedent for civil cause and the advocate of the empire for criminal and three man call the capital of senator degree plead and prove inditement against malefactor but at Zurech and Schafhusen the new senate judge capital cause yet the consul or burgomaster be not then precedent as at other time but the advocate of the empire who the senate by special privilege choose yearly out of their own body and at Basil capital judgement be give in a public place but at Zurech in a close private court with the door shut and at Schafhusen the accusation and defence be make in open court but all be exclude when the senate give judgement the lesser court of the great town at Basil do only determine small controversy not exceed the value of ten pound the court of justice in the lesser town of Basill have his own burgomaster or consul and determine all cause except criminal at schafbusen the city court of justice determine of debt contract and the like but if the sum of the controversy exceed the value of one hundred gold gulden the senate iudgo it and this city court have twenty assessor namely one of each tribe and eight other choose by the senate it have another court of justice for the mulct or fine consist of twelve man and the advocate of the empire be precedent thereof and this court impose fine and judge the criminal cause of less weight as small injury and vulgar reproach for the senate determine of the great touch the magistrate and officer of these city the consul call burgomaster be of chief dignity than the tribune then diverse treasurer and officer about the revenue and tribute the next degree be of those officer who have the care of public building and work than those who have the care of victual as those who look to the weight and goodness of bread and those who oversee the shambles that no unsound meat be sell and that all thing be sell at a moderate price which they set down and appoint how flesh shall be sell by the pound in like sort the overseer of the fish market and salt meat and butter and cheese likewise the officer who protect orphan and widow who dispense public aim govern those house and who oversee weight and measure and the public school some of the prefecture or governement belong to the city be govern by the senate of the city so as the governor remain senator in the city and only at-set day go to the village for administration of justice but the senate only judge of capital cause but to those governement which be large and far distant they send governor who judge not only civil but most capital cause in privilege custom and peculiar court of judgement where the prefecture have power to choose judge among themselves the governor alter nothing therein but only sit as precedent in their judgement these their right always preserve thus among other the city of Zurech have two pleasant town subject to it which be rule by the law of Zurech but have their own magistrate and serve Zurech in war but under their own colour and this shall suffice touch the commonwealth general and particular of the canton among the fellow in league be the abbot and town of saint Gallus the of the fellow in league abbot be number among the prince of the empire but his power be much diminish in these day yet he set governor over many place and his Ammans do justice in his name also he have institute a high court of justice to which appeal of the abbot and town of s. Gallus be make from the lesser court and beside he have officer of all kind after the manner of prince the town as likewise that of mulhuse and Rotevil be number among the city of the empire and it as the other two have the form of a common wealth former describe save that this town of saint Gallus have some peculiar thing it have six tribe whereof one be of gentleman it have two senate the great and the lesser in which lesser senate be four and twenty senator namely three consul nine senator and twelve master of the tribe for each tribe have three master choose by the tribe and confirm by the lesser counsel or senate and one of they yearly by course govern each tribe be six in number the other two be of the senate and make twelve and twice every year be the choice make of the senate and magistrate the first of the three consul exercise that office for the present year the second do exercise it the year before and the three be judge of capital crime and the consul be yearly choose by the whole assembly of the people the great senate consist of sixty six man this town have also a inferior consul or as I may say a deputy consul the lesser senate judge civil cause the great meet five time in the year and judge of appeal and of take new inhabitant and the like and extraordinary it be call often as for judge capital cause at which time the advocate of the empire who I say to be the three consul be precedent of the counsel the whole people be call together thrice in the year first when the consul be choose 2. when oath be give to the new consul three when the ordination of tribute be read before the people and the law divide into three part be read before the pepole at these three meeting the first court of justice be of five man which judge of debt of wage or hire of victual of injury and fine without appeal the court of justice for the city be of twelve man change twice each year and from it appeal be admit to the lesser senate so the cause be above the value of five pound but if he that appeal lose the cause he pay a fine to the judge the common people of the town and country live by make woollen cloth whereupon strict law be make for the same that the web undress be view by three skilful man and be mark according to the goodness and if it be faulty be rend in the midst through the breadth or be burn where any great fault be find and that public beside a fine impose upon the weaver after swear man measure and mark the cloth beside other officer who curious and particular view each cloth I say before in the history of this town that it make war upon the abbot when he seek to remove from it to another town more absolute in his power not only the gainful trade of clothworking but also the holy relic whereby in those day great gain come to they among the rhetian or grison each convent or meeting or community have his amman and chief magistrate yearly choose and a general governor of the whole of the grison league call Landtrichter that be justice of the land yearly choose at the public meeting they have many convent or meeting but only three league the head of the second league call the house of God be the city of Chur which have a cathedral church and the commonwealth thereof be not unlike that of Zurech the three league have but one commonwealth for howsoever most place have their own magistrate and law or rather custom and court of justice aswell for civil as criminal cause yet the chief power be in the common or public senate of the three league consist of the burgess of the several convent not unlike the general senate of the sweitzer and the meeting of all the people be seldom call but they have another counsel or senate of the chief man namely the provincial judge of the upper league the consul of Chur for the league of the house of God and the amman of the three league of the ten jurisdiction with other choose man join to they but this counsel have not full power for the act thereof be refer to the community of the league and that stand in force which the great part of they do confirm and the judgement of such cause as be refer to the several community be register in a write book they determine controversy and give judgement as the sweitzer do among their statute it be decree by common consent that the bishop of Chur or any ecclesiastical person shall not appoint any civil magistrate but that they shall be choose by the voice of the people the three league have their prefecture or government under they and the governor of their italian prefecture as of those under the sweitzer be vulgar call Podesta from who the subject may appeal the three league by course appoint these governor for two year and the convente or community by course in their own league name the say governor for two year touch the valesian the convent of upper Valesia be seven and of the low of the valesian be six the bishop of sedune be the prince of the country or region who be name the earl and governor of the same and he be choose by the cannon of the church at sedune and by the burgess send from the seven convent of upper Valesia the captain of the country be next to the bishop and be choose by the bishop and the say burgess for two year and confirm by the publke consent of the several convent and to he all civil cause be refer each convent have a chief magistrate or mayor or Castellan who with the senate of that counent judge civil and capital cause and under he be the amman whichis the high officer in the cautons dwelling in village appeal be admit from all the several convent to the public senate of Valesia consist of burgess choose by the convent and this senate meet at sedune twice every year and the bish op sit in that counsel and the baly take the voice by this senate the commonwealth be govern the governor and public officer be choose and it be call the high court from which there be no appeal the lord of chiurone of old be of great authority and be the marshal of the bishopric of sedune viscount of sedune and seneschal or steward of Valesia the valesian have a peculiar statute to repress the violence of mighty man the common wealth be govern by the bishop and the seven conuent of upper Valesia who lower Valesia obey be distribute into six prefecture or government and three other prefecture out of Valesia take or subdue in the savoian war be also subject to they the town of Bipenne have league with the three canton for civil cause acknowledge of the town of Bipenne the bishop of Basil and for ecclesiastical jurisdiction be under the bishop of Losanna but have cast of the yoke of the papacy and obtain immunity from the bishop of Losanna when that bishopric and city be take and make subject to the canton of bern. the bishop of Basil appoint the mayor out of the senate of the town and the mayor take a oath from the citizen and they likewise a oath from he and he with the senate judge criminal cause and be precedent for capital judgement the bishop have half of all fine above three pound and certain tithe with some other revenue but the custom imposition and tribute belong to the city the citizen serve the bishop of Basil in war but no further from the town than they may return home the same day but if he will use they further he must hire they with pay the same privilege be grant to this town by the bishop in the year 1382 which he grant to the lesser town of basil. the public senate as well the great as the lesser be yearly choose by all the citizen and the master of the citizen or burgomaster be next in authority to the mayor and be choose by both the senate and when they consult of the commonwealth the mayor and the officer of the bishop go out of the counsel the consul tribune judge and other officer be choose by both the senate only the ensign be choose by all the people and he with the consul have the care of pupil this town have some subject and their conuent without any governor exercise judgement but the great matter be refer to the senate of the town the stipendiary town or city of the canton have two counsel or senate of the stipendiary city and he that be precedent of the public counsel be call schuldthess as set over debt and at baden he be choose by both the senate also they have their officer their exchequer and tribute belongiug to each city but at baden the custom at the gate belong to the town but the imposition upon merchandise belong to the canton to which the town be subject last they have jurisdiction in civil criminal and capital cause among they the town of Frawenfeld redeem itself from the servitude of the monastery of Auria for no small part of the citizen be ecclesiastical slave to that monastery at this day it give a oath to the lord of Auria the privilege always preserve and that monastery be incorporate to the bishopric of Constantia vulgar call Costnetz the city judge have also power to judge and punish capital touch the prefecture or governemente under the canton the governor of the governement be send by course from the canton for two year who judge according to the law of the several people and for those beyond the Alps the governor have assistant of the country choose and join with he to judge of capital and more weighty cause but in civil cause he judge alone though sometime he call some of the wise inhabitant to advise he therein the governor about the solstice of the year yield account before the senate of Sweitzerland which then judge the appeal make by the subject they serve the canton in war to which they be subject and they follow the standard of that canton which for the present year give they a governor and in civil war they be bind to follow the great part of the canton to which they be subject the governor of baden be present in the public senate of the sweitzer he take the voice and they be equal be the arbiter of the difference but he have only power in the territory of baden not in the city and there he appoint capital judge for life for their manner be that the judge once choose by he exercise that place so long as they live and the same governor have the power to mitigate their judgement the next in authority be the clerk or secretary and the under or deputy governor two little town of the county or territory of baden have governor from the bishop of Costnetz but they serve the sweitzer in their war and the governor of baden be their judge for capital cause the prefecture or government of most large of all the rest have 50 parish whereof some have their own immunity or privilege the rest be subject to diverse jurisdiction but the sovereign power be in the governor send and choose by the canton except Cella where the citizen have their own government the bishop of Costnetz have only the keep of the castle and half the mulct or fine the seven canton with consent of the lord in the several jurisdiction of all this prefecture of Torg appoint one form of Iustice and the judge impose very great fine which belong to the canton and especial upon crime which have coherence with capital offence namely foul injury breach of peace violence offer by the high way challenger of public way or passage changer of landmark or good commit in trust to their keep breaker of public faith and those who scandal or reproach any magistrate the prefecture of the saranete and the Rhegusci and those of Italy have each a governor vulgar call commissary send from the sweitzer and because the people speak the italian tongue he have a sweitzer skilful in that tongue for his interpreter the people have the power to choose their own magistrate and officer and to determine of thing concern their commonwealth the commissary not intermeddle therewith chap. 5 of the netherlander commonwealth according to the foresay subject of the former chapter lower Germany call of old Belgia and now common the commonwealth of Netherlan in general netherlands which the french name that be low country be divide into seventeen province as I have former show in the geographical description thereof namely seven county of Flaunders of Artois of Hannaw of Holland of Zealand of zutphane and of Namurtz four dukedom of luzenburg of Limburg of Brabant and of Gelderland the lordship or dominion of west Freisland three country or territory or place of jurisdiction of grove of Vtrecht and of Transisola vulgar dland over Ysel to which fifteen province that the number of seventeen may be complete some add the county of Walkenburg which be part of the dukedom of Limburg and other add the two dominion of mecblin and Antwerp which be contain under the dukedom of and how soever it be not my purpose to speak of any other province than those which they call unite and through which only I pass yet it be not amiss in a word or two to show how these principality at first have several prince by little and little grow into one body and in our day through civil war become divide into two part the one of diverse province unite for defeuce of their liberty the other of the rest remain under the obedience of their prince the county of Flaunders have give the name of fleming to all the inhabitant of these provincess before name and the earl thereof when other province Flaunders be erect to dukedom do obstinate retain their own degree lest they shall disgrace their antiquitic with the newness of any ducal or other title and it be manifest that this earl be the first peer of France have the prerogative to carry the sword before the king of France at his coronation and to gird the same to his side be not bind to appear in judgement before his compeer except some controversy be about the property of his earldom or he shall deny justice to his subject and final have the badge of sovereign majesty to raise a army to make war and peace to yield no tribute or subjection to the king of France to punish or pardon his subject to make statute to grant privilege to coin money and to write himself by the grace of God earl which no other prince of France may do but only the duke of Brittany baldwyn earl of Flaunders in the year 1202 become emperor of Constantinople and hold that dignity sixty year after which time the empire return to the greek earl Lodowick die in the year 1383 and Margaret his daughter and heir be marry to Philip duke of Burgundy who by her right become earl of Flaunders Charles duke of Burgundy die in the year 1477 and Marie his daughter and heir be marry to the emperor Maximilian and so Flaunders become subject to the house of Austria for Philip son to Maximilian die before his father and leave two son whereof Charles the elder be emperor the five of that name and heir to his grandfather the house of Austria Maximilian and Charles the emperor take the king of France Francis the first prisoner in the battle of Pavia in the year 1525 force he to renounce all sovereign power over Flaunders and Artois and to yield the right of the house of Anjou to the kingdom of Naples and of the house of Orleans to the dukedom of Milan and of Genoa Charles die and his young brother Ferdinand succeed he in the empire be long before design his successor by be choose king of the roman but he leave all his state of inheritance to his elder son Philip king of Spain the rest of the province by like right of marriage become subject to Maximilian and so fall to Charles and last to the king of Spain except Virecht and the Transisolan dominion which by the yield of the bishop reserve his spiritual right and of the state of those province be join to the rest and so final fall to Philip king of Spain history witness that some of these province do owe homage to the empire the unite province and the rest to the king of France till they fall into the hand of the powerful duke of Borgundy who by diverse transaction take all right from the king of France and because the empire have be ever since in the house of Austria it can seem strange the king of Spain be of the same house that these province have be free of the homage due to the empire the emperor Charles the five happy govern these province with great judgement handle the people gentle who have always be hold under a gentle yoke by their prince enjoy great privilege inviolable keep to they never use to absolute government but have often take arm when their prince impose exaction upon they or break any of their privilege and so bring their prince to just and equal term but his son Philip k. of Spain and many other kingdom stray from his father example in the government of netherlands and obstinate despise his counsel which at his death as it be by his last testament he give he to handle this people gentle and not endure their voluntary and free subjection have cause the great or at least the rich part of these province to fall from he and his heir for upon the first dissension about religion pope Pius the four induce Philip king of Spain to publish a decree in netherlands for the establish of the infamous inquisition first invent in Spain of late to punish the jew and saracen who be christian yet retain their own rite and also for the execution of the decree make in the council of Trent which do more than 400 gentleman make petition to the king to abolish this decree and join the intercession of the emperor send this petition to the king by the hand of diverse lord and gentleman whereof the prince of Egmond be one who have do the king very great service in the battle of saint these petitioner be despise by the spaniard and call geuse that be beggar or poor slave and the king send they back unregarded and send the duke of Alva to go vern netherlands who cruel rage against the professor of the reform religion and behead the prince of Egmond and the earl of horn both knight of the golden fleece and on all side proceed butcherly in the mean time the prince of Orange who former have in vain persuade the prince of Egmond to fly foresee this tyranny with other banish gentleman be go out of netherlands and flee to the prince of Condy in France at last the duke of Alva have bring all in subjection reform the policy and impose a exaction of the ten penny be recall into Spain whither he return with much treasure he have extort and don juvan of Austria succeed in that government in who time the fatal civil war begin in Flaunders and short after mutinous troop call male content join together the civil war neither acknowledge the king nor the state of the province and while don junan pursue they he die in the camp in the year 1578. then Alexander Farnese duke of Parma be make governor of netherlands and the king persist in his purpose to bring that people to absolute subjection and the professor of the reform religion be grievous persecute and all the people be murinous affect for the new and tyrannical exaction of the ten penny without consent of the general state the trouble still continue in Flaunders at last some few province have the unite state the prince of orange for their general in the war strict combine themselves in league for mutual defence so Flaunders and the firm land be leave under the spanish yoke but the confederate province firm resolve to cast off all subjection to the king of Spain institute a new form of common wealth for the prince of Orange wise and variant procure the public good be in the year 1584. traitorous slay with a by a desperate rogue whereupon the city of Flaunders lay the prince of Orange kill open to the duke of Parma but the foresay unite province cast themselves into the protection of the queen of England and if my memory fail not they be thus name Holland Zealand vtreiht England protect they groan west Friesland beside many town for Gelderland some fort and strong city of Brabant and Ostend in Flaunders a town for nearness fit to annoy the enemy and the foresay fort and strong city for the most part lie upon the coast of the sea within land and upon the mouth of the Rhine where it fall into the sea give free traffic by sea to the unite province and forbid the same to the city within land and beside yield this commodity that as the spanish soldier from their fort send frey booter to spoil the unite country of Gelderland groan and Friesland so the soldier of the state may fronthence make incursion upon the country subject to the king of Spain whereby the country people be force to pay large yearly contribution to be free from this spoil the few inhabitant of these small province who man will judge but a breakfast to the spanish army notwithstanding have not only be able to this day to keep out these powerful force from enter their territory but may just brag that they have win many strong fort and town from the Spaniard and carry their army into Flaunders where in a field fight at Newport they obtain a glorious victory against the spaniard and so much in small progress of time have their just and moderate counsel increase their commonwealth govern with great equity and equality as at last forsake as it be by the king of France and for the time have little help from England they alone do not only long defend themselves from the powerful revenge of the Spaniard but stout bear out the war to a wish peace be now no more to be pity but in common judgement rather to be envy and fear by their neighbour mention have be make of the prince of Orange and hereafter mention be to be the house of Nassaw make of his son count Maurice therefore it will not be amiss to say something of this noble family the unite province consist of citizen and the common people there be few gentleman in Friesland and few or none in Holland and Zealand and such kind of plebeian man unfit to lead army they aswell for the commonwealth sake first take the prince of orange for their head as after for thankfulness to he much esteem the family of Nassaw and beside other of that family govern in Friesland and other part make choice of the say prince son Count Maurice to be general of their army but with limit power from the state and he have a double as I think voice in their public meeting in which notwithstanding he seldom or never use to be present his father the prince of Orange have all his inheritance in Brabant and Flaunders except the principality of orange seat in France near the city of marseit and when he suspect the counsel of the king of Spain into who net Count Egmond confident in his innocency and great service do to the king and the count of horn unadvised fall he first withdraw himself into France and after into Holland where as I say he join with the state of the unite province and be much respect and love of they all enjoy from they honourable mean of maintenance well deserve in that his name and assistance much profit the common cause he have many wife first he marry the countess of buren sole daughter and heir to her father and she bear he a son who in the begin of these trouble be a student in the university of Lovan whence the governor call he and send he into Spain and she bear he likewise a daughter which be marry to count hollock a german well respect by the state for his warlike reputation and good service do to they and he with his wife live in Holland when I pass through these part his second wife be the sister to the elector of Saxony by who he have the foresay count Maurice who yet be young succeed his father in the general conduct of the state army and about this time whereof I write have take two strong city in Brabant the inheritance of the prince of Orange namely bredaw and Getrudenberg and because they be part of the earldom of buren some difference be then about they between the say count Maurice and his say sister by the father side wherein it be general say that the state favour the count also the prince have by this wife a daughter after marry to the governor of Friesland his three wife be sister to the duke of Mompensier in France which have be a nun and by she he have six daughter Lewis marry to the palatine of the Rhein Marie then live at hage the three live then in France the four with the count of Schwarthenburg and Francis also then live at hage and a six then bring up in the county of Nassaw his four wife be a french lady of the family Chastillon famous in that worthy admiral of France kill in the parisian massacre and this wife after the prince be slay live then at hage with her only son by he who be bear at Delft in Holland be therefore and for many respect much regard by the hollander and yet be a child be honour with military command and a large stipend for his maintenance and short after have the title of colonel of Holland with no small addition to his mean be now to speak of the magistrate law and degree of order in this commonwealth it will not be amisie first for conjecture of the general estate of netherlands to write some few thing out of Marchantius a Fleming and other approve author particular of the county of Flanders for the pre-eminence it always have over the rest of the province the chief strength of the commonwealth of the commonwealth of Flanders Flanders be in the counsel or parliament of the three general state namely of the clergy the nobility and the four member in steed of the people make the three estate in other kingdom and without the consent of these the earl be never wont to exact money or make war and howsoever the king of Spain have weaken the authority of this counsel yet when subsidy be impose the very form of old proceed comfort the people as a shadow of their old liberty the earl by writing be to appoint the time and place of this assembly or in the earl name the counsel of Flanders use to call together the burgess or deputy thereof in these three state the clergy be of chief dignity as well for their degree as for the greatness of their revenue and many territory under their command and among these be only five bishop till pope Pius the four in the year 1560 establish three new seat of bishop at gant Bruges and Ypre the first degree of nobility be that of baron have their name of banner which they be bind to follow whereof there be very many in Flanders and of they some in late time have be raise to the title of earl and prince in the second rank be the lord of town and village whence gentleman have their surname and they can be number without tediousness but almost all of they have possession give they from some of the feudatory court of the earl of Flanders and differ in many custom but in this all agree that he who have this fee can alienate it without the consent of the prince and the next heir or upon oath give in court that he do it for poverty and want the inheritance of fee descend to the elder son a three part reserve for the young brother so they give over to the elder their part in the other good that be not in Fee and it be a high fault if these lord impose any tribute upon their subject except it be with the consent of the earl of Flaunders there be some of these that be call vassal whereof some be client of a high some of a low degree and the earl of Flanders have about seventeen feudatory court and the number be very great of client in fee depend immediate upon one or other of the say court whereby the earl have many pecuniary profit and other service upon fine and alienation of inheritance in the three rank of gentleman be they who hold inheritance in fee whereof some be tie to the jurisdiction and counsel of the earl as the chancellor so call of correct or cancel write ill draw with blot outlines which dignity be tie to the prepositure of Bruges church and before the counsel of Flaunders be erect this office be of great authority than now it be other of this kind be burgrave or castellane or high sheriff who be set judge over town and castle with prerogative to have a proportion of the mulct or fine which dignity belong to certain family and may be alienate to other by sale or for dowry in marriage and all have not the like but diverse jurisdiction and pre-eminence the Burgrave of (@) have the lordship or command of the town which no other Burgrave have the rule of the weight in the market the custom at the gate capital judgement the fine that be under three pound of Paris and a part with the earl of the great fine and the power to appoint the bailie Scabins and burgomafter and a three part of the good of bastard die without child also the Burgrave of Ypre take a oath to himself of the officer of that town as well as to the earl and he have the fine and power to appoint magistrate other of this three rank of gentleman have warlike office by inheritance as the constable so call of conine and stapel as the stay and uphold of the king who have the high command in the war and the admiral so call of a greek word who have the chief command in naval affair then two marshal so call as skilful in horsemanship for the old dutch call a horse-mar and now a mare be by they call and schalc signify cunning also the steward of the house and the military title still remain hereditary to diverse family but the exercise of the office be take from they other gentleman of this three rank be by inheritance officer to oversee the revenue and to take account such be the treasurer and receiver for the prince rent for perpetual tribute of land and these honour still remain to certain family though these rent be now bring in ready money into the exchequer other gentleman of this three rank have office in court as the master of the household chamberlain cupbearer which office be proper by inheritance to certain family but the master of the game as well for hunt as hawk and the water-grave oversee lake and river for swan fish and other like thing be office give at the prince pleasure and not proper to any family the four rank of gentleman be of those who be adorn with the knightly girdle and they be call guild knight of their golden spur and other ornament which honour the prince give for great service create they with lay a draw sword on their left shoulder and with certain solemnity of word and those who have this title be they never so mean be make gentleman with their posterity and if they be gentleman yet it add dignity to they and because I have make this mention of knight give i leave to add a word of the knightly order of the golden fleece institute by the duke of Burgundy Philip the good in the year 1429 upon the very day of his marriage with Elizabeth of Portugal in imitation of gedions fleece and of the golden fleece fetch by the argonaut of Greece he receive into this order gentleman unblameable for life and valour in arm whereof the prince and his successor be the head or chief precedent and he give each of they a scarlet gown of woollen cloth which his son Charles change into a red velvet gown and a gold chain with his father emblem of a steel strike fire out of a flint upon which chain hang the golden fleece and upon the death of any knight this badge be send back to be bestow upon some other gentleman of merit at first the number of these knight with the prince their head be twenty five but within five year they be increase to thirty five and the emperor Charles the five in the year 1516 make the number fifty one at the first institution this order have four officer a chancellor a treasurer a king at arm and a secretary and in the court of this order the unlawful fly of any knight out of the field and all other crime and the dissension among they be judge without appeal the feast of the order have be keep in diverse place according to the prince pleasure but the arm of the knight be set up in the chancel of the chief church at Bruges where the feast thereof be keep at the first institution in general Flaunders have a great number of lord and gentleman as likewise the dukedom of Luxenburg and adjoin province and they exercise themselves in feed of cattle and tillage but judge ignoble all trade of merchant and profession of manual art they have no immunity as in Artois Henault and all France but bear the same burden of tribute with the people to keep they from sedition while the gentleman hate by they bear the same burden as they do have speak of the two state of the clergy and gentleman it remain to add something of the three state namely the four member which have the place of the common people in other kingdom and they be Ghant Bruges Ypre and Franca that be the free land which four territory have the chief or rather all authority in Flaunders each of these member be exempt from all confiscation of good by old privilege confirm by the emperor Charles the five in the year 1549. other city howsoever they have their magistrate law and revenue to themselves yet in the common counsel or parliament for impose tribute or levy of soldier they follow the four member and all Flaunders be bind to their decree in this general meeting of the state the clergy gentleman lord and burgess of other city consult with the four member but they challenge all authority to decree and sole represent the whole body of Flaunders in the general meeting of all netherlands John duke of Burgundy remove from lile to Ghant the senate call the counsel of Flaunders and give the law to all Flaunders Bruges a most pleasant city be the second member have this privilege above all other city that he who be free of the same by birth gift buy or marriage be free from all confiscation of any good wheresoever find no crime or case except whereas the privilege of other city always except violence offer to the person of the prince his wife and child also Bruges have a stately mint-house with privilege to coin money Ypre be the three member which city I pass over for fear to be tedious the four member be Terra Franca add to the rest be but three at the first institution by Philip the good in the year 1437 with intent to bridle the power of Bruges which city then much repine at the same and never cease to raise tumult till Marie wife to the emperor Maximilian abolish this four member which Charles the five their grandchild short after restore to that dignity among the magistrate some of they do proper belong to the prince affair namely the legal chamber consist of the prince counsellor and be as it be the head of other court the meeting and number whereof be at the prince pleasure but common the meeting be at Ghant and they consult of weighty affair which since have be refer to the prince privy counsel or to the counsel of Flaunders seat at Ghant and to the same be refer all controversy touch fee and appeal from feodnary court which be judge without appeal in the presence of the earl or his bailie there be a chair cushion and sword of estate also the chamber that be the supreme court of account consist of hereditary treasurer yearly meeting at lile for three day who judge without appeal all thing touch receiver with personal and real action belong to the prince patrimony and give oath to new treasurer and dary the three court of account establish at lile consist of a precedent four master five helper and two clerk it examine the account of revenue by rent of land wood custom at gate confiscation fine good leave to the prince as by shipwreck and bastard die without child by homage pension and like profit and all hereditary treasurer and the two general receiver give account in this court Philip the bold give this court great authority but John his son remove the counsellor thereof to the office of justice in Ghant and leave the court at lile to register the prince edict and privilege grant by he four the court call the counsel of Flaunders which I say be remove from lile to Ghant and seem chief in dignity first institute partly by little and little to draw Flaunders from the jurisdiction of Paris in imitation of Brabant Hennault and Holland for which howsoever the prince do homage to the emperor yet they belong not to the jurisdiction of the empire homage and jurisdiction by nature and in themselves be much different but the chief cause of the institution be the long absence of Philip the bold in France during the infirmity of the french king in who time this court former keep in diverse place at the prince pleasure be settle at Ghant and to this court be refer all thing belong to the prince right and authority and the controversy of coiner of the church of the province and of city among themselves and with other and appeal from magistrate and ratify the prince pardon for crime the counsel consist of a precedent of a knightly degree by virtue of his office eight counsellor have yearly stipend four commissary have part of the profit by information and for assessor the procurator and the advocate of the prince the treasurer of the revenue a secretary and a notary beside these court and this say counsel Marchantius mention a court of justice high and without appeal over all netherlands institute by Charles last duke of Burgundy in the year 1473 at Mechlin as be in the centre of netherlands and it judge after that be equal and good in imitation of the parliament of Paris so as suitor need not to follow the earl Court and the prince be chief head of this counsel or in his absence the chancellor he be not present the bishop of Tornay with two precedent ten lay and nine clergy counsellor six master of request who be command to ride on horseback to the senate clad in purple but marry the daughter of the say Charles fear the french and civil war command the cease of judgement in this court which Philip her son restore and in the year 1493 reestablish that court at Mechlin but less and more weak as it still remain and this shall suffice of the magistrate belong to the prince affair other belong to the subject in several country and city such be the scabines and the baily scabines be so call of a german word Schaffen that be to dispatch or of a Hebrew word as the german say these defend the right and privilege of the people determine controversy by the statute and municipal custom or for want of they by the write law and be present when any be torture and judge capital cause the pardon whereof be rather permit to the prince then much use by he and these magistrate be diverse name in diverse place as voegte tutor Portmeister officer of the port or haven Lanthouder that be keeper of the land Kourcher that be choose lord and burgermaster that be master of the citizen under they be the treasurer or receiver in each city and aswell they as the scabines be choose by the commissary of the prince next be the baylies so call of a french word as tutor and keeper and they be diverse call in diverse place namely Schuldheten as judge of debt and they differ from the scabines in that the scabines judge the baily execute their judgement and the prince edict they have stipend these be pay out of the fine they be change after one or two year these continue long in office last they respect the right of the people these of the prince in the village they have officer call (@) who (@) the edict of the magistrate and warn debtor to make payment and upon long delay than be permit by the municipal law sell their good at the outery they have a supreme judge of capital cause who they call sovereign Baily institute in the year 1374 to apprehend murderer and banish man and to put they to death or otherwise punish they with the assistance of two gentleman have fee or be feodatory client to the prince and to this officer authority be late give and confirm by the emperor Charles the five to release banishment and for the eve and man slayer by chance or upon their own defence and like offender upon satisfaction make to the next kinsman of the man-killer and to he that be rob not only to give they safe conduct to pass for forty day but also to pardon their crime so as the mulct or fine be gather for the prince not to his behoof and the counsel of Flanders approve the confession of the offender to be true but in case the magistrate of the place where the offender dwell require he to be there try it can be withstand and this office be of such dignity as knight for long time have execute the same many tribute be of old grant to the prince as perpetual tribute of the field of corn Oates cheese and lord which thing for food have long time be redeem with money the price be yearly set diverse by the counsel of account seat at lile and no doubt through trouble and civil war from the begin to this day all like burden be great increase both in number and measure which may more easy be conjecture by that which shall be say of this subject in the discourse of the unite province Flanders be most rule by municipal law and custom of town and city and for want of they by the civil law the law of Flanders forbid any man to give in legacy by his last will and testament more than the three of his good wherein be comprehend land in fee or that any stranger shall bear the office of magistracy yet stranger may there inherit their kinsman good contrary to the custom of France England and Scotland where the king have the good of all stranger die intestate and have there no child in Flanders no man be deprive of his mother inheritance for bastardy no not the child of a noble woman be a concubine except some municipal statute make by the prince do in some place prejudice they the citizen of about the year 1557 and those of Ghant some six year after have exclude those who be bear in adultery or incest from their mother inheritance but the provincial counsel of Flanders in the year 1532 give sentence that a bastard shall succeed in the see of his mother with privilege of age and sex even where the parent leave child lawful beget I return to the foresay province which I say to be unite in mutual league for of the commonwealth of the unite province protect by the queen of England their defence against the spaniard the say province at the first break out of the civil war when Antwerp be besiege humble and instant beseech Elizabeth queen of England to undertake their patronage and defence and to encourage and give she more power offer she the sovereignty of those province but the most wise queen with grave counsel and for weighty reason refuse to take they for subject perhaps among other reason of great weight fear lest undertake that war as queen of the province most part of the burden thereof shall fall upon her english subject think it probable that the netherlander be a people which have often take arm against their prince of all other thing lest bear new tax and imposition which they profess next the persecution for religion to be the chief cause of this war will always be apt to stir up sedition when her majesty as their prince shall impose but half the tribute and custom which themselves by general consent and for love of liberty have impose and bear with incredible patience during this war and howsoever her majesty desire their liberty shall be preserve yet the peace between England and Spain howsoever shake by many injury on both side provoke desire of revenge notwithstanding be not yet full break and it seem probable to i not know those counsel but by conjecture that her majesty be a woman the king of Spain be powerful and some of her subject be alienate from she for the reformation of religion think it more wisdom to suffer war for her just defence than herself open to begin the same yet will she not altogether neglect the afflict people of those province but resolve with the state thereof that they shall make count Maurice son to the prince of Orange general of their army govern their own affair and her majesty shall profess the defence of that afflict people with who England always have strict league of trade and amity till mean may be use for restore they to the king of Spain favour whereupon at the instant suit of the state the ten of August in the year 1585. her majesty grant they a aid of five thousand foot and a thousand horse to who her majesty be to give pay during the war yet so as the province be bind to make restitution of all her expense when the war shall be compose and for pledge of performance shall give into her majesty hand the town of Vlishing in Zealand with the adjoin castle of rammekin to be keep with a garrison of seven hundred english foot and the town of Brill with some adjoin fort to be keep with a garrison of 450 english foot the say province be bind as I say to make real satisfaction to her majesty at the end of the war for all expense aswell of the say force as of these garrison which amount yearly to the sum of one hundred twenty six thousand pound sterling and her majesty for the safety of her neighbour bear this intolerable burden till the year 1594 at which time sir Thomas bodley knight her majesty ambassador for those province by a new transaction diminish those great expense the wealth of those province be then much increase aswell by the concourse of merchant leave desolate Flanders to dwell in that flourish state as because they have bring many country by right of war to yield they contribution namely all the sea coast of Brabant some part of Flanders with the country vulgar call Ommelands Drent tgo Linghen Limbrough and Walkenbrough and have great increase their tribute aswell in Holland Zealand Freesland and Vtrecht as in Guelderland Zutphan Dlandt over ysel and last have take many strong town of no small moment namely deventer Zutphan Nimmenghen Stonwicke Bredaw Hulst Steneberg and Groninghen the state of those province be as I say thus increase and her majesty be force for many year to keep a strong army at home to subdue the irish rebel her majesty ambassador at the foresay time make a new transaction with the state for diminish the charge of the english force serve they and this help so long give by her majesty to the unite province can seem of small moment for howsoever the queen do not always keep the full number of the say force and sometime call home or cashier part of they yet she do always maintain the great part decrease or increase the same according to the necessity of the present affair and employ the force call home only in voyage by sea profitable aswell to the unite province as to England and that for a short time of summer service after send they back to serve the state the state who govern these province if they have make no change in particular which at pleasure they both can and use to do be grave man counsellor or the state or chief governor burgess vulgar call state choose by the people of each city and town not for a limit time but during pleasure and with full power who reside in the chief city of the province have care all joint of the provincial affair and each particular of his city or town affair and this counsel must needs be distract with diverse opinion arise from the diverse affair of each province city and town and the several commandment they receive at home these provincial state choose among themselves one two or three burgess for each province according to the condition and capacity of those that be choose for how many soever they be they have but one voice for their province at general meeting and these they send to reside at hage in Holland with like authority as they have there to govern the public affair of all the unite province and they be call the general state and as the provincial state may be diminish in number or increase according to the occasion of the public business or of any particular meeting and may be call home by the citizen who choose they so the general state choose by they to reside at hage enjoy their place upon like condition and out of these general state certain choose man be make counsellor to order the affair of war and to assist and direct the general of the army therein other be set over the affair of the admiralty other over the chancery of Brabant and other over diverse particular office I call they counsellor of the chancery of Brabant who manage the affair of Brabant belong to Holland this must always be understand that the burgess or state of Holland in respect of the dignity of that province many way increase and enrich above the rest have somewhat more authority and respect than any other but the wheel of the public state be turn by the senate of the general state reside at hage yet so as they do not take upon they to determine difficult matter without some diffidence till they have the consent of their particular city and province except they be make confident by the concur of eminent man who can draw or lead the people to approve of their do or in such case as by long practice they full know not unpleasing to the people so wary be they notwithstanding the provincial state from their community and the general state at hage from they have most ample power and absolute commission in express word to do any thing they judge profitable for the commonwealth and it be a remarkable thing to observe their art when in difficult case they desire to protract time or delude agent how the general state answer that they must first consult with the provincial state and they again answer that they must first know the pleasure of their community before they can determine and each of they have nothing more in his mouth than the consent of his superior for so they call they whereas if business be so to be dispatch no doubt great difficulty will arise in all particular action in the senate of the general state beside the state themselves count Maurice have as I think a double voice yet I never observe he to be present at their assembly the ambassador of England have likewise his voice and count Solms as I hear because he marry the widow of count Egmond and for his good desert in the service of the unite province have for himself and his heir the like privilege thus the commonwealth in general be aristocratical that be of the best man save that the people choose the great senate which rule all touch the commonwealth of particular city Amsterdam be the chief city of Holland where the great senate consist of thirty six chief citizen whereof commonwealth of particular city one die another be choose into his place and this senate yearly choose four consul who judge civil cause and have power to appoint ten judge of criminal cause vulgar call scout though they be not of that senate the other city be in like sort govern but according to the greatness of the city or town they have great or lesser number of senator the tribute tax and custom of all kind impose by mutual consent so great be the love of liberty or freedom be very burdensome and they willing bear they though for much less exaction impose by the king of Spain as they hold contrary to right and without consent of his subject they have the boldness to make war against a prince of such great power yet in respect of the unequal proportion of all contribution they be somewhat at odds among themselves and many time jar so as it seem no difficult thing to break their concord have not the common enemy and the eminent danger of spanish revenge together with the sweetness of freedom once taste force they to constant unity this I dare say that when they humble offer themselves vassal to the queen of England in the first infancy of their commonwealth if her majesty or any other prince whosoever undertake their protection have burden they with half the exaction they now bear it be more than probable that they will thereby have be so exasperate as they will have be more ready to have return under the obedience of the king of Spain who anger they have high provoke then to endure the yoke of such a protector for each tun of beer which they large swallow they pay into the exchequer six flemish shilling each shilling be six stiver I mean of beer sell abroad for they pay only four shilling for such beer as man brew for the use of their private family which frugality few or none use except perhaps some brew small beer for their family and indeed I doubt they will find small frugality in brew other beer for themselves if the cellar lie open to their servant and howsoever the ton be of diverse price according to the goodness of the beer namely of two three four five or six gulden the tun though at Leyden only the brewer may not sell beer of diverse price for fear of fraud in mix they yet there be no difference of the tribute they have excellent fat pasture whereof each acre be worth forty pound or more to be purchase
